Genesis1:1 In the beginning God created the heavens and the earth.
Genesis1:2 The earth was without form
Genesis1:3 Then God said
Genesis1:4 And God saw the light
Genesis1:5 God called the light Day
Genesis1:6 Then God said
Genesis1:7 Thus God made the firmament
Genesis1:8 And God called the firmament Heaven. So the evening and the morning were the second day.
Genesis1:9 Then God said
Genesis1:10 And God called the dry land Earth
Genesis1:11 Then God said
Genesis1:12 And the earth brought forth grass
Genesis1:13 So the evening and the morning were the third day.
Genesis1:14 Then God said
Genesis1:15 and let them be for lights in the firmament of the heavens to give light on the earth”; and it was so.
Genesis1:16 Then God made two great lights: the greater light to rule the day
Genesis1:17 God set them in the firmament of the heavens to give light on the earth
Genesis1:18 and to rule over the day and over the night
Genesis1:19 So the evening and the morning were the fourth day.
Genesis1:20 Then God said
Genesis1:21 So God created great sea creatures and every living thing that moves
Genesis1:22 And God blessed them
Genesis1:23 So the evening and the morning were the fifth day.
Genesis1:24 Then God said
Genesis1:25 And God made the beast of the earth according to its kind
Genesis1:26 Then God said
Genesis1:27 So God created man in His own image; in the image of God He created him; male and female He created them.
Genesis1:28 Then God blessed them
Genesis1:29 And God said
Genesis1:30 Also
Genesis1:31 Then God saw everything that He had made
Genesis2:1 Thus the heavens and the earth
Genesis2:2 And on the seventh day God ended His work which He had done
Genesis2:3 Then God blessed the seventh day and sanctified it
Genesis2:4 This is the history of the heavens and the earth when they were created
Genesis2:5 before any plant of the field was in the earth and before any herb of the field had grown. For the Lord God had not caused it to rain on the earth
Genesis2:6 but a mist went up from the earth and watered the whole face of the ground.
Genesis2:7 And the Lord God formed man of the dust of the ground
Genesis2:8 The Lord God planted a garden eastward in Eden
Genesis2:9 And out of the ground the Lord God made every tree grow that is pleasant to the sight and good for food. The tree of life was also in the midst of the garden
Genesis2:10 Now a river went out of Eden to water the garden
Genesis2:11 The name of the first is Pishon; it is the one which skirts the whole land of Havilah
Genesis2:12 And the gold of that land is good. Bdellium and the onyx stone are there.
Genesis2:13 The name of the second river is Gihon; it is the one which goes around the whole land of Cush.
Genesis2:14 The name of the third river is Hiddekel; it is the one which goes toward the east of Assyria. The fourth river is the Euphrates.
Genesis2:15 Then the Lord God took the man and put him in the garden of Eden to tend and keep it.
Genesis2:16 And the Lord God commanded the man
Genesis2:17 but of the tree of the knowledge of good and evil you shall not eat
Genesis2:18 And the Lord God said
Genesis2:19 Out of the ground the Lord God formed every beast of the field and every bird of the air
Genesis2:20 So Adam gave names to all cattle
Genesis2:21 And the Lord God caused a deep sleep to fall on Adam
Genesis2:22 Then the rib which the Lord God had taken from man He made into a woman
Genesis2:23 And Adam said: “This is now bone of my bones And flesh of my flesh; She shall be called Woman
Genesis2:24 Therefore a man shall leave his father and mother and be joined to his wife
Genesis2:25 And they were both naked

Genesis3:2 And the woman said to the serpent

Genesis3:4 Then the serpent said to the woman
Genesis3:5 For God knows that in the day you eat of it your eyes will be opened
Genesis3:6 So when the woman saw that the tree was good for food
Genesis3:7 Then the eyes of both of them were opened
Genesis3:8 And they heard the sound of the Lord God walking in the garden in the cool of the day
Genesis3:9 Then the Lord God called to Adam and said to him
Genesis3:10 So he said
Genesis3:11 And He said
Genesis3:12 Then the man said
Genesis3:13 And the Lord God said to the woman

Genesis3:15 And I will put enmity Between you and the woman
Genesis3:16 To the woman He said: “I will greatly multiply your sorrow and your conception; In pain you shall bring forth children; Your desire shall be for your husband
Genesis3:17 Then to Adam He said
Genesis3:18 Both thorns and thistles it shall bring forth for you
Genesis3:19 In the sweat of your face you shall eat bread Till you return to the ground
Genesis3:20 And Adam called his wife’s name Eve
Genesis3:21 Also for Adam and his wife the Lord God made tunics of skin
Genesis3:22 Then the Lord God said
Genesis3:23 therefore the Lord God sent him out of the garden of Eden to till the ground from which he was taken.

Genesis4:1 Now Adam knew Eve his wife
Genesis4:2 Then she bore again
Genesis4:3 And in the process of time it came to pass that Cain brought an offering of the fruit of the ground to the Lord.
Genesis4:4 Abel also brought of the firstborn of his flock and of their fat. And the Lord respected Abel and his offering
Genesis4:5 but He did not respect Cain and his offering. And Cain was very angry
Genesis4:6 So the Lord said to Cain
Genesis4:7 If you do well
Genesis4:8 Now Cain talked with Abel his brother; and it came to pass
Genesis4:9 Then the Lord said to Cain

Genesis4:12 When you till the ground
Genesis4:13 And Cain said to the Lord

Genesis4:15 And the Lord said to him
Genesis4:16 Then Cain went out from the presence of the Lord and dwelt in the land of Nod on the east of Eden.

Genesis4:20 And Adah bore Jabal. He was the father of those who dwell in tents and have livestock.

Genesis5:1 This is the book of the genealogy of Adam. In the day that God created man
Genesis5:2 He created them male and female
Genesis5:3 And Adam lived one hundred and thirty years
Genesis5:4 After he begot Seth
Genesis5:5 So all the days that Adam lived were nine hundred and thirty years; and he died.
Genesis5:6 Seth lived one hundred and five years
Genesis5:7 After he begot Enosh
Genesis5:8 So all the days of Seth were nine hundred and twelve years; and he died.
Genesis5:9 Enosh lived ninety years
Genesis5:10 After he begot Cainan
Genesis5:11 So all the days of Enosh were nine hundred and five years; and he died.
Genesis5:12 Cainan lived seventy years
Genesis5:13 After he begot Mahalalel
Genesis5:14 So all the days of Cainan were nine hundred and ten years; and he died.
Genesis5:15 Mahalalel lived sixty-five years
Genesis5:16 After he begot Jared
Genesis5:17 So all the days of Mahalalel were eight hundred and ninety-five years; and he died.
Genesis5:18 Jared lived one hundred and sixty-two years
Genesis5:19 After he begot Enoch
Genesis5:20 So all the days of Jared were nine hundred and sixty-two years; and he died.
Genesis5:21 Enoch lived sixty-five years
Genesis5:22 After he begot Methuselah
Genesis5:23 So all the days of Enoch were three hundred and sixty-five years.
Genesis5:24 And Enoch walked with God; and he was not
Genesis5:25 Methuselah lived one hundred and eighty-seven years
Genesis5:26 After he begot Lamech
Genesis5:27 So all the days of Methuselah were nine hundred and sixty-nine years; and he died.
Genesis5:28 Lamech lived one hundred and eighty-two years
Genesis5:29 And he called his name Noah
Genesis5:30 After he begot Noah
Genesis5:31 So all the days of Lamech were seven hundred and seventy-seven years; and he died.
Genesis5:32 And Noah was five hundred years old

Genesis6:2 that the sons of God saw the daughters of men
Genesis6:3 And the Lord said
Genesis6:4 There were giants on the earth in those days

Genesis6:9 This is the genealogy of Noah. Noah was a just man

Genesis6:12 So God looked upon the earth

Genesis6:15 And this is how you shall make it: The length of the ark shall be three hundred cubits
Genesis6:16 You shall make a window for the ark
Genesis6:17 And behold
Genesis6:18 But I will establish My covenant with you; and you shall go into the ark—you
Genesis6:19 And of every living thing of all flesh you shall bring two of every sort into the ark
Genesis6:20 Of the birds after their kind
Genesis6:21 And you shall take for yourself of all food that is eaten
Genesis6:22 Thus Noah did; according to all that God commanded him

Genesis7:2 You shall take with you seven each of every clean animal
Genesis7:3 also seven each of birds of the air
Genesis7:4 For after seven more days I will cause it to rain on the earth forty days and forty nights
Genesis7:5 And Noah did according to all that the Lord commanded him.

Genesis7:19 And the waters prevailed exceedingly on the earth
Genesis7:20 The waters prevailed fifteen cubits upward
Genesis7:21 And all flesh died that moved on the earth: birds and cattle and beasts and every creeping thing that creeps on the earth
Genesis7:22 All in whose nostrils was the breath of the spirit of life
Genesis7:23 So He destroyed all living things which were on the face of the ground: both man and cattle
Genesis7:24 And the waters prevailed on the earth one hundred and fifty days.
Genesis8:1 Then God remembered Noah
Genesis8:2 The fountains of the deep and the windows of heaven were also stopped
Genesis8:3 And the waters receded continually from the earth. At the end of the hundred and fifty days the waters decreased.
Genesis8:4 Then the ark rested in the seventh month
Genesis8:5 And the waters decreased continually until the tenth month. In the tenth month
Genesis8:6 So it came to pass
Genesis8:7 Then he sent out a raven
Genesis8:8 He also sent out from himself a dove
Genesis8:9 But the dove found no resting place for the sole of her foot
Genesis8:10 And he waited yet another seven days
Genesis8:11 Then the dove came to him in the evening
Genesis8:12 So he waited yet another seven days and sent out the dove
Genesis8:13 And it came to pass in the six hundred and first year
Genesis8:14 And in the second month

Exodus12:1 Now the Lord spoke to Moses and Aaron in the land of Egypt
Exodus12:2 “This month shall be your beginning of months; it shall be the first month of the year to you.
Exodus12:3 Speak to all the congregation of Israel
Exodus12:4 And if the household is too small for the lamb
Exodus12:5 Your lamb shall be without blemish
Exodus12:6 Now you shall keep it until the fourteenth day of the same month. Then the whole assembly of the congregation of Israel shall kill it at twilight.
Exodus12:7 And they shall take some of the blood and put it on the two doorposts and on the lintel of the houses where they eat it.
Exodus12:8 Then they shall eat the flesh on that night; roasted in fire
Exodus12:9 Do not eat it raw
Exodus12:10 You shall let none of it remain until morning
Exodus12:11 And thus you shall eat it: with a belt on your waist
Exodus12:12 ‘For I will pass through the land of Egypt on that night
Exodus12:13 Now the blood shall be a sign for you on the houses where you are. And when I see the blood
Exodus12:14 ‘So this day shall be to you a memorial; and you shall keep it as a feast to the Lord throughout your generations. You shall keep it as a feast by an everlasting ordinance.
Exodus12:15 Seven days you shall eat unleavened bread. On the first day you shall remove leaven from your houses. For whoever eats leavened bread from the first day until the seventh day
Exodus12:16 On the first day there shall be a holy convocation
Exodus12:17 So you shall observe the Feast of Unleavened Bread
Exodus12:18 In the first month
Exodus12:19 For seven days no leaven shall be found in your houses
Exodus12:20 You shall eat nothing leavened; in all your dwellings you shall eat unleavened bread.’ ”

Exodus12:22 And you shall take a bunch of hyssop

Exodus12:24 And you shall observe this thing as an ordinance for you and your sons forever.
Exodus12:25 It will come to pass when you come to the land which the Lord will give you
Exodus12:26 And it shall be
Exodus12:27 that you shall say

Exodus27:2 You shall make its horns on its four corners; its horns shall be of one piece with it. And you shall overlay it with bronze.
Exodus27:3 Also you shall make its pans to receive its ashes
Exodus27:4 You shall make a grate for it
Exodus27:5 You shall put it under the rim of the altar beneath
Exodus27:6 And you shall make poles for the altar
Exodus27:7 The poles shall be put in the rings
Exodus27:8 You shall make it hollow with boards; as it was shown you on the mountain
Exodus27:9 “You shall also make the court of the tabernacle. For the south side there shall be hangings for the court made of fine woven linen
Exodus27:10 And its twenty pillars and their twenty sockets shall be bronze. The hooks of the pillars and their bands shall be silver.
Exodus27:11 Likewise along the length of the north side there shall be hangings one hundred cubits long
Exodus27:12 “And along the width of the court on the west side shall be hangings of fifty cubits
Exodus27:13 The width of the court on the east side shall be fifty cubits.
Exodus27:14 The hangings on one side of the gate shall be fifteen cubits
Exodus27:15 And on the other side shall be hangings of fifteen cubits
Exodus27:16 “For the gate of the court there shall be a screen twenty cubits long
Exodus27:17 All the pillars around the court shall have bands of silver; their hooks shall be of silver and their sockets of bronze.

Exodus28:1 “Now take Aaron your brother
Exodus28:2 And you shall make holy garments for Aaron your brother
Exodus28:3 So you shall speak to all who are gifted artisans
Exodus28:4 And these are the garments which they shall make: a breastplate
Exodus28:5 “They shall take the gold
Exodus28:6 and they shall make the ephod of gold
Exodus28:7 It shall have two shoulder straps joined at its two edges
Exodus28:8 And the intricately woven band of the ephod
Exodus28:9 “Then you shall take two onyx stones and engrave on them the names of the sons of Israel:
Exodus28:10 six of their names on one stone and six names on the other stone
Exodus28:11 With the work of an engraver in stone
Exodus28:12 And you shall put the two stones on the shoulders of the ephod as memorial stones for the sons of Israel. So Aaron shall bear their names before the Lord on his two shoulders as a memorial.
Exodus28:13 You shall also make settings of gold
Exodus28:14 and you shall make two chains of pure gold like braided cords
Exodus28:15 “You shall make the breastplate of judgment. Artistically woven according to the workmanship of the ephod you shall make it: of gold
Exodus28:16 It shall be doubled into a square: a span shall be its length
Exodus28:17 And you shall put settings of stones in it
Exodus28:18 the second row shall be a turquoise
Exodus28:19 the third row
Exodus28:20 and the fourth row
Exodus28:21 And the stones shall have the names of the sons of Israel
Exodus28:22 “You shall make chains for the breastplate at the end
Exodus28:23 And you shall make two rings of gold for the breastplate
Exodus28:24 Then you shall put the two braided chains of gold in the two rings which are on the ends of the breastplate;
Exodus28:25 and the other two ends of the two braided chains you shall fasten to the two settings
Exodus28:26 “You shall make two rings of gold
Exodus28:27 And two other rings of gold you shall make
Exodus28:28 They shall bind the breastplate by means of its rings to the rings of the ephod
Exodus28:29 “So Aaron shall bear the names of the sons of Israel on the breastplate of judgment over his heart
Exodus28:30 And you shall put in the breastplate of judgment the Urim and the Thummim
Exodus28:31 “You shall make the robe of the ephod all of blue.
Exodus28:32 There shall be an opening for his head in the middle of it; it shall have a woven binding all around its opening
Exodus28:33 And upon its hem you shall make pomegranates of blue
Exodus28:34 a golden bell and a pomegranate
Exodus28:35 And it shall be upon Aaron when he ministers
Exodus28:36 “You shall also make a plate of pure gold and engrave on it
Exodus28:37 And you shall put it on a blue cord
Exodus28:38 So it shall be on Aaron’s forehead
Exodus28:39 “You shall skillfully weave the tunic of fine linen thread
Exodus28:40 “For Aaron’s sons you shall make tunics
Exodus28:41 So you shall put them on Aaron your brother and on his sons with him. You shall anoint them
Exodus28:42 And you shall make for them linen trousers to cover their nakedness; they shall reach from the waist to the thighs.
Exodus28:43 They shall be on Aaron and on his sons when they come into the tabernacle of meeting
Exodus29:1 “And this is what you shall do to them to hallow them for ministering to Me as priests: Take one young bull and two rams without blemish
Exodus29:2 and unleavened bread
Exodus29:3 You shall put them in one basket and bring them in the basket
Exodus29:4 “And Aaron and his sons you shall bring to the door of the tabernacle of meeting
Exodus29:5 Then you shall take the garments
Exodus29:6 You shall put the turban on his head
Exodus29:7 And you shall take the anointing oil
Exodus29:8 Then you shall bring his sons and put tunics on them.
Exodus29:9 And you shall gird them with sashes
Exodus29:10 “You shall also have the bull brought before the tabernacle of meeting
Exodus29:11 Then you shall kill the bull before the Lord
Exodus29:12 You shall take some of the blood of the bull and put it on the horns of the altar with your finger
Exodus29:13 And you shall take all the fat that covers the entrails
Exodus29:14 But the flesh of the bull
Exodus29:15 “You shall also take one ram
Exodus29:16 and you shall kill the ram
Exodus29:17 Then you shall cut the ram in pieces
Exodus29:18 And you shall burn the whole ram on the altar. It is a burnt offering to the Lord; it is a sweet aroma
Exodus29:19 “You shall also take the other ram
Exodus29:20 Then you shall kill the ram
Exodus29:21 And you shall take some of the blood that is on the altar
Exodus29:22 “Also you shall take the fat of the ram
Exodus29:23 one loaf of bread
Exodus29:24 and you shall put all these in the hands of Aaron and in the hands of his sons
Exodus29:25 You shall receive them back from their hands and burn them on the altar as a burnt offering
Exodus29:26 “Then you shall take the breast of the ram of Aaron’s consecration and wave it as a wave offering before the Lord; and it shall be your portion.
Exodus29:27 And from the ram of the consecration you shall consecrate the breast of the wave offering which is waved
Exodus29:28 It shall be from the children of Israel for Aaron and his sons by a statute forever. For it is a heave offering; it shall be a heave offering from the children of Israel from the sacrifices of their peace offerings
Exodus29:29 “And the holy garments of Aaron shall be his sons’ after him
Exodus29:30 That son who becomes priest in his place shall put them on for seven days
Exodus29:31 “And you shall take the ram of the consecration and boil its flesh in the holy place.
Exodus29:32 Then Aaron and his sons shall eat the flesh of the ram
Exodus29:33 They shall eat those things with which the atonement was made
Exodus29:34 And if any of the flesh of the consecration offerings
Exodus29:35 “Thus you shall do to Aaron and his sons
Exodus29:36 And you shall offer a bull every day as a sin offering for atonement. You shall cleanse the altar when you make atonement for it
Exodus29:37 Seven days you shall make atonement for the altar and sanctify it. And the altar shall be most holy. Whatever touches the altar must be holy.
Exodus29:38 “Now this is what you shall offer on the altar: two lambs of the first year
Exodus29:39 One lamb you shall offer in the morning
Exodus29:40 With the one lamb shall be one-tenth of an ephah of flour mixed with one-fourth of a hin of pressed oil
Exodus29:41 And the other lamb you shall offer at twilight; and you shall offer with it the grain offering and the drink offering
Exodus29:42 This shall be a continual burnt offering throughout your generations at the door of the tabernacle of meeting before the Lord
Exodus29:43 And there I will meet with the children of Israel
Exodus29:44 So I will consecrate the tabernacle of meeting and the altar. I will also consecrate both Aaron and his sons to minister to Me as priests.
Exodus29:45 I will dwell among the children of Israel and will be their God.
Exodus29:46 And they shall know that I am the Lord their God

Exodus30:15 The rich shall not give more and the poor shall not give less than half a shekel
Exodus30:16 And you shall take the atonement money of the children of Israel

Exodus39:18 The two ends of the two braided chains they fastened in the two settings
Exodus39:19 And they made two rings of gold and put them on the two ends of the breastplate
Exodus39:20 They made two other gold rings and put them on the two shoulder straps
Exodus39:21 And they bound the breastplate by means of its rings to the rings of the ephod with a blue cord
Exodus39:22 He made the robe of the ephod of woven work
Exodus39:23 And there was an opening in the middle of the robe
Exodus39:24 They made on the hem of the robe pomegranates of blue
Exodus39:25 And they made bells of pure gold
Exodus39:26 a bell and a pomegranate
Exodus39:27 They made tunics
Exodus39:28 a turban of fine linen
Exodus39:29 and a sash of fine woven linen with blue
Exodus39:30 Then they made the plate of the holy crown of pure gold
Exodus39:31 And they tied to it a blue cord
Exodus39:32 Thus all the work of the tabernacle of the tent of meeting was finished. And the children of Israel did according to all that the Lord had commanded Moses; so they did.
Exodus39:33 And they brought the tabernacle to Moses
Exodus39:34 the covering of ram skins dyed red
Exodus39:35 the ark of the Testimony with its poles
Exodus39:36 the table
Exodus39:37 the pure gold lampstand with its lamps (the lamps set in order)
Exodus39:38 the gold altar
Exodus39:39 the bronze altar
Exodus39:40 the hangings of the court
Exodus39:41 and the garments of ministry
Exodus39:42 According to all that the Lord had commanded Moses
Exodus39:43 Then Moses looked over all the work
Exodus40:1 Then the Lord spoke to Moses
Exodus40:2 “On the first day of the first month you shall set up the tabernacle of the tent of meeting.
Exodus40:3 You shall put in it the ark of the Testimony
Exodus40:4 You shall bring in the table and arrange the things that are to be set in order on it; and you shall bring in the lampstand and light its lamps.
Exodus40:5 You shall also set the altar of gold for the incense before the ark of the Testimony
Exodus40:6 Then you shall set the altar of the burnt offering before the door of the tabernacle of the tent of meeting.
Exodus40:7 And you shall set the laver between the tabernacle of meeting and the altar
Exodus40:8 You shall set up the court all around
Exodus40:9 “And you shall take the anointing oil
Exodus40:10 You shall anoint the altar of the burnt offering and all its utensils
Exodus40:11 And you shall anoint the laver and its base
Exodus40:12 “Then you shall bring Aaron and his sons to the door of the tabernacle of meeting and wash them with water.
Exodus40:13 You shall put the holy garments on Aaron
Exodus40:14 And you shall bring his sons and clothe them with tunics.
Exodus40:15 You shall anoint them
Exodus40:16 Thus Moses did; according to all that the Lord had commanded him
Exodus40:17 And it came to pass in the first month of the second year
Exodus40:18 So Moses raised up the tabernacle
Exodus40:19 And he spread out the tent over the tabernacle and put the covering of the tent on top of it
Exodus40:20 He took the Testimony and put it into the ark
Exodus40:21 And he brought the ark into the tabernacle
Exodus40:22 He put the table in the tabernacle of meeting
Exodus40:23 and he set the bread in order upon it before the Lord
Exodus40:24 He put the lampstand in the tabernacle of meeting
Exodus40:25 and he lit the lamps before the Lord
Exodus40:26 He put the gold altar in the tabernacle of meeting in front of the veil;
Exodus40:27 and he burned sweet incense on it
Exodus40:28 He hung up the screen at the door of the tabernacle.
Exodus40:29 And he put the altar of burnt offering before the door of the tabernacle of the tent of meeting
Exodus40:30 He set the laver between the tabernacle of meeting and the altar
Exodus40:31 and Moses
Exodus40:32 Whenever they went into the tabernacle of meeting
Exodus40:33 And he raised up the court all around the tabernacle and the altar
Exodus40:34 Then the cloud covered the tabernacle of meeting
Exodus40:35 And Moses was not able to enter the tabernacle of meeting
Exodus40:36 Whenever the cloud was taken up from above the tabernacle
Exodus40:37 But if the cloud was not taken up
Exodus40:38 For the cloud of the Lord was above the tabernacle by day
Leviticus1:1 Now the Lord called to Moses
Leviticus1:2 “Speak to the children of Israel
Leviticus1:3 ‘If his offering is a burnt sacrifice of the herd
Leviticus1:4 Then he shall put his hand on the head of the burnt offering
Leviticus1:5 He shall kill the bull before the Lord; and the priests
Leviticus1:6 And he shall skin the burnt offering and cut it into its pieces.
Leviticus1:7 The sons of Aaron the priest shall put fire on the altar
Leviticus1:8 Then the priests
Leviticus1:9 but he shall wash its entrails and its legs with water. And the priest shall burn all on the altar as a burnt sacrifice
Leviticus1:10 ‘If his offering is of the flocks—of the sheep or of the goats—as a burnt sacrifice
Leviticus1:11 He shall kill it on the north side of the altar before the Lord; and the priests
Leviticus1:12 And he shall cut it into its pieces
Leviticus1:13 but he shall wash the entrails and the legs with water. Then the priest shall bring it all and burn it on the altar; it is a burnt sacrifice
Leviticus1:14 ‘And if the burnt sacrifice of his offering to the Lord is of birds
Leviticus1:15 The priest shall bring it to the altar
Leviticus1:16 And he shall remove its crop with its feathers and cast it beside the altar on the east side
Leviticus1:17 Then he shall split it at its wings
Leviticus2:1 ‘When anyone offers a grain offering to the Lord
Leviticus2:2 He shall bring it to Aaron’s sons
Leviticus2:3 The rest of the grain offering shall be Aaron’s and his sons’. It is most holy of the offerings to the Lord made by fire.
Leviticus2:4 ‘And if you bring as an offering a grain offering baked in the oven
Leviticus2:5 But if your offering is a grain offering baked in a pan
Leviticus2:6 You shall break it in pieces and pour oil on it; it is a grain offering.
Leviticus2:7 ‘If your offering is a grain offering baked in a covered pan
Leviticus2:8 You shall bring the grain offering that is made of these things to the Lord. And when it is presented to the priest
Leviticus2:9 Then the priest shall take from the grain offering a memorial portion
Leviticus2:10 And what is left of the grain offering shall be Aaron’s and his sons’. It is most holy of the offerings to the Lord made by fire.
Leviticus2:11 ‘No grain offering which you bring to the Lord shall be made with leaven
Leviticus2:12 As for the offering of the firstfruits
Leviticus2:13 And every offering of your grain offering you shall season with salt; you shall not allow the salt of the covenant of your God to be lacking from your grain offering. With all your offerings you shall offer salt.
Leviticus2:14 ‘If you offer a grain offering of your firstfruits to the Lord
Leviticus2:15 And you shall put oil on it
Leviticus2:16 Then the priest shall burn the memorial portion: part of its beaten grain and part of its oil
Leviticus3:1 ‘When his offering is a sacrifice of a peace offering
Leviticus3:2 And he shall lay his hand on the head of his offering
Leviticus3:3 Then he shall offer from the sacrifice of the peace offering an offering made by fire to the Lord. The fat that covers the entrails and all the fat that is on the entrails
Leviticus3:4 the two kidneys and the fat that is on them by the flanks
Leviticus3:5 and Aaron’s sons shall burn it on the altar upon the burnt sacrifice
Leviticus3:6 ‘If his offering as a sacrifice of a peace offering to the Lord is of the flock
Leviticus3:7 If he offers a lamb as his offering
Leviticus3:8 And he shall lay his hand on the head of his offering
Leviticus3:9 ‘Then he shall offer from the sacrifice of the peace offering
Leviticus3:10 the two kidneys and the fat that is on them by the flanks
Leviticus3:11 and the priest shall burn them on the altar as food
Leviticus3:12 ‘And if his offering is a goat
Leviticus3:13 He shall lay his hand on its head and kill it before the tabernacle of meeting; and the sons of Aaron shall sprinkle its blood all around on the altar.
Leviticus3:14 Then he shall offer from it his offering
Leviticus3:15 the two kidneys and the fat that is on them by the flanks
Leviticus3:16 and the priest shall burn them on the altar as food
Leviticus3:17 ‘This shall be a perpetual statute throughout your generations in all your dwellings: you shall eat neither fat nor blood.’ ”
Leviticus4:1 Now the Lord spoke to Moses
Leviticus4:2 “Speak to the children of Israel
Leviticus4:3 if the anointed priest sins
Leviticus4:4 He shall bring the bull to the door of the tabernacle of meeting before the Lord
Leviticus4:5 Then the anointed priest shall take some of the bull’s blood and bring it to the tabernacle of meeting.
Leviticus4:6 The priest shall dip his finger in the blood and sprinkle some of the blood seven times before the Lord
Leviticus4:7 And the priest shall put some of the blood on the horns of the altar of sweet incense before the Lord
Leviticus4:8 He shall take from it all the fat of the bull as the sin offering. The fat that covers the entrails and all the fat which is on the entrails
Leviticus4:9 the two kidneys and the fat that is on them by the flanks
Leviticus4:10 as it was taken from the bull of the sacrifice of the peace offering; and the priest shall burn them on the altar of the burnt offering.
Leviticus4:11 But the bull’s hide and all its flesh
Leviticus4:12 the whole bull he shall carry outside the camp to a clean place
Leviticus4:13 ‘Now if the whole congregation of Israel sins unintentionally
Leviticus4:14 when the sin which they have committed becomes known
Leviticus4:15 And the elders of the congregation shall lay their hands on the head of the bull before the Lord. Then the bull shall be killed before the Lord.
Leviticus4:16 The anointed priest shall bring some of the bull’s blood to the tabernacle of meeting.
Leviticus4:17 Then the priest shall dip his finger in the blood and sprinkle it seven times before the Lord
Leviticus4:18 And he shall put some of the blood on the horns of the altar which is before the Lord
Leviticus4:19 He shall take all the fat from it and burn it on the altar.
Leviticus4:20 And he shall do with the bull as he did with the bull as a sin offering; thus he shall do with it. So the priest shall make atonement for them
Leviticus4:21 Then he shall carry the bull outside the camp
Leviticus4:22 ‘When a ruler has sinned
Leviticus4:23 or if his sin which he has committed comes to his knowledge
Leviticus4:24 And he shall lay his hand on the head of the goat
Leviticus4:25 The priest shall take some of the blood of the sin offering with his finger
Leviticus4:26 And he shall burn all its fat on the altar
Leviticus4:27 ‘If anyone of the common people sins unintentionally by doing something against any of the commandments of the Lord in anything which ought not to be done
Leviticus4:28 or if his sin which he has committed comes to his knowledge
Leviticus4:29 And he shall lay his hand on the head of the sin offering
Leviticus4:30 Then the priest shall take some of its blood with his finger
Leviticus4:31 He shall remove all its fat
Leviticus4:32 ‘If he brings a lamb as his sin offering
Leviticus4:33 Then he shall lay his hand on the head of the sin offering
Leviticus4:34 The priest shall take some of the blood of the sin offering with his finger
Leviticus4:35 He shall remove all its fat
Leviticus5:1 ‘If a person sins in hearing the utterance of an oath
Leviticus5:2 ‘Or if a person touches any unclean thing
Leviticus5:3 Or if he touches human uncleanness—whatever uncleanness with which a man may be defiled
Leviticus5:4 ‘Or if a person swears
Leviticus5:5 ‘And it shall be
Leviticus5:6 and he shall bring his trespass offering to the Lord for his sin which he has committed
Leviticus5:7 ‘If he is not able to bring a lamb
Leviticus5:8 And he shall bring them to the priest
Leviticus5:9 Then he shall sprinkle some of the blood of the sin offering on the side of the altar
Leviticus5:10 And he shall offer the second as a burnt offering according to the prescribed manner. So the priest shall make atonement on his behalf for his sin which he has committed
Leviticus5:11 ‘But if he is not able to bring two turtledoves or two young pigeons
Leviticus5:12 Then he shall bring it to the priest
Leviticus5:13 The priest shall make atonement for him
Leviticus5:14 Then the Lord spoke to Moses
Leviticus5:15 “If a person commits a trespass
Leviticus5:16 And he shall make restitution for the harm that he has done in regard to the holy thing
Leviticus5:17 “If a person sins
Leviticus5:18 And he shall bring to the priest a ram without blemish from the flock
Leviticus5:19 It is a trespass offering; he has certainly trespassed against the Lord.”
Leviticus6:1 And the Lord spoke to Moses
Leviticus6:2 “If a person sins and commits a trespass against the Lord by lying to his neighbor about what was delivered to him for safekeeping
Leviticus6:3 or if he has found what was lost and lies concerning it
Leviticus6:4 then it shall be
Leviticus6:5 or all that about which he has sworn falsely. He shall restore its full value
Leviticus6:6 And he shall bring his trespass offering to the Lord
Leviticus6:7 So the priest shall make atonement for him before the Lord
Leviticus6:8 Then the Lord spoke to Moses
Leviticus6:9 “Command Aaron and his sons
Leviticus6:10 And the priest shall put on his linen garment
Leviticus6:11 Then he shall take off his garments
Leviticus6:12 And the fire on the altar shall be kept burning on it; it shall not be put out. And the priest shall burn wood on it every morning
Leviticus6:13 A fire shall always be burning on the altar; it shall never go out.
Leviticus6:14 ‘This is the law of the grain offering: The sons of Aaron shall offer it on the altar before the Lord.
Leviticus6:15 He shall take from it his handful of the fine flour of the grain offering
Leviticus6:16 And the remainder of it Aaron and his sons shall eat; with unleavened bread it shall be eaten in a holy place; in the court of the tabernacle of meeting they shall eat it.
Leviticus6:17 It shall not be baked with leaven. I have given it as their portion of My offerings made by fire; it is most holy
Leviticus6:18 All the males among the children of Aaron may eat it. It shall be a statute forever in your generations concerning the offerings made by fire to the Lord. Everyone who touches them must be holy.’ ”
Leviticus6:19 And the Lord spoke to Moses
Leviticus6:20 “This is the offering of Aaron and his sons
Leviticus6:21 It shall be made in a pan with oil. When it is mixed
Leviticus6:22 The priest from among his sons
Leviticus6:23 For every grain offering for the priest shall be wholly burned. It shall not be eaten.”
Leviticus6:24 Also the Lord spoke to Moses
Leviticus6:25 “Speak to Aaron and to his sons
Leviticus6:26 The priest who offers it for sin shall eat it. In a holy place it shall be eaten
Leviticus6:27 Everyone who touches its flesh must be holy. And when its blood is sprinkled on any garment
Leviticus6:28 But the earthen vessel in which it is boiled shall be broken. And if it is boiled in a bronze pot
Leviticus6:29 All the males among the priests may eat it. It is most holy.
Leviticus6:30 But no sin offering from which any of the blood is brought into the tabernacle of meeting
Leviticus7:1 ‘Likewise this is the law of the trespass offering (it is most holy):
Leviticus7:2 In the place where they kill the burnt offering they shall kill the trespass offering. And its blood he shall sprinkle all around on the altar.
Leviticus7:3 And he shall offer from it all its fat. The fat tail and the fat that covers the entrails
Leviticus7:4 the two kidneys and the fat that is on them by the flanks
Leviticus7:5 and the priest shall burn them on the altar as an offering made by fire to the Lord. It is a trespass offering.
Leviticus7:6 Every male among the priests may eat it. It shall be eaten in a holy place. It is most holy.
Leviticus7:7 The trespass offering is like the sin offering; there is one law for them both: the priest who makes atonement with it shall have it.
Leviticus7:8 And the priest who offers anyone’s burnt offering
Leviticus7:9 Also every grain offering that is baked in the oven and all that is prepared in the covered pan
Leviticus7:10 Every grain offering
Leviticus7:11 ‘This is the law of the sacrifice of peace offerings which he shall offer to the Lord:
Leviticus7:12 If he offers it for a thanksgiving
Leviticus7:13 Besides the cakes
Leviticus7:14 And from it he shall offer one cake from each offering as a heave offering to the Lord. It shall belong to the priest who sprinkles the blood of the peace offering.
Leviticus7:15 ‘The flesh of the sacrifice of his peace offering for thanksgiving shall be eaten the same day it is offered. He shall not leave any of it until morning.
Leviticus7:16 But if the sacrifice of his offering is a vow or a voluntary offering
Leviticus7:17 the remainder of the flesh of the sacrifice on the third day must be burned with fire.
Leviticus7:18 And if any of the flesh of the sacrifice of his peace offering is eaten at all on the third day
Leviticus7:19 ‘The flesh that touches any unclean thing shall not be eaten. It shall be burned with fire. And as for the clean flesh
Leviticus7:20 But the person who eats the flesh of the sacrifice of the peace offering that belongs to the Lord
Leviticus7:21 Moreover the person who touches any unclean thing
Leviticus7:22 And the Lord spoke to Moses
Leviticus7:23 “Speak to the children of Israel
Leviticus7:24 And the fat of an animal that dies naturally
Leviticus7:25 For whoever eats the fat of the animal of which men offer an offering made by fire to the Lord
Leviticus7:26 Moreover you shall not eat any blood in any of your dwellings
Leviticus7:27 Whoever eats any blood
Leviticus7:28 Then the Lord spoke to Moses
Leviticus7:29 “Speak to the children of Israel
Leviticus7:30 His own hands shall bring the offerings made by fire to the Lord. The fat with the breast he shall bring
Leviticus7:31 And the priest shall burn the fat on the altar
Leviticus7:32 Also the right thigh you shall give to the priest as a heave offering from the sacrifices of your peace offerings.
Leviticus7:33 He among the sons of Aaron
Leviticus7:34 For the breast of the wave offering and the thigh of the heave offering I have taken from the children of Israel
Leviticus7:35 This is the consecrated portion for Aaron and his sons
Leviticus7:36 The Lord commanded this to be given to them by the children of Israel
Leviticus7:37 This is the law of the burnt offering
Leviticus7:38 which the Lord commanded Moses on Mount Sinai

Leviticus8:2 “Take Aaron and his sons with him
Leviticus8:3 and gather all the congregation together at the door of the tabernacle of meeting.”
Leviticus8:4 So Moses did as the Lord commanded him. And the congregation was gathered together at the door of the tabernacle of meeting.
Leviticus8:5 And Moses said to the congregation
Leviticus8:6 Then Moses brought Aaron and his sons and washed them with water.
Leviticus8:7 And he put the tunic on him
Leviticus8:8 Then he put the breastplate on him
Leviticus8:9 And he put the turban on his head. Also on the turban
Leviticus8:10 Also Moses took the anointing oil
Leviticus8:11 He sprinkled some of it on the altar seven times
Leviticus8:12 And he poured some of the anointing oil on Aaron’s head and anointed him
Leviticus8:13 Then Moses brought Aaron’s sons and put tunics on them
Leviticus8:14 And he brought the bull for the sin offering. Then Aaron and his sons laid their hands on the head of the bull for the sin offering
Leviticus8:15 and Moses killed it. Then he took the blood
Leviticus8:16 Then he took all the fat that was on the entrails
Leviticus8:17 But the bull
Leviticus8:18 Then he brought the ram as the burnt offering. And Aaron and his sons laid their hands on the head of the ram
Leviticus8:19 and Moses killed it. Then he sprinkled the blood all around on the altar.
Leviticus8:20 And he cut the ram into pieces; and Moses burned the head
Leviticus8:21 Then he washed the entrails and the legs in water. And Moses burned the whole ram on the altar. It was a burnt sacrifice for a sweet aroma
Leviticus8:22 And he brought the second ram
Leviticus8:23 and Moses killed it. Also he took some of its blood and put it on the tip of Aaron’s right ear
Leviticus8:24 Then he brought Aaron’s sons. And Moses put some of the blood on the tips of their right ears
Leviticus8:25 Then he took the fat and the fat tail
Leviticus8:26 and from the basket of unleavened bread that was before the Lord he took one unleavened cake
Leviticus8:27 and he put all these in Aaron’s hands and in his sons’ hands
Leviticus8:28 Then Moses took them from their hands and burned them on the altar
Leviticus8:29 And Moses took the breast and waved it as a wave offering before the Lord. It was Moses’ part of the ram of consecration
Leviticus8:30 Then Moses took some of the anointing oil and some of the blood which was on the altar
Leviticus8:31 And Moses said to Aaron and his sons
Leviticus8:32 What remains of the flesh and of the bread you shall burn with fire.
Leviticus8:33 And you shall not go outside the door of the tabernacle of meeting for seven days
Leviticus8:34 As he has done this day
Leviticus8:35 Therefore you shall stay at the door of the tabernacle of meeting day and night for seven days
Leviticus8:36 So Aaron and his sons did all the things that the Lord had commanded by the hand of Moses.

Leviticus9:3 And to the children of Israel you shall speak
Leviticus9:4 also a bull and a ram as peace offerings
Leviticus9:5 So they brought what Moses commanded before the tabernacle of meeting. And all the congregation drew near and stood before the Lord.

Leviticus9:7 And Moses said to Aaron
Leviticus9:8 Aaron therefore went to the altar and killed the calf of the sin offering
Leviticus9:9 Then the sons of Aaron brought the blood to him. And he dipped his finger in the blood
Leviticus9:10 But the fat
Leviticus9:11 The flesh and the hide he burned with fire outside the camp.
Leviticus9:12 And he killed the burnt offering; and Aaron’s sons presented to him the blood
Leviticus9:13 Then they presented the burnt offering to him
Leviticus9:14 And he washed the entrails and the legs
Leviticus9:15 Then he brought the people’s offering
Leviticus9:16 And he brought the burnt offering and offered it according to the prescribed manner.
Leviticus9:17 Then he brought the grain offering
Leviticus9:18 He also killed the bull and the ram as sacrifices of peace offerings
Leviticus9:19 and the fat from the bull and the ram—the fatty tail
Leviticus9:20 and they put the fat on the breasts. Then he burned the fat on the altar;

Leviticus9:22 Then Aaron lifted his hand toward the people
Leviticus9:23 And Moses and Aaron went into the tabernacle of meeting
Leviticus9:24 and fire came out from before the Lord and consumed the burnt offering and the fat on the altar. When all the people saw it

Leviticus10:2 So fire went out from the Lord and devoured them

Leviticus10:5 So they went near and carried them by their tunics out of the camp

Leviticus10:7 You shall not go out from the door of the tabernacle of meeting

Leviticus10:9 “Do not drink wine or intoxicating drink
Leviticus10:10 that you may distinguish between holy and unholy
Leviticus10:11 and that you may teach the children of Israel all the statutes which the Lord has spoken to them by the hand of Moses.”

Leviticus10:13 You shall eat it in a holy place
Leviticus10:14 The breast of the wave offering and the thigh of the heave offering you shall eat in a clean place
Leviticus10:15 The thigh of the heave offering and the breast of the wave offering they shall bring with the offerings of fat made by fire

Leviticus10:17 “Why have you not eaten the sin offering in a holy place
Leviticus10:18 See! Its blood was not brought inside the holy place; indeed you should have eaten it in a holy place
Leviticus10:19 And Aaron said to Moses
Leviticus10:20 So when Moses heard that
Leviticus11:1 Now the Lord spoke to Moses and Aaron
Leviticus11:2 “Speak to the children of Israel
Leviticus11:3 Among the animals
Leviticus11:4 Nevertheless these you shall not eat among those that chew the cud or those that have cloven hooves: the camel
Leviticus11:5 the rock hyrax
Leviticus11:6 the hare
Leviticus11:7 and the swine
Leviticus11:8 Their flesh you shall not eat
Leviticus11:9 ‘These you may eat of all that are in the water: whatever in the water has fins and scales
Leviticus11:10 But all in the seas or in the rivers that do not have fins and scales
Leviticus11:11 They shall be an abomination to you; you shall not eat their flesh
Leviticus11:12 Whatever in the water does not have fins or scales—that shall be an abomination to you.
Leviticus11:13 ‘And these you shall regard as an abomination among the birds; they shall not be eaten
Leviticus11:14 the kite
Leviticus11:15 every raven after its kind
Leviticus11:16 the ostrich
Leviticus11:17 the little owl
Leviticus11:18 the white owl
Leviticus11:19 the stork
Leviticus11:20 ‘All flying insects that creep on all fours shall be an abomination to you.
Leviticus11:21 Yet these you may eat of every flying insect that creeps on all fours: those which have jointed legs above their feet with which to leap on the earth.
Leviticus11:22 These you may eat: the locust after its kind
Leviticus11:23 But all other flying insects which have four feet shall be an abomination to you.
Leviticus11:24 ‘By these you shall become unclean; whoever touches the carcass of any of them shall be unclean until evening;
Leviticus11:25 whoever carries part of the carcass of any of them shall wash his clothes and be unclean until evening:
Leviticus11:26 The carcass of any animal which divides the foot
Leviticus11:27 And whatever goes on its paws
Leviticus11:28 Whoever carries any such carcass shall wash his clothes and be unclean until evening. It is unclean to you.
Leviticus11:29 ‘These also shall be unclean to you among the creeping things that creep on the earth: the mole
Leviticus11:30 the gecko
Leviticus11:31 These are unclean to you among all that creep. Whoever touches them when they are dead shall be unclean until evening.
Leviticus11:32 Anything on which any of them falls
Leviticus11:33 Any earthen vessel into which any of them falls you shall break; and whatever is in it shall be unclean:
Leviticus11:34 in such a vessel
Leviticus11:35 And everything on which a part of any such carcass falls shall be unclean; whether it is an oven or cooking stove
Leviticus11:36 Nevertheless a spring or a cistern
Leviticus11:37 And if a part of any such carcass falls on any planting seed which is to be sown
Leviticus11:38 But if water is put on the seed
Leviticus11:39 ‘And if any animal which you may eat dies
Leviticus11:40 He who eats of its carcass shall wash his clothes and be unclean until evening. He also who carries its carcass shall wash his clothes and be unclean until evening.
Leviticus11:41 ‘And every creeping thing that creeps on the earth shall be an abomination. It shall not be eaten.
Leviticus11:42 Whatever crawls on its belly
Leviticus11:43 You shall not make yourselves abominable with any creeping thing that creeps; nor shall you make yourselves unclean with them
Leviticus11:44 For I am the Lord your God. You shall therefore consecrate yourselves
Leviticus11:45 For I am the Lord who brings you up out of the land of Egypt
Leviticus11:46 ‘This is the law of the animals and the birds and every living creature that moves in the waters
Leviticus11:47 to distinguish between the unclean and the clean

Leviticus12:2 “Speak to the children of Israel
Leviticus12:3 And on the eighth day the flesh of his foreskin shall be circumcised.
Leviticus12:4 She shall then continue in the blood of her purification thirty-three days. She shall not touch any hallowed thing
Leviticus12:5 ‘But if she bears a female child
Leviticus12:6 ‘When the days of her purification are fulfilled
Leviticus12:7 Then he shall offer it before the Lord
Leviticus12:8 ‘And if she is not able to bring a lamb
Leviticus13:1 And the Lord spoke to Moses and Aaron
Leviticus13:2 “When a man has on the skin of his body a swelling
Leviticus13:3 The priest shall examine the sore on the skin of the body; and if the hair on the sore has turned white
Leviticus13:4 But if the bright spot is white on the skin of his body
Leviticus13:5 And the priest shall examine him on the seventh day; and indeed if the sore appears to be as it was
Leviticus13:6 Then the priest shall examine him again on the seventh day; and indeed if the sore has faded
Leviticus13:7 But if the scab should at all spread over the skin
Leviticus13:8 And if the priest sees that the scab has indeed spread on the skin
Leviticus13:9 “When the leprous sore is on a person
Leviticus13:10 And the priest shall examine him; and indeed if the swelling on the skin is white
Leviticus13:11 it is an old leprosy on the skin of his body. The priest shall pronounce him unclean
Leviticus13:12 “And if leprosy breaks out all over the skin
Leviticus13:13 then the priest shall consider; and indeed if the leprosy has covered all his body
Leviticus13:14 But when raw flesh appears on him
Leviticus13:15 And the priest shall examine the raw flesh and pronounce him to be unclean; for the raw flesh is unclean. It is leprosy.
Leviticus13:16 Or if the raw flesh changes and turns white again
Leviticus13:17 And the priest shall examine him; and indeed if the sore has turned white
Leviticus13:18 “If the body develops a boil in the skin
Leviticus13:19 and in the place of the boil there comes a white swelling or a bright spot
Leviticus13:20 and if
Leviticus13:21 But if the priest examines it
Leviticus13:22 and if it should at all spread over the skin
Leviticus13:23 But if the bright spot stays in one place
Leviticus13:24 “Or if the body receives a burn on its skin by fire
Leviticus13:25 then the priest shall examine it; and indeed if the hair of the bright spot has turned white
Leviticus13:26 But if the priest examines it
Leviticus13:27 And the priest shall examine him on the seventh day. If it has at all spread over the skin
Leviticus13:28 But if the bright spot stays in one place
Leviticus13:29 “If a man or woman has a sore on the head or the beard
Leviticus13:30 then the priest shall examine the sore; and indeed if it appears deeper than the skin
Leviticus13:31 But if the priest examines the scaly sore
Leviticus13:32 And on the seventh day the priest shall examine the sore; and indeed if the scale has not spread
Leviticus13:33 he shall shave himself
Leviticus13:34 On the seventh day the priest shall examine the scale; and indeed if the scale has not spread over the skin
Leviticus13:35 But if the scale should at all spread over the skin after his cleansing
Leviticus13:36 then the priest shall examine him; and indeed if the scale has spread over the skin
Leviticus13:37 But if the scale appears to be at a standstill
Leviticus13:38 “If a man or a woman has bright spots on the skin of the body
Leviticus13:39 then the priest shall look; and indeed if the bright spots on the skin of the body are dull white
Leviticus13:40 “As for the man whose hair has fallen from his head
Leviticus13:41 He whose hair has fallen from his forehead
Leviticus13:42 And if there is on the bald head or bald forehead a reddish-white sore
Leviticus13:43 Then the priest shall examine it; and indeed if the swelling of the sore is reddish-white on his bald head or on his bald forehead
Leviticus13:44 he is a leprous man. He is unclean. The priest shall surely pronounce him unclean; his sore is on his head.
Leviticus13:45 “Now the leper on whom the sore is
Leviticus13:46 He shall be unclean. All the days he has the sore he shall be unclean. He is unclean
Leviticus13:47 “Also
Leviticus13:48 whether it is in the warp or woof of linen or wool
Leviticus13:49 and if the plague is greenish or reddish in the garment or in the leather
Leviticus13:50 The priest shall examine the plague and isolate that which has the plague seven days.
Leviticus13:51 And he shall examine the plague on the seventh day. If the plague has spread in the garment
Leviticus13:52 He shall therefore burn that garment in which is the plague
Leviticus13:53 “But if the priest examines it
Leviticus13:54 then the priest shall command that they wash the thing in which is the plague; and he shall isolate it another seven days.
Leviticus13:55 Then the priest shall examine the plague after it has been washed; and indeed if the plague has not changed its color
Leviticus13:56 If the priest examines it
Leviticus13:57 But if it appears again in the garment
Leviticus13:58 And if you wash the garment
Leviticus13:59 “This is the law of the leprous plague in a garment of wool or linen
Leviticus14:1 Then the Lord spoke to Moses
Leviticus14:2 “This shall be the law of the leper for the day of his cleansing: He shall be brought to the priest.
Leviticus14:3 And the priest shall go out of the camp
Leviticus14:4 then the priest shall command to take for him who is to be cleansed two living and clean birds
Leviticus14:5 And the priest shall command that one of the birds be killed in an earthen vessel over running water.
Leviticus14:6 As for the living bird
Leviticus14:7 And he shall sprinkle it seven times on him who is to be cleansed from the leprosy
Leviticus14:8 He who is to be cleansed shall wash his clothes
Leviticus14:9 But on the seventh day he shall shave all the hair off his head and his beard and his eyebrows—all his hair he shall shave off. He shall wash his clothes and wash his body in water
Leviticus14:10 “And on the eighth day he shall take two male lambs without blemish
Leviticus14:11 Then the priest who makes him clean shall present the man who is to be made clean
Leviticus14:12 And the priest shall take one male lamb and offer it as a trespass offering
Leviticus14:13 Then he shall kill the lamb in the place where he kills the sin offering and the burnt offering
Leviticus14:14 The priest shall take some of the blood of the trespass offering
Leviticus14:15 And the priest shall take some of the log of oil
Leviticus14:16 Then the priest shall dip his right finger in the oil that is in his left hand
Leviticus14:17 And of the rest of the oil in his hand
Leviticus14:18 The rest of the oil that is in the priest’s hand he shall put on the head of him who is to be cleansed. So the priest shall make atonement for him before the Lord.
Leviticus14:19 “Then the priest shall offer the sin offering
Leviticus14:20 And the priest shall offer the burnt offering and the grain offering on the altar. So the priest shall make atonement for him
Leviticus14:21 “But if he is poor and cannot afford it
Leviticus14:22 and two turtledoves or two young pigeons
Leviticus14:23 He shall bring them to the priest on the eighth day for his cleansing
Leviticus14:24 And the priest shall take the lamb of the trespass offering and the log of oil
Leviticus14:25 Then he shall kill the lamb of the trespass offering
Leviticus14:26 And the priest shall pour some of the oil into the palm of his own left hand.
Leviticus14:27 Then the priest shall sprinkle with his right finger some of the oil that is in his left hand seven times before the Lord.
Leviticus14:28 And the priest shall put some of the oil that is in his hand on the tip of the right ear of him who is to be cleansed
Leviticus14:29 The rest of the oil that is in the priest’s hand he shall put on the head of him who is to be cleansed
Leviticus14:30 And he shall offer one of the turtledoves or young pigeons
Leviticus14:31 such as he is able to afford
Leviticus14:32 This is the law for one who had a leprous sore
Leviticus14:33 And the Lord spoke to Moses and Aaron
Leviticus14:34 “When you have come into the land of Canaan
Leviticus14:35 and he who owns the house comes and tells the priest
Leviticus14:36 then the priest shall command that they empty the house
Leviticus14:37 And he shall examine the plague; and indeed if the plague is on the walls of the house with ingrained streaks
Leviticus14:38 then the priest shall go out of the house
Leviticus14:39 And the priest shall come again on the seventh day and look; and indeed if the plague has spread on the walls of the house
Leviticus14:40 then the priest shall command that they take away the stones in which is the plague
Leviticus14:41 And he shall cause the house to be scraped inside
Leviticus14:42 Then they shall take other stones and put them in the place of those stones
Leviticus14:43 “Now if the plague comes back and breaks out in the house
Leviticus14:44 then the priest shall come and look; and indeed if the plague has spread in the house
Leviticus14:45 And he shall break down the house
Leviticus14:46 Moreover he who goes into the house at all while it is shut up shall be unclean until evening.
Leviticus14:47 And he who lies down in the house shall wash his clothes
Leviticus14:48 “But if the priest comes in and examines it
Leviticus14:49 And he shall take
Leviticus14:50 Then he shall kill one of the birds in an earthen vessel over running water;
Leviticus14:51 and he shall take the cedar wood
Leviticus14:52 And he shall cleanse the house with the blood of the bird and the running water and the living bird
Leviticus14:53 Then he shall let the living bird loose outside the city in the open field
Leviticus14:54 “This is the law for any leprous sore and scale
Leviticus14:55 for the leprosy of a garment and of a house
Leviticus14:56 for a swelling and a scab and a bright spot
Leviticus14:57 to teach when it is unclean and when it is clean. This is the law of leprosy.”
Leviticus15:1 And the Lord spoke to Moses and Aaron
Leviticus15:2 “Speak to the children of Israel
Leviticus15:3 And this shall be his uncleanness in regard to his discharge—whether his body runs with his discharge
Leviticus15:4 Every bed is unclean on which he who has the discharge lies
Leviticus15:5 And whoever touches his bed shall wash his clothes and bathe in water
Leviticus15:6 He who sits on anything on which he who has the discharge sat shall wash his clothes and bathe in water
Leviticus15:7 And he who touches the body of him who has the discharge shall wash his clothes and bathe in water
Leviticus15:8 If he who has the discharge spits on him who is clean
Leviticus15:9 Any saddle on which he who has the discharge rides shall be unclean.
Leviticus15:10 Whoever touches anything that was under him shall be unclean until evening. He who carries any of those things shall wash his clothes and bathe in water
Leviticus15:11 And whomever the one who has the discharge touches
Leviticus15:12 The vessel of earth that he who has the discharge touches shall be broken
Leviticus15:13 ‘And when he who has a discharge is cleansed of his discharge
Leviticus15:14 On the eighth day he shall take for himself two turtledoves or two young pigeons
Leviticus15:15 Then the priest shall offer them
Leviticus15:16 ‘If any man has an emission of semen
Leviticus15:17 And any garment and any leather on which there is semen
Leviticus15:18 Also
Leviticus15:19 ‘If a woman has a discharge
Leviticus15:20 Everything that she lies on during her impurity shall be unclean; also everything that she sits on shall be unclean.
Leviticus15:21 Whoever touches her bed shall wash his clothes and bathe in water
Leviticus15:22 And whoever touches anything that she sat on shall wash his clothes and bathe in water
Leviticus15:23 If anything is on her bed or on anything on which she sits
Leviticus15:24 And if any man lies with her at all
Leviticus15:25 ‘If a woman has a discharge of blood for many days
Leviticus15:26 Every bed on which she lies all the days of her discharge shall be to her as the bed of her impurity; and whatever she sits on shall be unclean
Leviticus15:27 Whoever touches those things shall be unclean; he shall wash his clothes and bathe in water
Leviticus15:28 ‘But if she is cleansed of her discharge
Leviticus15:29 And on the eighth day she shall take for herself two turtledoves or two young pigeons
Leviticus15:30 Then the priest shall offer the one as a sin offering and the other as a burnt offering
Leviticus15:31 ‘Thus you shall separate the children of Israel from their uncleanness
Leviticus15:32 This is the law for one who has a discharge
Leviticus15:33 and for her who is indisposed because of her customary impurity
Leviticus16:1 Now the Lord spoke to Moses after the death of the two sons of Aaron
Leviticus16:2 and the Lord said to Moses: “Tell Aaron your brother not to come at just any time into the Holy Place inside the veil
Leviticus16:3 “Thus Aaron shall come into the Holy Place: with the blood of a young bull as a sin offering
Leviticus16:4 He shall put the holy linen tunic and the linen trousers on his body; he shall be girded with a linen sash
Leviticus16:5 And he shall take from the congregation of the children of Israel two kids of the goats as a sin offering
Leviticus16:6 “Aaron shall offer the bull as a sin offering
Leviticus16:7 He shall take the two goats and present them before the Lord at the door of the tabernacle of meeting.
Leviticus16:8 Then Aaron shall cast lots for the two goats: one lot for the Lord and the other lot for the scapegoat.
Leviticus16:9 And Aaron shall bring the goat on which the Lord’s lot fell
Leviticus16:10 But the goat on which the lot fell to be the scapegoat shall be presented alive before the Lord
Leviticus16:11 “And Aaron shall bring the bull of the sin offering
Leviticus16:12 Then he shall take a censer full of burning coals of fire from the altar before the Lord
Leviticus16:13 And he shall put the incense on the fire before the Lord
Leviticus16:14 He shall take some of the blood of the bull and sprinkle it with his finger on the mercy seat on the east side; and before the mercy seat he shall sprinkle some of the blood with his finger seven times.
Leviticus16:15 “Then he shall kill the goat of the sin offering
Leviticus16:16 So he shall make atonement for the Holy Place
Leviticus16:17 There shall be no man in the tabernacle of meeting when he goes in to make atonement in the Holy Place
Leviticus16:18 And he shall go out to the altar that is before the Lord
Leviticus16:19 Then he shall sprinkle some of the blood on it with his finger seven times
Leviticus16:20 “And when he has made an end of atoning for the Holy Place
Leviticus16:21 Aaron shall lay both his hands on the head of the live goat
Leviticus16:22 The goat shall bear on itself all their iniquities to an uninhabited land; and he shall release the goat in the wilderness.
Leviticus16:23 “Then Aaron shall come into the tabernacle of meeting
Leviticus16:24 And he shall wash his body with water in a holy place
Leviticus16:25 The fat of the sin offering he shall burn on the altar.
Leviticus16:26 And he who released the goat as the scapegoat shall wash his clothes and bathe his body in water
Leviticus16:27 The bull for the sin offering and the goat for the sin offering
Leviticus16:28 Then he who burns them shall wash his clothes and bathe his body in water
Leviticus16:29 “This shall be a statute forever for you: In the seventh month
Leviticus16:30 For on that day the priest shall make atonement for you
Leviticus16:31 It is a sabbath of solemn rest for you
Leviticus16:32 And the priest
Leviticus16:33 then he shall make atonement for the Holy Sanctuary
Leviticus16:34 This shall be an everlasting statute for you

Leviticus18:1 Then the Lord spoke to Moses
Leviticus18:2 “Speak to the children of Israel
Leviticus18:3 According to the doings of the land of Egypt
Leviticus18:4 You shall observe My judgments and keep My ordinances
Leviticus18:5 You shall therefore keep My statutes and My judgments
Leviticus18:6 ‘None of you shall approach anyone who is near of kin to him
Leviticus18:7 The nakedness of your father or the nakedness of your mother you shall not uncover. She is your mother; you shall not uncover her nakedness.
Leviticus18:8 The nakedness of your father’s wife you shall not uncover; it is your father’s nakedness.
Leviticus18:9 The nakedness of your sister
Leviticus18:10 The nakedness of your son’s daughter or your daughter’s daughter
Leviticus18:11 The nakedness of your father’s wife’s daughter
Leviticus18:12 You shall not uncover the nakedness of your father’s sister; she is near of kin to your father.
Leviticus18:13 You shall not uncover the nakedness of your mother’s sister
Leviticus18:14 You shall not uncover the nakedness of your father’s brother. You shall not approach his wife; she is your aunt.
Leviticus18:15 You shall not uncover the nakedness of your daughter-in-law—she is your son’s wife—you shall not uncover her nakedness.
Leviticus18:16 You shall not uncover the nakedness of your brother’s wife; it is your brother’s nakedness.
Leviticus18:17 You shall not uncover the nakedness of a woman and her daughter
Leviticus18:18 Nor shall you take a woman as a rival to her sister
Leviticus18:19 ‘Also you shall not approach a woman to uncover her nakedness as long as she is in her customary impurity.
Leviticus18:20 Moreover you shall not lie carnally with your neighbor’s wife
Leviticus18:21 And you shall not let any of your descendants pass through the fire to Molech
Leviticus18:22 You shall not lie with a male as with a woman. It is an abomination.
Leviticus18:23 Nor shall you mate with any animal
Leviticus18:24 ‘Do not defile yourselves with any of these things; for by all these the nations are defiled
Leviticus18:25 For the land is defiled; therefore I visit the punishment of its iniquity upon it
Leviticus18:26 You shall therefore keep My statutes and My judgments
Leviticus18:27 (for all these abominations the men of the land have done
Leviticus18:28 lest the land vomit you out also when you defile it
Leviticus18:29 For whoever commits any of these abominations
Leviticus18:30 ‘Therefore you shall keep My ordinance
Leviticus19:1 And the Lord spoke to Moses
Leviticus19:2 “Speak to all the congregation of the children of Israel
Leviticus19:3 ‘Every one of you shall revere his mother and his father
Leviticus19:4 ‘Do not turn to idols
Leviticus19:5 ‘And if you offer a sacrifice of a peace offering to the Lord
Leviticus19:6 It shall be eaten the same day you offer it
Leviticus19:7 And if it is eaten at all on the third day
Leviticus19:8 Therefore everyone who eats it shall bear his iniquity
Leviticus19:9 ‘When you reap the harvest of your land
Leviticus19:10 And you shall not glean your vineyard
Leviticus19:11 ‘You shall not steal
Leviticus19:12 And you shall not swear by My name falsely
Leviticus19:13 ‘You shall not cheat your neighbor
Leviticus19:14 You shall not curse the deaf
Leviticus19:15 ‘You shall do no injustice in judgment. You shall not be partial to the poor
Leviticus19:16 You shall not go about as a talebearer among your people; nor shall you take a stand against the life of your neighbor: I am the Lord.
Leviticus19:17 ‘You shall not hate your brother in your heart. You shall surely rebuke your neighbor
Leviticus19:18 You shall not take vengeance
Leviticus19:19 ‘You shall keep My statutes. You shall not let your livestock breed with another kind. You shall not sow your field with mixed seed. Nor shall a garment of mixed linen and wool come upon you.
Leviticus19:20 ‘Whoever lies carnally with a woman who is betrothed to a man as a concubine
Leviticus19:21 And he shall bring his trespass offering to the Lord
Leviticus19:22 The priest shall make atonement for him with the ram of the trespass offering before the Lord for his sin which he has committed. And the sin which he has committed shall be forgiven him.
Leviticus19:23 ‘When you come into the land
Leviticus19:24 But in the fourth year all its fruit shall be holy
Leviticus19:25 And in the fifth year you may eat its fruit
Leviticus19:26 ‘You shall not eat anything with the blood
Leviticus19:27 You shall not shave around the sides of your head
Leviticus19:28 You shall not make any cuttings in your flesh for the dead
Leviticus19:29 ‘Do not prostitute your daughter
Leviticus19:30 ‘You shall keep My Sabbaths and reverence My sanctuary: I am the Lord.
Leviticus19:31 ‘Give no regard to mediums and familiar spirits; do not seek after them
Leviticus19:32 ‘You shall rise before the gray headed and honor the presence of an old man
Leviticus19:33 ‘And if a stranger dwells with you in your land
Leviticus19:34 The stranger who dwells among you shall be to you as one born among you
Leviticus19:35 ‘You shall do no injustice in judgment
Leviticus19:36 You shall have honest scales
Leviticus19:37 ‘Therefore you shall observe all My statutes and all My judgments

Leviticus20:26 And you shall be holy to Me

Leviticus22:7 And when the sun goes down he shall be clean; and afterward he may eat the holy offerings

Leviticus22:21 And whoever offers a sacrifice of a peace offering to the Lord
Leviticus22:22 Those that are blind or broken or maimed

Leviticus22:29 And when you offer a sacrifice of thanksgiving to the Lord
Leviticus22:30 On the same day it shall be eaten; you shall leave none of it until morning: I am the Lord.
