Leviticus23:1 And the Lord spoke to Moses
Leviticus23:2 “Speak to the children of Israel
Leviticus23:3 ‘Six days shall work be done
Leviticus23:4 ‘These are the feasts of the Lord
Leviticus23:5 On the fourteenth day of the first month at twilight is the Lord’s Passover.
Leviticus23:6 And on the fifteenth day of the same month is the Feast of Unleavened Bread to the Lord; seven days you must eat unleavened bread.
Leviticus23:7 On the first day you shall have a holy convocation; you shall do no customary work on it.
Leviticus23:8 But you shall offer an offering made by fire to the Lord for seven days. The seventh day shall be a holy convocation; you shall do no customary work on it.’ ”
Leviticus23:9 And the Lord spoke to Moses
Leviticus23:10 “Speak to the children of Israel
Leviticus23:11 He shall wave the sheaf before the Lord
Leviticus23:12 And you shall offer on that day
Leviticus23:13 Its grain offering shall be two-tenths of an ephah of fine flour mixed with oil
Leviticus23:14 You shall eat neither bread nor parched grain nor fresh grain until the same day that you have brought an offering to your God; it shall be a statute forever throughout your generations in all your dwellings.
Leviticus23:15 ‘And you shall count for yourselves from the day after the Sabbath
Leviticus23:16 Count fifty days to the day after the seventh Sabbath; then you shall offer a new grain offering to the Lord.
Leviticus23:17 You shall bring from your dwellings two wave loaves of two-tenths of an ephah. They shall be of fine flour; they shall be baked with leaven. They are the firstfruits to the Lord.
Leviticus23:18 And you shall offer with the bread seven lambs of the first year
Leviticus23:19 Then you shall sacrifice one kid of the goats as a sin offering
Leviticus23:20 The priest shall wave them with the bread of the firstfruits as a wave offering before the Lord
Leviticus23:21 And you shall proclaim on the same day that it is a holy convocation to you. You shall do no customary work on it. It shall be a statute forever in all your dwellings throughout your generations.
Leviticus23:22 ‘When you reap the harvest of your land
Leviticus23:23 Then the Lord spoke to Moses
Leviticus23:24 “Speak to the children of Israel
Leviticus23:25 You shall do no customary work on it; and you shall offer an offering made by fire to the Lord.’ ”
Leviticus23:26 And the Lord spoke to Moses
Leviticus23:27 “Also the tenth day of this seventh month shall be the Day of Atonement. It shall be a holy convocation for you; you shall afflict your souls
Leviticus23:28 And you shall do no work on that same day
Leviticus23:29 For any person who is not afflicted in soul on that same day shall be cut off from his people.
Leviticus23:30 And any person who does any work on that same day
Leviticus23:31 You shall do no manner of work; it shall be a statute forever throughout your generations in all your dwellings.
Leviticus23:32 It shall be to you a sabbath of solemn rest
Leviticus23:33 Then the Lord spoke to Moses
Leviticus23:34 “Speak to the children of Israel
Leviticus23:35 On the first day there shall be a holy convocation. You shall do no customary work on it.
Leviticus23:36 For seven days you shall offer an offering made by fire to the Lord. On the eighth day you shall have a holy convocation
Leviticus23:37 ‘These are the feasts of the Lord which you shall proclaim to be holy convocations
Leviticus23:38 besides the Sabbaths of the Lord
Leviticus23:39 ‘Also on the fifteenth day of the seventh month
Leviticus23:40 And you shall take for yourselves on the first day the fruit of beautiful trees
Leviticus23:41 You shall keep it as a feast to the Lord for seven days in the year. It shall be a statute forever in your generations. You shall celebrate it in the seventh month.
Leviticus23:42 You shall dwell in booths for seven days. All who are native Israelites shall dwell in booths
Leviticus23:43 that your generations may know that I made the children of Israel dwell in booths when I brought them out of the land of Egypt: I am the Lord your God.’ ”
Leviticus23:44 So Moses declared to the children of Israel the feasts of the Lord.
Leviticus24:1 Then the Lord spoke to Moses
Leviticus24:2 “Command the children of Israel that they bring to you pure oil of pressed olives for the light
Leviticus24:3 Outside the veil of the Testimony
Leviticus24:4 He shall be in charge of the lamps on the pure gold lampstand before the Lord continually.
Leviticus24:5 “And you shall take fine flour and bake twelve cakes with it. Two-tenths of an ephah shall be in each cake.
Leviticus24:6 You shall set them in two rows
Leviticus24:7 And you shall put pure frankincense on each row
Leviticus24:8 Every Sabbath he shall set it in order before the Lord continually
Leviticus24:9 And it shall be for Aaron and his sons
Leviticus24:10 Now the son of an Israelite woman
Leviticus24:11 And the Israelite woman’s son blasphemed the name of the Lord and cursed; and so they brought him to Moses. (His mother’s name was Shelomith the daughter of Dibri
Leviticus24:12 Then they put him in custody
Leviticus24:13 And the Lord spoke to Moses
Leviticus24:14 “Take outside the camp him who has cursed; then let all who heard him lay their hands on his head
Leviticus24:15 “Then you shall speak to the children of Israel
Leviticus24:16 And whoever blasphemes the name of the Lord shall surely be put to death. All the congregation shall certainly stone him
Leviticus24:17 ‘Whoever kills any man shall surely be put to death.
Leviticus24:18 Whoever kills an animal shall make it good
Leviticus24:19 ‘If a man causes disfigurement of his neighbor
Leviticus24:20 fracture for fracture
Leviticus24:21 And whoever kills an animal shall restore it; but whoever kills a man shall be put to death.
Leviticus24:22 You shall have the same law for the stranger and for one from your own country; for I am the Lord your God.’ ”
Leviticus24:23 Then Moses spoke to the children of Israel; and they took outside the camp him who had cursed
Leviticus25:1 And the Lord spoke to Moses on Mount Sinai
Leviticus25:2 “Speak to the children of Israel
Leviticus25:3 Six years you shall sow your field
Leviticus25:4 but in the seventh year there shall be a sabbath of solemn rest for the land
Leviticus25:5 What grows of its own accord of your harvest you shall not reap
Leviticus25:6 And the sabbath produce of the land shall be food for you: for you
Leviticus25:7 for your livestock and the beasts that are in your land—all its produce shall be for food.
Leviticus25:8 ‘And you shall count seven sabbaths of years for yourself
Leviticus25:9 Then you shall cause the trumpet of the Jubilee to sound on the tenth day of the seventh month; on the Day of Atonement you shall make the trumpet to sound throughout all your land.
Leviticus25:10 And you shall consecrate the fiftieth year
Leviticus25:11 That fiftieth year shall be a Jubilee to you; in it you shall neither sow nor reap what grows of its own accord
Leviticus25:12 For it is the Jubilee; it shall be holy to you; you shall eat its produce from the field.
Leviticus25:13 ‘In this Year of Jubilee
Leviticus25:14 And if you sell anything to your neighbor or buy from your neighbor’s hand
Leviticus25:15 According to the number of years after the Jubilee you shall buy from your neighbor
Leviticus25:16 According to the multitude of years you shall increase its price
Leviticus25:17 Therefore you shall not oppress one another
Leviticus25:18 ‘So you shall observe My statutes and keep My judgments
Leviticus25:19 Then the land will yield its fruit
Leviticus25:20 ‘And if you say
Leviticus25:21 Then I will command My blessing on you in the sixth year
Leviticus25:22 And you shall sow in the eighth year
Leviticus25:23 ‘The land shall not be sold permanently
Leviticus25:24 And in all the land of your possession you shall grant redemption of the land.
Leviticus25:25 ‘If one of your brethren becomes poor
Leviticus25:26 Or if the man has no one to redeem it
Leviticus25:27 then let him count the years since its sale
Leviticus25:28 But if he is not able to have it restored to himself
Leviticus25:29 ‘If a man sells a house in a walled city
Leviticus25:30 But if it is not redeemed within the space of a full year
Leviticus25:31 However the houses of villages which have no wall around them shall be counted as the fields of the country. They may be redeemed
Leviticus25:32 Nevertheless the cities of the Levites
Leviticus25:33 And if a man purchases a house from the Levites
Leviticus25:34 But the field of the common-land of their cities may not be sold
Leviticus25:35 ‘If one of your brethren becomes poor
Leviticus25:36 Take no usury or interest from him; but fear your God
Leviticus25:37 You shall not lend him your money for usury
Leviticus25:38 I am the Lord your God
Leviticus25:39 ‘And if one of your brethren who dwells by you becomes poor
Leviticus25:40 As a hired servant and a sojourner he shall be with you
Leviticus25:41 And then he shall depart from you—he and his children with him—and shall return to his own family. He shall return to the possession of his fathers.
Leviticus25:42 For they are My servants
Leviticus25:43 You shall not rule over him with rigor
Leviticus25:44 And as for your male and female slaves whom you may have—from the nations that are around you
Leviticus25:45 Moreover you may buy the children of the strangers who dwell among you
Leviticus25:46 And you may take them as an inheritance for your children after you
Leviticus25:47 ‘Now if a sojourner or stranger close to you becomes rich
Leviticus25:48 after he is sold he may be redeemed again. One of his brothers may redeem him;
Leviticus25:49 or his uncle or his uncle’s son may redeem him; or anyone who is near of kin to him in his family may redeem him; or if he is able he may redeem himself.
Leviticus25:50 Thus he shall reckon with him who bought him: The price of his release shall be according to the number of years
Leviticus25:51 If there are still many years remaining
Leviticus25:52 And if there remain but a few years until the Year of Jubilee
Leviticus25:53 He shall be with him as a yearly hired servant
Leviticus25:54 And if he is not redeemed in these years
Leviticus25:55 For the children of Israel are servants to Me; they are My servants whom I brought out of the land of Egypt: I am the Lord your God.
Leviticus26:1 ‘You shall not make idols for yourselves; neither a carved image nor a sacred pillar shall you rear up for yourselves; nor shall you set up an engraved stone in your land
Leviticus26:2 You shall keep My Sabbaths and reverence My sanctuary: I am the Lord.
Leviticus26:3 ‘If you walk in My statutes and keep My commandments
Leviticus26:4 then I will give you rain in its season
Leviticus26:5 Your threshing shall last till the time of vintage
Leviticus26:6 I will give peace in the land
Leviticus26:7 You will chase your enemies
Leviticus26:8 Five of you shall chase a hundred
Leviticus26:9 ‘For I will look on you favorably and make you fruitful
Leviticus26:10 You shall eat the old harvest
Leviticus26:11 I will set My tabernacle among you
Leviticus26:12 I will walk among you and be your God
Leviticus26:13 I am the Lord your God
Leviticus26:14 ‘But if you do not obey Me
Leviticus26:15 and if you despise My statutes
Leviticus26:16 I also will do this to you: I will even appoint terror over you
Leviticus26:17 I will set My face against you
Leviticus26:18 ‘And after all this
Leviticus26:19 I will break the pride of your power; I will make your heavens like iron and your earth like bronze.
Leviticus26:20 And your strength shall be spent in vain; for your land shall not yield its produce
Leviticus26:21 ‘Then
Leviticus26:22 I will also send wild beasts among you
Leviticus26:23 ‘And if by these things you are not reformed by Me
Leviticus26:24 then I also will walk contrary to you
Leviticus26:25 And I will bring a sword against you that will execute the vengeance of the covenant; when you are gathered together within your cities I will send pestilence among you; and you shall be delivered into the hand of the enemy.
Leviticus26:26 When I have cut off your supply of bread
Leviticus26:27 ‘And after all this
Leviticus26:28 then I also will walk contrary to you in fury; and I
Leviticus26:29 You shall eat the flesh of your sons
Leviticus26:30 I will destroy your high places
Leviticus26:31 I will lay your cities waste and bring your sanctuaries to desolation
Leviticus26:32 I will bring the land to desolation
Leviticus26:33 I will scatter you among the nations and draw out a sword after you; your land shall be desolate and your cities waste.
Leviticus26:34 Then the land shall enjoy its sabbaths as long as it lies desolate and you are in your enemies’ land; then the land shall rest and enjoy its sabbaths.
Leviticus26:35 As long as it lies desolate it shall rest— for the time it did not rest on your sabbaths when you dwelt in it.
Leviticus26:36 ‘And as for those of you who are left
Leviticus26:37 They shall stumble over one another
Leviticus26:38 You shall perish among the nations
Leviticus26:39 And those of you who are left shall waste away in their iniquity in your enemies’ lands; also in their fathers’ iniquities
Leviticus26:40 ‘But if they confess their iniquity and the iniquity of their fathers
Leviticus26:41 and that I also have walked contrary to them and have brought them into the land of their enemies; if their uncircumcised hearts are humbled
Leviticus26:42 then I will remember My covenant with Jacob
Leviticus26:43 The land also shall be left empty by them
Leviticus26:44 Yet for all that
Leviticus26:45 But for their sake I will remember the covenant of their ancestors
Leviticus26:46 These are the statutes and judgments and laws which the Lord made between Himself and the children of Israel on Mount Sinai by the hand of Moses.
Leviticus27:1 Now the Lord spoke to Moses
Leviticus27:2 “Speak to the children of Israel
Leviticus27:3 if your valuation is of a male from twenty years old up to sixty years old
Leviticus27:4 If it is a female
Leviticus27:5 and if from five years old up to twenty years old
Leviticus27:6 and if from a month old up to five years old
Leviticus27:7 and if from sixty years old and above
Leviticus27:8 ‘But if he is too poor to pay your valuation
Leviticus27:9 ‘If it is an animal that men may bring as an offering to the Lord
Leviticus27:10 He shall not substitute it or exchange it
Leviticus27:11 If it is an unclean animal which they do not offer as a sacrifice to the Lord
Leviticus27:12 and the priest shall set a value for it
Leviticus27:13 But if he wants at all to redeem it
Leviticus27:14 ‘And when a man dedicates his house to be holy to the Lord
Leviticus27:15 If he who dedicated it wants to redeem his house
Leviticus27:16 ‘If a man dedicates to the Lord part of a field of his possession
Leviticus27:17 If he dedicates his field from the Year of Jubilee
Leviticus27:18 But if he dedicates his field after the Jubilee
Leviticus27:19 And if he who dedicates the field ever wishes to redeem it
Leviticus27:20 But if he does not want to redeem the field
Leviticus27:21 but the field
Leviticus27:22 ‘And if a man dedicates to the Lord a field which he has bought
Leviticus27:23 then the priest shall reckon to him the worth of your valuation
Leviticus27:24 In the Year of Jubilee the field shall return to him from whom it was bought
Leviticus27:25 And all your valuations shall be according to the shekel of the sanctuary: twenty gerahs to the shekel.
Leviticus27:26 ‘But the firstborn of the animals
Leviticus27:27 And if it is an unclean animal
Leviticus27:28 ‘Nevertheless no devoted offering that a man may devote to the Lord of all that he has
Leviticus27:29 No person under the ban
Leviticus27:30 And all the tithe of the land
Leviticus27:31 If a man wants at all to redeem any of his tithes
Leviticus27:32 And concerning the tithe of the herd or the flock
Leviticus27:33 He shall not inquire whether it is good or bad
Leviticus27:34 These are the commandments which the Lord commanded Moses for the children of Israel on Mount Sinai.
Numbers1:1 Now the Lord spoke to Moses in the Wilderness of Sinai
Numbers1:2 “Take a census of all the congregation of the children of Israel
Numbers1:3 from twenty years old and above—all who are able to go to war in Israel. You and Aaron shall number them by their armies.
Numbers1:4 And with you there shall be a man from every tribe
Numbers1:5 “These are the names of the men who shall stand with you: from Reuben
Numbers1:6 from Simeon
Numbers1:7 from Judah
Numbers1:8 from Issachar
Numbers1:9 from Zebulun
Numbers1:10 from the sons of Joseph: from Ephraim
Numbers1:11 from Benjamin
Numbers1:12 from Dan
Numbers1:13 from Asher
Numbers1:14 from Gad
Numbers1:15 from Naphtali
Numbers1:16 These were chosen from the congregation
Numbers1:17 Then Moses and Aaron took these men who had been mentioned by name
Numbers1:18 and they assembled all the congregation together on the first day of the second month; and they recited their ancestry by families
Numbers1:19 As the Lord commanded Moses
Numbers1:20 Now the children of Reuben
Numbers1:21 those who were numbered of the tribe of Reuben were forty-six thousand five hundred.
Numbers1:22 From the children of Simeon
Numbers1:23 those who were numbered of the tribe of Simeon were fifty-nine thousand three hundred.
Numbers1:24 From the children of Gad
Numbers1:25 those who were numbered of the tribe of Gad were forty-five thousand six hundred and fifty.
Numbers1:26 From the children of Judah
Numbers1:27 those who were numbered of the tribe of Judah were seventy-four thousand six hundred.
Numbers1:28 From the children of Issachar
Numbers1:29 those who were numbered of the tribe of Issachar were fifty-four thousand four hundred.
Numbers1:30 From the children of Zebulun
Numbers1:31 those who were numbered of the tribe of Zebulun were fifty-seven thousand four hundred.
Numbers1:32 From the sons of Joseph
Numbers1:33 those who were numbered of the tribe of Ephraim were forty thousand five hundred.
Numbers1:34 From the children of Manasseh
Numbers1:35 those who were numbered of the tribe of Manasseh were thirty-two thousand two hundred.
Numbers1:36 From the children of Benjamin
Numbers1:37 those who were numbered of the tribe of Benjamin were thirty-five thousand four hundred.
Numbers1:38 From the children of Dan
Numbers1:39 those who were numbered of the tribe of Dan were sixty-two thousand seven hundred.
Numbers1:40 From the children of Asher
Numbers1:41 those who were numbered of the tribe of Asher were forty-one thousand five hundred.
Numbers1:42 From the children of Naphtali
Numbers1:43 those who were numbered of the tribe of Naphtali were fifty-three thousand four hundred.
Numbers1:44 These are the ones who were numbered
Numbers1:45 So all who were numbered of the children of Israel
Numbers1:46 all who were numbered were six hundred and three thousand five hundred and fifty.
Numbers1:47 But the Levites were not numbered among them by their fathers’ tribe;
Numbers1:48 for the Lord had spoken to Moses
Numbers1:49 “Only the tribe of Levi you shall not number
Numbers1:50 but you shall appoint the Levites over the tabernacle of the Testimony
Numbers1:51 And when the tabernacle is to go forward
Numbers1:52 The children of Israel shall pitch their tents
Numbers1:53 but the Levites shall camp around the tabernacle of the Testimony
Numbers1:54 Thus the children of Israel did; according to all that the Lord commanded Moses
Numbers2:1 And the Lord spoke to Moses and Aaron
Numbers2:2 “Everyone of the children of Israel shall camp by his own standard
Numbers2:3 On the east side
Numbers2:4 And his army was numbered at seventy-four thousand six hundred.
Numbers2:5 “Those who camp next to him shall be the tribe of Issachar
Numbers2:6 And his army was numbered at fifty-four thousand four hundred.
Numbers2:7 “Then comes the tribe of Zebulun
Numbers2:8 And his army was numbered at fifty-seven thousand four hundred.
Numbers2:9 “All who were numbered according to their armies of the forces with Judah
Numbers2:10 “On the south side shall be the standard of the forces with Reuben according to their armies
Numbers2:11 And his army was numbered at forty-six thousand five hundred.
Numbers2:12 “Those who camp next to him shall be the tribe of Simeon
Numbers2:13 And his army was numbered at fifty-nine thousand three hundred.
Numbers2:14 “Then comes the tribe of Gad
Numbers2:15 And his army was numbered at forty-five thousand six hundred and fifty.
Numbers2:16 “All who were numbered according to their armies of the forces with Reuben
Numbers2:17 “And the tabernacle of meeting shall move out with the camp of the Levites in the middle of the camps; as they camp
Numbers2:18 “On the west side shall be the standard of the forces with Ephraim according to their armies
Numbers2:19 And his army was numbered at forty thousand five hundred.
Numbers2:20 “Next to him comes the tribe of Manasseh
Numbers2:21 And his army was numbered at thirty-two thousand two hundred.
Numbers2:22 “Then comes the tribe of Benjamin
Numbers2:23 And his army was numbered at thirty-five thousand four hundred.
Numbers2:24 “All who were numbered according to their armies of the forces with Ephraim
Numbers2:25 “The standard of the forces with Dan shall be on the north side according to their armies
Numbers2:26 And his army was numbered at sixty-two thousand seven hundred.
Numbers2:27 “Those who camp next to him shall be the tribe of Asher
Numbers2:28 And his army was numbered at forty-one thousand five hundred.
Numbers2:29 “Then comes the tribe of Naphtali
Numbers2:30 And his army was numbered at fifty-three thousand four hundred.
Numbers2:31 “All who were numbered of the forces with Dan
Numbers2:32 These are the ones who were numbered of the children of Israel by their fathers’ houses. All who were numbered according to their armies of the forces were six hundred and three thousand five hundred and fifty.
Numbers2:33 But the Levites were not numbered among the children of Israel
Numbers2:34 Thus the children of Israel did according to all that the Lord commanded Moses; so they camped by their standards and so they broke camp
Numbers3:1 Now these are the records of Aaron and Moses when the Lord spoke with Moses on Mount Sinai.
Numbers3:2 And these are the names of the sons of Aaron: Nadab
Numbers3:3 These are the names of the sons of Aaron
Numbers3:4 Nadab and Abihu had died before the Lord when they offered profane fire before the Lord in the Wilderness of Sinai; and they had no children. So Eleazar and Ithamar ministered as priests in the presence of Aaron their father.
Numbers3:5 And the Lord spoke to Moses
Numbers3:6 “Bring the tribe of Levi near
Numbers3:7 And they shall attend to his needs and the needs of the whole congregation before the tabernacle of meeting
Numbers3:8 Also they shall attend to all the furnishings of the tabernacle of meeting
Numbers3:9 And you shall give the Levites to Aaron and his sons; they are given entirely to him from among the children of Israel.
Numbers3:10 So you shall appoint Aaron and his sons
Numbers3:11 Then the Lord spoke to Moses
Numbers3:12 “Now behold
Numbers3:13 because all the firstborn are Mine. On the day that I struck all the firstborn in the land of Egypt
Numbers3:14 Then the Lord spoke to Moses in the Wilderness of Sinai
Numbers3:15 “Number the children of Levi by their fathers’ houses
Numbers3:16 So Moses numbered them according to the word of the Lord
Numbers3:17 These were the sons of Levi by their names: Gershon
Numbers3:18 And these are the names of the sons of Gershon by their families: Libni and Shimei.
Numbers3:19 And the sons of Kohath by their families: Amram
Numbers3:20 And the sons of Merari by their families: Mahli and Mushi. These are the families of the Levites by their fathers’ houses.
Numbers3:21 From Gershon came the family of the Libnites and the family of the Shimites; these were the families of the Gershonites.
Numbers3:22 Those who were numbered
Numbers3:23 The families of the Gershonites were to camp behind the tabernacle westward.
Numbers3:24 And the leader of the father’s house of the Gershonites was Eliasaph the son of Lael.
Numbers3:25 The duties of the children of Gershon in the tabernacle of meeting included the tabernacle
Numbers3:26 the screen for the door of the court
Numbers3:27 From Kohath came the family of the Amramites
Numbers3:28 According to the number of all the males
Numbers3:29 The families of the children of Kohath were to camp on the south side of the tabernacle.
Numbers3:30 And the leader of the fathers’ house of the families of the Kohathites was Elizaphan the son of Uzziel.
Numbers3:31 Their duty included the ark
Numbers3:32 And Eleazar the son of Aaron the priest was to be chief over the leaders of the Levites
Numbers3:33 From Merari came the family of the Mahlites and the family of the Mushites; these were the families of Merari.
Numbers3:34 And those who were numbered
Numbers3:35 The leader of the fathers’ house of the families of Merari was Zuriel the son of Abihail. These were to camp on the north side of the tabernacle.
Numbers3:36 And the appointed duty of the children of Merari included the boards of the tabernacle
Numbers3:37 and the pillars of the court all around
Numbers3:38 Moreover those who were to camp before the tabernacle on the east
Numbers3:39 All who were numbered of the Levites
Numbers3:40 Then the Lord said to Moses: “Number all the firstborn males of the children of Israel from a month old and above
Numbers3:41 And you shall take the Levites for Me—I am the Lord—instead of all the firstborn among the children of Israel
Numbers3:42 So Moses numbered all the firstborn among the children of Israel
Numbers3:43 And all the firstborn males
Numbers3:44 Then the Lord spoke to Moses
Numbers3:45 “Take the Levites instead of all the firstborn among the children of Israel
Numbers3:46 And for the redemption of the two hundred and seventy-three of the firstborn of the children of Israel
Numbers3:47 you shall take five shekels for each one individually; you shall take them in the currency of the shekel of the sanctuary
Numbers3:48 And you shall give the money
Numbers3:49 So Moses took the redemption money from those who were over and above those who were redeemed by the Levites.
Numbers3:50 From the firstborn of the children of Israel he took the money
Numbers3:51 And Moses gave their redemption money to Aaron and his sons
Numbers4:1 Then the Lord spoke to Moses and Aaron
Numbers4:2 “Take a census of the sons of Kohath from among the children of Levi
Numbers4:3 from thirty years old and above
Numbers4:4 “This is the service of the sons of Kohath in the tabernacle of meeting
Numbers4:5 When the camp prepares to journey
Numbers4:6 Then they shall put on it a covering of badger skins
Numbers4:7 “On the table of showbread they shall spread a blue cloth
Numbers4:8 They shall spread over them a scarlet cloth
Numbers4:9 And they shall take a blue cloth and cover the lampstand of the light
Numbers4:10 Then they shall put it with all its utensils in a covering of badger skins
Numbers4:11 “Over the golden altar they shall spread a blue cloth
Numbers4:12 Then they shall take all the utensils of service with which they minister in the sanctuary
Numbers4:13 Also they shall take away the ashes from the altar
Numbers4:14 They shall put on it all its implements with which they minister there—the firepans
Numbers4:15 And when Aaron and his sons have finished covering the sanctuary and all the furnishings of the sanctuary
Numbers4:16 “The appointed duty of Eleazar the son of Aaron the priest is the oil for the light
Numbers4:17 Then the Lord spoke to Moses and Aaron
Numbers4:18 “Do not cut off the tribe of the families of the Kohathites from among the Levites;
Numbers4:19 but do this in regard to them
Numbers4:20 But they shall not go in to watch while the holy things are being covered
Numbers4:21 Then the Lord spoke to Moses
Numbers4:22 “Also take a census of the sons of Gershon
Numbers4:23 From thirty years old and above
Numbers4:24 This is the service of the families of the Gershonites
Numbers4:25 They shall carry the curtains of the tabernacle and the tabernacle of meeting with its covering
Numbers4:26 the screen for the door of the gate of the court
Numbers4:27 “Aaron and his sons shall assign all the service of the sons of the Gershonites
Numbers4:28 This is the service of the families of the sons of Gershon in the tabernacle of meeting. And their duties shall be under the authority of Ithamar the son of Aaron the priest.
Numbers4:29 “ As for the sons of Merari
Numbers4:30 From thirty years old and above
Numbers4:31 And this is what they must carry as all their service for the tabernacle of meeting: the boards of the tabernacle
Numbers4:32 and the pillars around the court with their sockets
Numbers4:33 This is the service of the families of the sons of Merari
Numbers4:34 And Moses
Numbers4:35 from thirty years old and above
Numbers4:36 and those who were numbered by their families were two thousand seven hundred and fifty.
Numbers4:37 These were the ones who were numbered of the families of the Kohathites
Numbers4:38 And those who were numbered of the sons of Gershon
Numbers4:39 from thirty years old and above
Numbers4:40 those who were numbered by their families
Numbers4:41 These are the ones who were numbered of the families of the sons of Gershon
Numbers4:42 Those of the families of the sons of Merari who were numbered
Numbers4:43 from thirty years old and above
Numbers4:44 those who were numbered by their families were three thousand two hundred.
Numbers4:45 These are the ones who were numbered of the families of the sons of Merari
Numbers4:46 All who were numbered of the Levites
Numbers4:47 from thirty years old and above
Numbers4:48 those who were numbered were eight thousand five hundred and eighty.
Numbers4:49 According to the commandment of the Lord they were numbered by the hand of Moses
Numbers5:1 And the Lord spoke to Moses
Numbers5:2 “Command the children of Israel that they put out of the camp every leper
Numbers5:3 You shall put out both male and female; you shall put them outside the camp
Numbers5:4 And the children of Israel did so
Numbers5:5 Then the Lord spoke to Moses
Numbers5:6 “Speak to the children of Israel: ‘When a man or woman commits any sin that men commit in unfaithfulness against the Lord
Numbers5:7 then he shall confess the sin which he has committed. He shall make restitution for his trespass in full
Numbers5:8 But if the man has no relative to whom restitution may be made for the wrong
Numbers5:9 Every offering of all the holy things of the children of Israel
Numbers5:10 And every man’s holy things shall be his; whatever any man gives the priest shall be his.’ ”
Numbers5:11 And the Lord spoke to Moses
Numbers5:12 “Speak to the children of Israel
Numbers5:13 and a man lies with her carnally
Numbers5:14 if the spirit of jealousy comes upon him and he becomes jealous of his wife
Numbers5:15 then the man shall bring his wife to the priest. He shall bring the offering required for her
Numbers5:16 ‘And the priest shall bring her near
Numbers5:17 The priest shall take holy water in an earthen vessel
Numbers5:18 Then the priest shall stand the woman before the Lord
Numbers5:19 And the priest shall put her under oath
Numbers5:20 But if you have gone astray while under your husband’s authority
Numbers5:21 then the priest shall put the woman under the oath of the curse
Numbers5:22 and may this water that causes the curse go into your stomach
Numbers5:23 ‘Then the priest shall write these curses in a book
Numbers5:24 And he shall make the woman drink the bitter water that brings a curse
Numbers5:25 Then the priest shall take the grain offering of jealousy from the woman’s hand
Numbers5:26 and the priest shall take a handful of the offering
Numbers5:27 When he has made her drink the water
Numbers5:28 But if the woman has not defiled herself
Numbers5:29 ‘This is the law of jealousy
Numbers5:30 or when the spirit of jealousy comes upon a man
Numbers5:31 Then the man shall be free from iniquity
Numbers6:1 Then the Lord spoke to Moses
Numbers6:2 “Speak to the children of Israel
Numbers6:3 he shall separate himself from wine and similar drink; he shall drink neither vinegar made from wine nor vinegar made from similar drink; neither shall he drink any grape juice
Numbers6:4 All the days of his separation he shall eat nothing that is produced by the grapevine
Numbers6:5 ‘All the days of the vow of his separation no razor shall come upon his head; until the days are fulfilled for which he separated himself to the Lord
Numbers6:6 All the days that he separates himself to the Lord he shall not go near a dead body.
Numbers6:7 He shall not make himself unclean even for his father or his mother
Numbers6:8 All the days of his separation he shall be holy to the Lord.
Numbers6:9 ‘And if anyone dies very suddenly beside him
Numbers6:10 Then on the eighth day he shall bring two turtledoves or two young pigeons to the priest
Numbers6:11 and the priest shall offer one as a sin offering and the other as a burnt offering
Numbers6:12 He shall consecrate to the Lord the days of his separation
Numbers6:13 ‘Now this is the law of the Nazirite: When the days of his separation are fulfilled
Numbers6:14 And he shall present his offering to the Lord: one male lamb in its first year without blemish as a burnt offering
Numbers6:15 a basket of unleavened bread
Numbers6:16 Then the priest shall bring them before the Lord and offer his sin offering and his burnt offering;
Numbers6:17 and he shall offer the ram as a sacrifice of a peace offering to the Lord
Numbers6:18 Then the Nazirite shall shave his consecrated head at the door of the tabernacle of meeting
Numbers6:19 ‘And the priest shall take the boiled shoulder of the ram
Numbers6:20 and the priest shall wave them as a wave offering before the Lord; they are holy for the priest
Numbers6:21 “This is the law of the Nazirite who vows to the Lord the offering for his separation
Numbers6:22 And the Lord spoke to Moses
Numbers6:23 “Speak to Aaron and his sons
Numbers6:24 “The Lord bless you and keep you;
Numbers6:25 The Lord make His face shine upon you
Numbers6:26 The Lord lift up His countenance upon you
Numbers6:27 “So they shall put My name on the children of Israel

Numbers7:79 His offering was one silver platter
Numbers7:80 one gold pan of ten shekels
Numbers7:81 one young bull
Numbers7:82 one kid of the goats as a sin offering;
Numbers7:83 and as the sacrifice of peace offerings: two oxen
Numbers7:84 This was the dedication offering for the altar from the leaders of Israel
Numbers7:85 Each silver platter weighed one hundred and thirty shekels and each bowl seventy shekels. All the silver of the vessels weighed two thousand four hundred shekels
Numbers7:86 The twelve gold pans full of incense weighed ten shekels apiece
Numbers7:87 All the oxen for the burnt offering were twelve young bulls
Numbers7:88 And all the oxen for the sacrifice of peace offerings were twenty-four bulls
Numbers7:89 Now when Moses went into the tabernacle of meeting to speak with Him
Numbers8:1 And the Lord spoke to Moses
Numbers8:2 “Speak to Aaron
Numbers8:3 And Aaron did so; he arranged the lamps to face toward the front of the lampstand
Numbers8:4 Now this workmanship of the lampstand was hammered gold; from its shaft to its flowers it was hammered work. According to the pattern which the Lord had shown Moses
Numbers8:5 Then the Lord spoke to Moses
Numbers8:6 “Take the Levites from among the children of Israel and cleanse them ceremonially.
Numbers8:7 Thus you shall do to them to cleanse them: Sprinkle water of purification on them
Numbers8:8 Then let them take a young bull with its grain offering of fine flour mixed with oil
Numbers8:9 And you shall bring the Levites before the tabernacle of meeting
Numbers8:10 So you shall bring the Levites before the Lord
Numbers8:11 and Aaron shall offer the Levites before the Lord like a wave offering from the children of Israel
Numbers8:12 Then the Levites shall lay their hands on the heads of the young bulls
Numbers8:13 “And you shall stand the Levites before Aaron and his sons
Numbers8:14 Thus you shall separate the Levites from among the children of Israel
Numbers8:15 After that the Levites shall go in to service the tabernacle of meeting. So you shall cleanse them and offer them like a wave offering.
Numbers8:16 For they are wholly given to Me from among the children of Israel; I have taken them for Myself instead of all who open the womb
Numbers8:17 For all the firstborn among the children of Israel are Mine
Numbers8:18 I have taken the Levites instead of all the firstborn of the children of Israel.
Numbers8:19 And I have given the Levites as a gift to Aaron and his sons from among the children of Israel
Numbers8:20 Thus Moses and Aaron and all the congregation of the children of Israel did to the Levites; according to all that the Lord commanded Moses concerning the Levites
Numbers8:21 And the Levites purified themselves and washed their clothes; then Aaron presented them like a wave offering before the Lord
Numbers8:22 After that the Levites went in to do their work in the tabernacle of meeting before Aaron and his sons; as the Lord commanded Moses concerning the Levites
Numbers8:23 Then the Lord spoke to Moses
Numbers8:24 “This is what pertains to the Levites: From twenty-five years old and above one may enter to perform service in the work of the tabernacle of meeting;
Numbers8:25 and at the age of fifty years they must cease performing this work
Numbers8:26 They may minister with their brethren in the tabernacle of meeting

Numbers9:3 On the fourteenth day of this month
Numbers9:4 So Moses told the children of Israel that they should keep the Passover.
Numbers9:5 And they kept the Passover on the fourteenth day of the first month
Numbers9:6 Now there were certain men who were defiled by a human corpse
Numbers9:7 And those men said to him
Numbers9:8 And Moses said to them
Numbers9:9 Then the Lord spoke to Moses
Numbers9:10 “Speak to the children of Israel
Numbers9:11 On the fourteenth day of the second month
Numbers9:12 They shall leave none of it until morning
Numbers9:13 But the man who is clean and is not on a journey
Numbers9:14 ‘And if a stranger dwells among you
Numbers9:15 Now on the day that the tabernacle was raised up
Numbers9:16 So it was always: the cloud covered it by day
Numbers9:17 Whenever the cloud was taken up from above the tabernacle
Numbers9:18 At the command of the Lord the children of Israel would journey
Numbers9:19 Even when the cloud continued long
Numbers9:20 So it was
Numbers9:21 So it was
Numbers9:22 Whether it was two days
Numbers9:23 At the command of the Lord they remained encamped
Numbers10:1 And the Lord spoke to Moses
Numbers10:2 “Make two silver trumpets for yourself; you shall make them of hammered work; you shall use them for calling the congregation and for directing the movement of the camps.
Numbers10:3 When they blow both of them
Numbers10:4 But if they blow only one
Numbers10:5 When you sound the advance
Numbers10:6 When you sound the advance the second time
Numbers10:7 And when the assembly is to be gathered together
Numbers10:8 The sons of Aaron
Numbers10:9 “When you go to war in your land against the enemy who oppresses you
Numbers10:10 Also in the day of your gladness
Numbers10:11 Now it came to pass on the twentieth day of the second month
Numbers10:12 And the children of Israel set out from the Wilderness of Sinai on their journeys; then the cloud settled down in the Wilderness of Paran.
Numbers10:13 So they started out for the first time according to the command of the Lord by the hand of Moses.
Numbers10:14 The standard of the camp of the children of Judah set out first according to their armies; over their army was Nahshon the son of Amminadab.
Numbers10:15 Over the army of the tribe of the children of Issachar was Nethanel the son of Zuar.
Numbers10:16 And over the army of the tribe of the children of Zebulun was Eliab the son of Helon.
Numbers10:17 Then the tabernacle was taken down; and the sons of Gershon and the sons of Merari set out
Numbers10:18 And the standard of the camp of Reuben set out according to their armies; over their army was Elizur the son of Shedeur.
Numbers10:19 Over the army of the tribe of the children of Simeon was Shelumiel the son of Zurishaddai.
Numbers10:20 And over the army of the tribe of the children of Gad was Eliasaph the son of Deuel.
Numbers10:21 Then the Kohathites set out
Numbers10:22 And the standard of the camp of the children of Ephraim set out according to their armies; over their army was Elishama the son of Ammihud.
Numbers10:23 Over the army of the tribe of the children of Manasseh was Gamaliel the son of Pedahzur.
Numbers10:24 And over the army of the tribe of the children of Benjamin was Abidan the son of Gideoni.
Numbers10:25 Then the standard of the camp of the children of Dan (the rear guard of all the camps) set out according to their armies; over their army was Ahiezer the son of Ammishaddai.
Numbers10:26 Over the army of the tribe of the children of Asher was Pagiel the son of Ocran.
Numbers10:27 And over the army of the tribe of the children of Naphtali was Ahira the son of Enan.
Numbers10:28 Thus was the order of march of the children of Israel

Numbers10:30 And he said to him
Numbers10:31 So Moses said
Numbers10:32 And it shall be
Numbers10:33 So they departed from the mountain of the Lord on a journey of three days; and the ark of the covenant of the Lord went before them for the three days’ journey
Numbers10:34 And the cloud of the Lord was above them by day when they went out from the camp.
Numbers10:35 So it was
Numbers10:36 And when it rested
Numbers11:1 Now when the people complained
Numbers11:2 Then the people cried out to Moses
Numbers11:3 So he called the name of the place Taberah
Numbers11:4 Now the mixed multitude who were among them yielded to intense craving; so the children of Israel also wept again and said: “Who will give us meat to eat?
Numbers11:5 We remember the fish which we ate freely in Egypt
Numbers11:6 but now our whole being is dried up; there is nothing at all except this manna before our eyes!”
Numbers11:7 Now the manna was like coriander seed
Numbers11:8 The people went about and gathered it
Numbers11:9 And when the dew fell on the camp in the night
Numbers11:10 Then Moses heard the people weeping throughout their families
Numbers11:11 So Moses said to the Lord
Numbers11:12 Did I conceive all these people? Did I beget them
Numbers11:13 Where am I to get meat to give to all these people? For they weep all over me
Numbers11:14 I am not able to bear all these people alone
Numbers11:15 If You treat me like this
Numbers11:16 So the Lord said to Moses: “Gather to Me seventy men of the elders of Israel
Numbers11:17 Then I will come down and talk with you there. I will take of the Spirit that is upon you and will put the same upon them; and they shall bear the burden of the people with you
Numbers11:18 Then you shall say to the people
Numbers11:19 You shall eat
Numbers11:20 but for a whole month
Numbers11:21 And Moses said
Numbers11:22 Shall flocks and herds be slaughtered for them
Numbers11:23 And the Lord said to Moses
Numbers11:24 So Moses went out and told the people the words of the Lord
Numbers11:25 Then the Lord came down in the cloud
Numbers11:26 But two men had remained in the camp: the name of one was Eldad
Numbers11:27 And a young man ran and told Moses
Numbers11:28 So Joshua the son of Nun
Numbers11:29 Then Moses said to him
Numbers11:30 And Moses returned to the camp
Numbers11:31 Now a wind went out from the Lord
Numbers11:32 And the people stayed up all that day
Numbers11:33 But while the meat was still between their teeth
Numbers11:34 So he called the name of that place Kibroth Hattaavah
Numbers11:35 From Kibroth Hattaavah the people moved to Hazeroth

Numbers13:6 from the tribe of Judah
Numbers13:7 from the tribe of Issachar
Numbers13:8 from the tribe of Ephraim
Numbers13:9 from the tribe of Benjamin
Numbers13:10 from the tribe of Zebulun
Numbers13:11 from the tribe of Joseph
Numbers13:12 from the tribe of Dan
Numbers13:13 from the tribe of Asher
Numbers13:14 from the tribe of Naphtali
Numbers13:15 from the tribe of Gad
Numbers13:16 These are the names of the men whom Moses sent to spy out the land. And Moses called Hoshea the son of Nun
Numbers13:17 Then Moses sent them to spy out the land of Canaan
Numbers13:18 and see what the land is like: whether the people who dwell in it are strong or weak
Numbers13:19 whether the land they dwell in is good or bad; whether the cities they inhabit are like camps or strongholds;
Numbers13:20 whether the land is rich or poor; and whether there are forests there or not. Be of good courage. And bring some of the fruit of the land.” Now the time was the season of the first ripe grapes.
Numbers13:21 So they went up and spied out the land from the Wilderness of Zin as far as Rehob
Numbers13:22 And they went up through the South and came to Hebron; Ahiman
Numbers13:23 Then they came to the Valley of Eshcol
Numbers13:24 The place was called the Valley of Eshcol
Numbers13:25 And they returned from spying out the land after forty days.
Numbers13:26 Now they departed and came back to Moses and Aaron and all the congregation of the children of Israel in the Wilderness of Paran
Numbers13:27 Then they told him
Numbers13:28 Nevertheless the people who dwell in the land are strong; the cities are fortified and very large; moreover we saw the descendants of Anak there.
Numbers13:29 The Amalekites dwell in the land of the South; the Hittites
Numbers13:30 Then Caleb quieted the people before Moses
Numbers13:31 But the men who had gone up with him said
Numbers13:32 And they gave the children of Israel a bad report of the land which they had spied out
Numbers13:33 There we saw the giants (the descendants of Anak came from the giants); and we were like grasshoppers in our own sight
Numbers14:1 So all the congregation lifted up their voices and cried
Numbers14:2 And all the children of Israel complained against Moses and Aaron
Numbers14:3 Why has the Lord brought us to this land to fall by the sword
Numbers14:4 So they said to one another
Numbers14:5 Then Moses and Aaron fell on their faces before all the assembly of the congregation of the children of Israel.
Numbers14:6 But Joshua the son of Nun and Caleb the son of Jephunneh
Numbers14:7 and they spoke to all the congregation of the children of Israel
Numbers14:8 If the Lord delights in us
Numbers14:9 Only do not rebel against the Lord
Numbers14:10 And all the congregation said to stone them with stones. Now the glory of the Lord appeared in the tabernacle of meeting before all the children of Israel.
Numbers14:11 Then the Lord said to Moses: “How long will these people reject Me? And how long will they not believe Me
Numbers14:12 I will strike them with the pestilence and disinherit them
Numbers14:13 And Moses said to the Lord: “Then the Egyptians will hear it
Numbers14:14 and they will tell it to the inhabitants of this land. They have heard that You
Numbers14:15 Now if You kill these people as one man
Numbers14:16 ‘Because the Lord was not able to bring this people to the land which He swore to give them
Numbers14:17 And now
Numbers14:18 ‘The Lord is longsuffering and abundant in mercy
Numbers14:19 Pardon the iniquity of this people
Numbers14:20 Then the Lord said: “I have pardoned
Numbers14:21 but truly
Numbers14:22 because all these men who have seen My glory and the signs which I did in Egypt and in the wilderness
Numbers14:23 they certainly shall not see the land of which I swore to their fathers
Numbers14:24 But My servant Caleb
Numbers14:25 Now the Amalekites and the Canaanites dwell in the valley; tomorrow turn and move out into the wilderness by the Way of the Red Sea.”
Numbers14:26 And the Lord spoke to Moses and Aaron
Numbers14:27 “How long shall I bear with this evil congregation who complain against Me? I have heard the complaints which the children of Israel make against Me.
Numbers14:28 Say to them
Numbers14:29 The carcasses of you who have complained against Me shall fall in this wilderness
Numbers14:30 Except for Caleb the son of Jephunneh and Joshua the son of Nun
Numbers14:31 But your little ones
Numbers14:32 But as for you
Numbers14:33 And your sons shall be shepherds in the wilderness forty years
Numbers14:34 According to the number of the days in which you spied out the land
Numbers14:35 I the Lord have spoken this. I will surely do so to all this evil congregation who are gathered together against Me. In this wilderness they shall be consumed
Numbers14:36 Now the men whom Moses sent to spy out the land
Numbers14:37 those very men who brought the evil report about the land
Numbers14:38 But Joshua the son of Nun and Caleb the son of Jephunneh remained alive
Numbers14:39 Then Moses told these words to all the children of Israel
Numbers14:40 And they rose early in the morning and went up to the top of the mountain
Numbers14:41 And Moses said
Numbers14:42 Do not go up
Numbers14:43 For the Amalekites and the Canaanites are there before you
Numbers14:44 But they presumed to go up to the mountaintop. Nevertheless
Numbers14:45 Then the Amalekites and the Canaanites who dwelt in that mountain came down and attacked them
Numbers15:1 And the Lord spoke to Moses
Numbers15:2 “Speak to the children of Israel
Numbers15:3 and you make an offering by fire to the Lord
Numbers15:4 then he who presents his offering to the Lord shall bring a grain offering of one-tenth of an ephah of fine flour mixed with one-fourth of a hin of oil;
Numbers15:5 and one-fourth of a hin of wine as a drink offering you shall prepare with the burnt offering or the sacrifice
Numbers15:6 Or for a ram you shall prepare as a grain offering two-tenths of an ephah of fine flour mixed with one-third of a hin of oil;
Numbers15:7 and as a drink offering you shall offer one-third of a hin of wine as a sweet aroma to the Lord.
Numbers15:8 And when you prepare a young bull as a burnt offering
Numbers15:9 then shall be offered with the young bull a grain offering of three-tenths of an ephah of fine flour mixed with half a hin of oil;
Numbers15:10 and you shall bring as the drink offering half a hin of wine as an offering made by fire
Numbers15:11 ‘Thus it shall be done for each young bull
Numbers15:12 According to the number that you prepare
Numbers15:13 All who are native-born shall do these things in this manner
Numbers15:14 And if a stranger dwells with you
Numbers15:15 One ordinance shall be for you of the assembly and for the stranger who dwells with you
Numbers15:16 One law and one custom shall be for you and for the stranger who dwells with you.’ ”
Numbers15:17 Again the Lord spoke to Moses
Numbers15:18 “Speak to the children of Israel
Numbers15:19 then it will be
Numbers15:20 You shall offer up a cake of the first of your ground meal as a heave offering; as a heave offering of the threshing floor
Numbers15:21 Of the first of your ground meal you shall give to the Lord a heave offering throughout your generations.
Numbers15:22 ‘If you sin unintentionally
Numbers15:23 all that the Lord has commanded you by the hand of Moses
Numbers15:24 then it will be
Numbers15:25 So the priest shall make atonement for the whole congregation of the children of Israel
Numbers15:26 It shall be forgiven the whole congregation of the children of Israel and the stranger who dwells among them
Numbers15:27 ‘And if a person sins unintentionally
Numbers15:28 So the priest shall make atonement for the person who sins unintentionally
Numbers15:29 You shall have one law for him who sins unintentionally
Numbers15:30 ‘But the person who does anything presumptuously
Numbers15:31 Because he has despised the word of the Lord
Numbers15:32 Now while the children of Israel were in the wilderness
Numbers15:33 And those who found him gathering sticks brought him to Moses and Aaron
Numbers15:34 They put him under guard
Numbers15:35 Then the Lord said to Moses
Numbers15:36 So
Numbers15:37 Again the Lord spoke to Moses
Numbers15:38 “Speak to the children of Israel: Tell them to make tassels on the corners of their garments throughout their generations
Numbers15:39 And you shall have the tassel
Numbers15:40 and that you may remember and do all My commandments
Numbers15:41 I am the Lord your God
Numbers16:1 Now Korah the son of Izhar
Numbers16:2 and they rose up before Moses with some of the children of Israel
Numbers16:3 They gathered together against Moses and Aaron
Numbers16:4 So when Moses heard it
Numbers16:5 and he spoke to Korah and all his company
Numbers16:6 Do this: Take censers
Numbers16:7 put fire in them and put incense in them before the Lord tomorrow
Numbers16:8 Then Moses said to Korah
Numbers16:9 Is it a small thing to you that the God of Israel has separated you from the congregation of Israel
Numbers16:10 and that He has brought you near to Himself
Numbers16:11 Therefore you and all your company are gathered together against the Lord. And what is Aaron that you complain against him?”
Numbers16:12 And Moses sent to call Dathan and Abiram the sons of Eliab
Numbers16:13 Is it a small thing that you have brought us up out of a land flowing with milk and honey
Numbers16:14 Moreover you have not brought us into a land flowing with milk and honey
Numbers16:15 Then Moses was very angry
Numbers16:16 And Moses said to Korah
Numbers16:17 Let each take his censer and put incense in it
Numbers16:18 So every man took his censer
Numbers16:19 And Korah gathered all the congregation against them at the door of the tabernacle of meeting. Then the glory of the Lord appeared to all the congregation.
Numbers16:20 And the Lord spoke to Moses and Aaron
Numbers16:21 “Separate yourselves from among this congregation
Numbers16:22 Then they fell on their faces
Numbers16:23 So the Lord spoke to Moses
Numbers16:24 “Speak to the congregation
Numbers16:25 Then Moses rose and went to Dathan and Abiram
Numbers16:26 And he spoke to the congregation
Numbers16:27 So they got away from around the tents of Korah
Numbers16:28 And Moses said: “By this you shall know that the Lord has sent me to do all these works
Numbers16:29 If these men die naturally like all men
Numbers16:30 But if the Lord creates a new thing
Numbers16:31 Now it came to pass
Numbers16:32 and the earth opened its mouth and swallowed them up
Numbers16:33 So they and all those with them went down alive into the pit; the earth closed over them
Numbers16:34 Then all Israel who were around them fled at their cry
Numbers16:35 And a fire came out from the Lord and consumed the two hundred and fifty men who were offering incense.
Numbers16:36 Then the Lord spoke to Moses
Numbers16:37 “Tell Eleazar
Numbers16:38 The censers of these men who sinned against their own souls
Numbers16:39 So Eleazar the priest took the bronze censers
Numbers16:40 to be a memorial to the children of Israel that no outsider
Numbers16:41 On the next day all the congregation of the children of Israel complained against Moses and Aaron
Numbers16:42 Now it happened
Numbers16:43 Then Moses and Aaron came before the tabernacle of meeting.
Numbers16:44 And the Lord spoke to Moses
Numbers16:45 “Get away from among this congregation
Numbers16:46 So Moses said to Aaron
Numbers16:47 Then Aaron took it as Moses commanded
Numbers16:48 And he stood between the dead and the living; so the plague was stopped.
Numbers16:49 Now those who died in the plague were fourteen thousand seven hundred

Numbers18:1 Then the Lord said to Aaron: “You and your sons and your father’s house with you shall bear the iniquity related to the sanctuary
Numbers18:2 Also bring with you your brethren of the tribe of Levi
Numbers18:3 They shall attend to your needs and all the needs of the tabernacle; but they shall not come near the articles of the sanctuary and the altar
Numbers18:4 They shall be joined with you and attend to the needs of the tabernacle of meeting
Numbers18:5 And you shall attend to the duties of the sanctuary and the duties of the altar
Numbers18:6 Behold
Numbers18:7 Therefore you and your sons with you shall attend to your priesthood for everything at the altar and behind the veil; and you shall serve. I give your priesthood to you as a gift for service
Numbers18:8 And the Lord spoke to Aaron: “Here
Numbers18:9 This shall be yours of the most holy things reserved from the fire: every offering of theirs
Numbers18:10 In a most holy place you shall eat it; every male shall eat it. It shall be holy to you.
Numbers18:11 “This also is yours: the heave offering of their gift
Numbers18:12 “All the best of the oil
Numbers18:13 Whatever first ripe fruit is in their land
Numbers18:14 “Every devoted thing in Israel shall be yours.

Numbers18:25 Then the Lord spoke to Moses
Numbers18:26 “Speak thus to the Levites
Numbers18:27 And your heave offering shall be reckoned to you as though it were the grain of the threshing floor and as the fullness of the winepress.
Numbers18:28 Thus you shall also offer a heave offering to the Lord from all your tithes which you receive from the children of Israel
Numbers18:29 Of all your gifts you shall offer up every heave offering due to the Lord
Numbers18:30 Therefore you shall say to them: ‘When you have lifted up the best of it
Numbers18:31 You may eat it in any place
Numbers18:32 And you shall bear no sin because of it
Numbers19:1 Now the Lord spoke to Moses and Aaron
Numbers19:2 “This is the ordinance of the law which the Lord has commanded
Numbers19:3 You shall give it to Eleazar the priest
Numbers19:4 and Eleazar the priest shall take some of its blood with his finger
Numbers19:5 Then the heifer shall be burned in his sight: its hide
Numbers19:6 And the priest shall take cedar wood and hyssop and scarlet
Numbers19:7 Then the priest shall wash his clothes
Numbers19:8 And the one who burns it shall wash his clothes in water
Numbers19:9 Then a man who is clean shall gather up the ashes of the heifer
Numbers19:10 And the one who gathers the ashes of the heifer shall wash his clothes
Numbers19:11 ‘He who touches the dead body of anyone shall be unclean seven days.
Numbers19:12 He shall purify himself with the water on the third day and on the seventh day; then he will be clean. But if he does not purify himself on the third day and on the seventh day
Numbers19:13 Whoever touches the body of anyone who has died
Numbers19:14 ‘This is the law when a man dies in a tent: All who come into the tent and all who are in the tent shall be unclean seven days;
Numbers19:15 and every open vessel
Numbers19:16 Whoever in the open field touches one who is slain by a sword or who has died
Numbers19:17 ‘And for an unclean person they shall take some of the ashes of the heifer burnt for purification from sin
Numbers19:18 A clean person shall take hyssop and dip it in the water
Numbers19:19 The clean person shall sprinkle the unclean on the third day and on the seventh day; and on the seventh day he shall purify himself
Numbers19:20 ‘But the man who is unclean and does not purify himself
Numbers19:21 It shall be a perpetual statute for them. He who sprinkles the water of purification shall wash his clothes; and he who touches the water of purification shall be unclean until evening.
Numbers19:22 Whatever the unclean person touches shall be unclean; and the person who touches it shall be unclean until evening.’ ”

Numbers28:1 Now the Lord spoke to Moses
Numbers28:2 “Command the children of Israel
Numbers28:3 “And you shall say to them
Numbers28:4 The one lamb you shall offer in the morning
Numbers28:5 and one-tenth of an ephah of fine flour as a grain offering mixed with one-fourth of a hin of pressed oil.
Numbers28:6 It is a regular burnt offering which was ordained at Mount Sinai for a sweet aroma
Numbers28:7 And its drink offering shall be one-fourth of a hin for each lamb; in a holy place you shall pour out the drink to the Lord as an offering.
Numbers28:8 The other lamb you shall offer in the evening; as the morning grain offering and its drink offering
Numbers28:9 ‘And on the Sabbath day two lambs in their first year
Numbers28:10 this is the burnt offering for every Sabbath
Numbers28:11 ‘At the beginnings of your months you shall present a burnt offering to the Lord: two young bulls
Numbers28:12 three-tenths of an ephah of fine flour as a grain offering
Numbers28:13 and one-tenth of an ephah of fine flour
Numbers28:14 Their drink offering shall be half a hin of wine for a bull
Numbers28:15 Also one kid of the goats as a sin offering to the Lord shall be offered
Numbers28:16 ‘On the fourteenth day of the first month is the Passover of the Lord.
Numbers28:17 And on the fifteenth day of this month is the feast; unleavened bread shall be eaten for seven days.
Numbers28:18 On the first day you shall have a holy convocation. You shall do no customary work.
Numbers28:19 And you shall present an offering made by fire as a burnt offering to the Lord: two young bulls
Numbers28:20 Their grain offering shall be of fine flour mixed with oil: three-tenths of an ephah you shall offer for a bull
Numbers28:21 you shall offer one-tenth of an ephah for each of the seven lambs;
Numbers28:22 also one goat as a sin offering
Numbers28:23 You shall offer these besides the burnt offering of the morning
Numbers28:24 In this manner you shall offer the food of the offering made by fire daily for seven days
Numbers28:25 And on the seventh day you shall have a holy convocation. You shall do no customary work.
Numbers28:26 ‘Also on the day of the firstfruits
Numbers28:27 You shall present a burnt offering as a sweet aroma to the Lord: two young bulls
Numbers28:28 with their grain offering of fine flour mixed with oil: three-tenths of an ephah for each bull
Numbers28:29 and one-tenth for each of the seven lambs;
Numbers28:30 also one kid of the goats
Numbers28:31 Be sure they are without blemish. You shall present them with their drink offerings
Numbers29:1 ‘And in the seventh month
Numbers29:2 You shall offer a burnt offering as a sweet aroma to the Lord: one young bull
Numbers29:3 Their grain offering shall be fine flour mixed with oil: three-tenths of an ephah for the bull
Numbers29:4 and one-tenth for each of the seven lambs;
Numbers29:5 also one kid of the goats as a sin offering
Numbers29:6 besides the burnt offering with its grain offering for the New Moon
Numbers29:7 ‘On the tenth day of this seventh month you shall have a holy convocation. You shall afflict your souls; you shall not do any work.
Numbers29:8 You shall present a burnt offering to the Lord as a sweet aroma: one young bull
Numbers29:9 Their grain offering shall be of fine flour mixed with oil: three-tenths of an ephah for the bull
Numbers29:10 and one-tenth for each of the seven lambs;
Numbers29:11 also one kid of the goats as a sin offering
Numbers29:12 ‘On the fifteenth day of the seventh month you shall have a holy convocation. You shall do no customary work

Numbers29:25 also one kid of the goats as a sin offering

Numbers29:27 and their grain offering and their drink offerings for the bulls

Numbers30:1 Then Moses spoke to the heads of the tribes concerning the children of Israel
Numbers30:2 If a man makes a vow to the Lord
Numbers30:3 “Or if a woman makes a vow to the Lord
Numbers30:4 and her father hears her vow and the agreement by which she has bound herself
Numbers30:5 But if her father overrules her on the day that he hears
Numbers30:6 “If indeed she takes a husband
Numbers30:7 and her husband hears it
Numbers30:8 But if her husband overrules her on the day that he hears it
Numbers30:9 “Also any vow of a widow or a divorced woman
Numbers30:10 “If she vowed in her husband’s house
Numbers30:11 and her husband heard it
Numbers30:12 But if her husband truly made them void on the day he heard them
Numbers30:13 Every vow and every binding oath to afflict her soul
Numbers30:14 Now if her husband makes no response whatever to her from day to day
Numbers30:15 But if he does make them void after he has heard them
Numbers30:16 These are the statutes which the Lord commanded Moses
