Numbers31:2 “Take vengeance on the Midianites for the children of Israel. Afterward you shall be gathered to your people.”
Numbers31:3 So Moses spoke to the people
Numbers31:4 A thousand from each tribe of all the tribes of Israel you shall send to the war.”
Numbers31:5 So there were recruited from the divisions of Israel one thousand from each tribe
Numbers31:6 Then Moses sent them to the war
Numbers31:7 And they warred against the Midianites
Numbers31:8 They killed the kings of Midian with the rest of those who were killed— Evi
Numbers31:9 And the children of Israel took the women of Midian captive
Numbers31:10 They also burned with fire all the cities where they dwelt
Numbers31:11 And they took all the spoil and all the booty—of man and beast.
Numbers31:12 Then they brought the captives
Numbers31:13 And Moses
Numbers31:14 But Moses was angry with the officers of the army
Numbers31:15 And Moses said to them: “Have you kept all the women alive?
Numbers31:16 Look
Numbers31:17 Now therefore
Numbers31:18 But keep alive for yourselves all the young girls who have not known a man intimately.
Numbers31:19 And as for you
Numbers31:20 Purify every garment
Numbers31:21 Then Eleazar the priest said to the men of war who had gone to the battle
Numbers31:22 Only the gold
Numbers31:23 everything that can endure fire
Numbers31:24 And you shall wash your clothes on the seventh day and be clean
Numbers31:25 Now the Lord spoke to Moses
Numbers31:26 “Count up the plunder that was taken—of man and beast—you and Eleazar the priest and the chief fathers of the congregation;
Numbers31:27 and divide the plunder into two parts
Numbers31:28 And levy a tribute for the Lord on the men of war who went out to battle: one of every five hundred of the persons
Numbers31:29 take it from their half
Numbers31:30 And from the children of Israel’s half you shall take one of every fifty
Numbers31:31 So Moses and Eleazar the priest did as the Lord commanded Moses.
Numbers31:32 The booty remaining from the plunder
Numbers31:33 seventy-two thousand cattle
Numbers31:34 sixty-one thousand donkeys
Numbers31:35 and thirty-two thousand persons in all
Numbers31:36 And the half
Numbers31:37 and the Lord’s tribute of the sheep was six hundred and seventy-five.
Numbers31:38 The cattle were thirty-six thousand
Numbers31:39 The donkeys were thirty thousand five hundred
Numbers31:40 The persons were sixteen thousand
Numbers31:41 So Moses gave the tribute which was the Lord’s heave offering to Eleazar the priest
Numbers31:42 And from the children of Israel’s half
Numbers31:43 now the half belonging to the congregation was three hundred and thirty-seven thousand five hundred sheep
Numbers31:44 thirty-six thousand cattle
Numbers31:45 thirty thousand five hundred donkeys
Numbers31:46 and sixteen thousand persons—
Numbers31:47 and from the children of Israel’s half Moses took one of every fifty
Numbers31:48 Then the officers who were over thousands of the army
Numbers31:49 and they said to Moses
Numbers31:50 Therefore we have brought an offering for the Lord
Numbers31:51 So Moses and Eleazar the priest received the gold from them
Numbers31:52 And all the gold of the offering that they offered to the Lord
Numbers31:53 (The men of war had taken spoil
Numbers31:54 And Moses and Eleazar the priest received the gold from the captains of thousands and of hundreds
Numbers32:1 Now the children of Reuben and the children of Gad had a very great multitude of livestock; and when they saw the land of Jazer and the land of Gilead
Numbers32:2 the children of Gad and the children of Reuben came and spoke to Moses
Numbers32:3 “Ataroth
Numbers32:4 the country which the Lord defeated before the congregation of Israel
Numbers32:5 Therefore they said
Numbers32:6 And Moses said to the children of Gad and to the children of Reuben: “Shall your brethren go to war while you sit here?
Numbers32:7 Now why will you discourage the heart of the children of Israel from going over into the land which the Lord has given them?
Numbers32:8 Thus your fathers did when I sent them away from Kadesh Barnea to see the land.
Numbers32:9 For when they went up to the Valley of Eshcol and saw the land
Numbers32:10 So the Lord’s anger was aroused on that day
Numbers32:11 ‘Surely none of the men who came up from Egypt
Numbers32:12 except Caleb the son of Jephunneh
Numbers32:13 So the Lord’s anger was aroused against Israel
Numbers32:14 And look! You have risen in your fathers’ place
Numbers32:15 For if you turn away from following Him
Numbers32:16 Then they came near to him and said: “We will build sheepfolds here for our livestock
Numbers32:17 but we ourselves will be armed
Numbers32:18 We will not return to our homes until every one of the children of Israel has received his inheritance.
Numbers32:19 For we will not inherit with them on the other side of the Jordan and beyond
Numbers32:20 Then Moses said to them: “If you do this thing
Numbers32:21 and all your armed men cross over the Jordan before the Lord until He has driven out His enemies from before Him
Numbers32:22 and the land is subdued before the Lord
Numbers32:23 But if you do not do so
Numbers32:24 Build cities for your little ones and folds for your sheep
Numbers32:25 And the children of Gad and the children of Reuben spoke to Moses
Numbers32:26 Our little ones
Numbers32:27 but your servants will cross over
Numbers32:28 So Moses gave command concerning them to Eleazar the priest
Numbers32:29 And Moses said to them: “If the children of Gad and the children of Reuben cross over the Jordan with you
Numbers32:30 But if they do not cross over armed with you
Numbers32:31 Then the children of Gad and the children of Reuben answered
Numbers32:32 We will cross over armed before the Lord into the land of Canaan
Numbers32:33 So Moses gave to the children of Gad
Numbers32:34 And the children of Gad built Dibon and Ataroth and Aroer
Numbers32:35 Atroth and Shophan and Jazer and Jogbehah
Numbers32:36 Beth Nimrah and Beth Haran
Numbers32:37 And the children of Reuben built Heshbon and Elealeh and Kirjathaim
Numbers32:38 Nebo and Baal Meon (their names being changed) and Shibmah; and they gave other names to the cities which they built.
Numbers32:39 And the children of Machir the son of Manasseh went to Gilead and took it
Numbers32:40 So Moses gave Gilead to Machir the son of Manasseh
Numbers32:41 Also Jair the son of Manasseh went and took its small towns
Numbers32:42 Then Nobah went and took Kenath and its villages
Numbers33:1 These are the journeys of the children of Israel
Numbers33:2 Now Moses wrote down the starting points of their journeys at the command of the Lord. And these are their journeys according to their starting points:
Numbers33:3 They departed from Rameses in the first month
Numbers33:4 For the Egyptians were burying all their firstborn
Numbers33:5 Then the children of Israel moved from Rameses and camped at Succoth.
Numbers33:6 They departed from Succoth and camped at Etham
Numbers33:7 They moved from Etham and turned back to Pi Hahiroth
Numbers33:8 They departed from before Hahiroth and passed through the midst of the sea into the wilderness
Numbers33:9 They moved from Marah and came to Elim. At Elim were twelve springs of water and seventy palm trees; so they camped there.
Numbers33:10 They moved from Elim and camped by the Red Sea.
Numbers33:11 They moved from the Red Sea and camped in the Wilderness of Sin.
Numbers33:12 They journeyed from the Wilderness of Sin and camped at Dophkah.
Numbers33:13 They departed from Dophkah and camped at Alush.
Numbers33:14 They moved from Alush and camped at Rephidim
Numbers33:15 They departed from Rephidim and camped in the Wilderness of Sinai.
Numbers33:16 They moved from the Wilderness of Sinai and camped at Kibroth Hattaavah.
Numbers33:17 They departed from Kibroth Hattaavah and camped at Hazeroth.
Numbers33:18 They departed from Hazeroth and camped at Rithmah.
Numbers33:19 They departed from Rithmah and camped at Rimmon Perez.
Numbers33:20 They departed from Rimmon Perez and camped at Libnah.
Numbers33:21 They moved from Libnah and camped at Rissah.
Numbers33:22 They journeyed from Rissah and camped at Kehelathah.
Numbers33:23 They went from Kehelathah and camped at Mount Shepher.
Numbers33:24 They moved from Mount Shepher and camped at Haradah.
Numbers33:25 They moved from Haradah and camped at Makheloth.
Numbers33:26 They moved from Makheloth and camped at Tahath.
Numbers33:27 They departed from Tahath and camped at Terah.
Numbers33:28 They moved from Terah and camped at Mithkah.
Numbers33:29 They went from Mithkah and camped at Hashmonah.
Numbers33:30 They departed from Hashmonah and camped at Moseroth.
Numbers33:31 They departed from Moseroth and camped at Bene Jaakan.
Numbers33:32 They moved from Bene Jaakan and camped at Hor Hagidgad.
Numbers33:33 They went from Hor Hagidgad and camped at Jotbathah.
Numbers33:34 They moved from Jotbathah and camped at Abronah.
Numbers33:35 They departed from Abronah and camped at Ezion Geber.
Numbers33:36 They moved from Ezion Geber and camped in the Wilderness of Zin
Numbers33:37 They moved from Kadesh and camped at Mount Hor
Numbers33:38 Then Aaron the priest went up to Mount Hor at the command of the Lord
Numbers33:39 Aaron was one hundred and twenty-three years old when he died on Mount Hor.
Numbers33:40 Now the king of Arad
Numbers33:41 So they departed from Mount Hor and camped at Zalmonah.
Numbers33:42 They departed from Zalmonah and camped at Punon.
Numbers33:43 They departed from Punon and camped at Oboth.
Numbers33:44 They departed from Oboth and camped at Ije Abarim
Numbers33:45 They departed from Ijim and camped at Dibon Gad.
Numbers33:46 They moved from Dibon Gad and camped at Almon Diblathaim.
Numbers33:47 They moved from Almon Diblathaim and camped in the mountains of Abarim
Numbers33:48 They departed from the mountains of Abarim and camped in the plains of Moab by the Jordan
Numbers33:49 They camped by the Jordan
Numbers33:50 Now the Lord spoke to Moses in the plains of Moab by the Jordan
Numbers33:51 “Speak to the children of Israel
Numbers33:52 then you shall drive out all the inhabitants of the land from before you
Numbers33:53 you shall dispossess the inhabitants of the land and dwell in it
Numbers33:54 And you shall divide the land by lot as an inheritance among your families; to the larger you shall give a larger inheritance
Numbers33:55 But if you do not drive out the inhabitants of the land from before you
Numbers33:56 Moreover it shall be that I will do to you as I thought to do to them.’ ”
Numbers34:1 Then the Lord spoke to Moses
Numbers34:2 “Command the children of Israel
Numbers34:3 Your southern border shall be from the Wilderness of Zin along the border of Edom; then your southern border shall extend eastward to the end of the Salt Sea;
Numbers34:4 your border shall turn from the southern side of the Ascent of Akrabbim
Numbers34:5 the border shall turn from Azmon to the Brook of Egypt
Numbers34:6 ‘As for the western border
Numbers34:7 ‘And this shall be your northern border: From the Great Sea you shall mark out your border line to Mount Hor;
Numbers34:8 from Mount Hor you shall mark out your border to the entrance of Hamath; then the direction of the border shall be toward Zedad;
Numbers34:9 the border shall proceed to Ziphron
Numbers34:10 ‘You shall mark out your eastern border from Hazar Enan to Shepham;
Numbers34:11 the border shall go down from Shepham to Riblah on the east side of Ain; the border shall go down and reach to the eastern side of the Sea of Chinnereth;
Numbers34:12 the border shall go down along the Jordan
Numbers34:13 Then Moses commanded the children of Israel
Numbers34:14 For the tribe of the children of Reuben according to the house of their fathers
Numbers34:15 The two tribes and the half-tribe have received their inheritance on this side of the Jordan
Numbers34:16 And the Lord spoke to Moses
Numbers34:17 “These are the names of the men who shall divide the land among you as an inheritance: Eleazar the priest and Joshua the son of Nun.
Numbers34:18 And you shall take one leader of every tribe to divide the land for the inheritance.
Numbers34:19 These are the names of the men: from the tribe of Judah
Numbers34:20 from the tribe of the children of Simeon
Numbers34:21 from the tribe of Benjamin
Numbers34:22 a leader from the tribe of the children of Dan
Numbers34:23 from the sons of Joseph: a leader from the tribe of the children of Manasseh
Numbers34:24 and a leader from the tribe of the children of Ephraim
Numbers34:25 a leader from the tribe of the children of Zebulun
Numbers34:26 a leader from the tribe of the children of Issachar
Numbers34:27 a leader from the tribe of the children of Asher
Numbers34:28 and a leader from the tribe of the children of Naphtali
Numbers34:29 These are the ones the Lord commanded to divide the inheritance among the children of Israel in the land of Canaan.
Numbers35:1 And the Lord spoke to Moses in the plains of Moab by the Jordan across from Jericho
Numbers35:2 “Command the children of Israel that they give the Levites cities to dwell in from the inheritance of their possession
Numbers35:3 They shall have the cities to dwell in; and their common-land shall be for their cattle
Numbers35:4 The common-land of the cities which you will give the Levites shall extend from the wall of the city outward a thousand cubits all around.
Numbers35:5 And you shall measure outside the city on the east side two thousand cubits
Numbers35:6 “Now among the cities which you will give to the Levites you shall appoint six cities of refuge
Numbers35:7 So all the cities you will give to the Levites shall be forty-eight; these you shall give with their common-land.
Numbers35:8 And the cities which you will give shall be from the possession of the children of Israel; from the larger tribe you shall give many
Numbers35:9 Then the Lord spoke to Moses
Numbers35:10 “Speak to the children of Israel
Numbers35:11 then you shall appoint cities to be cities of refuge for you
Numbers35:12 They shall be cities of refuge for you from the avenger
Numbers35:13 And of the cities which you give
Numbers35:14 You shall appoint three cities on this side of the Jordan
Numbers35:15 These six cities shall be for refuge for the children of Israel
Numbers35:16 ‘But if he strikes him with an iron implement
Numbers35:17 And if he strikes him with a stone in the hand
Numbers35:18 Or if he strikes him with a wooden hand weapon
Numbers35:19 The avenger of blood himself shall put the murderer to death; when he meets him
Numbers35:20 If he pushes him out of hatred or
Numbers35:21 or in enmity he strikes him with his hand so that he dies
Numbers35:22 ‘However
Numbers35:23 or uses a stone
Numbers35:24 then the congregation shall judge between the manslayer and the avenger of blood according to these judgments.
Numbers35:25 So the congregation shall deliver the manslayer from the hand of the avenger of blood
Numbers35:26 But if the manslayer at any time goes outside the limits of the city of refuge where he fled
Numbers35:27 and the avenger of blood finds him outside the limits of his city of refuge
Numbers35:28 because he should have remained in his city of refuge until the death of the high priest. But after the death of the high priest the manslayer may return to the land of his possession.
Numbers35:29 ‘And these things shall be a statute of judgment to you throughout your generations in all your dwellings.
Numbers35:30 Whoever kills a person
Numbers35:31 Moreover you shall take no ransom for the life of a murderer who is guilty of death
Numbers35:32 And you shall take no ransom for him who has fled to his city of refuge
Numbers35:33 So you shall not pollute the land where you are; for blood defiles the land
Numbers35:34 Therefore do not defile the land which you inhabit

Deuteronomy4:1 “Now
Deuteronomy4:2 You shall not add to the word which I command you
Deuteronomy4:3 Your eyes have seen what the Lord did at Baal Peor; for the Lord your God has destroyed from among you all the men who followed Baal of Peor.
Deuteronomy4:4 But you who held fast to the Lord your God are alive today
Deuteronomy4:5 “Surely I have taught you statutes and judgments
Deuteronomy4:6 Therefore be careful to observe them; for this is your wisdom and your understanding in the sight of the peoples who will hear all these statutes
Deuteronomy4:7 “For what great nation is there that has God so near to it
Deuteronomy4:8 And what great nation is there that has such statutes and righteous judgments as are in all this law which I set before you this day?
Deuteronomy4:9 Only take heed to yourself
Deuteronomy4:10 especially concerning the day you stood before the Lord your God in Horeb
Deuteronomy4:11 “Then you came near and stood at the foot of the mountain
Deuteronomy4:12 And the Lord spoke to you out of the midst of the fire. You heard the sound of the words
Deuteronomy4:13 So He declared to you His covenant which He commanded you to perform
Deuteronomy4:14 And the Lord commanded me at that time to teach you statutes and judgments
Deuteronomy4:15 “Take careful heed to yourselves
Deuteronomy4:16 lest you act corruptly and make for yourselves a carved image in the form of any figure: the likeness of male or female
Deuteronomy4:17 the likeness of any animal that is on the earth or the likeness of any winged bird that flies in the air
Deuteronomy4:18 the likeness of anything that creeps on the ground or the likeness of any fish that is in the water beneath the earth.
Deuteronomy4:19 And take heed
Deuteronomy4:20 But the Lord has taken you and brought you out of the iron furnace
Deuteronomy4:21 Furthermore the Lord was angry with me for your sakes
Deuteronomy4:22 But I must die in this land
Deuteronomy4:23 Take heed to yourselves
Deuteronomy4:24 For the Lord your God is a consuming fire
Deuteronomy4:25 “When you beget children and grandchildren and have grown old in the land
Deuteronomy4:26 I call heaven and earth to witness against you this day
Deuteronomy4:27 And the Lord will scatter you among the peoples
Deuteronomy4:28 And there you will serve gods
Deuteronomy4:29 But from there you will seek the Lord your God
Deuteronomy4:30 When you are in distress
Deuteronomy4:31 (for the Lord your God is a merciful God)
Deuteronomy4:32 “For ask now concerning the days that are past
Deuteronomy4:33 Did any people ever hear the voice of God speaking out of the midst of the fire
Deuteronomy4:34 Or did God ever try to go and take for Himself a nation from the midst of another nation
Deuteronomy4:35 To you it was shown
Deuteronomy4:36 Out of heaven He let you hear His voice
Deuteronomy4:37 And because He loved your fathers
Deuteronomy4:38 driving out from before you nations greater and mightier than you
Deuteronomy4:39 Therefore know this day
Deuteronomy4:40 You shall therefore keep His statutes and His commandments which I command you today
Deuteronomy4:41 Then Moses set apart three cities on this side of the Jordan
Deuteronomy4:42 that the manslayer might flee there
Deuteronomy4:43 Bezer in the wilderness on the plateau for the Reubenites
Deuteronomy4:44 Now this is the law which Moses set before the children of Israel.
Deuteronomy4:45 These are the testimonies
Deuteronomy4:46 on this side of the Jordan
Deuteronomy4:47 And they took possession of his land and the land of Og king of Bashan
Deuteronomy4:48 from Aroer
Deuteronomy4:49 and all the plain on the east side of the Jordan as far as the Sea of the Arabah
Deuteronomy5:1 And Moses called all Israel
Deuteronomy5:2 The Lord our God made a covenant with us in Horeb.
Deuteronomy5:3 The Lord did not make this covenant with our fathers
Deuteronomy5:4 The Lord talked with you face to face on the mountain from the midst of the fire.
Deuteronomy5:5 I stood between the Lord and you at that time
Deuteronomy5:6 ‘I am the Lord your God who brought you out of the land of Egypt
Deuteronomy5:7 ‘You shall have no other gods before Me.
Deuteronomy5:8 ‘You shall not make for yourself a carved image—any likeness of anything that is in heaven above
Deuteronomy5:9 you shall not bow down to them nor serve them. For I
Deuteronomy5:10 but showing mercy to thousands
Deuteronomy5:11 ‘You shall not take the name of the Lord your God in vain
Deuteronomy5:12 ‘Observe the Sabbath day
Deuteronomy5:13 Six days you shall labor and do all your work
Deuteronomy5:14 but the seventh day is the Sabbath of the Lord your God. In it you shall do no work: you
Deuteronomy5:15 And remember that you were a slave in the land of Egypt
Deuteronomy5:16 ‘Honor your father and your mother
Deuteronomy5:17 ‘You shall not murder.
Deuteronomy5:18 ‘You shall not commit adultery.
Deuteronomy5:19 ‘You shall not steal.

Deuteronomy9:5 It is not because of your righteousness or the uprightness of your heart that you go in to possess their land

Deuteronomy15:1 “At the end of every seven years you shall grant a release of debts.
Deuteronomy15:2 And this is the form of the release: Every creditor who has lent anything to his neighbor shall release it; he shall not require it of his neighbor or his brother
Deuteronomy15:3 Of a foreigner you may require it; but you shall give up your claim to what is owed by your brother
Deuteronomy15:4 except when there may be no poor among you; for the Lord will greatly bless you in the land which the Lord your God is giving you to possess as an inheritance—
Deuteronomy15:5 only if you carefully obey the voice of the Lord your God
Deuteronomy15:6 For the Lord your God will bless you just as He promised you; you shall lend to many nations
Deuteronomy15:7 “If there is among you a poor man of your brethren
Deuteronomy15:8 but you shall open your hand wide to him and willingly lend him sufficient for his need
Deuteronomy15:9 Beware lest there be a wicked thought in your heart
Deuteronomy15:10 You shall surely give to him
Deuteronomy15:11 For the poor will never cease from the land; therefore I command you
Deuteronomy15:12 “If your brother
Deuteronomy15:13 And when you send him away free from you
Deuteronomy15:14 you shall supply him liberally from your flock
Deuteronomy15:15 You shall remember that you were a slave in the land of Egypt
Deuteronomy15:16 And if it happens that he says to you
Deuteronomy15:17 then you shall take an awl and thrust it through his ear to the door
Deuteronomy15:18 It shall not seem hard to you when you send him away free from you; for he has been worth a double hired servant in serving you six years. Then the Lord your God will bless you in all that you do.
Deuteronomy15:19 “All the firstborn males that come from your herd and your flock you shall sanctify to the Lord your God; you shall do no work with the firstborn of your herd
Deuteronomy15:20 You and your household shall eat it before the Lord your God year by year in the place which the Lord chooses.
Deuteronomy15:21 But if there is a defect in it
Deuteronomy15:22 You may eat it within your gates; the unclean and the clean person alike may eat it
Deuteronomy15:23 Only you shall not eat its blood; you shall pour it on the ground like water.

Deuteronomy21:1 “If anyone is found slain
Deuteronomy21:2 then your elders and your judges shall go out and measure the distance from the slain man to the surrounding cities.
Deuteronomy21:3 And it shall be that the elders of the city nearest to the slain man will take a heifer which has not been worked and which has not pulled with a yoke.
Deuteronomy21:4 The elders of that city shall bring the heifer down to a valley with flowing water
Deuteronomy21:5 Then the priests

Deuteronomy34:5 So Moses the servant of the Lord died there in the land of Moab
Deuteronomy34:6 And He buried him in a valley in the land of Moab
Deuteronomy34:7 Moses was one hundred and twenty years old when he died. His eyes were not dim nor his natural vigor diminished.
Deuteronomy34:8 And the children of Israel wept for Moses in the plains of Moab thirty days. So the days of weeping and mourning for Moses ended.
Deuteronomy34:9 Now Joshua the son of Nun was full of the spirit of wisdom
Deuteronomy34:10 But since then there has not arisen in Israel a prophet like Moses
Deuteronomy34:11 in all the signs and wonders which the Lord sent him to do in the land of Egypt
Deuteronomy34:12 and by all that mighty power and all the great terror which Moses performed in the sight of all Israel.
Joshua1:1 After the death of Moses the servant of the Lord
Joshua1:2 “Moses My servant is dead. Now therefore
Joshua1:3 Every place that the sole of your foot will tread upon I have given you
Joshua1:4 From the wilderness and this Lebanon as far as the great river
Joshua1:5 No man shall be able to stand before you all the days of your life; as I was with Moses
Joshua1:6 Be strong and of good courage
Joshua1:7 Only be strong and very courageous
Joshua1:8 This Book of the Law shall not depart from your mouth
Joshua1:9 Have I not commanded you? Be strong and of good courage; do not be afraid
Joshua1:10 Then Joshua commanded the officers of the people
Joshua1:11 “Pass through the camp and command the people
Joshua1:12 And to the Reubenites
Joshua1:13 “Remember the word which Moses the servant of the Lord commanded you
Joshua1:14 Your wives
Joshua1:15 until the Lord has given your brethren rest
Joshua1:16 So they answered Joshua
Joshua1:17 Just as we heeded Moses in all things
Joshua1:18 Whoever rebels against your command and does not heed your words
Joshua2:1 Now Joshua the son of Nun sent out two men from Acacia Grove to spy secretly
Joshua2:2 And it was told the king of Jericho
Joshua2:3 So the king of Jericho sent to Rahab
Joshua2:4 Then the woman took the two men and hid them. So she said
Joshua2:5 And it happened as the gate was being shut
Joshua2:6 (But she had brought them up to the roof and hidden them with the stalks of flax
Joshua2:7 Then the men pursued them by the road to the Jordan
Joshua2:8 Now before they lay down
Joshua2:9 and said to the men: “I know that the Lord has given you the land
Joshua2:10 For we have heard how the Lord dried up the water of the Red Sea for you when you came out of Egypt
Joshua2:11 And as soon as we heard these things
Joshua2:12 Now therefore
Joshua2:13 and spare my father
Joshua2:14 So the men answered her
Joshua2:15 Then she let them down by a rope through the window
Joshua2:16 And she said to them
Joshua2:17 So the men said to her: “We will be blameless of this oath of yours which you have made us swear
Joshua2:18 unless
Joshua2:19 So it shall be that whoever goes outside the doors of your house into the street
Joshua2:20 And if you tell this business of ours
Joshua2:21 Then she said
Joshua2:22 They departed and went to the mountain
Joshua2:23 So the two men returned
Joshua2:24 And they said to Joshua

Joshua3:2 So it was
Joshua3:3 and they commanded the people

Joshua3:7 And the Lord said to Joshua

Joshua3:9 So Joshua said to the children of Israel
Joshua3:10 And Joshua said
Joshua3:11 Behold
Joshua3:12 Now therefore
Joshua3:13 And it shall come to pass

Joshua3:16 that the waters which came down from upstream stood still
Joshua3:17 Then the priests who bore the ark of the covenant of the Lord stood firm on dry ground in the midst of the Jordan; and all Israel crossed over on dry ground
Joshua4:1 And it came to pass
Joshua4:2 “Take for yourselves twelve men from the people
Joshua4:3 and command them
Joshua4:4 Then Joshua called the twelve men whom he had appointed from the children of Israel

Joshua4:6 that this may be a sign among you when your children ask in time to come
Joshua4:7 Then you shall answer them that the waters of the Jordan were cut off before the ark of the covenant of the Lord; when it crossed over the Jordan
Joshua4:8 And the children of Israel did so
Joshua4:9 Then Joshua set up twelve stones in the midst of the Jordan
Joshua4:10 So the priests who bore the ark stood in the midst of the Jordan until everything was finished that the Lord had commanded Joshua to speak to the people
Joshua4:11 Then it came to pass
Joshua4:12 And the men of Reuben
Joshua4:13 About forty thousand prepared for war crossed over before the Lord for battle
Joshua4:14 On that day the Lord exalted Joshua in the sight of all Israel; and they feared him
Joshua4:15 Then the Lord spoke to Joshua

Joshua4:18 And it came to pass
Joshua4:19 Now the people came up from the Jordan on the tenth day of the first month
Joshua4:20 And those twelve stones which they took out of the Jordan
Joshua4:21 Then he spoke to the children of Israel
Joshua4:22 then you shall let your children know
Joshua4:23 for the Lord your God dried up the waters of the Jordan before you until you had crossed over
Joshua4:24 that all the peoples of the earth may know the hand of the Lord

Joshua5:6 For the children of Israel walked forty years in the wilderness

Joshua6:1 Now Jericho was securely shut up because of the children of Israel; none went out
Joshua6:2 And the Lord said to Joshua: “See! I have given Jericho into your hand
Joshua6:3 You shall march around the city
Joshua6:4 And seven priests shall bear seven trumpets of rams’ horns before the ark. But the seventh day you shall march around the city seven times
Joshua6:5 It shall come to pass
Joshua6:6 Then Joshua the son of Nun called the priests and said to them
Joshua6:7 And he said to the people
Joshua6:8 So it was
Joshua6:9 The armed men went before the priests who blew the trumpets
Joshua6:10 Now Joshua had commanded the people
Joshua6:11 So he had the ark of the Lord circle the city
Joshua6:12 And Joshua rose early in the morning
Joshua6:13 Then seven priests bearing seven trumpets of rams’ horns before the ark of the Lord went on continually and blew with the trumpets. And the armed men went before them. But the rear guard came after the ark of the Lord
Joshua6:14 And the second day they marched around the city once and returned to the camp. So they did six days.
Joshua6:15 But it came to pass on the seventh day that they rose early
Joshua6:16 And the seventh time it happened
Joshua6:17 Now the city shall be doomed by the Lord to destruction
Joshua6:18 And you
Joshua6:19 But all the silver and gold
Joshua6:20 So the people shouted when the priests blew the trumpets. And it happened when the people heard the sound of the trumpet
Joshua6:21 And they utterly destroyed all that was in the city
Joshua6:22 But Joshua had said to the two men who had spied out the country
Joshua6:23 And the young men who had been spies went in and brought out Rahab
Joshua6:24 But they burned the city and all that was in it with fire. Only the silver and gold
Joshua6:25 And Joshua spared Rahab the harlot
Joshua6:26 Then Joshua charged them at that time
Joshua6:27 So the Lord was with Joshua
Joshua7:1 But the children of Israel committed a trespass regarding the accursed things
Joshua7:2 Now Joshua sent men from Jericho to Ai
Joshua7:3 And they returned to Joshua and said to him
Joshua7:4 So about three thousand men went up there from the people
Joshua7:5 And the men of Ai struck down about thirty-six men
Joshua7:6 Then Joshua tore his clothes
Joshua7:7 And Joshua said
Joshua7:8 O Lord
Joshua7:9 For the Canaanites and all the inhabitants of the land will hear it
Joshua7:10 So the Lord said to Joshua: “Get up! Why do you lie thus on your face?
Joshua7:11 Israel has sinned
Joshua7:12 Therefore the children of Israel could not stand before their enemies
Joshua7:13 Get up
Joshua7:14 In the morning therefore you shall be brought according to your tribes. And it shall be that the tribe which the Lord takes shall come according to families; and the family which the Lord takes shall come by households; and the household which the Lord takes shall come man by man.
Joshua7:15 Then it shall be that he who is taken with the accursed thing shall be burned with fire
Joshua7:16 So Joshua rose early in the morning and brought Israel by their tribes
Joshua7:17 He brought the clan of Judah
Joshua7:18 Then he brought his household man by man

Joshua7:23 And they took them from the midst of the tent
Joshua7:24 Then Joshua

Joshua8:1 Now the Lord said to Joshua: “Do not be afraid
Joshua8:2 And you shall do to Ai and its king as you did to Jericho and its king. Only its spoil and its cattle you shall take as booty for yourselves. Lay an ambush for the city behind it.”
Joshua8:3 So Joshua arose
Joshua8:4 And he commanded them
Joshua8:5 Then I and all the people who are with me will approach the city; and it will come about
Joshua8:6 For they will come out after us till we have drawn them from the city
Joshua8:7 Then you shall rise from the ambush and seize the city
Joshua8:8 And it will be
Joshua8:9 Joshua therefore sent them out; and they went to lie in ambush
Joshua8:10 Then Joshua rose up early in the morning and mustered the people
Joshua8:11 And all the people of war who were with him went up and drew near; and they came before the city and camped on the north side of Ai. Now a valley lay between them and Ai.
Joshua8:12 So he took about five thousand men and set them in ambush between Bethel and Ai
Joshua8:13 And when they had set the people
Joshua8:14 Now it happened
Joshua8:15 And Joshua and all Israel made as if they were beaten before them
Joshua8:16 So all the people who were in Ai were called together to pursue them. And they pursued Joshua and were drawn away from the city.
Joshua8:17 There was not a man left in Ai or Bethel who did not go out after Israel. So they left the city open and pursued Israel.
Joshua8:18 Then the Lord said to Joshua
Joshua8:19 So those in ambush arose quickly out of their place; they ran as soon as he had stretched out his hand
Joshua8:20 And when the men of Ai looked behind them
Joshua8:21 Now when Joshua and all Israel saw that the ambush had taken the city and that the smoke of the city ascended
Joshua8:22 Then the others came out of the city against them; so they were caught in the midst of Israel
Joshua8:23 But the king of Ai they took alive
Joshua8:24 And it came to pass when Israel had made an end of slaying all the inhabitants of Ai in the field
Joshua8:25 So it was that all who fell that day
Joshua8:26 For Joshua did not draw back his hand
Joshua8:27 Only the livestock and the spoil of that city Israel took as booty for themselves
Joshua8:28 So Joshua burned Ai and made it a heap forever
Joshua8:29 And the king of Ai he hanged on a tree until evening. And as soon as the sun was down
Joshua8:30 Now Joshua built an altar to the Lord God of Israel in Mount Ebal
Joshua8:31 as Moses the servant of the Lord had commanded the children of Israel
Joshua8:32 And there
Joshua8:33 Then all Israel
Joshua8:34 And afterward he read all the words of the law
Joshua8:35 There was not a word of all that Moses had commanded which Joshua did not read before all the assembly of Israel

Joshua9:2 that they gathered together to fight with Joshua and Israel with one accord.
Joshua9:3 But when the inhabitants of Gibeon heard what Joshua had done to Jericho and Ai
Joshua9:4 they worked craftily
Joshua9:5 old and patched sandals on their feet
Joshua9:6 And they went to Joshua

Joshua9:8 But they said to Joshua

Joshua9:11 Therefore our elders and all the inhabitants of our country spoke to us
Joshua9:12 This bread of ours we took hot for our provision from our houses on the day we departed to come to you. But now look
Joshua9:13 And these wineskins which we filled were new
Joshua9:14 Then the men of Israel took some of their provisions; but they did not ask counsel of the Lord.
Joshua9:15 So Joshua made peace with them
Joshua9:16 And it happened at the end of three days
Joshua9:17 Then the children of Israel journeyed and came to their cities on the third day. Now their cities were Gibeon
Joshua9:18 But the children of Israel did not attack them

Joshua9:24 So they answered Joshua and said
Joshua9:25 And now
Joshua9:26 So he did to them
Joshua9:27 And that day Joshua made them woodcutters and water carriers for the congregation and for the altar of the Lord
Joshua10:1 Now it came to pass when Adoni-Zedek king of Jerusalem heard how Joshua had taken Ai and had utterly destroyed it— as he had done to Jericho and its king
Joshua10:2 that they feared greatly
Joshua10:3 Therefore Adoni-Zedek king of Jerusalem sent to Hoham king of Hebron
Joshua10:4 “Come up to me and help me

Joshua10:9 Joshua therefore came upon them suddenly
Joshua10:10 So the Lord routed them before Israel
Joshua10:11 And it happened
Joshua10:12 Then Joshua spoke to the Lord in the day when the Lord delivered up the Amorites before the children of Israel
Joshua10:13 So the sun stood still
Joshua10:14 And there has been no day like that
Joshua10:15 Then Joshua returned

Joshua10:24 So it was

Joshua10:26 And afterward Joshua struck them and killed them

Joshua10:29 Then Joshua passed from Makkedah
Joshua10:30 And the Lord also delivered it and its king into the hand of Israel; he struck it and all the people who were in it with the edge of the sword. He let none remain in it
Joshua10:31 Then Joshua passed from Libnah
Joshua10:32 And the Lord delivered Lachish into the hand of Israel
Joshua10:33 Then Horam king of Gezer came up to help Lachish; and Joshua struck him and his people
Joshua10:34 From Lachish Joshua passed to Eglon
Joshua10:35 They took it on that day and struck it with the edge of the sword; all the people who were in it he utterly destroyed that day
Joshua10:36 So Joshua went up from Eglon
Joshua10:37 And they took it and struck it with the edge of the sword—its king
Joshua10:38 Then Joshua returned
Joshua10:39 And he took it and its king and all its cities; they struck them with the edge of the sword and utterly destroyed all the people who were in it. He left none remaining; as he had done to Hebron
Joshua10:40 So Joshua conquered all the land: the mountain country and the South and the lowland and the wilderness slopes
Joshua10:41 And Joshua conquered them from Kadesh Barnea as far as Gaza
Joshua10:42 All these kings and their land Joshua took at one time
Joshua10:43 Then Joshua returned

Joshua11:22 None of the Anakim were left in the land of the children of Israel; they remained only in Gaza

Joshua13:27 and in the valley Beth Haram
Joshua13:28 This is the inheritance of the children of Gad according to their families

Joshua13:30 Their territory was from Mahanaim
Joshua13:31 half of Gilead
Joshua13:32 These are the areas which Moses had distributed as an inheritance in the plains of Moab on the other side of the Jordan
Joshua13:33 But to the tribe of Levi Moses had given no inheritance; the Lord God of Israel was their inheritance
Joshua14:1 These are the areas which the children of Israel inherited in the land of Canaan
Joshua14:2 Their inheritance was by lot
Joshua14:3 For Moses had given the inheritance of the two tribes and the half-tribe on the other side of the Jordan; but to the Levites he had given no inheritance among them.
Joshua14:4 For the children of Joseph were two tribes: Manasseh and Ephraim. And they gave no part to the Levites in the land
Joshua14:5 As the Lord had commanded Moses
Joshua14:6 Then the children of Judah came to Joshua in Gilgal. And Caleb the son of Jephunneh the Kenizzite said to him: “You know the word which the Lord said to Moses the man of God concerning you and me in Kadesh Barnea.
Joshua14:7 I was forty years old when Moses the servant of the Lord sent me from Kadesh Barnea to spy out the land
Joshua14:8 Nevertheless my brethren who went up with me made the heart of the people melt
Joshua14:9 So Moses swore on that day
Joshua14:10 And now
Joshua14:11 As yet I am as strong this day as on the day that Moses sent me; just as my strength was then
Joshua14:12 Now therefore
Joshua14:13 And Joshua blessed him
Joshua14:14 Hebron therefore became the inheritance of Caleb the son of Jephunneh the Kenizzite to this day
Joshua14:15 And the name of Hebron formerly was Kirjath Arba (Arba was the greatest man among the Anakim). Then the land had rest from war.
Joshua15:1 So this was the lot of the tribe of the children of Judah according to their families: The border of Edom at the Wilderness of Zin southward was the extreme southern boundary.
Joshua15:2 And their southern border began at the shore of the Salt Sea
Joshua15:3 Then it went out to the southern side of the Ascent of Akrabbim
Joshua15:4 From there it passed toward Azmon and went out to the Brook of Egypt; and the border ended at the sea. This shall be your southern border.
Joshua15:5 The east border was the Salt Sea as far as the mouth of the Jordan. And the border on the northern quarter began at the bay of the sea at the mouth of the Jordan.
Joshua15:6 The border went up to Beth Hoglah and passed north of Beth Arabah; and the border went up to the stone of Bohan the son of Reuben.
Joshua15:7 Then the border went up toward Debir from the Valley of Achor
Joshua15:8 And the border went up by the Valley of the Son of Hinnom to the southern slope of the Jebusite city (which is Jerusalem). The border went up to the top of the mountain that lies before the Valley of Hinnom westward
Joshua15:9 Then the border went around from the top of the hill to the fountain of the water of Nephtoah
Joshua15:10 Then the border turned westward from Baalah to Mount Seir
Joshua15:11 And the border went out to the side of Ekron northward. Then the border went around to Shicron
Joshua15:12 The west border was the coastline of the Great Sea. This is the boundary of the children of Judah all around according to their families.
Joshua15:13 Now to Caleb the son of Jephunneh he gave a share among the children of Judah
Joshua15:14 Caleb drove out the three sons of Anak from there: Sheshai
Joshua15:15 Then he went up from there to the inhabitants of Debir (formerly the name of Debir was Kirjath Sepher).

Joshua18:2 But there remained among the children of Israel seven tribes which had not yet received their inheritance.

1Samuel1:2 And he had two wives: the name of one was Hannah

1Samuel1:28 Therefore I also have lent him to the Lord; as long as he lives he shall be lent to the Lord.” So they worshiped the Lord there.
1Samuel2:1 And Hannah prayed and said: “My heart rejoices in the Lord; My horn is exalted in the Lord. I smile at my enemies
1Samuel2:2 “No one is holy like the Lord
1Samuel2:3 “Talk no more so very proudly; Let no arrogance come from your mouth
1Samuel2:4 “The bows of the mighty men are broken
1Samuel2:5 Those who were full have hired themselves out for bread
1Samuel2:6 “The Lord kills and makes alive; He brings down to the grave and brings up.
1Samuel2:7 The Lord makes poor and makes rich; He brings low and lifts up.
1Samuel2:8 He raises the poor from the dust And lifts the beggar from the ash heap
1Samuel2:9 He will guard the feet of His saints
1Samuel2:10 The adversaries of the Lord shall be broken in pieces; From heaven He will thunder against them. The Lord will judge the ends of the earth. “He will give strength to His king

1Samuel2:14 Then he would thrust it into the pan

1Samuel2:20 And Eli would bless Elkanah and his wife

1Samuel2:25 If one man sins against another

1Samuel2:28 Did I not choose him out of all the tribes of Israel to be My priest
1Samuel2:29 Why do you kick at My sacrifice and My offering which I have commanded in My dwelling place
1Samuel2:30 Therefore the Lord God of Israel says: ‘I said indeed that your house and the house of your father would walk before Me forever.’ But now the Lord says: ‘Far be it from Me; for those who honor Me I will honor
1Samuel2:31 Behold
1Samuel2:32 And you will see an enemy in My dwelling place
1Samuel2:33 But any of your men whom I do not cut off from My altar shall consume your eyes and grieve your heart. And all the descendants of your house shall die in the flower of their age.
1Samuel2:34 Now this shall be a sign to you that will come upon your two sons
1Samuel2:35 Then I will raise up for Myself a faithful priest who shall do according to what is in My heart and in My mind. I will build him a sure house
1Samuel2:36 And it shall come to pass that everyone who is left in your house will come and bow down to him for a piece of silver and a morsel of bread

2Samuel22:2 And he said: “The Lord is my rock and my fortress and my deliverer;

2Samuel22:7 In my distress I called upon the Lord

2Samuel22:9 Smoke went up from His nostrils
2Samuel22:10 He bowed the heavens also
2Samuel22:11 He rode upon a cherub
2Samuel22:12 He made darkness canopies around Him

2Samuel22:15 He sent out arrows and scattered them; Lightning bolts
2Samuel22:16 Then the channels of the sea were seen
2Samuel22:17 “He sent from above
2Samuel22:18 He delivered me from my strong enemy
2Samuel22:19 They confronted me in the day of my calamity
2Samuel22:20 He also brought me out into a broad place; He delivered me because He delighted in me.
2Samuel22:21 “The Lord rewarded me according to my righteousness; According to the cleanness of my hands He has recompensed me.
2Samuel22:22 For I have kept the ways of the Lord
2Samuel22:23 For all His judgments were before me; And as for His statutes
2Samuel22:24 I was also blameless before Him
2Samuel22:25 Therefore the Lord has recompensed me according to my righteousness
2Samuel22:26 “With the merciful You will show Yourself merciful; With a blameless man You will show Yourself blameless;
2Samuel22:27 With the pure You will show Yourself pure; And with the devious You will show Yourself shrewd.
2Samuel22:28 You will save the humble people; But Your eyes are on the haughty
2Samuel22:29 “For You are my lamp
2Samuel22:30 For by You I can run against a troop; By my God I can leap over a wall.
2Samuel22:31 As for God
2Samuel22:32 “For who is God
2Samuel22:33 God is my strength and power
2Samuel22:34 He makes my feet like the feet of deer
2Samuel22:35 He teaches my hands to make war
2Samuel22:36 “You have also given me the shield of Your salvation; Your gentleness has made me great.
2Samuel22:37 You enlarged my path under me; So my feet did not slip.
2Samuel22:38 “I have pursued my enemies and destroyed them; Neither did I turn back again till they were destroyed.
2Samuel22:39 And I have destroyed them and wounded them
2Samuel22:40 For You have armed me with strength for the battle; You have subdued under me those who rose against me.
2Samuel22:41 You have also given me the necks of my enemies
2Samuel22:42 They looked
2Samuel22:43 Then I beat them as fine as the dust of the earth; I trod them like dirt in the streets
2Samuel22:44 “You have also delivered me from the strivings of my people; You have kept me as the head of the nations. A people I have not known shall serve me.
2Samuel22:45 The foreigners submit to me; As soon as they hear
2Samuel22:46 The foreigners fade away

2Samuel22:48 It is God who avenges me
2Samuel22:49 He delivers me from my enemies. You also lift me up above those who rise against me; You have delivered me from the violent man.
2Samuel22:50 Therefore I will give thanks to You

1Kings2:16 Now I ask one petition of you; do not deny me.” And she said to him
1Kings2:17 Then he said
1Kings2:18 So Bathsheba said
1Kings2:19 Bathsheba therefore went to King Solomon
1Kings2:20 Then she said
1Kings2:21 So she said
1Kings2:22 And King Solomon answered and said to his mother
1Kings2:23 Then King Solomon swore by the Lord
1Kings2:24 Now therefore
1Kings2:25 So King Solomon sent by the hand of Benaiah the son of Jehoiada; and he struck him down
1Kings2:26 And to Abiathar the priest the king said
1Kings2:27 So Solomon removed Abiathar from being priest to the Lord
1Kings2:28 Then news came to Joab
1Kings2:29 And King Solomon was told
1Kings2:30 So Benaiah went to the tabernacle of the Lord
1Kings2:31 Then the king said to him
1Kings2:32 So the Lord will return his blood on his head
1Kings2:33 Their blood shall therefore return upon the head of Joab and upon the head of his descendants forever. But upon David and his descendants
1Kings2:34 So Benaiah the son of Jehoiada went up and struck and killed him; and he was buried in his own house in the wilderness.
1Kings2:35 The king put Benaiah the son of Jehoiada in his place over the army
1Kings2:36 Then the king sent and called for Shimei
1Kings2:37 For it shall be
1Kings2:38 And Shimei said to the king
1Kings2:39 Now it happened at the end of three years
1Kings2:40 So Shimei arose
1Kings2:41 And Solomon was told that Shimei had gone from Jerusalem to Gath and had come back.
1Kings2:42 Then the king sent and called for Shimei
1Kings2:43 Why then have you not kept the oath of the Lord and the commandment that I gave you?”
1Kings2:44 The king said moreover to Shimei
1Kings2:45 But King Solomon shall be blessed
1Kings2:46 So the king commanded Benaiah the son of Jehoiada; and he went out and struck him down
1Kings3:1 Now Solomon made a treaty with Pharaoh king of Egypt
1Kings3:2 Meanwhile the people sacrificed at the high places
1Kings3:3 And Solomon loved the Lord
1Kings3:4 Now the king went to Gibeon to sacrifice there
1Kings3:5 At Gibeon the Lord appeared to Solomon in a dream by night; and God said
1Kings3:6 And Solomon said: “You have shown great mercy to Your servant David my father
1Kings3:7 Now
1Kings3:8 And Your servant is in the midst of Your people whom You have chosen
1Kings3:9 Therefore give to Your servant an understanding heart to judge Your people
1Kings3:10 The speech pleased the Lord
1Kings3:11 Then God said to him: “Because you have asked this thing
1Kings3:12 behold
1Kings3:13 And I have also given you what you have not asked: both riches and honor
1Kings3:14 So if you walk in My ways
1Kings3:15 Then Solomon awoke; and indeed it had been a dream. And he came to Jerusalem and stood before the ark of the covenant of the Lord
1Kings3:16 Now two women who were harlots came to the king
1Kings3:17 And one woman said
1Kings3:18 Then it happened
1Kings3:19 And this woman’s son died in the night
1Kings3:20 So she arose in the middle of the night and took my son from my side
1Kings3:21 And when I rose in the morning to nurse my son
1Kings3:22 Then the other woman said
1Kings3:23 And the king said
1Kings3:24 Then the king said
1Kings3:25 And the king said
1Kings3:26 Then the woman whose son was living spoke to the king
1Kings3:27 So the king answered and said
1Kings3:28 And all Israel heard of the judgment which the king had rendered; and they feared the king
1Kings4:1 So King Solomon was king over all Israel.
1Kings4:2 And these were his officials: Azariah the son of Zadok
1Kings4:3 Elihoreph and Ahijah
1Kings4:4 Benaiah the son of Jehoiada
1Kings4:5 Azariah the son of Nathan
1Kings4:6 Ahishar
1Kings4:7 And Solomon had twelve governors over all Israel
1Kings4:8 These are their names: Ben-Hur
1Kings4:9 Ben-Deker
1Kings4:10 Ben-Hesed
1Kings4:11 Ben-Abinadab
1Kings4:12 Baana the son of Ahilud
1Kings4:13 Ben-Geber
1Kings4:14 Ahinadab the son of Iddo
1Kings4:15 Ahimaaz
1Kings4:16 Baanah the son of Hushai
1Kings4:17 Jehoshaphat the son of Paruah
1Kings4:18 Shimei the son of Elah
1Kings4:19 Geber the son of Uri
1Kings4:20 Judah and Israel were as numerous as the sand by the sea in multitude
1Kings4:21 So Solomon reigned over all kingdoms from the River to the land of the Philistines
1Kings4:22 Now Solomon’s provision for one day was thirty kors of fine flour
1Kings4:23 ten fatted oxen
1Kings4:24 For he had dominion over all the region on this side of the River from Tiphsah even to Gaza
1Kings4:25 And Judah and Israel dwelt safely
1Kings4:26 Solomon had forty thousand stalls of horses for his chariots
1Kings4:27 And these governors
1Kings4:28 They also brought barley and straw to the proper place
1Kings4:29 And God gave Solomon wisdom and exceedingly great understanding
1Kings4:30 Thus Solomon’s wisdom excelled the wisdom of all the men of the East and all the wisdom of Egypt.
1Kings4:31 For he was wiser than all men— than Ethan the Ezrahite
1Kings4:32 He spoke three thousand proverbs
1Kings4:33 Also he spoke of trees
1Kings4:34 And men of all nations
1Kings5:1 Now Hiram king of Tyre sent his servants to Solomon
1Kings5:2 Then Solomon sent to Hiram
1Kings5:3 You know how my father David could not build a house for the name of the Lord his God because of the wars which were fought against him on every side
1Kings5:4 But now the Lord my God has given me rest on every side; there is neither adversary nor evil occurrence.
1Kings5:5 And behold
1Kings5:6 Now therefore
1Kings5:7 So it was
1Kings5:8 Then Hiram sent to Solomon
1Kings5:9 My servants shall bring them down from Lebanon to the sea; I will float them in rafts by sea to the place you indicate to me
1Kings5:10 Then Hiram gave Solomon cedar and cypress logs according to all his desire.
1Kings5:11 And Solomon gave Hiram twenty thousand kors of wheat as food for his household
1Kings5:12 So the Lord gave Solomon wisdom
1Kings5:13 Then King Solomon raised up a labor force out of all Israel; and the labor force was thirty thousand men.

1Kings6:1 And it came to pass in the four hundred and eightieth year after the children of Israel had come out of the land of Egypt
1Kings6:2 Now the house which King Solomon built for the Lord
1Kings6:3 The vestibule in front of the sanctuary of the house was twenty cubits long across the width of the house
1Kings6:4 And he made for the house windows with beveled frames.
1Kings6:5 Against the wall of the temple he built chambers all around
1Kings6:6 The lowest chamber was five cubits wide
1Kings6:7 And the temple
1Kings6:8 The doorway for the middle story was on the right side of the temple. They went up by stairs to the middle story
1Kings6:9 So he built the temple and finished it
1Kings6:10 And he built side chambers against the entire temple
1Kings6:11 Then the word of the Lord came to Solomon
1Kings6:12 “ Concerning this temple which you are building
1Kings6:13 And I will dwell among the children of Israel
1Kings6:14 So Solomon built the temple and finished it.
1Kings6:15 And he built the inside walls of the temple with cedar boards; from the floor of the temple to the ceiling he paneled the inside with wood; and he covered the floor of the temple with planks of cypress.
1Kings6:16 Then he built the twenty-cubit room at the rear of the temple
1Kings6:17 And in front of it the temple sanctuary was forty cubits long.
1Kings6:18 The inside of the temple was cedar
1Kings6:19 And he prepared the inner sanctuary inside the temple
1Kings6:20 The inner sanctuary was twenty cubits long
1Kings6:21 So Solomon overlaid the inside of the temple with pure gold. He stretched gold chains across the front of the inner sanctuary
1Kings6:22 The whole temple he overlaid with gold
1Kings6:23 Inside the inner sanctuary he made two cherubim of olive wood
1Kings6:24 One wing of the cherub was five cubits
1Kings6:25 And the other cherub was ten cubits; both cherubim were of the same size and shape.
1Kings6:26 The height of one cherub was ten cubits
1Kings6:27 Then he set the cherubim inside the inner room; and they stretched out the wings of the cherubim so that the wing of the one touched one wall
1Kings6:28 Also he overlaid the cherubim with gold.
1Kings6:29 Then he carved all the walls of the temple all around
1Kings6:30 And the floor of the temple he overlaid with gold
1Kings6:31 For the entrance of the inner sanctuary he made doors of olive wood; the lintel and doorposts were one-fifth of the wall.
1Kings6:32 The two doors were of olive wood; and he carved on them figures of cherubim
1Kings6:33 So for the door of the sanctuary he also made doorposts of olive wood
1Kings6:34 And the two doors were of cypress wood; two panels comprised one folding door
1Kings6:35 Then he carved cherubim
1Kings6:36 And he built the inner court with three rows of hewn stone and a row of cedar beams.
1Kings6:37 In the fourth year the foundation of the house of the Lord was laid
1Kings6:38 And in the eleventh year
1Kings7:1 But Solomon took thirteen years to build his own house; so he finished all his house.
1Kings7:2 He also built the House of the Forest of Lebanon; its length was one hundred cubits
1Kings7:3 And it was paneled with cedar above the beams that were on forty-five pillars
1Kings7:4 There were windows with beveled frames in three rows
1Kings7:5 And all the doorways and doorposts had rectangular frames; and window was opposite window in three tiers.
1Kings7:6 He also made the Hall of Pillars: its length was fifty cubits
1Kings7:7 Then he made a hall for the throne
1Kings7:8 And the house where he dwelt had another court inside the hall
1Kings7:9 All these were of costly stones cut to size
1Kings7:10 The foundation was of costly stones
1Kings7:11 And above were costly stones
1Kings7:12 The great court was enclosed with three rows of hewn stones and a row of cedar beams. So were the inner court of the house of the Lord and the vestibule of the temple.
1Kings7:13 Now King Solomon sent and brought Huram from Tyre.
1Kings7:14 He was the son of a widow from the tribe of Naphtali
1Kings7:15 And he cast two pillars of bronze
1Kings7:16 Then he made two capitals of cast bronze
1Kings7:17 He made a lattice network
1Kings7:18 So he made the pillars
1Kings7:19 The capitals which were on top of the pillars in the hall were in the shape of lilies
1Kings7:20 The capitals on the two pillars also had pomegranates above
1Kings7:21 Then he set up the pillars by the vestibule of the temple; he set up the pillar on the right and called its name Jachin
1Kings7:22 The tops of the pillars were in the shape of lilies. So the work of the pillars was finished.
1Kings7:23 And he made the Sea of cast bronze
1Kings7:24 Below its brim were ornamental buds encircling it all around
1Kings7:25 It stood on twelve oxen: three looking toward the north
1Kings7:26 It was a handbreadth thick; and its brim was shaped like the brim of a cup
1Kings7:27 He also made ten carts of bronze; four cubits was the length of each cart
1Kings7:28 And this was the design of the carts: They had panels
1Kings7:29 on the panels that were between the frames were lions
1Kings7:30 Every cart had four bronze wheels and axles of bronze
1Kings7:31 Its opening inside the crown at the top was one cubit in diameter; and the opening was round
1Kings7:32 Under the panels were the four wheels
1Kings7:33 The workmanship of the wheels was like the workmanship of a chariot wheel; their axle pins
1Kings7:34 And there were four supports at the four corners of each cart; its supports were part of the cart itself.
1Kings7:35 On the top of the cart
1Kings7:36 On the plates of its flanges and on its panels he engraved cherubim
1Kings7:37 Thus he made the ten carts. All of them were of the same mold
1Kings7:38 Then he made ten lavers of bronze; each laver contained forty baths
1Kings7:39 And he put five carts on the right side of the house
1Kings7:40 Huram made the lavers and the shovels and the bowls. So Huram finished doing all the work that he was to do for King Solomon for the house of the Lord:
1Kings7:41 the two pillars
1Kings7:42 four hundred pomegranates for the two networks (two rows of pomegranates for each network
1Kings7:43 the ten carts
1Kings7:44 one Sea
1Kings7:45 the pots
1Kings7:46 In the plain of Jordan the king had them cast in clay molds
1Kings7:47 And Solomon did not weigh all the articles
1Kings7:48 Thus Solomon had all the furnishings made for the house of the Lord: the altar of gold
1Kings7:49 the lampstands of pure gold
1Kings7:50 the basins
1Kings7:51 So all the work that King Solomon had done for the house of the Lord was finished; and Solomon brought in the things which his father David had dedicated: the silver and the gold and the furnishings. He put them in the treasuries of the house of the Lord.
1Kings8:1 Now Solomon assembled the elders of Israel and all the heads of the tribes
1Kings8:2 Therefore all the men of Israel assembled with King Solomon at the feast in the month of Ethanim
1Kings8:3 So all the elders of Israel came
1Kings8:4 Then they brought up the ark of the Lord
1Kings8:5 Also King Solomon
1Kings8:6 Then the priests brought in the ark of the covenant of the Lord to its place
1Kings8:7 For the cherubim spread their two wings over the place of the ark
1Kings8:8 The poles extended so that the ends of the poles could be seen from the holy place
1Kings8:9 Nothing was in the ark except the two tablets of stone which Moses put there at Horeb
1Kings8:10 And it came to pass
1Kings8:11 so that the priests could not continue ministering because of the cloud; for the glory of the Lord filled the house of the Lord.
1Kings8:12 Then Solomon spoke: “The Lord said He would dwell in the dark cloud.
1Kings8:13 I have surely built You an exalted house
1Kings8:14 Then the king turned around and blessed the whole assembly of Israel
1Kings8:15 And he said: “Blessed be the Lord God of Israel
1Kings8:16 ‘Since the day that I brought My people Israel out of Egypt
1Kings8:17 Now it was in the heart of my father David to build a temple for the name of the Lord God of Israel.
1Kings8:18 But the Lord said to my father David
1Kings8:19 Nevertheless you shall not build the temple
1Kings8:20 So the Lord has fulfilled His word which He spoke; and I have filled the position of my father David
1Kings8:21 And there I have made a place for the ark
1Kings8:22 Then Solomon stood before the altar of the Lord in the presence of all the assembly of Israel
1Kings8:23 and he said: “ Lord God of Israel
1Kings8:24 You have kept what You promised Your servant David my father; You have both spoken with Your mouth and fulfilled it with Your hand
1Kings8:25 Therefore
1Kings8:26 And now I pray
1Kings8:27 “But will God indeed dwell on the earth? Behold
1Kings8:28 Yet regard the prayer of Your servant and his supplication
1Kings8:29 that Your eyes may be open toward this temple night and day
1Kings8:30 And may You hear the supplication of Your servant and of Your people Israel
1Kings8:31 “When anyone sins against his neighbor
1Kings8:32 then hear in heaven
1Kings8:33 “When Your people Israel are defeated before an enemy because they have sinned against You
1Kings8:34 then hear in heaven
1Kings8:35 “When the heavens are shut up and there is no rain because they have sinned against You
1Kings8:36 then hear in heaven
1Kings8:37 “When there is famine in the land
1Kings8:38 whatever prayer
1Kings8:39 then hear in heaven Your dwelling place
1Kings8:40 that they may fear You all the days that they live in the land which You gave to our fathers.
1Kings8:41 “Moreover
1Kings8:42 (for they will hear of Your great name and Your strong hand and Your outstretched arm)
1Kings8:43 hear in heaven Your dwelling place
1Kings8:44 “When Your people go out to battle against their enemy
1Kings8:45 then hear in heaven their prayer and their supplication
1Kings8:46 “When they sin against You (for there is no one who does not sin)
1Kings8:47 yet when they come to themselves in the land where they were carried captive
1Kings8:48 and when they return to You with all their heart and with all their soul in the land of their enemies who led them away captive
1Kings8:49 then hear in heaven Your dwelling place their prayer and their supplication
1Kings8:50 and forgive Your people who have sinned against You
1Kings8:51 (for they are Your people and Your inheritance
1Kings8:52 that Your eyes may be open to the supplication of Your servant and the supplication of Your people Israel
1Kings8:53 For You separated them from among all the peoples of the earth to be Your inheritance
1Kings8:54 And so it was
1Kings8:55 Then he stood and blessed all the assembly of Israel with a loud voice
1Kings8:56 “Blessed be the Lord
1Kings8:57 May the Lord our God be with us
1Kings8:58 that He may incline our hearts to Himself
1Kings8:59 And may these words of mine
1Kings8:60 that all the peoples of the earth may know that the Lord is God; there is no other.
1Kings8:61 Let your heart therefore be loyal to the Lord our God
1Kings8:62 Then the king and all Israel with him offered sacrifices before the Lord.
1Kings8:63 And Solomon offered a sacrifice of peace offerings
1Kings8:64 On the same day the king consecrated the middle of the court that was in front of the house of the Lord; for there he offered burnt offerings
1Kings8:65 At that time Solomon held a feast
1Kings8:66 On the eighth day he sent the people away; and they blessed the king
1Kings9:1 And it came to pass
1Kings9:2 that the Lord appeared to Solomon the second time
1Kings9:3 And the Lord said to him: “I have heard your prayer and your supplication that you have made before Me; I have consecrated this house which you have built to put My name there forever
1Kings9:4 Now if you walk before Me as your father David walked
1Kings9:5 then I will establish the throne of your kingdom over Israel forever
1Kings9:6 But if you or your sons at all turn from following Me
1Kings9:7 then I will cut off Israel from the land which I have given them; and this house which I have consecrated for My name I will cast out of My sight. Israel will be a proverb and a byword among all peoples.
1Kings9:8 And as for this house
1Kings9:9 Then they will answer
1Kings9:10 Now it happened at the end of twenty years

1Kings9:13 So he said

1Kings9:17 And Solomon built Gezer
1Kings9:18 Baalath
1Kings9:19 all the storage cities that Solomon had
1Kings9:20 All the people who were left of the Amorites
1Kings9:21 that is
1Kings9:22 But of the children of Israel Solomon made no forced laborers
1Kings9:23 Others were chiefs of the officials who were over Solomon’s work: five hundred and fifty
1Kings9:24 But Pharaoh’s daughter came up from the City of David to her house which Solomon had built for her. Then he built the Millo.

1Kings10:1 Now when the queen of Sheba heard of the fame of Solomon concerning the name of the Lord
1Kings10:2 She came to Jerusalem with a very great retinue
1Kings10:3 So Solomon answered all her questions; there was nothing so difficult for the king that he could not explain it to her.
1Kings10:4 And when the queen of Sheba had seen all the wisdom of Solomon
1Kings10:5 the food on his table
1Kings10:6 Then she said to the king: “It was a true report which I heard in my own land about your words and your wisdom.
1Kings10:7 However I did not believe the words until I came and saw with my own eyes; and indeed the half was not told me. Your wisdom and prosperity exceed the fame of which I heard.
1Kings10:8 Happy are your men and happy are these your servants
1Kings10:9 Blessed be the Lord your God
1Kings10:10 Then she gave the king one hundred and twenty talents of gold
1Kings10:11 Also
1Kings10:12 And the king made steps of the almug wood for the house of the Lord and for the king’s house
1Kings10:13 Now King Solomon gave the queen of Sheba all she desired
1Kings10:14 The weight of gold that came to Solomon yearly was six hundred and sixty-six talents of gold
1Kings10:15 besides that from the traveling merchants
1Kings10:16 And King Solomon made two hundred large shields of hammered gold; six hundred shekels of gold went into each shield.
1Kings10:17 He also made three hundred shields of hammered gold; three minas of gold went into each shield. The king put them in the House of the Forest of Lebanon.
1Kings10:18 Moreover the king made a great throne of ivory
1Kings10:19 The throne had six steps
1Kings10:20 Twelve lions stood there
1Kings10:21 All King Solomon’s drinking vessels were gold
1Kings10:22 For the king had merchant ships at sea with the fleet of Hiram. Once every three years the merchant ships came bringing gold
1Kings10:23 So King Solomon surpassed all the kings of the earth in riches and wisdom.
1Kings10:24 Now all the earth sought the presence of Solomon to hear his wisdom
1Kings10:25 Each man brought his present: articles of silver and gold
1Kings10:26 And Solomon gathered chariots and horsemen; he had one thousand four hundred chariots and twelve thousand horsemen
1Kings10:27 The king made silver as common in Jerusalem as stones
1Kings10:28 Also Solomon had horses imported from Egypt and Keveh; the king’s merchants bought them in Keveh at the current price.
1Kings10:29 Now a chariot that was imported from Egypt cost six hundred shekels of silver
1Kings11:1 But King Solomon loved many foreign women
1Kings11:2 from the nations of whom the Lord had said to the children of Israel
1Kings11:3 And he had seven hundred wives
1Kings11:4 For it was so
1Kings11:5 For Solomon went after Ashtoreth the goddess of the Sidonians
1Kings11:6 Solomon did evil in the sight of the Lord
1Kings11:7 Then Solomon built a high place for Chemosh the abomination of Moab
1Kings11:8 And he did likewise for all his foreign wives
1Kings11:9 So the Lord became angry with Solomon
1Kings11:10 and had commanded him concerning this thing
1Kings11:11 Therefore the Lord said to Solomon
1Kings11:12 Nevertheless I will not do it in your days
1Kings11:13 However I will not tear away the whole kingdom; I will give one tribe to your son for the sake of My servant David
1Kings11:14 Now the Lord raised up an adversary against Solomon
1Kings11:15 For it happened
1Kings11:16 (because for six months Joab remained there with all Israel
1Kings11:17 that Hadad fled to go to Egypt
1Kings11:18 Then they arose from Midian and came to Paran; and they took men with them from Paran and came to Egypt
1Kings11:19 And Hadad found great favor in the sight of Pharaoh
1Kings11:20 Then the sister of Tahpenes bore him Genubath his son
1Kings11:21 So when Hadad heard in Egypt that David rested with his fathers
1Kings11:22 Then Pharaoh said to him
1Kings11:23 And God raised up another adversary against him
1Kings11:24 So he gathered men to him and became captain over a band of raiders
1Kings11:25 He was an adversary of Israel all the days of Solomon (besides the trouble that Hadad caused); and he abhorred Israel
1Kings11:26 Then Solomon’s servant
1Kings11:27 And this is what caused him to rebel against the king: Solomon had built the Millo and repaired the damages to the City of David his father.
1Kings11:28 The man Jeroboam was a mighty man of valor; and Solomon
1Kings11:29 Now it happened at that time
1Kings11:30 Then Ahijah took hold of the new garment that was on him
1Kings11:31 And he said to Jeroboam
1Kings11:32 (but he shall have one tribe for the sake of My servant David
1Kings11:33 because they have forsaken Me
1Kings11:34 However I will not take the whole kingdom out of his hand
1Kings11:35 But I will take the kingdom out of his son’s hand and give it to you—ten tribes.
1Kings11:36 And to his son I will give one tribe
1Kings11:37 So I will take you
1Kings11:38 Then it shall be
1Kings11:39 And I will afflict the descendants of David because of this
1Kings11:40 Solomon therefore sought to kill Jeroboam. But Jeroboam arose and fled to Egypt
1Kings11:41 Now the rest of the acts of Solomon
1Kings11:42 And the period that Solomon reigned in Jerusalem over all Israel was forty years.
1Kings11:43 Then Solomon rested with his fathers
1Kings12:1 And Rehoboam went to Shechem
1Kings12:2 So it happened
1Kings12:3 that they sent and called him. Then Jeroboam and the whole assembly of Israel came and spoke to Rehoboam
1Kings12:4 “Your father made our yoke heavy; now therefore
1Kings12:5 So he said to them
1Kings12:6 Then King Rehoboam consulted the elders who stood before his father Solomon while he still lived
1Kings12:7 And they spoke to him
1Kings12:8 But he rejected the advice which the elders had given him
1Kings12:9 And he said to them
1Kings12:10 Then the young men who had grown up with him spoke to him
1Kings12:11 And now
1Kings12:12 So Jeroboam and all the people came to Rehoboam the third day
1Kings12:13 Then the king answered the people roughly
1Kings12:14 and he spoke to them according to the advice of the young men
1Kings12:15 So the king did not listen to the people; for the turn of events was from the Lord
1Kings12:16 Now when all Israel saw that the king did not listen to them
1Kings12:17 But Rehoboam reigned over the children of Israel who dwelt in the cities of Judah.
1Kings12:18 Then King Rehoboam sent Adoram
1Kings12:19 So Israel has been in rebellion against the house of David to this day.
1Kings12:20 Now it came to pass when all Israel heard that Jeroboam had come back
1Kings12:21 And when Rehoboam came to Jerusalem
1Kings12:22 But the word of God came to Shemaiah the man of God

1Kings12:24 ‘Thus says the Lord: “You shall not go up nor fight against your brethren the children of Israel. Let every man return to his house
1Kings12:25 Then Jeroboam built Shechem in the mountains of Ephraim
1Kings12:26 And Jeroboam said in his heart
1Kings12:27 If these people go up to offer sacrifices in the house of the Lord at Jerusalem
1Kings12:28 Therefore the king asked advice
1Kings12:29 And he set up one in Bethel
1Kings12:30 Now this thing became a sin

1Chronicles10:13 So Saul died for his unfaithfulness which he had committed against the Lord
1Chronicles10:14 But he did not inquire of the Lord; therefore He killed him

1Chronicles16:27 Honor and majesty are before Him; Strength and gladness are in His place.

1Chronicles18:17 Benaiah the son of Jehoiada was over the Cherethites and the Pelethites; and David’s sons were chief ministers at the king’s side.

2Chronicles3:1 Now Solomon began to build the house of the Lord at Jerusalem on Mount Moriah
2Chronicles3:2 And he began to build on the second day of the second month in the fourth year of his reign.
2Chronicles3:3 This is the foundation which Solomon laid for building the house of God: The length was sixty cubits (by cubits according to the former measure) and the width twenty cubits.
2Chronicles3:4 And the vestibule that was in front of the sanctuary was twenty cubits long across the width of the house
2Chronicles3:5 The larger room he paneled with cypress which he overlaid with fine gold
2Chronicles3:6 And he decorated the house with precious stones for beauty
2Chronicles3:7 He also overlaid the house—the beams and doorposts
2Chronicles3:8 And he made the Most Holy Place. Its length was according to the width of the house
2Chronicles3:9 The weight of the nails was fifty shekels of gold; and he overlaid the upper area with gold.
2Chronicles3:10 In the Most Holy Place he made two cherubim
2Chronicles3:11 The wings of the cherubim were twenty cubits in overall length: one wing of the one cherub was five cubits
2Chronicles3:12 one wing of the other cherub was five cubits
2Chronicles3:13 The wings of these cherubim spanned twenty cubits overall. They stood on their feet
2Chronicles3:14 And he made the veil of blue
2Chronicles3:15 Also he made in front of the temple two pillars thirty-five cubits high
2Chronicles3:16 He made wreaths of chainwork
2Chronicles3:17 Then he set up the pillars before the temple
2Chronicles4:1 Moreover he made a bronze altar: twenty cubits was its length
2Chronicles4:2 Then he made the Sea of cast bronze
2Chronicles4:3 And under it was the likeness of oxen encircling it all around
2Chronicles4:4 It stood on twelve oxen: three looking toward the north
2Chronicles4:5 It was a handbreadth thick; and its brim was shaped like the brim of a cup
2Chronicles4:6 He also made ten lavers
2Chronicles4:7 And he made ten lampstands of gold according to their design
2Chronicles4:8 He also made ten tables
2Chronicles4:9 Furthermore he made the court of the priests
2Chronicles4:10 He set the Sea on the right side
2Chronicles4:11 Then Huram made the pots and the shovels and the bowls. So Huram finished doing the work that he was to do for King Solomon for the house of God:
2Chronicles4:12 the two pillars and the bowl-shaped capitals that were on top of the two pillars; the two networks covering the two bowl-shaped capitals which were on top of the pillars;
2Chronicles4:13 four hundred pomegranates for the two networks (two rows of pomegranates for each network
2Chronicles4:14 he also made carts and the lavers on the carts;
2Chronicles4:15 one Sea and twelve oxen under it;
2Chronicles4:16 also the pots
2Chronicles4:17 In the plain of Jordan the king had them cast in clay molds
2Chronicles4:18 And Solomon had all these articles made in such great abundance that the weight of the bronze was not determined.
2Chronicles4:19 Thus Solomon had all the furnishings made for the house of God: the altar of gold and the tables on which was the showbread;
2Chronicles4:20 the lampstands with their lamps of pure gold
2Chronicles4:21 with the flowers and the lamps and the wick-trimmers of gold
2Chronicles4:22 the trimmers
