2Chronicles34:15 Then Hilkiah answered and said to Shaphan the scribe
2Chronicles34:16 So Shaphan carried the book to the king
2Chronicles34:17 And they have gathered the money that was found in the house of the Lord
2Chronicles34:18 Then Shaphan the scribe told the king
2Chronicles34:19 Thus it happened
2Chronicles34:20 Then the king commanded Hilkiah
2Chronicles34:21 “Go
2Chronicles34:22 So Hilkiah and those the king had appointed went to Huldah the prophetess
2Chronicles34:23 Then she answered them
2Chronicles34:24 “Thus says the Lord: ‘Behold
2Chronicles34:25 because they have forsaken Me and burned incense to other gods
2Chronicles34:26 But as for the king of Judah
2Chronicles34:27 because your heart was tender
2Chronicles34:28 “Surely I will gather you to your fathers
2Chronicles34:29 Then the king sent and gathered all the elders of Judah and Jerusalem.
2Chronicles34:30 The king went up to the house of the Lord
2Chronicles34:31 Then the king stood in his place and made a covenant before the Lord
2Chronicles34:32 And he made all who were present in Jerusalem and Benjamin take a stand. So the inhabitants of Jerusalem did according to the covenant of God
2Chronicles34:33 Thus Josiah removed all the abominations from all the country that belonged to the children of Israel
2Chronicles35:1 Now Josiah kept a Passover to the Lord in Jerusalem
2Chronicles35:2 And he set the priests in their duties and encouraged them for the service of the house of the Lord.
2Chronicles35:3 Then he said to the Levites who taught all Israel
2Chronicles35:4 Prepare yourselves according to your fathers’ houses
2Chronicles35:5 And stand in the holy place according to the divisions of the fathers’ houses of your brethren the lay people
2Chronicles35:6 So slaughter the Passover offerings
2Chronicles35:7 Then Josiah gave the lay people lambs and young goats from the flock
2Chronicles35:8 And his leaders gave willingly to the people
2Chronicles35:9 Also Conaniah
2Chronicles35:10 So the service was prepared
2Chronicles35:11 And they slaughtered the Passover offerings; and the priests sprinkled the blood with their hands
2Chronicles35:12 Then they removed the burnt offerings that they might give them to the divisions of the fathers’ houses of the lay people
2Chronicles35:13 Also they roasted the Passover offerings with fire according to the ordinance; but the other holy offerings they boiled in pots
2Chronicles35:14 Then afterward they prepared portions for themselves and for the priests
2Chronicles35:15 And the singers
2Chronicles35:16 So all the service of the Lord was prepared the same day
2Chronicles35:17 And the children of Israel who were present kept the Passover at that time
2Chronicles35:18 There had been no Passover kept in Israel like that since the days of Samuel the prophet; and none of the kings of Israel had kept such a Passover as Josiah kept
2Chronicles35:19 In the eighteenth year of the reign of Josiah this Passover was kept.
2Chronicles35:20 After all this
2Chronicles35:21 But he sent messengers to him
2Chronicles35:22 Nevertheless Josiah would not turn his face from him
2Chronicles35:23 And the archers shot King Josiah; and the king said to his servants
2Chronicles35:24 His servants therefore took him out of that chariot and put him in the second chariot that he had
2Chronicles35:25 Jeremiah also lamented for Josiah. And to this day all the singing men and the singing women speak of Josiah in their lamentations. They made it a custom in Israel; and indeed they are written in the Laments.
2Chronicles35:26 Now the rest of the acts of Josiah and his goodness
2Chronicles35:27 and his deeds from first to last
2Chronicles36:1 Then the people of the land took Jehoahaz the son of Josiah
2Chronicles36:2 Jehoahaz was twenty-three years old when he became king
2Chronicles36:3 Now the king of Egypt deposed him at Jerusalem; and he imposed on the land a tribute of one hundred talents of silver and a talent of gold.
2Chronicles36:4 Then the king of Egypt made Jehoahaz’s brother Eliakim king over Judah and Jerusalem
2Chronicles36:5 Jehoiakim was twenty-five years old when he became king
2Chronicles36:6 Nebuchadnezzar king of Babylon came up against him
2Chronicles36:7 Nebuchadnezzar also carried off some of the articles from the house of the Lord to Babylon
2Chronicles36:8 Now the rest of the acts of Jehoiakim
2Chronicles36:9 Jehoiachin was eight years old when he became king
2Chronicles36:10 At the turn of the year King Nebuchadnezzar summoned him and took him to Babylon
2Chronicles36:11 Zedekiah was twenty-one years old when he became king
2Chronicles36:12 He did evil in the sight of the Lord his God
2Chronicles36:13 And he also rebelled against King Nebuchadnezzar
2Chronicles36:14 Moreover all the leaders of the priests and the people transgressed more and more
2Chronicles36:15 And the Lord God of their fathers sent warnings to them by His messengers
2Chronicles36:16 But they mocked the messengers of God
2Chronicles36:17 Therefore He brought against them the king of the Chaldeans
2Chronicles36:18 And all the articles from the house of God
2Chronicles36:19 Then they burned the house of God
2Chronicles36:20 And those who escaped from the sword he carried away to Babylon
2Chronicles36:21 to fulfill the word of the Lord by the mouth of Jeremiah
2Chronicles36:22 Now in the first year of Cyrus king of Persia
2Chronicles36:23 Thus says Cyrus king of Persia: All the kingdoms of the earth the Lord God of heaven has given me. And He has commanded me to build Him a house at Jerusalem which is in Judah. Who is among you of all His people? May the Lord his God be with him

Ezra4:9 From Rehum the commander
Ezra4:10 and the rest of the nations whom the great and noble Osnapper took captive and settled in the cities of Samaria and the remainder beyond the River — and so forth.
Ezra4:11 (This is a copy of the letter that they sent him) To King Artaxerxes from your servants
Ezra4:12 Let it be known to the king that the Jews who came up from you have come to us at Jerusalem
Ezra4:13 Let it now be known to the king that
Ezra4:14 Now because we receive support from the palace
Ezra4:15 that search may be made in the book of the records of your fathers. And you will find in the book of the records and know that this city is a rebellious city
Ezra4:16 We inform the king that if this city is rebuilt and its walls are completed
Ezra4:17 The king sent an answer: To Rehum the commander
Ezra4:18 The letter which you sent to us has been clearly read before me.
Ezra4:19 And I gave the command
Ezra4:20 There have also been mighty kings over Jerusalem
Ezra4:21 Now give the command to make these men cease
Ezra4:22 Take heed now that you do not fail to do this. Why should damage increase to the hurt of the kings?
Ezra4:23 Now when the copy of King Artaxerxes’ letter was read before Rehum
Ezra4:24 Thus the work of the house of God which is at Jerusalem ceased

Nehemiah1:3 And they said to me
Nehemiah1:4 So it was
Nehemiah1:5 And I said: “I pray

Nehemiah1:8 Remember
Nehemiah1:9 but if you return to Me
Nehemiah1:10 Now these are Your servants and Your people
Nehemiah1:11 O Lord

Nehemiah2:20 So I answered them
Nehemiah3:1 Then Eliashib the high priest rose up with his brethren the priests and built the Sheep Gate; they consecrated it and hung its doors. They built as far as the Tower of the Hundred
Nehemiah3:2 Next to Eliashib the men of Jericho built. And next to them Zaccur the son of Imri built.
Nehemiah3:3 Also the sons of Hassenaah built the Fish Gate; they laid its beams and hung its doors with its bolts and bars.
Nehemiah3:4 And next to them Meremoth the son of Urijah
Nehemiah3:5 Next to them the Tekoites made repairs; but their nobles did not put their shoulders to the work of their Lord.
Nehemiah3:6 Moreover Jehoiada the son of Paseah and Meshullam the son of Besodeiah repaired the Old Gate; they laid its beams and hung its doors
Nehemiah3:7 And next to them Melatiah the Gibeonite
Nehemiah3:8 Next to him Uzziel the son of Harhaiah
Nehemiah3:9 And next to them Rephaiah the son of Hur
Nehemiah3:10 Next to them Jedaiah the son of Harumaph made repairs in front of his house. And next to him Hattush the son of Hashabniah made repairs.
Nehemiah3:11 Malchijah the son of Harim and Hashub the son of Pahath-Moab repaired another section
Nehemiah3:12 And next to him was Shallum the son of Hallohesh
Nehemiah3:13 Hanun and the inhabitants of Zanoah repaired the Valley Gate. They built it
Nehemiah3:14 Malchijah the son of Rechab
Nehemiah3:15 Shallun the son of Col-Hozeh
Nehemiah3:16 After him Nehemiah the son of Azbuk
Nehemiah3:17 After him the Levites
Nehemiah3:18 After him their brethren
Nehemiah3:19 And next to him Ezer the son of Jeshua
Nehemiah3:20 After him Baruch the son of Zabbai carefully repaired the other section

Nehemiah9:1 Now on the twenty-fourth day of this month the children of Israel were assembled with fasting
Nehemiah9:2 Then those of Israelite lineage separated themselves from all foreigners; and they stood and confessed their sins and the iniquities of their fathers.

Nehemiah9:7 “You are the Lord God
Nehemiah9:8 You found his heart faithful before You
Nehemiah9:9 “You saw the affliction of our fathers in Egypt
Nehemiah9:10 You showed signs and wonders against Pharaoh
Nehemiah9:11 And You divided the sea before them
Nehemiah9:12 Moreover You led them by day with a cloudy pillar
Nehemiah9:13 “You came down also on Mount Sinai
Nehemiah9:14 You made known to them Your holy Sabbath
Nehemiah9:15 You gave them bread from heaven for their hunger
Nehemiah9:16 “But they and our fathers acted proudly
Nehemiah9:17 They refused to obey
Nehemiah9:18 “Even when they made a molded calf for themselves
Nehemiah9:19 Yet in Your manifold mercies You did not forsake them in the wilderness. The pillar of the cloud did not depart from them by day
Nehemiah9:20 You also gave Your good Spirit to instruct them
Nehemiah9:21 Forty years You sustained them in the wilderness; They lacked nothing; Their clothes did not wear out And their feet did not swell.
Nehemiah9:22 “Moreover You gave them kingdoms and nations
Nehemiah9:23 You also multiplied their children as the stars of heaven
Nehemiah9:24 So the people went in And possessed the land; You subdued before them the inhabitants of the land
Nehemiah9:25 And they took strong cities and a rich land
Nehemiah9:26 “Nevertheless they were disobedient And rebelled against You
Nehemiah9:27 Therefore You delivered them into the hand of their enemies
Nehemiah9:28 “But after they had rest
Nehemiah9:29 And testified against them
Nehemiah9:30 Yet for many years You had patience with them
Nehemiah9:31 Nevertheless in Your great mercy You did not utterly consume them nor forsake them; For You are God
Nehemiah9:32 “Now therefore
Nehemiah9:33 However You are just in all that has befallen us; For You have dealt faithfully
Nehemiah9:34 Neither our kings nor our princes
Nehemiah9:35 For they have not served You in their kingdom
Nehemiah9:36 “Here we are
Nehemiah9:37 And it yields much increase to the kings You have set over us
Nehemiah9:38 “And because of all this

Esther1:1 Now it came to pass in the days of Ahasuerus (this was the Ahasuerus who reigned over one hundred and twenty-seven provinces
Esther1:2 in those days when King Ahasuerus sat on the throne of his kingdom
Esther1:3 that in the third year of his reign he made a feast for all his officials and servants—the powers of Persia and Media
Esther1:4 when he showed the riches of his glorious kingdom and the splendor of his excellent majesty for many days
Esther1:5 And when these days were completed
Esther1:6 There were white and blue linen curtains fastened with cords of fine linen and purple on silver rods and marble pillars; and the couches were of gold and silver on a mosaic pavement of alabaster
Esther1:7 And they served drinks in golden vessels
Esther1:8 In accordance with the law
Esther1:9 Queen Vashti also made a feast for the women in the royal palace which belonged to King Ahasuerus.
Esther1:10 On the seventh day
Esther1:11 to bring Queen Vashti before the king
Esther1:12 But Queen Vashti refused to come at the king’s command brought by his eunuchs; therefore the king was furious
Esther1:13 Then the king said to the wise men who understood the times (for this was the king’s manner toward all who knew law and justice
Esther1:14 those closest to him being Carshena
Esther1:15 “What shall we do to Queen Vashti
Esther1:16 And Memucan answered before the king and the princes: “Queen Vashti has not only wronged the king
Esther1:17 For the queen’s behavior will become known to all women
Esther1:18 This very day the noble ladies of Persia and Media will say to all the king’s officials that they have heard of the behavior of the queen. Thus there will be excessive contempt and wrath.
Esther1:19 If it pleases the king
Esther1:20 When the king’s decree which he will make is proclaimed throughout all his empire (for it is great)
Esther1:21 And the reply pleased the king and the princes
Esther1:22 Then he sent letters to all the king’s provinces

Esther2:2 Then the king’s servants who attended him said: “Let beautiful young virgins be sought for the king;
Esther2:3 and let the king appoint officers in all the provinces of his kingdom

Esther2:21 In those days
Esther2:22 So the matter became known to Mordecai

Esther4:17 So Mordecai went his way and did according to all that Esther commanded him.

Esther6:3 Then the king said
Esther6:4 So the king said
Esther6:5 The king’s servants said to him
Esther6:6 So Haman came in
Esther6:7 And Haman answered the king
Esther6:8 let a royal robe be brought which the king has worn
Esther6:9 Then let this robe and horse be delivered to the hand of one of the king’s most noble princes
Esther6:10 Then the king said to Haman
Esther6:11 So Haman took the robe and the horse
Esther6:12 Afterward Mordecai went back to the king’s gate. But Haman hurried to his house
Esther6:13 When Haman told his wife Zeresh and all his friends everything that had happened to him
Esther6:14 While they were still talking with him
Esther7:1 So the king and Haman went to dine with Queen Esther.
Esther7:2 And on the second day
Esther7:3 Then Queen Esther answered and said
Esther7:4 For we have been sold
Esther7:5 So King Ahasuerus answered and said to Queen Esther
Esther7:6 And Esther said
Esther7:7 Then the king arose in his wrath from the banquet of wine and went into the palace garden; but Haman stood before Queen Esther
Esther7:8 When the king returned from the palace garden to the place of the banquet of wine
Esther7:9 Now Harbonah
Esther7:10 So they hanged Haman on the gallows that he had prepared for Mordecai. Then the king’s wrath subsided.
Esther8:1 On that day King Ahasuerus gave Queen Esther the house of Haman
Esther8:2 So the king took off his signet ring
Esther8:3 Now Esther spoke again to the king
Esther8:4 And the king held out the golden scepter toward Esther. So Esther arose and stood before the king
Esther8:5 and said
Esther8:6 For how can I endure to see the evil that will come to my people? Or how can I endure to see the destruction of my countrymen?”
Esther8:7 Then King Ahasuerus said to Queen Esther and Mordecai the Jew
Esther8:8 You yourselves write a decree concerning the Jews
Esther8:9 So the king’s scribes were called at that time
Esther8:10 And he wrote in the name of King Ahasuerus
Esther8:11 By these letters the king permitted the Jews who were in every city to gather together and protect their lives—to destroy
Esther8:12 on one day in all the provinces of King Ahasuerus
Esther8:13 A copy of the document was to be issued as a decree in every province and published for all people
Esther8:14 The couriers who rode on royal horses went out
Esther8:15 So Mordecai went out from the presence of the king in royal apparel of blue and white
Esther8:16 The Jews had light and gladness
Esther8:17 And in every province and city
Esther9:1 Now in the twelfth month
Esther9:2 The Jews gathered together in their cities throughout all the provinces of King Ahasuerus to lay hands on those who sought their harm. And no one could withstand them
Esther9:3 And all the officials of the provinces
Esther9:4 For Mordecai was great in the king’s palace
Esther9:5 Thus the Jews defeated all their enemies with the stroke of the sword
Esther9:6 And in Shushan the citadel the Jews killed and destroyed five hundred men.
Esther9:7 Also Parshandatha
Esther9:8 Poratha
Esther9:9 Parmashta
Esther9:10 the ten sons of Haman the son of Hammedatha
Esther9:11 On that day the number of those who were killed in Shushan the citadel was brought to the king.
Esther9:12 And the king said to Queen Esther
Esther9:13 Then Esther said
Esther9:14 So the king commanded this to be done; the decree was issued in Shushan
Esther9:15 And the Jews who were in Shushan gathered together again on the fourteenth day of the month of Adar and killed three hundred men at Shushan; but they did not lay a hand on the plunder.
Esther9:16 The remainder of the Jews in the king’s provinces gathered together and protected their lives
Esther9:17 This was on the thirteenth day of the month of Adar. And on the fourteenth of the month they rested and made it a day of feasting and gladness.
Esther9:18 But the Jews who were at Shushan assembled together on the thirteenth day
Esther9:19 Therefore the Jews of the villages who dwelt in the unwalled towns celebrated the fourteenth day of the month of Adar with gladness and feasting
Esther9:20 And Mordecai wrote these things and sent letters to all the Jews
Esther9:21 to establish among them that they should celebrate yearly the fourteenth and fifteenth days of the month of Adar
Esther9:22 as the days on which the Jews had rest from their enemies
Esther9:23 So the Jews accepted the custom which they had begun
Esther9:24 because Haman
Esther9:25 but when Esther came before the king
Esther9:26 So they called these days Purim
Esther9:27 the Jews established and imposed it upon themselves and their descendants and all who would join them
Esther9:28 that these days should be remembered and kept throughout every generation
Esther9:29 Then Queen Esther
Esther9:30 And Mordecai sent letters to all the Jews
Esther9:31 to confirm these days of Purim at their appointed time
Esther9:32 So the decree of Esther confirmed these matters of Purim
Esther10:1 And King Ahasuerus imposed tribute on the land and on the islands of the sea.
Esther10:2 Now all the acts of his power and his might
Esther10:3 For Mordecai the Jew was second to King Ahasuerus
Job1:1 There was a man in the land of Uz
Job1:2 And seven sons and three daughters were born to him.
Job1:3 Also
Job1:4 And his sons would go and feast in their houses
Job1:5 So it was
Job1:6 Now there was a day when the sons of God came to present themselves before the Lord
Job1:7 And the Lord said to Satan
Job1:8 Then the Lord said to Satan
Job1:9 So Satan answered the Lord and said
Job1:10 Have You not made a hedge around him
Job1:11 But now
Job1:12 And the Lord said to Satan
Job1:13 Now there was a day when his sons and daughters were eating and drinking wine in their oldest brother’s house;
Job1:14 and a messenger came to Job and said
Job1:15 when the Sabeans raided them and took them away—indeed they have killed the servants with the edge of the sword; and I alone have escaped to tell you!”
Job1:16 While he was still speaking
Job1:17 While he was still speaking
Job1:18 While he was still speaking
Job1:19 and suddenly a great wind came from across the wilderness and struck the four corners of the house
Job1:20 Then Job arose
Job1:21 And he said: “Naked I came from my mother’s womb
Job1:22 In all this Job did not sin nor charge God with wrong.
Job2:1 Again there was a day when the sons of God came to present themselves before the Lord
Job2:2 And the Lord said to Satan
Job2:3 Then the Lord said to Satan
Job2:4 So Satan answered the Lord and said
Job2:5 But stretch out Your hand now
Job2:6 And the Lord said to Satan
Job2:7 So Satan went out from the presence of the Lord
Job2:8 And he took for himself a potsherd with which to scrape himself while he sat in the midst of the ashes.
Job2:9 Then his wife said to him
Job2:10 But he said to her
Job2:11 Now when Job’s three friends heard of all this adversity that had come upon him
Job2:12 And when they raised their eyes from afar
Job2:13 So they sat down with him on the ground seven days and seven nights
Job3:1 After this Job opened his mouth and cursed the day of his birth.
Job3:2 And Job spoke
Job3:3 “May the day perish on which I was born
Job3:4 May that day be darkness; May God above not seek it
Job3:5 May darkness and the shadow of death claim it; May a cloud settle on it; May the blackness of the day terrify it.
Job3:6 As for that night
Job3:7 Oh
Job3:8 May those curse it who curse the day
Job3:9 May the stars of its morning be dark; May it look for light
Job3:10 Because it did not shut up the doors of my mother’s womb
Job3:11 “Why did I not die at birth? Why did I not perish when I came from the womb?
Job3:12 Why did the knees receive me? Or why the breasts
Job3:13 For now I would have lain still and been quiet
Job3:14 With kings and counselors of the earth
Job3:15 Or with princes who had gold
Job3:16 Or why was I not hidden like a stillborn child
Job3:17 There the wicked cease from troubling
Job3:18 There the prisoners rest together; They do not hear the voice of the oppressor.
Job3:19 The small and great are there
Job3:20 “Why is light given to him who is in misery
Job3:21 Who long for death
Job3:22 Who rejoice exceedingly
Job3:23 Why is light given to a man whose way is hidden
Job3:24 For my sighing comes before I eat
Job3:25 For the thing I greatly feared has come upon me
Job3:26 I am not at ease
Job4:1 Then Eliphaz the Temanite answered and said:
Job4:2 “ If one attempts a word with you
Job4:3 Surely you have instructed many
Job4:4 Your words have upheld him who was stumbling
Job4:5 But now it comes upon you
Job4:6 Is not your reverence your confidence? And the integrity of your ways your hope?
Job4:7 “Remember now
Job4:8 Even as I have seen
Job4:9 By the blast of God they perish
Job4:10 The roaring of the lion
Job4:11 The old lion perishes for lack of prey
Job4:12 “Now a word was secretly brought to me
Job4:13 In disquieting thoughts from the visions of the night
Job4:14 Fear came upon me
Job4:15 Then a spirit passed before my face; The hair on my body stood up.
Job4:16 It stood still
Job4:17 ‘Can a mortal be more righteous than God? Can a man be more pure than his Maker?
Job4:18 If He puts no trust in His servants
Job4:19 How much more those who dwell in houses of clay
Job4:20 They are broken in pieces from morning till evening; They perish forever
Job4:21 Does not their own excellence go away? They die
Job5:1 “Call out now; Is there anyone who will answer you? And to which of the holy ones will you turn?
Job5:2 For wrath kills a foolish man
Job5:3 I have seen the foolish taking root
Job5:4 His sons are far from safety
Job5:5 Because the hungry eat up his harvest
Job5:6 For affliction does not come from the dust
Job5:7 Yet man is born to trouble
Job5:8 “But as for me
Job5:9 Who does great things
Job5:10 He gives rain on the earth
Job5:11 He sets on high those who are lowly
Job5:12 He frustrates the devices of the crafty
Job5:13 He catches the wise in their own craftiness
Job5:14 They meet with darkness in the daytime
Job5:15 But He saves the needy from the sword
Job5:16 So the poor have hope
Job5:17 “Behold
Job5:18 For He bruises
Job5:19 He shall deliver you in six troubles
Job5:20 In famine He shall redeem you from death
Job5:21 You shall be hidden from the scourge of the tongue
Job5:22 You shall laugh at destruction and famine
Job5:23 For you shall have a covenant with the stones of the field
Job5:24 You shall know that your tent is in peace; You shall visit your dwelling and find nothing amiss.
Job5:25 You shall also know that your descendants shall be many
Job5:26 You shall come to the grave at a full age
Job5:27 Behold
Job6:1 Then Job answered and said:
Job6:2 “Oh
Job6:3 For then it would be heavier than the sand of the sea— Therefore my words have been rash.
Job6:4 For the arrows of the Almighty are within me; My spirit drinks in their poison; The terrors of God are arrayed against me.
Job6:5 Does the wild donkey bray when it has grass
Job6:6 Can flavorless food be eaten without salt? Or is there any taste in the white of an egg?
Job6:7 My soul refuses to touch them; They are as loathsome food to me.
Job6:8 “Oh
Job6:9 That it would please God to crush me
Job6:10 Then I would still have comfort; Though in anguish I would exult
Job6:11 “What strength do I have
Job6:12 Is my strength the strength of stones? Or is my flesh bronze?
Job6:13 Is my help not within me? And is success driven from me?
Job6:14 “To him who is afflicted
Job6:15 My brothers have dealt deceitfully like a brook
Job6:16 Which are dark because of the ice
Job6:17 When it is warm
Job6:18 The paths of their way turn aside
Job6:19 The caravans of Tema look
Job6:20 They are disappointed because they were confident; They come there and are confused.
Job6:21 For now you are nothing
Job6:22 Did I ever say
Job6:23 Or
Job6:24 “Teach me
Job6:25 How forceful are right words! But what does your arguing prove?
Job6:26 Do you intend to rebuke my words
Job6:27 Yes
Job6:28 Now therefore
Job6:29 Yield now
Job6:30 Is there injustice on my tongue? Cannot my taste discern the unsavory?

Job7:2 Like a servant who earnestly desires the shade
Job7:3 So I have been allotted months of futility
Job7:4 When I lie down
Job7:5 My flesh is caked with worms and dust
Job7:6 “My days are swifter than a weaver’s shuttle
Job7:7 Oh
Job7:8 The eye of him who sees me will see me no more; While your eyes are upon me
Job7:9 As the cloud disappears and vanishes away
Job7:10 He shall never return to his house
Job7:11 “Therefore I will not restrain my mouth; I will speak in the anguish of my spirit; I will complain in the bitterness of my soul.
Job7:12 Am I a sea
Job7:13 When I say
Job7:14 Then You scare me with dreams And terrify me with visions
Job7:15 So that my soul chooses strangling And death rather than my body.
Job7:16 I loathe my life; I would not live forever. Let me alone
Job7:17 “What is man
Job7:18 That You should visit him every morning
Job7:19 How long? Will You not look away from me
Job7:20 Have I sinned? What have I done to You
Job7:21 Why then do You not pardon my transgression
Job8:1 Then Bildad the Shuhite answered and said:
Job8:2 “How long will you speak these things
Job8:3 Does God subvert judgment? Or does the Almighty pervert justice?
Job8:4 If your sons have sinned against Him
Job8:5 If you would earnestly seek God And make your supplication to the Almighty
Job8:6 If you were pure and upright
Job8:7 Though your beginning was small
Job8:8 “For inquire
Job8:9 For we were born yesterday
Job8:10 Will they not teach you and tell you
Job8:11 “Can the papyrus grow up without a marsh? Can the reeds flourish without water?
Job8:12 While it is yet green and not cut down
Job8:13 So are the paths of all who forget God; And the hope of the hypocrite shall perish
Job8:14 Whose confidence shall be cut off
Job8:15 He leans on his house
Job8:16 He grows green in the sun
Job8:17 His roots wrap around the rock heap
Job8:18 If he is destroyed from his place
Job8:19 “Behold
Job8:20 Behold
Job8:21 He will yet fill your mouth with laughing
Job8:22 Those who hate you will be clothed with shame
Job9:1 Then Job answered and said:
Job9:2 “Truly I know it is so
Job9:3 If one wished to contend with Him
Job9:4 God is wise in heart and mighty in strength. Who has hardened himself against Him and prospered?
Job9:5 He removes the mountains
Job9:6 He shakes the earth out of its place
Job9:7 He commands the sun
Job9:8 He alone spreads out the heavens
Job9:9 He made the Bear
Job9:10 He does great things past finding out
Job9:11 If He goes by me
Job9:12 If He takes away
Job9:13 God will not withdraw His anger
Job9:14 “How then can I answer Him
Job9:15 For though I were righteous
Job9:16 If I called and He answered me
Job9:17 For He crushes me with a tempest
Job9:18 He will not allow me to catch my breath
Job9:19 If it is a matter of strength
Job9:20 Though I were righteous
Job9:21 “I am blameless
Job9:22 It is all one thing; Therefore I say
Job9:23 If the scourge slays suddenly

Job9:25 “Now my days are swifter than a runner; They flee away
Job9:26 They pass by like swift ships
Job9:27 If I say
Job9:28 I am afraid of all my sufferings; I know that You will not hold me innocent.
Job9:29 If I am condemned
Job9:30 If I wash myself with snow water
Job9:31 Yet You will plunge me into the pit
Job9:32 “For He is not a man
Job9:33 Nor is there any mediator between us
Job9:34 Let Him take His rod away from me
Job9:35 Then I would speak and not fear Him

Job10:2 I will say to God
Job10:3 Does it seem good to You that You should oppress

Job10:5 Are Your days like the days of a mortal man? Are Your years like the days of a mighty man

Job10:14 If I sin

Job10:17 You renew Your witnesses against me

Job10:21 Before I go to the place from which I shall not return
Job10:22 A land as dark as darkness itself
Job11:1 Then Zophar the Naamathite answered and said:
Job11:2 “Should not the multitude of words be answered? And should a man full of talk be vindicated?
Job11:3 Should your empty talk make men hold their peace? And when you mock
Job11:4 For you have said
Job11:5 But oh
Job11:6 That He would show you the secrets of wisdom! For they would double your prudence. Know therefore that God exacts from you Less than your iniquity deserves.
Job11:7 “Can you search out the deep things of God? Can you find out the limits of the Almighty?
Job11:8 They are higher than heaven— what can you do? Deeper than Sheol— what can you know?
Job11:9 Their measure is longer than the earth And broader than the sea.
Job11:10 “If He passes by
Job11:11 For He knows deceitful men; He sees wickedness also. Will He not then consider it?
Job11:12 For an empty-headed man will be wise
Job11:13 “If you would prepare your heart
Job11:14 If iniquity were in your hand
Job11:15 Then surely you could lift up your face without spot; Yes
Job11:16 Because you would forget your misery
Job11:17 And your life would be brighter than noonday. Though you were dark
Job11:18 And you would be secure
Job11:19 You would also lie down
Job11:20 But the eyes of the wicked will fail
Job12:1 Then Job answered and said:
Job12:2 “No doubt you are the people
Job12:3 But I have understanding as well as you; I am not inferior to you. Indeed
Job12:4 “I am one mocked by his friends
Job12:5 A lamp is despised in the thought of one who is at ease; It is made ready for those whose feet slip.
Job12:6 The tents of robbers prosper
Job12:7 “But now ask the beasts
Job12:8 Or speak to the earth
Job12:9 Who among all these does not know That the hand of the Lord has done this
Job12:10 In whose hand is the life of every living thing
Job12:11 Does not the ear test words And the mouth taste its food?
Job12:12 Wisdom is with aged men
Job12:13 “With Him are wisdom and strength
Job12:14 If He breaks a thing down
Job12:15 If He withholds the waters
Job12:16 With Him are strength and prudence. The deceived and the deceiver are His.

Job12:23 He makes nations great

Job13:2 What you know

Job14:1 “Man who is born of woman Is of few days and full of trouble.
Job14:2 He comes forth like a flower and fades away; He flees like a shadow and does not continue.
Job14:3 And do You open Your eyes on such a one
Job14:4 Who can bring a clean thing out of an unclean? No one!
Job14:5 Since his days are determined
Job14:6 Look away from him that he may rest
Job14:7 “For there is hope for a tree
Job14:8 Though its root may grow old in the earth
Job14:9 Yet at the scent of water it will bud And bring forth branches like a plant.
Job14:10 But man dies and is laid away; Indeed he breathes his last And where is he?
Job14:11 As water disappears from the sea
Job14:12 So man lies down and does not rise. Till the heavens are no more

Job14:15 You shall call

Job14:17 My transgression is sealed up in a bag

Job14:19 As water wears away stones
Job14:20 You prevail forever against him
Job14:21 His sons come to honor
Job14:22 But his flesh will be in pain over it
Job15:1 Then Eliphaz the Temanite answered and said:
Job15:2 “Should a wise man answer with empty knowledge
Job15:3 Should he reason with unprofitable talk
Job15:4 Yes
Job15:5 For your iniquity teaches your mouth
Job15:6 Your own mouth condemns you
Job15:7 “ Are you the first man who was born? Or were you made before the hills?
Job15:8 Have you heard the counsel of God? Do you limit wisdom to yourself?
Job15:9 What do you know that we do not know? What do you understand that is not in us?
Job15:10 Both the gray-haired and the aged are among us
Job15:11 Are the consolations of God too small for you
Job15:12 Why does your heart carry you away
Job15:13 That you turn your spirit against God
Job15:14 “What is man
Job15:15 If God puts no trust in His saints
Job15:16 How much less man
Job15:17 “I will tell you
Job15:18 What wise men have told
Job15:19 To whom alone the land was given
Job15:20 The wicked man writhes with pain all his days
Job15:21 Dreadful sounds are in his ears; In prosperity the destroyer comes upon him.
Job15:22 He does not believe that he will return from darkness
Job15:23 He wanders about for bread
Job15:24 Trouble and anguish make him afraid; They overpower him
Job15:25 For he stretches out his hand against God
Job15:26 Running stubbornly against Him With his strong
Job15:27 “Though he has covered his face with his fatness
Job15:28 He dwells in desolate cities
Job15:29 He will not be rich
Job15:30 He will not depart from darkness; The flame will dry out his branches

Job15:32 It will be accomplished before his time
Job15:33 He will shake off his unripe grape like a vine
Job15:34 For the company of hypocrites will be barren
Job15:35 They conceive trouble and bring forth futility; Their womb prepares deceit.”
Job16:1 Then Job answered and said:
Job16:2 “I have heard many such things; Miserable comforters are you all!
Job16:3 Shall words of wind have an end? Or what provokes you that you answer?
Job16:4 I also could speak as you do
Job16:5 But I would strengthen you with my mouth
Job16:6 “Though I speak
Job16:7 But now He has worn me out; You have made desolate all my company.
Job16:8 You have shriveled me up
Job16:9 He tears me in His wrath
Job16:10 They gape at me with their mouth
Job16:11 God has delivered me to the ungodly
Job16:12 I was at ease
Job16:13 His archers surround me. He pierces my heart and does not pity; He pours out my gall on the ground.
Job16:14 He breaks me with wound upon wound; He runs at me like a warrior.
Job16:15 “I have sewn sackcloth over my skin
Job16:16 My face is flushed from weeping
Job16:17 Although no violence is in my hands

Job16:19 Surely even now my witness is in heaven
Job16:20 My friends scorn me; My eyes pour out tears to God.

Job17:7 My eye has also grown dim because of sorrow
Job17:8 Upright men are astonished at this

Job18:1 Then Bildad the Shuhite answered and said:
Job18:2 “How long till you put an end to words? Gain understanding
Job18:3 Why are we counted as beasts
Job18:4 You who tear yourself in anger
Job18:5 “The light of the wicked indeed goes out
Job18:6 The light is dark in his tent
Job18:7 The steps of his strength are shortened
Job18:8 For he is cast into a net by his own feet
Job18:9 The net takes him by the heel
Job18:10 A noose is hidden for him on the ground
Job18:11 Terrors frighten him on every side
Job18:12 His strength is starved
Job18:13 It devours patches of his skin; The firstborn of death devours his limbs.
Job18:14 He is uprooted from the shelter of his tent
Job18:15 They dwell in his tent who are none of his; Brimstone is scattered on his dwelling.
Job18:16 His roots are dried out below
Job18:17 The memory of him perishes from the earth
Job18:18 He is driven from light into darkness
Job18:19 He has neither son nor posterity among his people
Job18:20 Those in the west are astonished at his day
Job18:21 Surely such are the dwellings of the wicked
Job19:1 Then Job answered and said:
Job19:2 “How long will you torment my soul
Job19:3 These ten times you have reproached me; You are not ashamed that you have wronged me.
Job19:4 And if indeed I have erred
Job19:5 If indeed you exalt yourselves against me
Job19:6 Know then that God has wronged me
Job19:7 “If I cry out concerning wrong
Job19:8 He has fenced up my way
Job19:9 He has stripped me of my glory
Job19:10 He breaks me down on every side
Job19:11 He has also kindled His wrath against me
Job19:12 His troops come together And build up their road against me; They encamp all around my tent.
Job19:13 “He has removed my brothers far from me
Job19:14 My relatives have failed
Job19:15 Those who dwell in my house
Job19:16 I call my servant
Job19:17 My breath is offensive to my wife
Job19:18 Even young children despise me; I arise
Job19:19 All my close friends abhor me
Job19:20 My bone clings to my skin and to my flesh
Job19:21 “Have pity on me
Job19:22 Why do you persecute me as God does
Job19:23 “Oh
Job19:24 That they were engraved on a rock With an iron pen and lead
Job19:25 For I know that my Redeemer lives
Job19:26 And after my skin is destroyed
Job19:27 Whom I shall see for myself
Job19:28 If you should say
Job19:29 Be afraid of the sword for yourselves; For wrath brings the punishment of the sword
Job20:1 Then Zophar the Naamathite answered and said:
Job20:2 “Therefore my anxious thoughts make me answer
Job20:3 I have heard the rebuke that reproaches me
Job20:4 “Do you not know this of old
Job20:5 That the triumphing of the wicked is short
Job20:6 Though his haughtiness mounts up to the heavens
Job20:7 Yet he will perish forever like his own refuse; Those who have seen him will say
Job20:8 He will fly away like a dream
Job20:9 The eye that saw him will see him no more
Job20:10 His children will seek the favor of the poor
Job20:11 His bones are full of his youthful vigor
Job20:12 “Though evil is sweet in his mouth
Job20:13 Though he spares it and does not forsake it
Job20:14 Yet his food in his stomach turns sour; It becomes cobra venom within him.
Job20:15 He swallows down riches And vomits them up again; God casts them out of his belly.
Job20:16 He will suck the poison of cobras; The viper’s tongue will slay him.
Job20:17 He will not see the streams
Job20:18 He will restore that for which he labored
Job20:19 For he has oppressed and forsaken the poor
Job20:20 “Because he knows no quietness in his heart
Job20:21 Nothing is left for him to eat; Therefore his well-being will not last.
Job20:22 In his self-sufficiency he will be in distress; Every hand of misery will come against him.
Job20:23 When he is about to fill his stomach
Job20:24 He will flee from the iron weapon; A bronze bow will pierce him through.
Job20:25 It is drawn
Job20:26 Total darkness is reserved for his treasures. An unfanned fire will consume him; It shall go ill with him who is left in his tent.
Job20:27 The heavens will reveal his iniquity
Job20:28 The increase of his house will depart
Job20:29 This is the portion from God for a wicked man
Job21:1 Then Job answered and said:
Job21:2 “Listen carefully to my speech
Job21:3 Bear with me that I may speak
Job21:4 “As for me
Job21:5 Look at me and be astonished; Put your hand over your mouth.
Job21:6 Even when I remember I am terrified
Job21:7 Why do the wicked live and become old
Job21:8 Their descendants are established with them in their sight
Job21:9 Their houses are safe from fear
Job21:10 Their bull breeds without failure; Their cow calves without miscarriage.
Job21:11 They send forth their little ones like a flock
Job21:12 They sing to the tambourine and harp
Job21:13 They spend their days in wealth
Job21:14 Yet they say to God
Job21:15 Who is the Almighty
Job21:16 Indeed their prosperity is not in their hand; The counsel of the wicked is far from me.
Job21:17 “How often is the lamp of the wicked put out? How often does their destruction come upon them
Job21:18 They are like straw before the wind
Job21:19 They say
Job21:20 Let his eyes see his destruction
Job21:21 For what does he care about his household after him
Job21:22 “Can anyone teach God knowledge
Job21:23 One dies in his full strength
Job21:24 His pails are full of milk
Job21:25 Another man dies in the bitterness of his soul
Job21:26 They lie down alike in the dust
Job21:27 “Look
Job21:28 For you say
Job21:29 Have you not asked those who travel the road? And do you not know their signs?
Job21:30 For the wicked are reserved for the day of doom; They shall be brought out on the day of wrath.
Job21:31 Who condemns his way to his face? And who repays him for what he has done?
Job21:32 Yet he shall be brought to the grave
Job21:33 The clods of the valley shall be sweet to him; Everyone shall follow him
Job21:34 How then can you comfort me with empty words
Job22:1 Then Eliphaz the Temanite answered and said:
Job22:2 “Can a man be profitable to God
Job22:3 Is it any pleasure to the Almighty that you are righteous? Or is it gain to Him that you make your ways blameless?
Job22:4 “Is it because of your fear of Him that He corrects you
Job22:5 Is not your wickedness great
Job22:6 For you have taken pledges from your brother for no reason
Job22:7 You have not given the weary water to drink
Job22:8 But the mighty man possessed the land
Job22:9 You have sent widows away empty
Job22:10 Therefore snares are all around you
Job22:11 Or darkness so that you cannot see; And an abundance of water covers you.
Job22:12 “Is not God in the height of heaven? And see the highest stars
Job22:13 And you say
Job22:14 Thick clouds cover Him
Job22:15 Will you keep to the old way Which wicked men have trod
Job22:16 Who were cut down before their time
Job22:17 They said to God
Job22:18 Yet He filled their houses with good things; But the counsel of the wicked is far from me.
Job22:19 “The righteous see it and are glad
Job22:20 ‘Surely our adversaries are cut down
Job22:21 “Now acquaint yourself with Him
Job22:22 Receive
Job22:23 If you return to the Almighty
Job22:24 Then you will lay your gold in the dust
Job22:25 Yes
Job22:26 For then you will have your delight in the Almighty
Job22:27 You will make your prayer to Him
Job22:28 You will also declare a thing
Job22:29 When they cast you down
Job22:30 He will even deliver one who is not innocent; Yes
Job23:1 Then Job answered and said:
Job23:2 “Even today my complaint is bitter; My hand is listless because of my groaning.
Job23:3 Oh
Job23:4 I would present my case before Him
Job23:5 I would know the words which He would answer me
Job23:6 Would He contend with me in His great power? No! But He would take note of me.
Job23:7 There the upright could reason with Him
Job23:8 “Look
Job23:9 When He works on the left hand
Job23:10 But He knows the way that I take; When He has tested me
Job23:11 My foot has held fast to His steps; I have kept His way and not turned aside.
Job23:12 I have not departed from the commandment of His lips; I have treasured the words of His mouth More than my necessary food.
Job23:13 “But He is unique
Job23:14 For He performs what is appointed for me
Job23:15 Therefore I am terrified at His presence; When I consider this
Job23:16 For God made my heart weak
Job23:17 Because I was not cut off from the presence of darkness
Job24:1 “ Since times are not hidden from the Almighty
Job24:2 “ Some remove landmarks; They seize flocks violently and feed on them;
Job24:3 They drive away the donkey of the fatherless; They take the widow’s ox as a pledge.
Job24:4 They push the needy off the road; All the poor of the land are forced to hide.
Job24:5 Indeed
Job24:6 They gather their fodder in the field And glean in the vineyard of the wicked.
Job24:7 They spend the night naked
Job24:8 They are wet with the showers of the mountains
Job24:9 “ Some snatch the fatherless from the breast
Job24:10 They cause the poor to go naked
Job24:11 They press out oil within their walls
Job24:12 The dying groan in the city
Job24:13 “There are those who rebel against the light; They do not know its ways Nor abide in its paths.
Job24:14 The murderer rises with the light; He kills the poor and needy; And in the night he is like a thief.
Job24:15 The eye of the adulterer waits for the twilight
Job24:16 In the dark they break into houses Which they marked for themselves in the daytime; They do not know the light.
Job24:17 For the morning is the same to them as the shadow of death; If someone recognizes them
Job24:18 “They should be swift on the face of the waters
Job24:19 As drought and heat consume the snow waters
Job24:20 The womb should forget him
Job24:21 For he preys on the barren who do not bear
Job24:22 “But God draws the mighty away with His power; He rises up
Job24:23 He gives them security
Job24:24 They are exalted for a little while
Job24:25 “Now if it is not so
Job25:1 Then Bildad the Shuhite answered and said:
Job25:2 “Dominion and fear belong to Him; He makes peace in His high places.
Job25:3 Is there any number to His armies? Upon whom does His light not rise?
Job25:4 How then can man be righteous before God? Or how can he be pure who is born of a woman?
Job25:5 If even the moon does not shine
Job25:6 How much less man
Job26:1 But Job answered and said:
Job26:2 “How have you helped him who is without power? How have you saved the arm that has no strength?
Job26:3 How have you counseled one who has no wisdom? And how have you declared sound advice to many?
Job26:4 To whom have you uttered words? And whose spirit came from you?
Job26:5 “The dead tremble
Job26:6 Sheol is naked before Him
Job26:7 He stretches out the north over empty space; He hangs the earth on nothing.
Job26:8 He binds up the water in His thick clouds
Job26:9 He covers the face of His throne
Job26:10 He drew a circular horizon on the face of the waters
Job26:11 The pillars of heaven tremble
Job26:12 He stirs up the sea with His power
Job26:13 By His Spirit He adorned the heavens; His hand pierced the fleeing serpent.
Job26:14 Indeed these are the mere edges of His ways
Job27:1 Moreover Job continued his discourse
Job27:2 “ As God lives
Job27:3 As long as my breath is in me
Job27:4 My lips will not speak wickedness
Job27:5 Far be it from me That I should say you are right; Till I die I will not put away my integrity from me.
Job27:6 My righteousness I hold fast
Job27:7 “May my enemy be like the wicked
Job27:8 For what is the hope of the hypocrite
Job27:9 Will God hear his cry When trouble comes upon him?
Job27:10 Will he delight himself in the Almighty? Will he always call on God?
Job27:11 “I will teach you about the hand of God; What is with the Almighty I will not conceal.
Job27:12 Surely all of you have seen it; Why then do you behave with complete nonsense?
Job27:13 “This is the portion of a wicked man with God
Job27:14 If his children are multiplied
Job27:15 Those who survive him shall be buried in death
Job27:16 Though he heaps up silver like dust
Job27:17 He may pile it up
Job27:18 He builds his house like a moth
Job27:19 The rich man will lie down
Job27:20 Terrors overtake him like a flood; A tempest steals him away in the night.
Job27:21 The east wind carries him away
Job27:22 It hurls against him and does not spare; He flees desperately from its power.
Job27:23 Men shall clap their hands at him
Job28:1 “Surely there is a mine for silver
Job28:2 Iron is taken from the earth
Job28:3 Man puts an end to darkness
Job28:4 He breaks open a shaft away from people; In places forgotten by feet They hang far away from men; They swing to and fro.
Job28:5 As for the earth
Job28:6 Its stones are the source of sapphires
Job28:7 That path no bird knows
Job28:8 The proud lions have not trodden it
Job28:9 He puts his hand on the flint; He overturns the mountains at the roots.
Job28:10 He cuts out channels in the rocks
Job28:11 He dams up the streams from trickling; What is hidden he brings forth to light.
Job28:12 “But where can wisdom be found? And where is the place of understanding?
Job28:13 Man does not know its value
Job28:14 The deep says
Job28:15 It cannot be purchased for gold
Job28:16 It cannot be valued in the gold of Ophir
Job28:17 Neither gold nor crystal can equal it
Job28:18 No mention shall be made of coral or quartz
Job28:19 The topaz of Ethiopia cannot equal it
Job28:20 “From where then does wisdom come? And where is the place of understanding?
Job28:21 It is hidden from the eyes of all living
Job28:22 Destruction and Death say
Job28:23 God understands its way
Job28:24 For He looks to the ends of the earth
Job28:25 To establish a weight for the wind
Job28:26 When He made a law for the rain
Job28:27 Then He saw wisdom and declared it; He prepared it
Job28:28 And to man He said
Job29:1 Job further continued his discourse
Job29:2 “Oh
Job29:3 When His lamp shone upon my head
Job29:4 Just as I was in the days of my prime
Job29:5 When the Almighty was yet with me
Job29:6 When my steps were bathed with cream
Job29:7 “When I went out to the gate by the city
Job29:8 The young men saw me and hid
Job29:9 The princes refrained from talking
Job29:10 The voice of nobles was hushed
Job29:11 When the ear heard
Job29:12 Because I delivered the poor who cried out
Job29:13 The blessing of a perishing man came upon me
Job29:14 I put on righteousness
Job29:15 I was eyes to the blind
Job29:16 I was a father to the poor
Job29:17 I broke the fangs of the wicked
Job29:18 “Then I said
Job29:19 My root is spread out to the waters
Job29:20 My glory is fresh within me
Job29:21 “ Men listened to me and waited
Job29:22 After my words they did not speak again
Job29:23 They waited for me as for the rain
Job29:24 If I mocked at them
Job29:25 I chose the way for them
Job30:1 “But now they mock at me
Job30:2 Indeed
Job30:3 They are gaunt from want and famine
Job30:4 Who pluck mallow by the bushes
Job30:5 They were driven out from among men
Job30:6 They had to live in the clefts of the valleys
Job30:7 Among the bushes they brayed
Job30:8 They were sons of fools
Job30:9 “And now I am their taunting song; Yes
Job30:10 They abhor me
Job30:11 Because He has loosed my bowstring and afflicted me
Job30:12 At my right hand the rabble arises; They push away my feet
Job30:13 They break up my path
Job30:14 They come as broad breakers; Under the ruinous storm they roll along.
Job30:15 Terrors are turned upon me; They pursue my honor as the wind
Job30:16 “And now my soul is poured out because of my plight; The days of affliction take hold of me.
Job30:17 My bones are pierced in me at night
Job30:18 By great force my garment is disfigured; It binds me about as the collar of my coat.
Job30:19 He has cast me into the mire
Job30:20 “I cry out to You
Job30:21 But You have become cruel to me; With the strength of Your hand You oppose me.
Job30:22 You lift me up to the wind and cause me to ride on it; You spoil my success.
Job30:23 For I know that You will bring me to death
Job30:24 “Surely He would not stretch out His hand against a heap of ruins
Job30:25 Have I not wept for him who was in trouble? Has not my soul grieved for the poor?
Job30:26 But when I looked for good
Job30:27 My heart is in turmoil and cannot rest; Days of affliction confront me.
Job30:28 I go about mourning
Job30:29 I am a brother of jackals
Job30:30 My skin grows black and falls from me; My bones burn with fever.
Job30:31 My harp is turned to mourning
Job31:1 “I have made a covenant with my eyes; Why then should I look upon a young woman?
Job31:2 For what is the allotment of God from above
Job31:3 Is it not destruction for the wicked
Job31:4 Does He not see my ways
Job31:5 “If I have walked with falsehood
Job31:6 Let me be weighed on honest scales
Job31:7 If my step has turned from the way
Job31:8 Then let me sow
Job31:9 “If my heart has been enticed by a woman
Job31:10 Then let my wife grind for another
Job31:11 For that would be wickedness; Yes
Job31:12 For that would be a fire that consumes to destruction
Job31:13 “If I have despised the cause of my male or female servant When they complained against me
Job31:14 What then shall I do when God rises up? When He punishes
Job31:15 Did not He who made me in the womb make them? Did not the same One fashion us in the womb?
Job31:16 “If I have kept the poor from their desire
Job31:17 Or eaten my morsel by myself
Job31:18 (But from my youth I reared him as a father
Job31:19 If I have seen anyone perish for lack of clothing
Job31:20 If his heart has not blessed me
Job31:21 If I have raised my hand against the fatherless
Job31:22 Then let my arm fall from my shoulder
Job31:23 For destruction from God is a terror to me
Job31:24 “If I have made gold my hope
Job31:25 If I have rejoiced because my wealth was great
Job31:26 If I have observed the sun when it shines
Job31:27 So that my heart has been secretly enticed
Job31:28 This also would be an iniquity deserving of judgment
Job31:29 “If I have rejoiced at the destruction of him who hated me
Job31:30 (Indeed I have not allowed my mouth to sin By asking for a curse on his soul);
Job31:31 If the men of my tent have not said
Job31:32 (But no sojourner had to lodge in the street
Job31:33 If I have covered my transgressions as Adam
Job31:34 Because I feared the great multitude
Job31:35 Oh
Job31:36 Surely I would carry it on my shoulder
Job31:37 I would declare to Him the number of my steps; Like a prince I would approach Him.
Job31:38 “If my land cries out against me
Job31:39 If I have eaten its fruit without money
Job31:40 Then let thistles grow instead of wheat
Job32:1 So these three men ceased answering Job
Job32:2 Then the wrath of Elihu
Job32:3 Also against his three friends his wrath was aroused
Job32:4 Now because they were years older than he
Job32:5 When Elihu saw that there was no answer in the mouth of these three men
Job32:6 So Elihu
Job32:7 I said
Job32:8 But there is a spirit in man
Job32:9 Great men are not always wise
Job32:10 “Therefore I say
Job32:11 Indeed I waited for your words
Job32:12 I paid close attention to you; And surely not one of you convinced Job
Job32:13 Lest you say
Job32:14 Now he has not directed his words against me; So I will not answer him with your words.
Job32:15 “They are dismayed and answer no more; Words escape them.
Job32:16 And I have waited
Job32:17 I also will answer my part
Job32:18 For I am full of words; The spirit within me compels me.
Job32:19 Indeed my belly is like wine that has no vent; It is ready to burst like new wineskins.
Job32:20 I will speak
Job32:21 Let me not
Job32:22 For I do not know how to flatter
Job33:1 “But please
Job33:2 Now
Job33:3 My words come from my upright heart; My lips utter pure knowledge.
Job33:4 The Spirit of God has made me
Job33:5 If you can answer me
Job33:6 Truly I am as your spokesman before God; I also have been formed out of clay.
Job33:7 Surely no fear of me will terrify you
Job33:8 “Surely you have spoken in my hearing
Job33:9 ‘I am pure
Job33:10 Yet He finds occasions against me
Job33:11 He puts my feet in the stocks
Job33:12 “Look
Job33:13 Why do you contend with Him? For He does not give an accounting of any of His words.
Job33:14 For God may speak in one way
Job33:15 In a dream
Job33:16 Then He opens the ears of men
Job33:17 In order to turn man from his deed
Job33:18 He keeps back his soul from the Pit
Job33:19 “ Man is also chastened with pain on his bed
Job33:20 So that his life abhors bread
Job33:21 His flesh wastes away from sight
Job33:22 Yes
Job33:23 “If there is a messenger for him
Job33:24 Then He is gracious to him
Job33:25 His flesh shall be young like a child’s
Job33:26 He shall pray to God
Job33:27 Then he looks at men and says
Job33:28 He will redeem his soul from going down to the Pit
Job33:29 “Behold
Job33:30 To bring back his soul from the Pit
Job33:31 “Give ear

Job33:33 If not
Job34:1 Elihu further answered and said:
Job34:2 “Hear my words
Job34:3 For the ear tests words As the palate tastes food.
Job34:4 Let us choose justice for ourselves; Let us know among ourselves what is good.
Job34:5 “For Job has said
Job34:6 Should I lie concerning my right? My wound is incurable
Job34:7 What man is like Job
Job34:8 Who goes in company with the workers of iniquity
Job34:9 For he has said
Job34:10 “Therefore listen to me
Job34:11 For He repays man according to his work
Job34:12 Surely God will never do wickedly
Job34:13 Who gave Him charge over the earth? Or who appointed Him over the whole world?
Job34:14 If He should set His heart on it
Job34:15 All flesh would perish together
Job34:16 “If you have understanding
Job34:17 Should one who hates justice govern? Will you condemn Him who is most just?
Job34:18 Is it fitting to say to a king
Job34:19 Yet He is not partial to princes
Job34:20 In a moment they die
Job34:21 “For His eyes are on the ways of man
Job34:22 There is no darkness nor shadow of death Where the workers of iniquity may hide themselves.
Job34:23 For He need not further consider a man
Job34:24 He breaks in pieces mighty men without inquiry
Job34:25 Therefore He knows their works; He overthrows them in the night
Job34:26 He strikes them as wicked men In the open sight of others
Job34:27 Because they turned back from Him
Job34:28 So that they caused the cry of the poor to come to Him; For He hears the cry of the afflicted.
Job34:29 When He gives quietness
Job34:30 That the hypocrite should not reign
Job34:31 “For has anyone said to God
Job34:32 Teach me what I do not see; If I have done iniquity
Job34:33 Should He repay it according to your terms
Job34:34 “Men of understanding say to me
Job34:35 ‘Job speaks without knowledge
Job34:36 Oh
Job34:37 For he adds rebellion to his sin; He claps his hands among us
Job35:1 Moreover Elihu answered and said:
Job35:2 “Do you think this is right? Do you say
Job35:3 For you say
Job35:4 “I will answer you
Job35:5 Look to the heavens and see; And behold the clouds— They are higher than you.
Job35:6 If you sin
Job35:7 If you are righteous
Job35:8 Your wickedness affects a man such as you
Job35:9 “Because of the multitude of oppressions they cry out; They cry out for help because of the arm of the mighty.
Job35:10 But no one says
Job35:11 Who teaches us more than the beasts of the earth
Job35:12 There they cry out
Job35:13 Surely God will not listen to empty talk
Job35:14 Although you say you do not see Him
Job35:15 And now
Job35:16 Therefore Job opens his mouth in vain; He multiplies words without knowledge.”
Job36:1 Elihu also proceeded and said:
Job36:2 “Bear with me a little
Job36:3 I will fetch my knowledge from afar; I will ascribe righteousness to my Maker.
Job36:4 For truly my words are not false; One who is perfect in knowledge is with you.
Job36:5 “Behold
Job36:6 He does not preserve the life of the wicked
Job36:7 He does not withdraw His eyes from the righteous; But they are on the throne with kings
Job36:8 And if they are bound in fetters
Job36:9 Then He tells them their work and their transgressions— That they have acted defiantly.
Job36:10 He also opens their ear to instruction
Job36:11 If they obey and serve Him
Job36:12 But if they do not obey
Job36:13 “But the hypocrites in heart store up wrath; They do not cry for help when He binds them.
Job36:14 They die in youth
Job36:15 He delivers the poor in their affliction
Job36:16 “Indeed He would have brought you out of dire distress
Job36:17 But you are filled with the judgment due the wicked; Judgment and justice take hold of you.
Job36:18 Because there is wrath
Job36:19 Will your riches
Job36:20 Do not desire the night
Job36:21 Take heed
Job36:22 “Behold
Job36:23 Who has assigned Him His way
Job36:24 “Remember to magnify His work
Job36:25 Everyone has seen it; Man looks on it from afar.
Job36:26 “Behold
Job36:27 For He draws up drops of water
Job36:28 Which the clouds drop down And pour abundantly on man.
Job36:29 Indeed
Job36:30 Look
Job36:31 For by these He judges the peoples; He gives food in abundance.
Job36:32 He covers His hands with lightning
Job36:33 His thunder declares it
Job37:1 “At this also my heart trembles
Job37:2 Hear attentively the thunder of His voice
Job37:3 He sends it forth under the whole heaven
Job37:4 After it a voice roars; He thunders with His majestic voice
Job37:5 God thunders marvelously with His voice; He does great things which we cannot comprehend.
Job37:6 For He says to the snow
Job37:7 He seals the hand of every man
Job37:8 The beasts go into dens
Job37:9 From the chamber of the south comes the whirlwind
Job37:10 By the breath of God ice is given
Job37:11 Also with moisture He saturates the thick clouds; He scatters His bright clouds.
Job37:12 And they swirl about
Job37:13 He causes it to come
Job37:14 “Listen to this
Job37:15 Do you know when God dispatches them
Job37:16 Do you know how the clouds are balanced
Job37:17 Why are your garments hot
Job37:18 With Him
Job37:19 “Teach us what we should say to Him
Job37:20 Should He be told that I wish to speak? If a man were to speak
Job37:21 Even now men cannot look at the light when it is bright in the skies
Job37:22 He comes from the north as golden splendor; With God is awesome majesty.
Job37:23 As for the Almighty
Job37:24 Therefore men fear Him; He shows no partiality to any who are wise of heart.”
Job38:1 Then the Lord answered Job out of the whirlwind
Job38:2 “Who is this who darkens counsel By words without knowledge?
Job38:3 Now prepare yourself like a man; I will question you
Job38:4 “Where were you when I laid the foundations of the earth? Tell Me
Job38:5 Who determined its measurements? Surely you know! Or who stretched the line upon it?
Job38:6 To what were its foundations fastened? Or who laid its cornerstone
Job38:7 When the morning stars sang together
Job38:8 “Or who shut in the sea with doors
Job38:9 When I made the clouds its garment
Job38:10 When I fixed My limit for it
Job38:11 When I said
Job38:12 “Have you commanded the morning since your days began
Job38:13 That it might take hold of the ends of the earth
Job38:14 It takes on form like clay under a seal
Job38:15 From the wicked their light is withheld
Job38:16 “Have you entered the springs of the sea? Or have you walked in search of the depths?
Job38:17 Have the gates of death been revealed to you? Or have you seen the doors of the shadow of death?
Job38:18 Have you comprehended the breadth of the earth? Tell Me
Job38:19 “Where is the way to the dwelling of light? And darkness
Job38:20 That you may take it to its territory
Job38:21 Do you know it
Job38:22 “Have you entered the treasury of snow
Job38:23 Which I have reserved for the time of trouble
Job38:24 By what way is light diffused
Job38:25 “Who has divided a channel for the overflowing water
Job38:26 To cause it to rain on a land where there is no one
Job38:27 To satisfy the desolate waste
Job38:28 Has the rain a father? Or who has begotten the drops of dew?
Job38:29 From whose womb comes the ice? And the frost of heaven
Job38:30 The waters harden like stone
Job38:31 “Can you bind the cluster of the Pleiades
Job38:32 Can you bring out Mazzaroth in its season? Or can you guide the Great Bear with its cubs?
Job38:33 Do you know the ordinances of the heavens? Can you set their dominion over the earth?
Job38:34 “Can you lift up your voice to the clouds
Job38:35 Can you send out lightnings
Job38:36 Who has put wisdom in the mind? Or who has given understanding to the heart?
Job38:37 Who can number the clouds by wisdom? Or who can pour out the bottles of heaven
Job38:38 When the dust hardens in clumps
Job38:39 “Can you hunt the prey for the lion
Job38:40 When they crouch in their dens
Job38:41 Who provides food for the raven
Job39:1 “Do you know the time when the wild mountain goats bear young? Or can you mark when the deer gives birth?
Job39:2 Can you number the months that they fulfill? Or do you know the time when they bear young?
Job39:3 They bow down
Job39:4 Their young ones are healthy
Job39:5 “Who set the wild donkey free? Who loosed the bonds of the onager
Job39:6 Whose home I have made the wilderness
Job39:7 He scorns the tumult of the city; He does not heed the shouts of the driver.
Job39:8 The range of the mountains is his pasture
Job39:9 “Will the wild ox be willing to serve you? Will he bed by your manger?
Job39:10 Can you bind the wild ox in the furrow with ropes? Or will he plow the valleys behind you?
Job39:11 Will you trust him because his strength is great? Or will you leave your labor to him?
Job39:12 Will you trust him to bring home your grain
Job39:13 “The wings of the ostrich wave proudly
Job39:14 For she leaves her eggs on the ground
Job39:15 She forgets that a foot may crush them
Job39:16 She treats her young harshly
Job39:17 Because God deprived her of wisdom
Job39:18 When she lifts herself on high
Job39:19 “Have you given the horse strength? Have you clothed his neck with thunder?
Job39:20 Can you frighten him like a locust? His majestic snorting strikes terror.
Job39:21 He paws in the valley
Job39:22 He mocks at fear
Job39:23 The quiver rattles against him
Job39:24 He devours the distance with fierceness and rage; Nor does he come to a halt because the trumpet has sounded.
Job39:25 At the blast of the trumpet he says
Job39:26 “Does the hawk fly by your wisdom
Job39:27 Does the eagle mount up at your command
Job39:28 On the rock it dwells and resides
Job39:29 From there it spies out the prey; Its eyes observe from afar.
Job39:30 Its young ones suck up blood; And where the slain are
Job40:1 Moreover the Lord answered Job
Job40:2 “Shall the one who contends with the Almighty correct Him? He who rebukes God
Job40:3 Then Job answered the Lord and said:
Job40:4 “Behold
Job40:5 Once I have spoken
Job40:6 Then the Lord answered Job out of the whirlwind
Job40:7 “Now prepare yourself like a man; I will question you
Job40:8 “Would you indeed annul My judgment? Would you condemn Me that you may be justified?
Job40:9 Have you an arm like God? Or can you thunder with a voice like His?
Job40:10 Then adorn yourself with majesty and splendor
Job40:11 Disperse the rage of your wrath; Look on everyone who is proud
Job40:12 Look on everyone who is proud
Job40:13 Hide them in the dust together
Job40:14 Then I will also confess to you That your own right hand can save you.
Job40:15 “Look now at the behemoth
Job40:16 See now
Job40:17 He moves his tail like a cedar; The sinews of his thighs are tightly knit.
Job40:18 His bones are like beams of bronze
Job40:19 He is the first of the ways of God; Only He who made him can bring near His sword.
Job40:20 Surely the mountains yield food for him
Job40:21 He lies under the lotus trees
Job40:22 The lotus trees cover him with their shade; The willows by the brook surround him.
Job40:23 Indeed the river may rage
Job40:24 Though he takes it in his eyes
Job41:1 “Can you draw out Leviathan with a hook
Job41:2 Can you put a reed through his nose
Job41:3 Will he make many supplications to you? Will he speak softly to you?
Job41:4 Will he make a covenant with you? Will you take him as a servant forever?
Job41:5 Will you play with him as with a bird
Job41:6 Will your companions make a banquet of him? Will they apportion him among the merchants?
Job41:7 Can you fill his skin with harpoons
Job41:8 Lay your hand on him; Remember the battle— Never do it again!
Job41:9 Indeed
Job41:10 No one is so fierce that he would dare stir him up. Who then is able to stand against Me?
Job41:11 Who has preceded Me
Job41:12 “I will not conceal his limbs
Job41:13 Who can remove his outer coat? Who can approach him with a double bridle?
Job41:14 Who can open the doors of his face
Job41:15 His rows of scales are his pride
Job41:16 One is so near another That no air can come between them;
Job41:17 They are joined one to another
Job41:18 His sneezings flash forth light
Job41:19 Out of his mouth go burning lights; Sparks of fire shoot out.
Job41:20 Smoke goes out of his nostrils
Job41:21 His breath kindles coals
Job41:22 Strength dwells in his neck
Job41:23 The folds of his flesh are joined together; They are firm on him and cannot be moved.
Job41:24 His heart is as hard as stone
Job41:25 When he raises himself up
Job41:26 Though the sword reaches him
Job41:27 He regards iron as straw
Job41:28 The arrow cannot make him flee; Slingstones become like stubble to him.
Job41:29 Darts are regarded as straw; He laughs at the threat of javelins.
Job41:30 His undersides are like sharp potsherds; He spreads pointed marks in the mire.
Job41:31 He makes the deep boil like a pot; He makes the sea like a pot of ointment.
Job41:32 He leaves a shining wake behind him; One would think the deep had white hair.
Job41:33 On earth there is nothing like him
Job41:34 He beholds every high thing; He is king over all the children of pride.”
Job42:1 Then Job answered the Lord and said:
Job42:2 “I know that You can do everything
Job42:3 You asked
Job42:4 Listen
Job42:5 “I have heard of You by the hearing of the ear
Job42:6 Therefore I abhor myself
Job42:7 And so it was
Job42:8 Now therefore
Job42:9 So Eliphaz the Temanite and Bildad the Shuhite and Zophar the Naamathite went and did as the Lord commanded them; for the Lord had accepted Job.
Job42:10 And the Lord restored Job’s losses when he prayed for his friends. Indeed the Lord gave Job twice as much as he had before.
Job42:11 Then all his brothers
Job42:12 Now the Lord blessed the latter days of Job more than his beginning; for he had fourteen thousand sheep
Job42:13 He also had seven sons and three daughters.
Job42:14 And he called the name of the first Jemimah
Job42:15 In all the land were found no women so beautiful as the daughters of Job; and their father gave them an inheritance among their brothers.
Job42:16 After this Job lived one hundred and forty years
Job42:17 So Job died

Psalms1:2 But his delight is in the law of the Lord

Psalms1:4 The ungodly are not so
Psalms1:5 Therefore the ungodly shall not stand in the judgment
Psalms1:6 For the Lord knows the way of the righteous

Psalms3:1 Lord

Psalms3:6 I will not be afraid of ten thousands of people Who have set themselves against me all around.

Psalms3:8 Salvation belongs to the Lord. Your blessing is upon Your people.

Psalms4:2 How long

Psalms4:5 Offer the sacrifices of righteousness
Psalms4:6 There are many who say

Psalms5:2 Give heed to the voice of my cry
Psalms5:3 My voice You shall hear in the morning
Psalms5:4 For You are not a God who takes pleasure in wickedness
Psalms5:5 The boastful shall not stand in Your sight; You hate all workers of iniquity.
Psalms5:6 You shall destroy those who speak falsehood; The Lord abhors the bloodthirsty and deceitful man.
Psalms5:7 But as for me
Psalms5:8 Lead me
Psalms5:9 For there is no faithfulness in their mouth; Their inward part is destruction; Their throat is an open tomb; They flatter with their tongue.

Psalms5:11 But let all those rejoice who put their trust in You; Let them ever shout for joy
Psalms5:12 For You
Psalms6:1 O Lord
Psalms6:2 Have mercy on me
Psalms6:3 My soul also is greatly troubled; But You
Psalms6:4 Return
Psalms6:5 For in death there is no remembrance of You; In the grave who will give You thanks?
Psalms6:6 I am weary with my groaning; All night I make my bed swim; I drench my couch with my tears.
Psalms6:7 My eye wastes away because of grief; It grows old because of all my enemies.
Psalms6:8 Depart from me
Psalms6:9 The Lord has heard my supplication; The Lord will receive my prayer.
Psalms6:10 Let all my enemies be ashamed and greatly troubled; Let them turn back and be ashamed suddenly.

Psalms7:2 Lest they tear me like a lion

Psalms10:4 The wicked in his proud countenance does not seek God; God is in none of his thoughts.
Psalms10:5 His ways are always prospering; Your judgments are far above
Psalms10:6 He has said in his heart

Psalms10:10 So he crouches

Psalms18:1 I will love You
Psalms18:2 The Lord is my rock and my fortress and my deliverer; My God
Psalms18:3 I will call upon the Lord
Psalms18:4 The pangs of death surrounded me
Psalms18:5 The sorrows of Sheol surrounded me; The snares of death confronted me.
Psalms18:6 In my distress I called upon the Lord
Psalms18:7 Then the earth shook and trembled; The foundations of the hills also quaked and were shaken
Psalms18:8 Smoke went up from His nostrils
Psalms18:9 He bowed the heavens also
Psalms18:10 And He rode upon a cherub
Psalms18:11 He made darkness His secret place; His canopy around Him was dark waters And thick clouds of the skies.
Psalms18:12 From the brightness before Him
Psalms18:13 The Lord thundered from heaven
Psalms18:14 He sent out His arrows and scattered the foe
Psalms18:15 Then the channels of the sea were seen
Psalms18:16 He sent from above
Psalms18:17 He delivered me from my strong enemy
Psalms18:18 They confronted me in the day of my calamity
Psalms18:19 He also brought me out into a broad place; He delivered me because He delighted in me.
Psalms18:20 The Lord rewarded me according to my righteousness; According to the cleanness of my hands He has recompensed me.
Psalms18:21 For I have kept the ways of the Lord
Psalms18:22 For all His judgments were before me
Psalms18:23 I was also blameless before Him
Psalms18:24 Therefore the Lord has recompensed me according to my righteousness
Psalms18:25 With the merciful You will show Yourself merciful; With a blameless man You will show Yourself blameless;
Psalms18:26 With the pure You will show Yourself pure; And with the devious You will show Yourself shrewd.
Psalms18:27 For You will save the humble people
Psalms18:28 For You will light my lamp; The Lord my God will enlighten my darkness.
Psalms18:29 For by You I can run against a troop
Psalms18:30 As for God
Psalms18:31 For who is God
Psalms18:32 It is God who arms me with strength
Psalms18:33 He makes my feet like the feet of deer
Psalms18:34 He teaches my hands to make war
Psalms18:35 You have also given me the shield of Your salvation; Your right hand has held me up
Psalms18:36 You enlarged my path under me
Psalms18:37 I have pursued my enemies and overtaken them; Neither did I turn back again till they were destroyed.
Psalms18:38 I have wounded them
Psalms18:39 For You have armed me with strength for the battle; You have subdued under me those who rose up against me.
Psalms18:40 You have also given me the necks of my enemies
Psalms18:41 They cried out
Psalms18:42 Then I beat them as fine as the dust before the wind; I cast them out like dirt in the streets.
Psalms18:43 You have delivered me from the strivings of the people; You have made me the head of the nations; A people I have not known shall serve me.
Psalms18:44 As soon as they hear of me they obey me; The foreigners submit to me.
Psalms18:45 The foreigners fade away
Psalms18:46 The Lord lives! Blessed be my Rock! Let the God of my salvation be exalted.
Psalms18:47 It is God who avenges me
Psalms18:48 He delivers me from my enemies. You also lift me up above those who rise against me; You have delivered me from the violent man.
Psalms18:49 Therefore I will give thanks to You
Psalms18:50 Great deliverance He gives to His king

Psalms22:2 O My God
Psalms22:3 But You are holy
Psalms22:4 Our fathers trusted in You; They trusted
Psalms22:5 They cried to You
Psalms22:6 But I am a worm

Psalms22:9 But You are He who took Me out of the womb; You made Me trust while on My mother’s breasts.

Psalms22:12 Many bulls have surrounded Me; Strong bulls of Bashan have encircled Me.

Psalms22:19 But You
Psalms22:20 Deliver Me from the sword

Psalms22:26 The poor shall eat and be satisfied; Those who seek Him will praise the Lord. Let your heart live forever!

Psalms22:28 For the kingdom is the Lord’s

Psalms23:5 You prepare a table before me in the presence of my enemies; You anoint my head with oil; My cup runs over.

Psalms77:4 You hold my eyelids open; I am so troubled that I cannot speak.
Psalms77:5 I have considered the days of old

Psalms78:1 Give ear
Psalms78:2 I will open my mouth in a parable; I will utter dark sayings of old

Psalms78:5 For He established a testimony in Jacob
Psalms78:6 That the generation to come might know them
Psalms78:7 That they may set their hope in God
Psalms78:8 And may not be like their fathers
Psalms78:9 The children of Ephraim
Psalms78:10 They did not keep the covenant of God; They refused to walk in His law
Psalms78:11 And forgot His works And His wonders that He had shown them.
Psalms78:12 Marvelous things He did in the sight of their fathers
Psalms78:13 He divided the sea and caused them to pass through; And He made the waters stand up like a heap.
Psalms78:14 In the daytime also He led them with the cloud
Psalms78:15 He split the rocks in the wilderness
Psalms78:16 He also brought streams out of the rock
Psalms78:17 But they sinned even more against Him By rebelling against the Most High in the wilderness.
Psalms78:18 And they tested God in their heart By asking for the food of their fancy.
Psalms78:19 Yes
Psalms78:20 Behold
Psalms78:21 Therefore the Lord heard this and was furious; So a fire was kindled against Jacob
Psalms78:22 Because they did not believe in God
Psalms78:23 Yet He had commanded the clouds above
Psalms78:24 Had rained down manna on them to eat
Psalms78:25 Men ate angels’ food; He sent them food to the full.
Psalms78:26 He caused an east wind to blow in the heavens; And by His power He brought in the south wind.
Psalms78:27 He also rained meat on them like the dust
Psalms78:28 And He let them fall in the midst of their camp
Psalms78:29 So they ate and were well filled
Psalms78:30 They were not deprived of their craving; But while their food was still in their mouths
Psalms78:31 The wrath of God came against them
Psalms78:32 In spite of this they still sinned
Psalms78:33 Therefore their days He consumed in futility
Psalms78:34 When He slew them
Psalms78:35 Then they remembered that God was their rock
Psalms78:36 Nevertheless they flattered Him with their mouth
Psalms78:37 For their heart was not steadfast with Him
Psalms78:38 But He

Psalms78:40 How often they provoked Him in the wilderness
Psalms78:41 Yes
Psalms78:42 They did not remember His power: The day when He redeemed them from the enemy
Psalms78:43 When He worked His signs in Egypt
Psalms78:44 Turned their rivers into blood
Psalms78:45 He sent swarms of flies among them
Psalms78:46 He also gave their crops to the caterpillar
Psalms78:47 He destroyed their vines with hail
Psalms78:48 He also gave up their cattle to the hail
Psalms78:49 He cast on them the fierceness of His anger
Psalms78:50 He made a path for His anger; He did not spare their soul from death
Psalms78:51 And destroyed all the firstborn in Egypt
Psalms78:52 But He made His own people go forth like sheep
Psalms78:53 And He led them on safely
Psalms78:54 And He brought them to His holy border
Psalms78:55 He also drove out the nations before them
Psalms78:56 Yet they tested and provoked the Most High God
Psalms78:57 But turned back and acted unfaithfully like their fathers; They were turned aside like a deceitful bow.
Psalms78:58 For they provoked Him to anger with their high places
Psalms78:59 When God heard this
Psalms78:60 So that He forsook the tabernacle of Shiloh
Psalms78:61 And delivered His strength into captivity
Psalms78:62 He also gave His people over to the sword
Psalms78:63 The fire consumed their young men
Psalms78:64 Their priests fell by the sword
Psalms78:65 Then the Lord awoke as from sleep
Psalms78:66 And He beat back His enemies; He put them to a perpetual reproach.

Psalms78:68 But chose the tribe of Judah
Psalms78:69 And He built His sanctuary like the heights

Psalms78:71 From following the ewes that had young He brought him
Psalms78:72 So he shepherded them according to the integrity of his heart

Psalms105:15 Saying
