Psalms106:1 Praise the Lord! Oh
Psalms106:2 Who can utter the mighty acts of the Lord? Who can declare all His praise?
Psalms106:3 Blessed are those who keep justice
Psalms106:4 Remember me
Psalms106:5 That I may see the benefit of Your chosen ones
Psalms106:6 We have sinned with our fathers
Psalms106:7 Our fathers in Egypt did not understand Your wonders; They did not remember the multitude of Your mercies
Psalms106:8 Nevertheless He saved them for His name’s sake
Psalms106:9 He rebuked the Red Sea also
Psalms106:10 He saved them from the hand of him who hated them
Psalms106:11 The waters covered their enemies; There was not one of them left.
Psalms106:12 Then they believed His words; They sang His praise.
Psalms106:13 They soon forgot His works; They did not wait for His counsel
Psalms106:14 But lusted exceedingly in the wilderness
Psalms106:15 And He gave them their request
Psalms106:16 When they envied Moses in the camp
Psalms106:17 The earth opened up and swallowed Dathan
Psalms106:18 A fire was kindled in their company; The flame burned up the wicked.
Psalms106:19 They made a calf in Horeb
Psalms106:20 Thus they changed their glory Into the image of an ox that eats grass.
Psalms106:21 They forgot God their Savior
Psalms106:22 Wondrous works in the land of Ham
Psalms106:23 Therefore He said that He would destroy them
Psalms106:24 Then they despised the pleasant land; They did not believe His word
Psalms106:25 But complained in their tents
Psalms106:26 Therefore He raised His hand in an oath against them
Psalms106:27 To overthrow their descendants among the nations
Psalms106:28 They joined themselves also to Baal of Peor
Psalms106:29 Thus they provoked Him to anger with their deeds
Psalms106:30 Then Phinehas stood up and intervened
Psalms106:31 And that was accounted to him for righteousness To all generations forevermore.
Psalms106:32 They angered Him also at the waters of strife
Psalms106:33 Because they rebelled against His Spirit
Psalms106:34 They did not destroy the peoples
Psalms106:35 But they mingled with the Gentiles And learned their works;
Psalms106:36 They served their idols
Psalms106:37 They even sacrificed their sons And their daughters to demons
Psalms106:38 And shed innocent blood
Psalms106:39 Thus they were defiled by their own works
Psalms106:40 Therefore the wrath of the Lord was kindled against His people
Psalms106:41 And He gave them into the hand of the Gentiles
Psalms106:42 Their enemies also oppressed them
Psalms106:43 Many times He delivered them; But they rebelled in their counsel
Psalms106:44 Nevertheless He regarded their affliction
Psalms106:45 And for their sake He remembered His covenant
Psalms106:46 He also made them to be pitied By all those who carried them away captive.

Proverbs1:1 The proverbs of Solomon the son of David
Proverbs1:2 To know wisdom and instruction
Proverbs1:3 To receive the instruction of wisdom
Proverbs1:4 To give prudence to the simple
Proverbs1:5 A wise man will hear and increase learning
Proverbs1:6 To understand a proverb and an enigma
Proverbs1:7 The fear of the Lord is the beginning of knowledge
Proverbs1:8 My son
Proverbs1:9 For they will be a graceful ornament on your head
Proverbs1:10 My son
Proverbs1:11 If they say
Proverbs1:12 Let us swallow them alive like Sheol
Proverbs1:13 We shall find all kinds of precious possessions
Proverbs1:14 Cast in your lot among us
Proverbs1:15 My son
Proverbs1:16 For their feet run to evil
Proverbs1:17 Surely
Proverbs1:18 But they lie in wait for their own blood
Proverbs1:19 So are the ways of everyone who is greedy for gain; It takes away the life of its owners.
Proverbs1:20 Wisdom calls aloud outside; She raises her voice in the open squares.
Proverbs1:21 She cries out in the chief concourses
Proverbs1:22 “How long
Proverbs1:23 Turn at my rebuke; Surely I will pour out my spirit on you; I will make my words known to you.
Proverbs1:24 Because I have called and you refused
Proverbs1:25 Because you disdained all my counsel
Proverbs1:26 I also will laugh at your calamity; I will mock when your terror comes
Proverbs1:27 When your terror comes like a storm
Proverbs1:28 “Then they will call on me
Proverbs1:29 Because they hated knowledge And did not choose the fear of the Lord
Proverbs1:30 They would have none of my counsel And despised my every rebuke.
Proverbs1:31 Therefore they shall eat the fruit of their own way
Proverbs1:32 For the turning away of the simple will slay them
Proverbs1:33 But whoever listens to me will dwell safely
Proverbs2:1 My son
Proverbs2:2 So that you incline your ear to wisdom
Proverbs2:3 Yes
Proverbs2:4 If you seek her as silver
Proverbs2:5 Then you will understand the fear of the Lord
Proverbs2:6 For the Lord gives wisdom; From His mouth come knowledge and understanding;
Proverbs2:7 He stores up sound wisdom for the upright; He is a shield to those who walk uprightly;
Proverbs2:8 He guards the paths of justice
Proverbs2:9 Then you will understand righteousness and justice
Proverbs2:10 When wisdom enters your heart
Proverbs2:11 Discretion will preserve you; Understanding will keep you
Proverbs2:12 To deliver you from the way of evil
Proverbs2:13 From those who leave the paths of uprightness To walk in the ways of darkness;
Proverbs2:14 Who rejoice in doing evil
Proverbs2:15 Whose ways are crooked
Proverbs2:16 To deliver you from the immoral woman
Proverbs2:17 Who forsakes the companion of her youth
Proverbs2:18 For her house leads down to death
Proverbs2:19 None who go to her return
Proverbs2:20 So you may walk in the way of goodness
Proverbs2:21 For the upright will dwell in the land
Proverbs2:22 But the wicked will be cut off from the earth
Proverbs3:1 My son
Proverbs3:2 For length of days and long life And peace they will add to you.
Proverbs3:3 Let not mercy and truth forsake you; Bind them around your neck
Proverbs3:4 And so find favor and high esteem In the sight of God and man.
Proverbs3:5 Trust in the Lord with all your heart
Proverbs3:6 In all your ways acknowledge Him
Proverbs3:7 Do not be wise in your own eyes; Fear the Lord and depart from evil.
Proverbs3:8 It will be health to your flesh
Proverbs3:9 Honor the Lord with your possessions
Proverbs3:10 So your barns will be filled with plenty
Proverbs3:11 My son
Proverbs3:12 For whom the Lord loves He corrects
Proverbs3:13 Happy is the man who finds wisdom
Proverbs3:14 For her proceeds are better than the profits of silver
Proverbs3:15 She is more precious than rubies
Proverbs3:16 Length of days is in her right hand
Proverbs3:17 Her ways are ways of pleasantness
Proverbs3:18 She is a tree of life to those who take hold of her
Proverbs3:19 The Lord by wisdom founded the earth; By understanding He established the heavens;
Proverbs3:20 By His knowledge the depths were broken up
Proverbs3:21 My son
Proverbs3:22 So they will be life to your soul And grace to your neck.
Proverbs3:23 Then you will walk safely in your way
Proverbs3:24 When you lie down
Proverbs3:25 Do not be afraid of sudden terror
Proverbs3:26 For the Lord will be your confidence
Proverbs3:27 Do not withhold good from those to whom it is due
Proverbs3:28 Do not say to your neighbor
Proverbs3:29 Do not devise evil against your neighbor
Proverbs3:30 Do not strive with a man without cause
Proverbs3:31 Do not envy the oppressor
Proverbs3:32 For the perverse person is an abomination to the Lord
Proverbs3:33 The curse of the Lord is on the house of the wicked
Proverbs3:34 Surely He scorns the scornful
Proverbs3:35 The wise shall inherit glory
Proverbs4:1 Hear
Proverbs4:2 For I give you good doctrine: Do not forsake my law.
Proverbs4:3 When I was my father’s son
Proverbs4:4 He also taught me
Proverbs4:5 Get wisdom! Get understanding! Do not forget
Proverbs4:6 Do not forsake her
Proverbs4:7 Wisdom is the principal thing; Therefore get wisdom. And in all your getting
Proverbs4:8 Exalt her
Proverbs4:9 She will place on your head an ornament of grace; A crown of glory she will deliver to you.”
Proverbs4:10 Hear
Proverbs4:11 I have taught you in the way of wisdom; I have led you in right paths.
Proverbs4:12 When you walk
Proverbs4:13 Take firm hold of instruction
Proverbs4:14 Do not enter the path of the wicked
Proverbs4:15 Avoid it
Proverbs4:16 For they do not sleep unless they have done evil; And their sleep is taken away unless they make someone fall.
Proverbs4:17 For they eat the bread of wickedness
Proverbs4:18 But the path of the just is like the shining sun
Proverbs4:19 The way of the wicked is like darkness; They do not know what makes them stumble.
Proverbs4:20 My son
Proverbs4:21 Do not let them depart from your eyes; Keep them in the midst of your heart;
Proverbs4:22 For they are life to those who find them
Proverbs4:23 Keep your heart with all diligence
Proverbs4:24 Put away from you a deceitful mouth
Proverbs4:25 Let your eyes look straight ahead
Proverbs4:26 Ponder the path of your feet
Proverbs4:27 Do not turn to the right or the left; Remove your foot from evil.
Proverbs5:1 My son
Proverbs5:2 That you may preserve discretion
Proverbs5:3 For the lips of an immoral woman drip honey
Proverbs5:4 But in the end she is bitter as wormwood
Proverbs5:5 Her feet go down to death
Proverbs5:6 Lest you ponder her path of life— Her ways are unstable; You do not know them.
Proverbs5:7 Therefore hear me now
Proverbs5:8 Remove your way far from her
Proverbs5:9 Lest you give your honor to others
Proverbs5:10 Lest aliens be filled with your wealth
Proverbs5:11 And you mourn at last
Proverbs5:12 And say: “How I have hated instruction
Proverbs5:13 I have not obeyed the voice of my teachers
Proverbs5:14 I was on the verge of total ruin
Proverbs5:15 Drink water from your own cistern
Proverbs5:16 Should your fountains be dispersed abroad
Proverbs5:17 Let them be only your own
Proverbs5:18 Let your fountain be blessed
Proverbs5:19 As a loving deer and a graceful doe
Proverbs5:20 For why should you
Proverbs5:21 For the ways of man are before the eyes of the Lord
Proverbs5:22 His own iniquities entrap the wicked man
Proverbs5:23 He shall die for lack of instruction
Proverbs6:1 My son
Proverbs6:2 You are snared by the words of your mouth; You are taken by the words of your mouth.
Proverbs6:3 So do this
Proverbs6:4 Give no sleep to your eyes
Proverbs6:5 Deliver yourself like a gazelle from the hand of the hunter
Proverbs6:6 Go to the ant
Proverbs6:7 Which
Proverbs6:8 Provides her supplies in the summer
Proverbs6:9 How long will you slumber
Proverbs6:10 A little sleep
Proverbs6:11 So shall your poverty come on you like a prowler
Proverbs6:12 A worthless person
Proverbs6:13 He winks with his eyes
Proverbs6:14 Perversity is in his heart
Proverbs6:15 Therefore his calamity shall come suddenly; Suddenly he shall be broken without remedy.
Proverbs6:16 These six things the Lord hates
Proverbs6:17 A proud look
Proverbs6:18 A heart that devises wicked plans
Proverbs6:19 A false witness who speaks lies
Proverbs6:20 My son
Proverbs6:21 Bind them continually upon your heart; Tie them around your neck.
Proverbs6:22 When you roam
Proverbs6:23 For the commandment is a lamp
Proverbs6:24 To keep you from the evil woman
Proverbs6:25 Do not lust after her beauty in your heart
Proverbs6:26 For by means of a harlot A man is reduced to a crust of bread; And an adulteress will prey upon his precious life.
Proverbs6:27 Can a man take fire to his bosom
Proverbs6:28 Can one walk on hot coals
Proverbs6:29 So is he who goes in to his neighbor’s wife; Whoever touches her shall not be innocent.
Proverbs6:30 People do not despise a thief If he steals to satisfy himself when he is starving.
Proverbs6:31 Yet when he is found
Proverbs6:32 Whoever commits adultery with a woman lacks understanding; He who does so destroys his own soul.
Proverbs6:33 Wounds and dishonor he will get
Proverbs6:34 For jealousy is a husband’s fury; Therefore he will not spare in the day of vengeance.
Proverbs6:35 He will accept no recompense
Proverbs7:1 My son
Proverbs7:2 Keep my commands and live
Proverbs7:3 Bind them on your fingers; Write them on the tablet of your heart.
Proverbs7:4 Say to wisdom
Proverbs7:5 That they may keep you from the immoral woman
Proverbs7:6 For at the window of my house I looked through my lattice
Proverbs7:7 And saw among the simple
Proverbs7:8 Passing along the street near her corner; And he took the path to her house
Proverbs7:9 In the twilight
Proverbs7:10 And there a woman met him
Proverbs7:11 She was loud and rebellious
Proverbs7:12 At times she was outside
Proverbs7:13 So she caught him and kissed him; With an impudent face she said to him:
Proverbs7:14 “ I have peace offerings with me; Today I have paid my vows.
Proverbs7:15 So I came out to meet you
Proverbs7:16 I have spread my bed with tapestry
Proverbs7:17 I have perfumed my bed With myrrh
Proverbs7:18 Come
Proverbs7:19 For my husband is not at home; He has gone on a long journey;
Proverbs7:20 He has taken a bag of money with him
Proverbs7:21 With her enticing speech she caused him to yield
Proverbs7:22 Immediately he went after her
Proverbs7:23 Till an arrow struck his liver. As a bird hastens to the snare
Proverbs7:24 Now therefore
Proverbs7:25 Do not let your heart turn aside to her ways
Proverbs7:26 For she has cast down many wounded
Proverbs7:27 Her house is the way to hell
Proverbs8:1 Does not wisdom cry out
Proverbs8:2 She takes her stand on the top of the high hill
Proverbs8:3 She cries out by the gates
Proverbs8:4 “To you
Proverbs8:5 O you simple ones
Proverbs8:6 Listen
Proverbs8:7 For my mouth will speak truth; Wickedness is an abomination to my lips.
Proverbs8:8 All the words of my mouth are with righteousness; Nothing crooked or perverse is in them.
Proverbs8:9 They are all plain to him who understands
Proverbs8:10 Receive my instruction
Proverbs8:11 For wisdom is better than rubies
Proverbs8:12 “I
Proverbs8:13 The fear of the Lord is to hate evil; Pride and arrogance and the evil way And the perverse mouth I hate.
Proverbs8:14 Counsel is mine
Proverbs8:15 By me kings reign
Proverbs8:16 By me princes rule
Proverbs8:17 I love those who love me
Proverbs8:18 Riches and honor are with me
Proverbs8:19 My fruit is better than gold
Proverbs8:20 I traverse the way of righteousness
Proverbs8:21 That I may cause those who love me to inherit wealth
Proverbs8:22 “The Lord possessed me at the beginning of His way
Proverbs8:23 I have been established from everlasting
Proverbs8:24 When there were no depths I was brought forth
Proverbs8:25 Before the mountains were settled
Proverbs8:26 While as yet He had not made the earth or the fields
Proverbs8:27 When He prepared the heavens
Proverbs8:28 When He established the clouds above
Proverbs8:29 When He assigned to the sea its limit
Proverbs8:30 Then I was beside Him as a master craftsman; And I was daily His delight
Proverbs8:31 Rejoicing in His inhabited world
Proverbs8:32 “Now therefore
Proverbs8:33 Hear instruction and be wise
Proverbs8:34 Blessed is the man who listens to me
Proverbs8:35 For whoever finds me finds life
Proverbs8:36 But he who sins against me wrongs his own soul; All those who hate me love death.”

Proverbs25:1 These also are proverbs of Solomon which the men of Hezekiah king of Judah copied:
Proverbs25:2 It is the glory of God to conceal a matter
Proverbs25:3 As the heavens for height and the earth for depth
Proverbs25:4 Take away the dross from silver
Proverbs25:5 Take away the wicked from before the king
Proverbs25:6 Do not exalt yourself in the presence of the king
Proverbs25:7 For it is better that he say to you
Proverbs25:8 Do not go hastily to court; For what will you do in the end
Proverbs25:9 Debate your case with your neighbor
Proverbs25:10 Lest he who hears it expose your shame
Proverbs25:11 A word fitly spoken is like apples of gold In settings of silver.
Proverbs25:12 Like an earring of gold and an ornament of fine gold Is a wise rebuker to an obedient ear.
Proverbs25:13 Like the cold of snow in time of harvest Is a faithful messenger to those who send him
Proverbs25:14 Whoever falsely boasts of giving Is like clouds and wind without rain.
Proverbs25:15 By long forbearance a ruler is persuaded
Proverbs25:16 Have you found honey? Eat only as much as you need
Proverbs25:17 Seldom set foot in your neighbor’s house
Proverbs25:18 A man who bears false witness against his neighbor Is like a club
Proverbs25:19 Confidence in an unfaithful man in time of trouble Is like a bad tooth and a foot out of joint.
Proverbs25:20 Like one who takes away a garment in cold weather
Proverbs25:21 If your enemy is hungry
Proverbs25:22 For so you will heap coals of fire on his head
Proverbs25:23 The north wind brings forth rain
Proverbs25:24 It is better to dwell in a corner of a housetop
Proverbs25:25 As cold water to a weary soul
Proverbs25:26 A righteous man who falters before the wicked Is like a murky spring and a polluted well.
Proverbs25:27 It is not good to eat much honey; So to seek one’s own glory is not glory.
Proverbs25:28 Whoever has no rule over his own spirit Is like a city broken down

Proverbs30:1 The words of Agur the son of Jakeh
Proverbs30:2 Surely I am more stupid than any man
Proverbs30:3 I neither learned wisdom Nor have knowledge of the Holy One.
Proverbs30:4 Who has ascended into heaven
Proverbs30:5 Every word of God is pure; He is a shield to those who put their trust in Him.
Proverbs30:6 Do not add to His words
Proverbs30:7 Two things I request of You (Deprive me not before I die):
Proverbs30:8 Remove falsehood and lies far from me; Give me neither poverty nor riches— Feed me with the food allotted to me;
Proverbs30:9 Lest I be full and deny You
Proverbs30:10 Do not malign a servant to his master
Proverbs30:11 There is a generation that curses its father

Proverbs31:10 Who can find a virtuous wife? For her worth is far above rubies.
Proverbs31:11 The heart of her husband safely trusts her; So he will have no lack of gain.
Proverbs31:12 She does him good and not evil All the days of her life.
Proverbs31:13 She seeks wool and flax
Proverbs31:14 She is like the merchant ships
Proverbs31:15 She also rises while it is yet night
Proverbs31:16 She considers a field and buys it; From her profits she plants a vineyard.
Proverbs31:17 She girds herself with strength
Proverbs31:18 She perceives that her merchandise is good
Proverbs31:19 She stretches out her hands to the distaff
Proverbs31:20 She extends her hand to the poor
Proverbs31:21 She is not afraid of snow for her household
Proverbs31:22 She makes tapestry for herself; Her clothing is fine linen and purple.
Proverbs31:23 Her husband is known in the gates
Proverbs31:24 She makes linen garments and sells them
Proverbs31:25 Strength and honor are her clothing; She shall rejoice in time to come.
Proverbs31:26 She opens her mouth with wisdom
Proverbs31:27 She watches over the ways of her household
Proverbs31:28 Her children rise up and call her blessed; Her husband also
Proverbs31:29 “Many daughters have done well
Proverbs31:30 Charm is deceitful and beauty is passing
Proverbs31:31 Give her of the fruit of her hands
Ecclesiastes1:1 The words of the Preacher
Ecclesiastes1:2 “Vanity of vanities
Ecclesiastes1:3 What profit has a man from all his labor In which he toils under the sun?
Ecclesiastes1:4 One generation passes away
Ecclesiastes1:5 The sun also rises
Ecclesiastes1:6 The wind goes toward the south
Ecclesiastes1:7 All the rivers run into the sea
Ecclesiastes1:8 All things are full of labor; Man cannot express it. The eye is not satisfied with seeing
Ecclesiastes1:9 That which has been is what will be
Ecclesiastes1:10 Is there anything of which it may be said
Ecclesiastes1:11 There is no remembrance of former things
Ecclesiastes1:12 I
Ecclesiastes1:13 And I set my heart to seek and search out by wisdom concerning all that is done under heaven; this burdensome task God has given to the sons of man
Ecclesiastes1:14 I have seen all the works that are done under the sun; and indeed
Ecclesiastes1:15 What is crooked cannot be made straight
Ecclesiastes1:16 I communed with my heart
Ecclesiastes1:17 And I set my heart to know wisdom and to know madness and folly. I perceived that this also is grasping for the wind.
Ecclesiastes1:18 For in much wisdom is much grief
Ecclesiastes2:1 I said in my heart
Ecclesiastes2:2 I said of laughter—“Madness!”; and of mirth
Ecclesiastes2:3 I searched in my heart how to gratify my flesh with wine
Ecclesiastes2:4 I made my works great
Ecclesiastes2:5 I made myself gardens and orchards
Ecclesiastes2:6 I made myself water pools from which to water the growing trees of the grove.
Ecclesiastes2:7 I acquired male and female servants
Ecclesiastes2:8 I also gathered for myself silver and gold and the special treasures of kings and of the provinces. I acquired male and female singers
Ecclesiastes2:9 So I became great and excelled more than all who were before me in Jerusalem. Also my wisdom remained with me.
Ecclesiastes2:10 Whatever my eyes desired I did not keep from them. I did not withhold my heart from any pleasure
Ecclesiastes2:11 Then I looked on all the works that my hands had done And on the labor in which I had toiled; And indeed all was vanity and grasping for the wind. There was no profit under the sun.
Ecclesiastes2:12 Then I turned myself to consider wisdom and madness and folly; For what can the man do who succeeds the king?— Only what he has already done.
Ecclesiastes2:13 Then I saw that wisdom excels folly As light excels darkness.
Ecclesiastes2:14 The wise man’s eyes are in his head
Ecclesiastes2:15 So I said in my heart
Ecclesiastes2:16 For there is no more remembrance of the wise than of the fool forever
Ecclesiastes2:17 Therefore I hated life because the work that was done under the sun was distressing to me
Ecclesiastes2:18 Then I hated all my labor in which I had toiled under the sun
Ecclesiastes2:19 And who knows whether he will be wise or a fool? Yet he will rule over all my labor in which I toiled and in which I have shown myself wise under the sun. This also is vanity.
Ecclesiastes2:20 Therefore I turned my heart and despaired of all the labor in which I had toiled under the sun.
Ecclesiastes2:21 For there is a man whose labor is with wisdom
Ecclesiastes2:22 For what has man for all his labor
Ecclesiastes2:23 For all his days are sorrowful
Ecclesiastes2:24 Nothing is better for a man than that he should eat and drink
Ecclesiastes2:25 For who can eat
Ecclesiastes2:26 For God gives wisdom and knowledge and joy to a man who is good in His sight; but to the sinner He gives the work of gathering and collecting
Ecclesiastes3:1 To everything there is a season
Ecclesiastes3:2 A time to be born
Ecclesiastes3:3 A time to kill
Ecclesiastes3:4 A time to weep
Ecclesiastes3:5 A time to cast away stones
Ecclesiastes3:6 A time to gain
Ecclesiastes3:7 A time to tear
Ecclesiastes3:8 A time to love
Ecclesiastes3:9 What profit has the worker from that in which he labors?
Ecclesiastes3:10 I have seen the God-given task with which the sons of men are to be occupied.
Ecclesiastes3:11 He has made everything beautiful in its time. Also He has put eternity in their hearts
Ecclesiastes3:12 I know that nothing is better for them than to rejoice
Ecclesiastes3:13 and also that every man should eat and drink and enjoy the good of all his labor—it is the gift of God.
Ecclesiastes3:14 I know that whatever God does
Ecclesiastes3:15 That which is has already been
Ecclesiastes3:16 Moreover I saw under the sun: In the place of judgment
Ecclesiastes3:17 I said in my heart
Ecclesiastes3:18 I said in my heart
Ecclesiastes3:19 For what happens to the sons of men also happens to animals; one thing befalls them: as one dies
Ecclesiastes3:20 All go to one place: all are from the dust
Ecclesiastes3:21 Who knows the spirit of the sons of men
Ecclesiastes3:22 So I perceived that nothing is better than that a man should rejoice in his own works
Ecclesiastes4:1 Then I returned and considered all the oppression that is done under the sun: And look! The tears of the oppressed
Ecclesiastes4:2 Therefore I praised the dead who were already dead
Ecclesiastes4:3 Yet
Ecclesiastes4:4 Again
Ecclesiastes4:5 The fool folds his hands And consumes his own flesh.
Ecclesiastes4:6 Better a handful with quietness Than both hands full
Ecclesiastes4:7 Then I returned
Ecclesiastes4:8 There is one alone
Ecclesiastes4:9 Two are better than one
Ecclesiastes4:10 For if they fall
Ecclesiastes4:11 Again
Ecclesiastes4:12 Though one may be overpowered by another
Ecclesiastes4:13 Better a poor and wise youth Than an old and foolish king who will be admonished no more.
Ecclesiastes4:14 For he comes out of prison to be king
Ecclesiastes4:15 I saw all the living who walk under the sun; They were with the second youth who stands in his place.
Ecclesiastes4:16 There was no end of all the people over whom he was made king; Yet those who come afterward will not rejoice in him. Surely this also is vanity and grasping for the wind.
Ecclesiastes5:1 Walk prudently when you go to the house of God; and draw near to hear rather than to give the sacrifice of fools
Ecclesiastes5:2 Do not be rash with your mouth
Ecclesiastes5:3 For a dream comes through much activity
Ecclesiastes5:4 When you make a vow to God
Ecclesiastes5:5 Better not to vow than to vow and not pay.
Ecclesiastes5:6 Do not let your mouth cause your flesh to sin
Ecclesiastes5:7 For in the multitude of dreams and many words there is also vanity. But fear God.
Ecclesiastes5:8 If you see the oppression of the poor
Ecclesiastes5:9 Moreover the profit of the land is for all; even the king is served from the field.
Ecclesiastes5:10 He who loves silver will not be satisfied with silver; Nor he who loves abundance
Ecclesiastes5:11 When goods increase
Ecclesiastes5:12 The sleep of a laboring man is sweet
Ecclesiastes5:13 There is a severe evil which I have seen under the sun: Riches kept for their owner to his hurt.
Ecclesiastes5:14 But those riches perish through misfortune; When he begets a son
Ecclesiastes5:15 As he came from his mother’s womb
Ecclesiastes5:16 And this also is a severe evil— Just exactly as he came
Ecclesiastes5:17 All his days he also eats in darkness
Ecclesiastes5:18 Here is what I have seen: It is good and fitting for one to eat and drink
Ecclesiastes5:19 As for every man to whom God has given riches and wealth
Ecclesiastes5:20 For he will not dwell unduly on the days of his life
Ecclesiastes6:1 There is an evil which I have seen under the sun
Ecclesiastes6:2 A man to whom God has given riches and wealth and honor
Ecclesiastes6:3 If a man begets a hundred children and lives many years
Ecclesiastes6:4 for it comes in vanity and departs in darkness
Ecclesiastes6:5 Though it has not seen the sun or known anything
Ecclesiastes6:6 even if he lives a thousand years twice—but has not seen goodness. Do not all go to one place?
Ecclesiastes6:7 All the labor of man is for his mouth
Ecclesiastes6:8 For what more has the wise man than the fool? What does the poor man have
Ecclesiastes6:9 Better is the sight of the eyes than the wandering of desire. This also is vanity and grasping for the wind.
Ecclesiastes6:10 Whatever one is
Ecclesiastes6:11 Since there are many things that increase vanity
Ecclesiastes6:12 For who knows what is good for man in life
Ecclesiastes7:1 A good name is better than precious ointment
Ecclesiastes7:2 Better to go to the house of mourning Than to go to the house of feasting
Ecclesiastes7:3 Sorrow is better than laughter
Ecclesiastes7:4 The heart of the wise is in the house of mourning
Ecclesiastes7:5 It is better to hear the rebuke of the wise Than for a man to hear the song of fools.
Ecclesiastes7:6 For like the crackling of thorns under a pot
Ecclesiastes7:7 Surely oppression destroys a wise man’s reason
Ecclesiastes7:8 The end of a thing is better than its beginning; The patient in spirit is better than the proud in spirit.
Ecclesiastes7:9 Do not hasten in your spirit to be angry
Ecclesiastes7:10 Do not say
Ecclesiastes7:11 Wisdom is good with an inheritance
Ecclesiastes7:12 For wisdom is a defense as money is a defense
Ecclesiastes7:13 Consider the work of God; For who can make straight what He has made crooked?
Ecclesiastes7:14 In the day of prosperity be joyful
Ecclesiastes7:15 I have seen everything in my days of vanity: There is a just man who perishes in his righteousness
Ecclesiastes7:16 Do not be overly righteous
Ecclesiastes7:17 Do not be overly wicked
Ecclesiastes7:18 It is good that you grasp this
Ecclesiastes7:19 Wisdom strengthens the wise More than ten rulers of the city.
Ecclesiastes7:20 For there is not a just man on earth who does good And does not sin.
Ecclesiastes7:21 Also do not take to heart everything people say
Ecclesiastes7:22 For many times
Ecclesiastes7:23 All this I have proved by wisdom. I said
Ecclesiastes7:24 As for that which is far off and exceedingly deep
Ecclesiastes7:25 I applied my heart to know
Ecclesiastes7:26 And I find more bitter than death The woman whose heart is snares and nets
Ecclesiastes7:27 Here is what I have found
Ecclesiastes7:28 Which my soul still seeks but I cannot find: One man among a thousand I have found
Ecclesiastes7:29 Truly
Ecclesiastes8:1 Who is like a wise man? And who knows the interpretation of a thing? A man’s wisdom makes his face shine
Ecclesiastes8:2 I say
Ecclesiastes8:3 Do not be hasty to go from his presence. Do not take your stand for an evil thing
Ecclesiastes8:4 Where the word of a king is
Ecclesiastes8:5 He who keeps his command will experience nothing harmful; And a wise man’s heart discerns both time and judgment
Ecclesiastes8:6 Because for every matter there is a time and judgment
Ecclesiastes8:7 For he does not know what will happen; So who can tell him when it will occur?
Ecclesiastes8:8 No one has power over the spirit to retain the spirit
Ecclesiastes8:9 All this I have seen
Ecclesiastes8:10 Then I saw the wicked buried
Ecclesiastes8:11 Because the sentence against an evil work is not executed speedily
Ecclesiastes8:12 Though a sinner does evil a hundred times
Ecclesiastes8:13 But it will not be well with the wicked; nor will he prolong his days
Ecclesiastes8:14 There is a vanity which occurs on earth
Ecclesiastes8:15 So I commended enjoyment
Ecclesiastes8:16 When I applied my heart to know wisdom and to see the business that is done on earth
Ecclesiastes8:17 then I saw all the work of God
Ecclesiastes9:1 For I considered all this in my heart
Ecclesiastes9:2 All things come alike to all: One event happens to the righteous and the wicked; To the good
Ecclesiastes9:3 This is an evil in all that is done under the sun: that one thing happens to all. Truly the hearts of the sons of men are full of evil; madness is in their hearts while they live
Ecclesiastes9:4 But for him who is joined to all the living there is hope
Ecclesiastes9:5 For the living know that they will die; But the dead know nothing
Ecclesiastes9:6 Also their love
Ecclesiastes9:7 Go
Ecclesiastes9:8 Let your garments always be white
Ecclesiastes9:9 Live joyfully with the wife whom you love all the days of your vain life which He has given you under the sun
Ecclesiastes9:10 Whatever your hand finds to do
Ecclesiastes9:11 I returned and saw under the sun that— The race is not to the swift
Ecclesiastes9:12 For man also does not know his time: Like fish taken in a cruel net
Ecclesiastes9:13 This wisdom I have also seen under the sun
Ecclesiastes9:14 There was a little city with few men in it; and a great king came against it
Ecclesiastes9:15 Now there was found in it a poor wise man
Ecclesiastes9:16 Then I said: “Wisdom is better than strength. Nevertheless the poor man’s wisdom is despised
Ecclesiastes9:17 Words of the wise
Ecclesiastes9:18 Wisdom is better than weapons of war; But one sinner destroys much good.”
Ecclesiastes10:1 Dead flies putrefy the perfumer’s ointment
Ecclesiastes10:2 A wise man’s heart is at his right hand
Ecclesiastes10:3 Even when a fool walks along the way
Ecclesiastes10:4 If the spirit of the ruler rises against you
Ecclesiastes10:5 There is an evil I have seen under the sun
Ecclesiastes10:6 Folly is set in great dignity
Ecclesiastes10:7 I have seen servants on horses
Ecclesiastes10:8 He who digs a pit will fall into it
Ecclesiastes10:9 He who quarries stones may be hurt by them
Ecclesiastes10:10 If the ax is dull
Ecclesiastes10:11 A serpent may bite when it is not charmed; The babbler is no different.
Ecclesiastes10:12 The words of a wise man’s mouth are gracious
Ecclesiastes10:13 The words of his mouth begin with foolishness
Ecclesiastes10:14 A fool also multiplies words. No man knows what is to be; Who can tell him what will be after him?
Ecclesiastes10:15 The labor of fools wearies them
Ecclesiastes10:16 Woe to you
Ecclesiastes10:17 Blessed are you
Ecclesiastes10:18 Because of laziness the building decays
Ecclesiastes10:19 A feast is made for laughter
Ecclesiastes10:20 Do not curse the king
Ecclesiastes11:1 Cast your bread upon the waters
Ecclesiastes11:2 Give a serving to seven
Ecclesiastes11:3 If the clouds are full of rain
Ecclesiastes11:4 He who observes the wind will not sow
Ecclesiastes11:5 As you do not know what is the way of the wind
Ecclesiastes11:6 In the morning sow your seed
Ecclesiastes11:7 Truly the light is sweet
Ecclesiastes11:8 But if a man lives many years And rejoices in them all
Ecclesiastes11:9 Rejoice
Ecclesiastes11:10 Therefore remove sorrow from your heart
Ecclesiastes12:1 Remember now your Creator in the days of your youth
Ecclesiastes12:2 While the sun and the light
Ecclesiastes12:3 In the day when the keepers of the house tremble
Ecclesiastes12:4 When the doors are shut in the streets
Ecclesiastes12:5 Also they are afraid of height
Ecclesiastes12:6 Remember your Creator before the silver cord is loosed
Ecclesiastes12:7 Then the dust will return to the earth as it was
Ecclesiastes12:8 “Vanity of vanities
Ecclesiastes12:9 And moreover
Ecclesiastes12:10 The Preacher sought to find acceptable words; and what was written was upright—words of truth.
Ecclesiastes12:11 The words of the wise are like goads
Ecclesiastes12:12 And further
Ecclesiastes12:13 Let us hear the conclusion of the whole matter: Fear God and keep His commandments
Ecclesiastes12:14 For God will bring every work into judgment

Isaiah1:1 The vision of Isaiah the son of Amoz
Isaiah1:2 Hear
Isaiah1:3 The ox knows its owner And the donkey its master’s crib; But Israel does not know
Isaiah1:4 Alas
Isaiah1:5 Why should you be stricken again? You will revolt more and more. The whole head is sick
Isaiah1:6 From the sole of the foot even to the head
Isaiah1:7 Your country is desolate
Isaiah1:8 So the daughter of Zion is left as a booth in a vineyard
Isaiah1:9 Unless the Lord of hosts Had left to us a very small remnant
Isaiah1:10 Hear the word of the Lord
Isaiah1:11 “To what purpose is the multitude of your sacrifices to Me?” Says the Lord. “I have had enough of burnt offerings of rams And the fat of fed cattle. I do not delight in the blood of bulls
Isaiah1:12 “When you come to appear before Me
Isaiah1:13 Bring no more futile sacrifices; Incense is an abomination to Me. The New Moons
Isaiah1:14 Your New Moons and your appointed feasts My soul hates; They are a trouble to Me
Isaiah1:15 When you spread out your hands
Isaiah1:16 “Wash yourselves
Isaiah1:17 Learn to do good; Seek justice
Isaiah1:18 “Come now
Isaiah1:19 If you are willing and obedient
Isaiah1:20 But if you refuse and rebel
Isaiah1:21 How the faithful city has become a harlot! It was full of justice; Righteousness lodged in it
Isaiah1:22 Your silver has become dross
Isaiah1:23 Your princes are rebellious
Isaiah1:24 Therefore the Lord says
Isaiah1:25 I will turn My hand against you
Isaiah1:26 I will restore your judges as at the first
Isaiah1:27 Zion shall be redeemed with justice
Isaiah1:28 The destruction of transgressors and of sinners shall be together
Isaiah1:29 For they shall be ashamed of the terebinth trees Which you have desired; And you shall be embarrassed because of the gardens Which you have chosen.
Isaiah1:30 For you shall be as a terebinth whose leaf fades
Isaiah1:31 The strong shall be as tinder
Isaiah2:1 The word that Isaiah the son of Amoz saw concerning Judah and Jerusalem.
Isaiah2:2 Now it shall come to pass in the latter days That the mountain of the Lord’s house Shall be established on the top of the mountains
Isaiah2:3 Many people shall come and say
Isaiah2:4 He shall judge between the nations
Isaiah2:5 O house of Jacob
Isaiah2:6 For You have forsaken Your people
Isaiah2:7 Their land is also full of silver and gold
Isaiah2:8 Their land is also full of idols; They worship the work of their own hands
Isaiah2:9 People bow down
Isaiah2:10 Enter into the rock
Isaiah2:11 The lofty looks of man shall be humbled
Isaiah2:12 For the day of the Lord of hosts Shall come upon everything proud and lofty
Isaiah2:13 Upon all the cedars of Lebanon that are high and lifted up
Isaiah2:14 Upon all the high mountains
Isaiah2:15 Upon every high tower
Isaiah2:16 Upon all the ships of Tarshish
Isaiah2:17 The loftiness of man shall be bowed down
Isaiah2:18 But the idols He shall utterly abolish.
Isaiah2:19 They shall go into the holes of the rocks
Isaiah2:20 In that day a man will cast away his idols of silver And his idols of gold
Isaiah2:21 To go into the clefts of the rocks
Isaiah2:22 Sever yourselves from such a man

Isaiah3:2 The mighty man and the man of war
Isaiah3:3 The captain of fifty and the honorable man

Isaiah3:5 The people will be oppressed
Isaiah3:6 When a man takes hold of his brother In the house of his father
Isaiah3:7 In that day he will protest
Isaiah3:8 For Jerusalem stumbled
Isaiah3:9 The look on their countenance witnesses against them
Isaiah3:10 “Say to the righteous that it shall be well with them
Isaiah3:11 Woe to the wicked! It shall be ill with him
Isaiah3:12 As for My people
Isaiah3:13 The Lord stands up to plead
Isaiah3:14 The Lord will enter into judgment With the elders of His people And His princes: “For you have eaten up the vineyard; The plunder of the poor is in your houses.
Isaiah3:15 What do you mean by crushing My people And grinding the faces of the poor?” Says the Lord God of hosts.
Isaiah3:16 Moreover the Lord says: “Because the daughters of Zion are haughty
Isaiah3:17 Therefore the Lord will strike with a scab The crown of the head of the daughters of Zion

Isaiah3:19 The pendants
Isaiah3:20 The headdresses
Isaiah3:21 and the rings; The nose jewels
Isaiah3:22 the festal apparel
Isaiah3:23 and the mirrors; The fine linen

Isaiah3:25 Your men shall fall by the sword
Isaiah3:26 Her gates shall lament and mourn

Isaiah4:2 In that day the Branch of the Lord shall be beautiful and glorious; And the fruit of the earth shall be excellent and appealing For those of Israel who have escaped.
Isaiah4:3 And it shall come to pass that he who is left in Zion and remains in Jerusalem will be called holy—everyone who is recorded among the living in Jerusalem.

Isaiah5:3 “And now
Isaiah5:4 What more could have been done to My vineyard That I have not done in it? Why then

Isaiah5:10 For ten acres of vineyard shall yield one bath

Isaiah5:19 That say

Isaiah5:24 Therefore

Isaiah7:9 The head of Ephraim is Samaria

Isaiah9:2 The people who walked in darkness Have seen a great light; Those who dwelt in the land of the shadow of death

Isaiah9:5 For every warrior’s sandal from the noisy battle
Isaiah9:6 For unto us a Child is born
Isaiah9:7 Of the increase of His government and peace There will be no end

Isaiah9:9 All the people will know— Ephraim and the inhabitant of Samaria— Who say in pride and arrogance of heart:

Isaiah9:12 The Syrians before and the Philistines behind; And they shall devour Israel with an open mouth. For all this His anger is not turned away
Isaiah9:13 For the people do not turn to Him who strikes them
Isaiah9:14 Therefore the Lord will cut off head and tail from Israel
Isaiah9:15 The elder and honorable
Isaiah9:16 For the leaders of this people cause them to err
Isaiah9:17 Therefore the Lord will have no joy in their young men
Isaiah9:18 For wickedness burns as the fire; It shall devour the briers and thorns
Isaiah9:19 Through the wrath of the Lord of hosts The land is burned up
Isaiah9:20 And he shall snatch on the right hand And be hungry; He shall devour on the left hand And not be satisfied; Every man shall eat the flesh of his own arm.

Isaiah10:2 To rob the needy of justice
Isaiah10:3 What will you do in the day of punishment
Isaiah10:4 Without Me they shall bow down among the prisoners
Isaiah10:5 “Woe to Assyria
Isaiah10:6 I will send him against an ungodly nation
Isaiah10:7 Yet he does not mean so
Isaiah10:8 For he says
Isaiah10:9 Is not Calno like Carchemish? Is not Hamath like Arpad? Is not Samaria like Damascus?
Isaiah10:10 As my hand has found the kingdoms of the idols
Isaiah10:11 As I have done to Samaria and her idols
Isaiah10:12 Therefore it shall come to pass
Isaiah10:13 For he says: “By the strength of my hand I have done it
Isaiah10:14 My hand has found like a nest the riches of the people
Isaiah10:15 Shall the ax boast itself against him who chops with it? Or shall the saw exalt itself against him who saws with it? As if a rod could wield itself against those who lift it up
Isaiah10:16 Therefore the Lord
Isaiah10:17 So the Light of Israel will be for a fire
Isaiah10:18 And it will consume the glory of his forest and of his fruitful field
Isaiah10:19 Then the rest of the trees of his forest Will be so few in number That a child may write them.

Isaiah10:21 The remnant will return
Isaiah10:22 For though your people
Isaiah10:23 For the Lord God of hosts Will make a determined end In the midst of all the land.

Isaiah10:25 For yet a very little while and the indignation will cease
Isaiah10:26 And the Lord of hosts will stir up a scourge for him like the slaughter of Midian at the rock of Oreb; as His rod was on the sea

Isaiah10:28 He has come to Aiath
Isaiah10:29 They have gone along the ridge
Isaiah10:30 Lift up your voice
Isaiah10:31 Madmenah has fled
Isaiah10:32 As yet he will remain at Nob that day; He will shake his fist at the mount of the daughter of Zion
Isaiah10:33 Behold
Isaiah10:34 He will cut down the thickets of the forest with iron
Isaiah11:1 There shall come forth a Rod from the stem of Jesse
Isaiah11:2 The Spirit of the Lord shall rest upon Him
Isaiah11:3 His delight is in the fear of the Lord
Isaiah11:4 But with righteousness He shall judge the poor
Isaiah11:5 Righteousness shall be the belt of His loins
Isaiah11:6 “The wolf also shall dwell with the lamb
Isaiah11:7 The cow and the bear shall graze; Their young ones shall lie down together; And the lion shall eat straw like the ox.
Isaiah11:8 The nursing child shall play by the cobra’s hole
Isaiah11:9 They shall not hurt nor destroy in all My holy mountain
Isaiah11:10 “And in that day there shall be a Root of Jesse
Isaiah11:11 It shall come to pass in that day That the Lord shall set His hand again the second time To recover the remnant of His people who are left
Isaiah11:12 He will set up a banner for the nations
Isaiah11:13 Also the envy of Ephraim shall depart
Isaiah11:14 But they shall fly down upon the shoulder of the Philistines toward the west; Together they shall plunder the people of the East; They shall lay their hand on Edom and Moab; And the people of Ammon shall obey them.

Isaiah13:1 The burden against Babylon which Isaiah the son of Amoz saw.

Isaiah13:5 They come from a far country
Isaiah13:6 Wail
Isaiah13:7 Therefore all hands will be limp
Isaiah13:8 And they will be afraid. Pangs and sorrows will take hold of them; They will be in pain as a woman in childbirth; They will be amazed at one another; Their faces will be like flames.
Isaiah13:9 Behold
Isaiah13:10 For the stars of heaven and their constellations Will not give their light; The sun will be darkened in its going forth
Isaiah13:11 “I will punish the world for its evil
Isaiah13:12 I will make a mortal more rare than fine gold
Isaiah13:13 Therefore I will shake the heavens
Isaiah13:14 It shall be as the hunted gazelle
Isaiah13:15 Everyone who is found will be thrust through

Isaiah14:1 For the Lord will have mercy on Jacob
Isaiah14:2 Then people will take them and bring them to their place
Isaiah14:3 It shall come to pass in the day the Lord gives you rest from your sorrow
Isaiah14:4 that you will take up this proverb against the king of Babylon
Isaiah14:5 The Lord has broken the staff of the wicked
Isaiah14:6 He who struck the people in wrath with a continual stroke
Isaiah14:7 The whole earth is at rest and quiet; They break forth into singing.

Isaiah14:9 “Hell from beneath is excited about you

Isaiah14:14 I will ascend above the heights of the clouds

Isaiah14:17 Who made the world as a wilderness And destroyed its cities
Isaiah14:18 “All the kings of the nations

Isaiah15:1 The burden against Moab. Because in the night Ar of Moab is laid waste And destroyed

Isaiah15:7 Therefore the abundance they have gained
Isaiah15:8 For the cry has gone all around the borders of Moab

Isaiah16:2 For it shall be as a wandering bird thrown out of the nest; So shall be the daughters of Moab at the fords of the Arnon.

Isaiah19:1 The burden against Egypt. Behold

Isaiah19:5 The waters will fail from the sea
Isaiah19:6 The rivers will turn foul; The brooks of defense will be emptied and dried up; The reeds and rushes will wither.

Isaiah19:8 The fishermen also will mourn; All those will lament who cast hooks into the River
Isaiah19:9 Moreover those who work in fine flax And those who weave fine fabric will be ashamed;
Isaiah19:10 And its foundations will be broken. All who make wages will be troubled of soul.

Isaiah19:12 Where are they? Where are your wise men? Let them tell you now
Isaiah19:13 The princes of Zoan have become fools; The princes of Noph are deceived; They have also deluded Egypt

Isaiah35:10 And the ransomed of the Lord shall return
Isaiah36:1 Now it came to pass in the fourteenth year of King Hezekiah that Sennacherib king of Assyria came up against all the fortified cities of Judah and took them.
Isaiah36:2 Then the king of Assyria sent the Rabshakeh with a great army from Lachish to King Hezekiah at Jerusalem. And he stood by the aqueduct from the upper pool
Isaiah36:3 And Eliakim the son of Hilkiah
Isaiah36:4 Then the Rabshakeh said to them
Isaiah36:5 I say you speak of having plans and power for war; but they are mere words. Now in whom do you trust
Isaiah36:6 Look! You are trusting in the staff of this broken reed
Isaiah36:7 “But if you say to me
Isaiah36:8 Now therefore
Isaiah36:9 How then will you repel one captain of the least of my master’s servants
Isaiah36:10 Have I now come up without the Lord against this land to destroy it? The Lord said to me
Isaiah36:11 Then Eliakim
Isaiah36:12 But the Rabshakeh said
Isaiah36:13 Then the Rabshakeh stood and called out with a loud voice in Hebrew
Isaiah36:14 Thus says the king: ‘Do not let Hezekiah deceive you
Isaiah36:15 nor let Hezekiah make you trust in the Lord
Isaiah36:16 Do not listen to Hezekiah; for thus says the king of Assyria: ‘Make peace with me by a present and come out to me; and every one of you eat from his own vine and every one from his own fig tree
Isaiah36:17 until I come and take you away to a land like your own land
Isaiah36:18 Beware lest Hezekiah persuade you
Isaiah36:19 Where are the gods of Hamath and Arpad? Where are the gods of Sepharvaim? Indeed
Isaiah36:20 Who among all the gods of these lands have delivered their countries from my hand
Isaiah36:21 But they held their peace and answered him not a word; for the king’s commandment was
Isaiah36:22 Then Eliakim the son of Hilkiah
Isaiah37:1 And so it was
Isaiah37:2 Then he sent Eliakim
Isaiah37:3 And they said to him
Isaiah37:4 It may be that the Lord your God will hear the words of the Rabshakeh
Isaiah37:5 So the servants of King Hezekiah came to Isaiah.
Isaiah37:6 And Isaiah said to them
Isaiah37:7 Surely I will send a spirit upon him
Isaiah37:8 Then the Rabshakeh returned
Isaiah37:9 And the king heard concerning Tirhakah king of Ethiopia
Isaiah37:10 “Thus you shall speak to Hezekiah king of Judah
Isaiah37:11 Look! You have heard what the kings of Assyria have done to all lands by utterly destroying them; and shall you be delivered?
Isaiah37:12 Have the gods of the nations delivered those whom my fathers have destroyed
Isaiah37:13 Where is the king of Hamath
Isaiah37:14 And Hezekiah received the letter from the hand of the messengers
Isaiah37:15 Then Hezekiah prayed to the Lord
Isaiah37:16 “O Lord of hosts
Isaiah37:17 Incline Your ear
Isaiah37:18 Truly
Isaiah37:19 and have cast their gods into the fire; for they were not gods
Isaiah37:20 Now therefore
Isaiah37:21 Then Isaiah the son of Amoz sent to Hezekiah
Isaiah37:22 this is the word which the Lord has spoken concerning him: “The virgin
Isaiah37:23 “Whom have you reproached and blasphemed? Against whom have you raised your voice
Isaiah37:24 By your servants you have reproached the Lord
Isaiah37:25 I have dug and drunk water
Isaiah37:26 “Did you not hear long ago How I made it
Isaiah37:27 Therefore their inhabitants had little power; They were dismayed and confounded; They were as the grass of the field And the green herb
Isaiah37:28 “But I know your dwelling place
Isaiah37:29 Because your rage against Me and your tumult Have come up to My ears
Isaiah37:30 “This shall be a sign to you: You shall eat this year such as grows of itself
Isaiah37:31 And the remnant who have escaped of the house of Judah Shall again take root downward
Isaiah37:32 For out of Jerusalem shall go a remnant
Isaiah37:33 “Therefore thus says the Lord concerning the king of Assyria: ‘He shall not come into this city
Isaiah37:34 By the way that he came
Isaiah37:35 ‘For I will defend this city
Isaiah37:36 Then the angel of the Lord went out
Isaiah37:37 So Sennacherib king of Assyria departed and went away
Isaiah37:38 Now it came to pass

Isaiah38:2 Then Hezekiah turned his face toward the wall
Isaiah38:3 and said
Isaiah38:4 And the word of the Lord came to Isaiah
Isaiah38:5 “Go and tell Hezekiah
Isaiah38:6 I will deliver you and this city from the hand of the king of Assyria
Isaiah38:7 And this is the sign to you from the Lord
Isaiah38:8 “Behold
Isaiah38:9 This is the writing of Hezekiah king of Judah
Isaiah38:10 I said
Isaiah38:11 I said
Isaiah38:12 My life span is gone
Isaiah38:13 I have considered until morning— Like a lion
Isaiah38:14 Like a crane or a swallow
Isaiah38:15 “What shall I say? He has both spoken to me
Isaiah38:16 O Lord
Isaiah38:17 Indeed it was for my own peace That I had great bitterness; But You have lovingly delivered my soul from the pit of corruption
Isaiah38:18 For Sheol cannot thank You
Isaiah38:19 The living

Isaiah40:20 Whoever is too impoverished for such a contribution Chooses a tree that will not rot; He seeks for himself a skillful workman To prepare a carved image that will not totter.
Isaiah40:21 Have you not known? Have you not heard? Has it not been told you from the beginning? Have you not understood from the foundations of the earth?
Isaiah40:22 It is He who sits above the circle of the earth
Isaiah40:23 He brings the princes to nothing; He makes the judges of the earth useless.

Isaiah59:1 Behold
Isaiah59:2 But your iniquities have separated you from your God; And your sins have hidden His face from you
Isaiah59:3 For your hands are defiled with blood
Isaiah59:4 No one calls for justice
Isaiah59:5 They hatch vipers’ eggs and weave the spider’s web; He who eats of their eggs dies
Isaiah59:6 Their webs will not become garments
Isaiah59:7 Their feet run to evil
Isaiah59:8 The way of peace they have not known
Isaiah59:9 Therefore justice is far from us
Isaiah59:10 We grope for the wall like the blind
Isaiah59:11 We all growl like bears
Isaiah59:12 For our transgressions are multiplied before You
Isaiah59:13 In transgressing and lying against the Lord
Isaiah59:14 Justice is turned back
Isaiah59:15 So truth fails
Isaiah59:16 He saw that there was no man
Isaiah59:17 For He put on righteousness as a breastplate
Isaiah59:18 According to their deeds
Isaiah59:19 So shall they fear The name of the Lord from the west
Isaiah59:20 “The Redeemer will come to Zion
Isaiah59:21 “As for Me
Isaiah60:1 Arise
Isaiah60:2 For behold
Isaiah60:3 The Gentiles shall come to your light
Isaiah60:4 “Lift up your eyes all around
Isaiah60:5 Then you shall see and become radiant
Isaiah60:6 The multitude of camels shall cover your land
Isaiah60:7 All the flocks of Kedar shall be gathered together to you
Isaiah60:8 “Who are these who fly like a cloud
Isaiah60:9 Surely the coastlands shall wait for Me; And the ships of Tarshish will come first
Isaiah60:10 “The sons of foreigners shall build up your walls
Isaiah60:11 Therefore your gates shall be open continually; They shall not be shut day or night
Isaiah60:12 For the nation and kingdom which will not serve you shall perish
Isaiah60:13 “The glory of Lebanon shall come to you
Isaiah60:14 Also the sons of those who afflicted you Shall come bowing to you
Isaiah60:15 “Whereas you have been forsaken and hated
Isaiah60:16 You shall drink the milk of the Gentiles
Isaiah60:17 “Instead of bronze I will bring gold
Isaiah60:18 Violence shall no longer be heard in your land
Isaiah60:19 “The sun shall no longer be your light by day
Isaiah60:20 Your sun shall no longer go down
Isaiah60:21 Also your people shall all be righteous; They shall inherit the land forever
Isaiah60:22 A little one shall become a thousand

Isaiah61:2 To proclaim the acceptable year of the Lord
Isaiah61:3 To console those who mourn in Zion
Isaiah61:4 And they shall rebuild the old ruins
Isaiah61:5 Strangers shall stand and feed your flocks
Isaiah61:6 But you shall be named the priests of the Lord
Isaiah61:7 Instead of your shame you shall have double honor
Isaiah61:8 “For I
Isaiah61:9 Their descendants shall be known among the Gentiles
Isaiah61:10 I will greatly rejoice in the Lord
Isaiah61:11 For as the earth brings forth its bud
Isaiah62:1 For Zion’s sake I will not hold My peace
Isaiah62:2 The Gentiles shall see your righteousness
Isaiah62:3 You shall also be a crown of glory In the hand of the Lord
Isaiah62:4 You shall no longer be termed Forsaken
Isaiah62:5 For as a young man marries a virgin
Isaiah62:6 I have set watchmen on your walls
Isaiah62:7 And give Him no rest till He establishes And till He makes Jerusalem a praise in the earth.
Isaiah62:8 The Lord has sworn by His right hand And by the arm of His strength: “Surely I will no longer give your grain As food for your enemies; And the sons of the foreigner shall not drink your new wine
Isaiah62:9 But those who have gathered it shall eat it
Isaiah62:10 Go through
Isaiah62:11 Indeed the Lord has proclaimed To the end of the world: “Say to the daughter of Zion

Isaiah63:2 Why is Your apparel red

Isaiah64:1 Oh
Isaiah64:2 As fire burns brushwood
Isaiah64:3 When You did awesome things for which we did not look
Isaiah64:4 For since the beginning of the world Men have not heard nor perceived by the ear

Isaiah64:6 But we are all like an unclean thing
Isaiah64:7 And there is no one who calls on Your name

Isaiah65:1 “I was sought by those who did not ask for Me; I was found by those who did not seek Me. I said
Isaiah65:2 I have stretched out My hands all day long to a rebellious people
Isaiah65:3 A people who provoke Me to anger continually to My face; Who sacrifice in gardens
Isaiah65:4 Who sit among the graves
Isaiah65:5 Who say
Isaiah65:6 “Behold

Isaiah65:10 Sharon shall be a fold of flocks
Isaiah65:11 “But you are those who forsake the Lord
Isaiah65:12 Therefore I will number you for the sword
Isaiah65:13 Therefore thus says the Lord God: “Behold
Isaiah65:14 Behold
Isaiah65:15 You shall leave your name as a curse to My chosen; For the Lord God will slay you
Isaiah65:16 So that he who blesses himself in the earth Shall bless himself in the God of truth; And he who swears in the earth Shall swear by the God of truth; Because the former troubles are forgotten
Isaiah65:17 “For behold
Isaiah65:18 But be glad and rejoice forever in what I create; For behold
Isaiah65:19 I will rejoice in Jerusalem
Isaiah65:20 “No more shall an infant from there live but a few days
Isaiah65:21 They shall build houses and inhabit them; They shall plant vineyards and eat their fruit.
Isaiah65:22 They shall not build and another inhabit; They shall not plant and another eat; For as the days of a tree
Isaiah65:23 They shall not labor in vain

Isaiah66:1 Thus says the Lord: “Heaven is My throne
Isaiah66:2 For all those things My hand has made
Isaiah66:3 “He who kills a bull is as if he slays a man; He who sacrifices a lamb
Isaiah66:4 So will I choose their delusions
Isaiah66:5 Hear the word of the Lord
Isaiah66:6 The sound of noise from the city! A voice from the temple! The voice of the Lord
Isaiah66:7 “Before she was in labor
Isaiah66:8 Who has heard such a thing? Who has seen such things? Shall the earth be made to give birth in one day? Or shall a nation be born at once? For as soon as Zion was in labor
Isaiah66:9 Shall I bring to the time of birth
Isaiah66:10 “Rejoice with Jerusalem
Isaiah66:11 That you may feed and be satisfied With the consolation of her bosom
Isaiah66:12 For thus says the Lord: “Behold
Isaiah66:13 As one whom his mother comforts
Isaiah66:14 When you see this
Isaiah66:15 For behold
Isaiah66:16 For by fire and by His sword The Lord will judge all flesh; And the slain of the Lord shall be many.
Isaiah66:17 “Those who sanctify themselves and purify themselves
Isaiah66:18 “For I know their works and their thoughts. It shall be that I will gather all nations and tongues; and they shall come and see My glory.
Isaiah66:19 I will set a sign among them; and those among them who escape I will send to the nations: to Tarshish and Pul and Lud
Isaiah66:20 Then they shall bring all your brethren for an offering to the Lord out of all nations
Isaiah66:21 And I will also take some of them for priests and Levites
Isaiah66:22 “For as the new heavens and the new earth Which I will make shall remain before Me
Isaiah66:23 And it shall come to pass That from one New Moon to another
Isaiah66:24 “And they shall go forth and look Upon the corpses of the men Who have transgressed against Me. For their worm does not die
Jeremiah1:1 The words of Jeremiah the son of Hilkiah
Jeremiah1:2 to whom the word of the Lord came in the days of Josiah the son of Amon
Jeremiah1:3 It came also in the days of Jehoiakim the son of Josiah
Jeremiah1:4 Then the word of the Lord came to me
Jeremiah1:5 “Before I formed you in the womb I knew you; Before you were born I sanctified you; I ordained you a prophet to the nations.”
Jeremiah1:6 Then said I: “Ah
Jeremiah1:7 But the Lord said to me: “Do not say
Jeremiah1:8 Do not be afraid of their faces
Jeremiah1:9 Then the Lord put forth His hand and touched my mouth
Jeremiah1:10 See
Jeremiah1:11 Moreover the word of the Lord came to me
Jeremiah1:12 Then the Lord said to me
Jeremiah1:13 And the word of the Lord came to me the second time
Jeremiah1:14 Then the Lord said to me: “Out of the north calamity shall break forth On all the inhabitants of the land.
Jeremiah1:15 For behold
Jeremiah1:16 I will utter My judgments Against them concerning all their wickedness
Jeremiah1:17 “Therefore prepare yourself and arise
Jeremiah1:18 For behold
Jeremiah1:19 They will fight against you
Jeremiah2:1 Moreover the word of the Lord came to me
Jeremiah2:2 “Go and cry in the hearing of Jerusalem
Jeremiah2:3 Israel was holiness to the Lord
Jeremiah2:4 Hear the word of the Lord
Jeremiah2:5 Thus says the Lord: “What injustice have your fathers found in Me
Jeremiah2:6 Neither did they say
Jeremiah2:7 I brought you into a bountiful country
Jeremiah2:8 The priests did not say
Jeremiah2:9 “Therefore I will yet bring charges against you
Jeremiah2:10 For pass beyond the coasts of Cyprus and see
Jeremiah2:11 Has a nation changed its gods
Jeremiah2:12 Be astonished
Jeremiah2:13 “For My people have committed two evils: They have forsaken Me
Jeremiah2:14 “ Is Israel a servant? Is he a homeborn slave? Why is he plundered?
Jeremiah2:15 The young lions roared at him
Jeremiah2:16 Also the people of Noph and Tahpanhes Have broken the crown of your head.
Jeremiah2:17 Have you not brought this on yourself
Jeremiah2:18 And now why take the road to Egypt
Jeremiah2:19 Your own wickedness will correct you
Jeremiah2:20 “For of old I have broken your yoke and burst your bonds; And you said
Jeremiah2:21 Yet I had planted you a noble vine
Jeremiah2:22 For though you wash yourself with lye
Jeremiah2:23 “How can you say
Jeremiah2:24 A wild donkey used to the wilderness
Jeremiah2:25 Withhold your foot from being unshod
Jeremiah2:26 “As the thief is ashamed when he is found out
Jeremiah2:27 Saying to a tree
Jeremiah2:28 But where are your gods that you have made for yourselves? Let them arise
Jeremiah2:29 “Why will you plead with Me? You all have transgressed against Me
Jeremiah2:30 “In vain I have chastened your children; They received no correction. Your sword has devoured your prophets Like a destroying lion.
Jeremiah2:31 “O generation
Jeremiah2:32 Can a virgin forget her ornaments
Jeremiah2:33 “Why do you beautify your way to seek love? Therefore you have also taught The wicked women your ways.
Jeremiah2:34 Also on your skirts is found The blood of the lives of the poor innocents. I have not found it by secret search
Jeremiah2:35 Yet you say
Jeremiah2:36 Why do you gad about so much to change your way? Also you shall be ashamed of Egypt as you were ashamed of Assyria.
Jeremiah2:37 Indeed you will go forth from him With your hands on your head; For the Lord has rejected your trusted allies

Jeremiah3:2 “Lift up your eyes to the desolate heights and see: Where have you not lain with men? By the road you have sat for them Like an Arabian in the wilderness; And you have polluted the land With your harlotries and your wickedness.
Jeremiah3:3 Therefore the showers have been withheld
Jeremiah3:4 Will you not from this time cry to Me
Jeremiah3:5 Will He remain angry forever? Will He keep it to the end?’ Behold

Jeremiah3:7 And I said
Jeremiah3:8 Then I saw that for all the causes for which backsliding Israel had committed adultery
Jeremiah3:9 So it came to pass
Jeremiah3:10 And yet for all this her treacherous sister Judah has not turned to Me with her whole heart
Jeremiah3:11 Then the Lord said to me
Jeremiah3:12 Go and proclaim these words toward the north
Jeremiah3:13 Only acknowledge your iniquity

Jeremiah10:1 Hear the word which the Lord speaks to you
Jeremiah10:2 Thus says the Lord: “Do not learn the way of the Gentiles; Do not be dismayed at the signs of heaven
Jeremiah10:3 For the customs of the peoples are futile; For one cuts a tree from the forest
Jeremiah10:4 They decorate it with silver and gold; They fasten it with nails and hammers So that it will not topple.
Jeremiah10:5 They are upright
Jeremiah10:6 Inasmuch as there is none like You
Jeremiah10:7 Who would not fear You
Jeremiah10:8 But they are altogether dull-hearted and foolish; A wooden idol is a worthless doctrine.
Jeremiah10:9 Silver is beaten into plates; It is brought from Tarshish
Jeremiah10:10 But the Lord is the true God; He is the living God and the everlasting King. At His wrath the earth will tremble
Jeremiah10:11 Thus you shall say to them: “The gods that have not made the heavens and the earth shall perish from the earth and from under these heavens.”
Jeremiah10:12 He has made the earth by His power
Jeremiah10:13 When He utters His voice
Jeremiah10:14 Everyone is dull-hearted
Jeremiah10:15 They are futile
Jeremiah10:16 The Portion of Jacob is not like them
Jeremiah10:17 Gather up your wares from the land

Jeremiah29:1 Now these are the words of the letter that Jeremiah the prophet sent from Jerusalem to the remainder of the elders who were carried away captive—to the priests
Jeremiah29:2 (This happened after Jeconiah the king
Jeremiah29:3 The letter was sent by the hand of Elasah the son of Shaphan
Jeremiah29:4 Thus says the Lord of hosts
Jeremiah29:5 Build houses and dwell in them; plant gardens and eat their fruit.
Jeremiah29:6 Take wives and beget sons and daughters; and take wives for your sons and give your daughters to husbands
Jeremiah29:7 And seek the peace of the city where I have caused you to be carried away captive
Jeremiah29:8 For thus says the Lord of hosts
Jeremiah29:9 For they prophesy falsely to you in My name; I have not sent them
Jeremiah29:10 For thus says the Lord: After seventy years are completed at Babylon
Jeremiah29:11 For I know the thoughts that I think toward you
Jeremiah29:12 Then you will call upon Me and go and pray to Me
Jeremiah29:13 And you will seek Me and find Me
Jeremiah29:14 I will be found by you
Jeremiah29:15 Because you have said
Jeremiah29:16 therefore thus says the Lord concerning the king who sits on the throne of David
Jeremiah29:17 thus says the Lord of hosts: Behold
Jeremiah29:18 And I will pursue them with the sword
Jeremiah29:19 because they have not heeded My words
Jeremiah29:20 Therefore hear the word of the Lord
Jeremiah29:21 Thus says the Lord of hosts
Jeremiah29:22 And because of them a curse shall be taken up by all the captivity of Judah who are in Babylon
Jeremiah29:23 because they have done disgraceful things in Israel
Jeremiah29:24 You shall also speak to Shemaiah the Nehelamite
Jeremiah29:25 Thus speaks the Lord of hosts
Jeremiah29:26 “The Lord has made you priest instead of Jehoiada the priest
Jeremiah29:27 Now therefore
Jeremiah29:28 For he has sent to us in Babylon
Jeremiah29:29 Now Zephaniah the priest read this letter in the hearing of Jeremiah the prophet.
Jeremiah29:30 Then the word of the Lord came to Jeremiah
Jeremiah29:31 Send to all those in captivity
Jeremiah29:32 therefore thus says the Lord: Behold
Jeremiah30:1 The word that came to Jeremiah from the Lord
Jeremiah30:2 “Thus speaks the Lord God of Israel
Jeremiah30:3 For behold
Jeremiah30:4 Now these are the words that the Lord spoke concerning Israel and Judah.
Jeremiah30:5 “For thus says the Lord: ‘We have heard a voice of trembling
Jeremiah30:6 Ask now
Jeremiah30:7 Alas! For that day is great
Jeremiah30:8 ‘For it shall come to pass in that day
Jeremiah30:9 But they shall serve the Lord their God
Jeremiah30:10 ‘Therefore do not fear
Jeremiah30:11 For I am with you
Jeremiah30:12 “For thus says the Lord: ‘Your affliction is incurable
Jeremiah30:13 There is no one to plead your cause
Jeremiah30:14 All your lovers have forgotten you; They do not seek you; For I have wounded you with the wound of an enemy
Jeremiah30:15 Why do you cry about your affliction? Your sorrow is incurable. Because of the multitude of your iniquities
Jeremiah30:16 ‘Therefore all those who devour you shall be devoured; And all your adversaries
Jeremiah30:17 For I will restore health to you And heal you of your wounds
Jeremiah30:18 “Thus says the Lord: ‘Behold
Jeremiah30:19 Then out of them shall proceed thanksgiving And the voice of those who make merry; I will multiply them
Jeremiah30:20 Their children also shall be as before
Jeremiah30:21 Their nobles shall be from among them
Jeremiah30:22 ‘You shall be My people
Jeremiah30:23 Behold
Jeremiah30:24 The fierce anger of the Lord will not return until He has done it
Jeremiah31:1 “At the same time
Jeremiah31:2 Thus says the Lord: “The people who survived the sword Found grace in the wilderness— Israel
Jeremiah31:3 The Lord has appeared of old to me
Jeremiah31:4 Again I will build you
Jeremiah31:5 You shall yet plant vines on the mountains of Samaria; The planters shall plant and eat them as ordinary food.
Jeremiah31:6 For there shall be a day When the watchmen will cry on Mount Ephraim
Jeremiah31:7 For thus says the Lord: “Sing with gladness for Jacob
Jeremiah31:8 Behold
Jeremiah31:9 They shall come with weeping
Jeremiah31:10 “Hear the word of the Lord
Jeremiah31:11 For the Lord has redeemed Jacob
Jeremiah31:12 Therefore they shall come and sing in the height of Zion
Jeremiah31:13 “Then shall the virgin rejoice in the dance
Jeremiah31:14 I will satiate the soul of the priests with abundance
Jeremiah31:15 Thus says the Lord: “A voice was heard in Ramah
Jeremiah31:16 Thus says the Lord: “Refrain your voice from weeping
Jeremiah31:17 There is hope in your future
Jeremiah31:18 “I have surely heard Ephraim bemoaning himself: ‘You have chastised me
Jeremiah31:19 Surely
Jeremiah31:20 Is Ephraim My dear son? Is he a pleasant child? For though I spoke against him
Jeremiah31:21 “Set up signposts
Jeremiah31:22 How long will you gad about
Jeremiah31:23 Thus says the Lord of hosts
Jeremiah31:24 And there shall dwell in Judah itself
Jeremiah31:25 For I have satiated the weary soul
Jeremiah31:26 After this I awoke and looked around
Jeremiah31:27 “Behold
Jeremiah31:28 And it shall come to pass
Jeremiah31:29 In those days they shall say no more: ‘The fathers have eaten sour grapes
Jeremiah31:30 But every one shall die for his own iniquity; every man who eats the sour grapes
Jeremiah31:31 “Behold
Jeremiah31:32 not according to the covenant that I made with their fathers in the day that I took them by the hand to lead them out of the land of Egypt
Jeremiah31:33 But this is the covenant that I will make with the house of Israel after those days
Jeremiah31:34 No more shall every man teach his neighbor
Jeremiah31:35 Thus says the Lord
Jeremiah31:36 “If those ordinances depart From before Me
Jeremiah31:37 Thus says the Lord: “If heaven above can be measured
Jeremiah31:38 “Behold
Jeremiah31:39 The surveyor’s line shall again extend straight forward over the hill Gareb; then it shall turn toward Goath.
Jeremiah31:40 And the whole valley of the dead bodies and of the ashes
Jeremiah32:1 The word that came to Jeremiah from the Lord in the tenth year of Zedekiah king of Judah
Jeremiah32:2 For then the king of Babylon’s army besieged Jerusalem
Jeremiah32:3 For Zedekiah king of Judah had shut him up
Jeremiah32:4 and Zedekiah king of Judah shall not escape from the hand of the Chaldeans
Jeremiah32:5 then he shall lead Zedekiah to Babylon
Jeremiah32:6 And Jeremiah said
Jeremiah32:7 ‘Behold
Jeremiah32:8 Then Hanamel my uncle’s son came to me in the court of the prison according to the word of the Lord
Jeremiah32:9 So I bought the field from Hanamel
Jeremiah32:10 And I signed the deed and sealed it
Jeremiah32:11 So I took the purchase deed
Jeremiah32:12 and I gave the purchase deed to Baruch the son of Neriah
Jeremiah32:13 “Then I charged Baruch before them
Jeremiah32:14 ‘Thus says the Lord of hosts
Jeremiah32:15 For thus says the Lord of hosts
Jeremiah32:16 “Now when I had delivered the purchase deed to Baruch the son of Neriah
Jeremiah32:17 ‘Ah
Jeremiah32:18 You show lovingkindness to thousands
Jeremiah32:19 You are great in counsel and mighty in work
Jeremiah32:20 You have set signs and wonders in the land of Egypt
Jeremiah32:21 You have brought Your people Israel out of the land of Egypt with signs and wonders
Jeremiah32:22 You have given them this land
Jeremiah32:23 And they came in and took possession of it
Jeremiah32:24 ‘Look
Jeremiah32:25 And You have said to me
Jeremiah32:26 Then the word of the Lord came to Jeremiah
Jeremiah32:27 “Behold
Jeremiah32:28 Therefore thus says the Lord: ‘Behold
Jeremiah32:29 And the Chaldeans who fight against this city shall come and set fire to this city and burn it
Jeremiah32:30 because the children of Israel and the children of Judah have done only evil before Me from their youth. For the children of Israel have provoked Me only to anger with the work of their hands
Jeremiah32:31 ‘For this city has been to Me a provocation of My anger and My fury from the day that they built it
Jeremiah32:32 because of all the evil of the children of Israel and the children of Judah
Jeremiah32:33 And they have turned to Me the back
Jeremiah32:34 But they set their abominations in the house which is called by My name
Jeremiah32:35 And they built the high places of Baal which are in the Valley of the Son of Hinnom
Jeremiah32:36 “Now therefore
Jeremiah32:37 Behold
Jeremiah32:38 They shall be My people
Jeremiah32:39 then I will give them one heart and one way
Jeremiah32:40 And I will make an everlasting covenant with them
Jeremiah32:41 Yes
Jeremiah32:42 “For thus says the Lord: ‘Just as I have brought all this great calamity on this people
Jeremiah32:43 And fields will be bought in this land of which you say
Jeremiah32:44 Men will buy fields for money
Jeremiah33:1 Moreover the word of the Lord came to Jeremiah a second time
Jeremiah33:2 “Thus says the Lord who made it
Jeremiah33:3 ‘Call to Me
Jeremiah33:4 “For thus says the Lord
Jeremiah33:5 ‘They come to fight with the Chaldeans
Jeremiah33:6 Behold
Jeremiah33:7 And I will cause the captives of Judah and the captives of Israel to return
Jeremiah33:8 I will cleanse them from all their iniquity by which they have sinned against Me
Jeremiah33:9 Then it shall be to Me a name of joy
Jeremiah33:10 “Thus says the Lord: ‘Again there shall be heard in this place— of which you say
Jeremiah33:11 the voice of joy and the voice of gladness
Jeremiah33:12 “Thus says the Lord of hosts: ‘In this place which is desolate
Jeremiah33:13 In the cities of the mountains
Jeremiah33:14 ‘Behold
Jeremiah33:15 ‘In those days and at that time I will cause to grow up to David A Branch of righteousness; He shall execute judgment and righteousness in the earth.
Jeremiah33:16 In those days Judah will be saved
Jeremiah33:17 “For thus says the Lord: ‘David shall never lack a man to sit on the throne of the house of Israel;
Jeremiah33:18 nor shall the priests
Jeremiah33:19 And the word of the Lord came to Jeremiah
Jeremiah33:20 “Thus says the Lord: ‘If you can break My covenant with the day and My covenant with the night
Jeremiah33:21 then My covenant may also be broken with David My servant
Jeremiah33:22 As the host of heaven cannot be numbered
Jeremiah33:23 Moreover the word of the Lord came to Jeremiah
Jeremiah33:24 “Have you not considered what these people have spoken
Jeremiah33:25 “Thus says the Lord: ‘If My covenant is not with day and night
Jeremiah33:26 then I will cast away the descendants of Jacob and David My servant

Jeremiah34:2 “Thus says the Lord
Jeremiah34:3 And you shall not escape from his hand
Jeremiah34:4 Yet hear the word of the Lord
Jeremiah34:5 You shall die in peace; as in the ceremonies of your fathers
Jeremiah34:6 Then Jeremiah the prophet spoke all these words to Zedekiah king of Judah in Jerusalem
Jeremiah34:7 when the king of Babylon’s army fought against Jerusalem and all the cities of Judah that were left
Jeremiah34:8 This is the word that came to Jeremiah from the Lord
Jeremiah34:9 that every man should set free his male and female slave—a Hebrew man or woman— that no one should keep a Jewish brother in bondage.
Jeremiah34:10 Now when all the princes and all the people
Jeremiah34:11 But afterward they changed their minds and made the male and female slaves return
Jeremiah34:12 Therefore the word of the Lord came to Jeremiah from the Lord
Jeremiah34:13 “Thus says the Lord
Jeremiah34:14 “At the end of seven years let every man set free his Hebrew brother
Jeremiah34:15 Then you recently turned and did what was right in My sight—every man proclaiming liberty to his neighbor; and you made a covenant before Me in the house which is called by My name.
Jeremiah34:16 Then you turned around and profaned My name
Jeremiah34:17 “Therefore thus says the Lord: ‘You have not obeyed Me in proclaiming liberty
Jeremiah34:18 And I will give the men who have transgressed My covenant
Jeremiah34:19 the princes of Judah
Jeremiah34:20 I will give them into the hand of their enemies and into the hand of those who seek their life. Their dead bodies shall be for meat for the birds of the heaven and the beasts of the earth.
Jeremiah34:21 And I will give Zedekiah king of Judah and his princes into the hand of their enemies
Jeremiah34:22 Behold

Jeremiah35:4 and I brought them into the house of the Lord

Jeremiah36:1 Now it came to pass in the fourth year of Jehoiakim the son of Josiah
Jeremiah36:2 “Take a scroll of a book and write on it all the words that I have spoken to you against Israel
Jeremiah36:3 It may be that the house of Judah will hear all the adversities which I purpose to bring upon them
Jeremiah36:4 Then Jeremiah called Baruch the son of Neriah; and Baruch wrote on a scroll of a book
Jeremiah36:5 And Jeremiah commanded Baruch
Jeremiah36:6 You go
Jeremiah36:7 It may be that they will present their supplication before the Lord
Jeremiah36:8 And Baruch the son of Neriah did according to all that Jeremiah the prophet commanded him
Jeremiah36:9 Now it came to pass in the fifth year of Jehoiakim the son of Josiah
Jeremiah36:10 Then Baruch read from the book the words of Jeremiah in the house of the Lord
Jeremiah36:11 When Michaiah the son of Gemariah
Jeremiah36:12 he then went down to the king’s house
Jeremiah36:13 Then Michaiah declared to them all the words that he had heard when Baruch read the book in the hearing of the people.
Jeremiah36:14 Therefore all the princes sent Jehudi the son of Nethaniah
Jeremiah36:15 And they said to him
Jeremiah36:16 Now it happened
Jeremiah36:17 And they asked Baruch
Jeremiah36:18 So Baruch answered them
Jeremiah36:19 Then the princes said to Baruch
Jeremiah36:20 And they went to the king
Jeremiah36:21 So the king sent Jehudi to bring the scroll
Jeremiah36:22 Now the king was sitting in the winter house in the ninth month
Jeremiah36:23 And it happened
Jeremiah36:24 Yet they were not afraid
Jeremiah36:25 Nevertheless Elnathan
Jeremiah36:26 And the king commanded Jerahmeel the king’s son
Jeremiah36:27 Now after the king had burned the scroll with the words which Baruch had written at the instruction of Jeremiah
Jeremiah36:28 “Take yet another scroll
Jeremiah36:29 And you shall say to Jehoiakim king of Judah
