Jeremiah36:31 I will punish him
Jeremiah36:32 Then Jeremiah took another scroll and gave it to Baruch the scribe
Jeremiah37:1 Now King Zedekiah the son of Josiah reigned instead of Coniah the son of Jehoiakim
Jeremiah37:2 But neither he nor his servants nor the people of the land gave heed to the words of the Lord which He spoke by the prophet Jeremiah.
Jeremiah37:3 And Zedekiah the king sent Jehucal the son of Shelemiah
Jeremiah37:4 Now Jeremiah was coming and going among the people
Jeremiah37:5 Then Pharaoh’s army came up from Egypt; and when the Chaldeans who were besieging Jerusalem heard news of them
Jeremiah37:6 Then the word of the Lord came to the prophet Jeremiah
Jeremiah37:7 “Thus says the Lord
Jeremiah37:8 And the Chaldeans shall come back and fight against this city
Jeremiah37:9 Thus says the Lord: ‘Do not deceive yourselves
Jeremiah37:10 For though you had defeated the whole army of the Chaldeans who fight against you
Jeremiah37:11 And it happened
Jeremiah37:12 that Jeremiah went out of Jerusalem to go into the land of Benjamin to claim his property there among the people.
Jeremiah37:13 And when he was in the Gate of Benjamin
Jeremiah37:14 Then Jeremiah said
Jeremiah37:15 Therefore the princes were angry with Jeremiah
Jeremiah37:16 When Jeremiah entered the dungeon and the cells
Jeremiah37:17 then Zedekiah the king sent and took him out. The king asked him secretly in his house
Jeremiah37:18 Moreover Jeremiah said to King Zedekiah
Jeremiah37:19 Where now are your prophets who prophesied to you
Jeremiah37:20 Therefore please hear now
Jeremiah37:21 Then Zedekiah the king commanded that they should commit Jeremiah to the court of the prison
Jeremiah38:1 Now Shephatiah the son of Mattan
Jeremiah38:2 “Thus says the Lord: ‘He who remains in this city shall die by the sword
Jeremiah38:3 Thus says the Lord: ‘This city shall surely be given into the hand of the king of Babylon’s army
Jeremiah38:4 Therefore the princes said to the king
Jeremiah38:5 Then Zedekiah the king said
Jeremiah38:6 So they took Jeremiah and cast him into the dungeon of Malchiah the king’s son
Jeremiah38:7 Now Ebed-Melech the Ethiopian
Jeremiah38:8 Ebed-Melech went out of the king’s house and spoke to the king
Jeremiah38:9 “My lord the king
Jeremiah38:10 Then the king commanded Ebed-Melech the Ethiopian
Jeremiah38:11 So Ebed-Melech took the men with him and went into the house of the king under the treasury
Jeremiah38:12 Then Ebed-Melech the Ethiopian said to Jeremiah
Jeremiah38:13 So they pulled Jeremiah up with ropes and lifted him out of the dungeon. And Jeremiah remained in the court of the prison.
Jeremiah38:14 Then Zedekiah the king sent and had Jeremiah the prophet brought to him at the third entrance of the house of the Lord. And the king said to Jeremiah
Jeremiah38:15 Jeremiah said to Zedekiah
Jeremiah38:16 So Zedekiah the king swore secretly to Jeremiah
Jeremiah38:17 Then Jeremiah said to Zedekiah
Jeremiah38:18 But if you do not surrender to the king of Babylon’s princes
Jeremiah38:19 And Zedekiah the king said to Jeremiah
Jeremiah38:20 But Jeremiah said
Jeremiah38:21 But if you refuse to surrender
Jeremiah38:22 ‘Now behold
Jeremiah38:23 ‘So they shall surrender all your wives and children to the Chaldeans. You shall not escape from their hand
Jeremiah38:24 Then Zedekiah said to Jeremiah
Jeremiah38:25 But if the princes hear that I have talked with you
Jeremiah38:26 then you shall say to them
Jeremiah38:27 Then all the princes came to Jeremiah and asked him. And he told them according to all these words that the king had commanded. So they stopped speaking with him
Jeremiah38:28 Now Jeremiah remained in the court of the prison until the day that Jerusalem was taken. And he was there when Jerusalem was taken.
Jeremiah39:1 In the ninth year of Zedekiah king of Judah
Jeremiah39:2 In the eleventh year of Zedekiah
Jeremiah39:3 Then all the princes of the king of Babylon came in and sat in the Middle Gate: Nergal-Sharezer
Jeremiah39:4 So it was
Jeremiah39:5 But the Chaldean army pursued them and overtook Zedekiah in the plains of Jericho. And when they had captured him
Jeremiah39:6 Then the king of Babylon killed the sons of Zedekiah before his eyes in Riblah; the king of Babylon also killed all the nobles of Judah.
Jeremiah39:7 Moreover he put out Zedekiah’s eyes
Jeremiah39:8 And the Chaldeans burned the king’s house and the houses of the people with fire
Jeremiah39:9 Then Nebuzaradan the captain of the guard carried away captive to Babylon the remnant of the people who remained in the city and those who defected to him
Jeremiah39:10 But Nebuzaradan the captain of the guard left in the land of Judah the poor people
Jeremiah39:11 Now Nebuchadnezzar king of Babylon gave charge concerning Jeremiah to Nebuzaradan the captain of the guard

Jeremiah39:16 “Go and speak to Ebed-Melech the Ethiopian
Jeremiah39:17 But I will deliver you in that day
Jeremiah39:18 For I will surely deliver you

Jeremiah40:2 And the captain of the guard took Jeremiah and said to him: “The Lord your God has pronounced this doom on this place.
Jeremiah40:3 Now the Lord has brought it
Jeremiah40:4 And now look

Jeremiah40:9 And Gedaliah the son of Ahikam
Jeremiah40:10 As for me
Jeremiah40:11 Likewise
Jeremiah40:12 then all the Jews returned out of all places where they had been driven

Jeremiah41:1 Now it came to pass in the seventh month that Ishmael the son of Nethaniah
Jeremiah41:2 Then Ishmael the son of Nethaniah
Jeremiah41:3 Ishmael also struck down all the Jews who were with him
Jeremiah41:4 And it happened
Jeremiah41:5 that certain men came from Shechem
Jeremiah41:6 Now Ishmael the son of Nethaniah went out from Mizpah to meet them
Jeremiah41:7 So it was
Jeremiah41:8 But ten men were found among them who said to Ishmael
Jeremiah41:9 Now the pit into which Ishmael had cast all the dead bodies of the men whom he had slain
Jeremiah41:10 Then Ishmael carried away captive all the rest of the people who were in Mizpah
Jeremiah41:11 But when Johanan the son of Kareah and all the captains of the forces that were with him heard of all the evil that Ishmael the son of Nethaniah had done
Jeremiah41:12 they took all the men and went to fight with Ishmael the son of Nethaniah; and they found him by the great pool that is in Gibeon.
Jeremiah41:13 So it was
Jeremiah41:14 Then all the people whom Ishmael had carried away captive from Mizpah turned around and came back

Jeremiah41:17 And they departed and dwelt in the habitation of Chimham
Jeremiah41:18 because of the Chaldeans; for they were afraid of them
Jeremiah42:1 Now all the captains of the forces
Jeremiah42:2 and said to Jeremiah the prophet
Jeremiah42:3 that the Lord your God may show us the way in which we should walk and the thing we should do.”
Jeremiah42:4 Then Jeremiah the prophet said to them
Jeremiah42:5 So they said to Jeremiah
Jeremiah42:6 Whether it is pleasing or displeasing
Jeremiah42:7 And it happened after ten days that the word of the Lord came to Jeremiah.
Jeremiah42:8 Then he called Johanan the son of Kareah
Jeremiah42:9 and said to them
Jeremiah42:10 ‘If you will still remain in this land
Jeremiah42:11 Do not be afraid of the king of Babylon
Jeremiah42:12 And I will show you mercy
Jeremiah42:13 “But if you say
Jeremiah42:14 saying
Jeremiah42:15 Then hear now the word of the Lord
Jeremiah42:16 then it shall be that the sword which you feared shall overtake you there in the land of Egypt; the famine of which you were afraid shall follow close after you there in Egypt; and there you shall die.
Jeremiah42:17 So shall it be with all the men who set their faces to go to Egypt to dwell there. They shall die by the sword
Jeremiah42:18 “For thus says the Lord of hosts
Jeremiah42:19 “The Lord has said concerning you
Jeremiah42:20 For you were hypocrites in your hearts when you sent me to the Lord your God
Jeremiah42:21 And I have this day declared it to you
Jeremiah42:22 Now therefore
Jeremiah43:1 Now it happened
Jeremiah43:2 that Azariah the son of Hoshaiah
Jeremiah43:3 But Baruch the son of Neriah has set you against us
Jeremiah43:4 So Johanan the son of Kareah
Jeremiah43:5 But Johanan the son of Kareah and all the captains of the forces took all the remnant of Judah who had returned to dwell in the land of Judah
Jeremiah43:6 men
Jeremiah43:7 So they went to the land of Egypt
Jeremiah43:8 Then the word of the Lord came to Jeremiah in Tahpanhes
Jeremiah43:9 “Take large stones in your hand
Jeremiah43:10 and say to them
Jeremiah43:11 When he comes
Jeremiah43:12 I will kindle a fire in the houses of the gods of Egypt
Jeremiah43:13 He shall also break the sacred pillars of Beth Shemesh that are in the land of Egypt; and the houses of the gods of the Egyptians he shall burn with fire.” ’ ”
Jeremiah44:1 The word that came to Jeremiah concerning all the Jews who dwell in the land of Egypt
Jeremiah44:2 “Thus says the Lord of hosts
Jeremiah44:3 because of their wickedness which they have committed to provoke Me to anger
Jeremiah44:4 However I have sent to you all My servants the prophets
Jeremiah44:5 But they did not listen or incline their ear to turn from their wickedness
Jeremiah44:6 So My fury and My anger were poured out and kindled in the cities of Judah and in the streets of Jerusalem; and they are wasted and desolate
Jeremiah44:7 “Now therefore
Jeremiah44:8 in that you provoke Me to wrath with the works of your hands
Jeremiah44:9 Have you forgotten the wickedness of your fathers
Jeremiah44:10 They have not been humbled
Jeremiah44:11 “Therefore thus says the Lord of hosts
Jeremiah44:12 And I will take the remnant of Judah who have set their faces to go into the land of Egypt to dwell there
Jeremiah44:13 For I will punish those who dwell in the land of Egypt
Jeremiah44:14 so that none of the remnant of Judah who have gone into the land of Egypt to dwell there shall escape or survive
Jeremiah44:15 Then all the men who knew that their wives had burned incense to other gods
Jeremiah44:16 “ As for the word that you have spoken to us in the name of the Lord
Jeremiah44:17 But we will certainly do whatever has gone out of our own mouth
Jeremiah44:18 But since we stopped burning incense to the queen of heaven and pouring out drink offerings to her
Jeremiah44:19 The women also said
Jeremiah44:20 Then Jeremiah spoke to all the people—the men
Jeremiah44:21 “The incense that you burned in the cities of Judah and in the streets of Jerusalem
Jeremiah44:22 So the Lord could no longer bear it

Jeremiah48:1 Against Moab. Thus says the Lord of hosts
Jeremiah48:2 No more praise of Moab. In Heshbon they have devised evil against her: ‘Come
Jeremiah48:3 A voice of crying shall be from Horonaim: ‘Plundering and great destruction!’
Jeremiah48:4 “Moab is destroyed; Her little ones have caused a cry to be heard;
Jeremiah48:5 For in the Ascent of Luhith they ascend with continual weeping; For in the descent of Horonaim the enemies have heard a cry of destruction.
Jeremiah48:6 “Flee
Jeremiah48:7 For because you have trusted in your works and your treasures
Jeremiah48:8 And the plunderer shall come against every city; No one shall escape. The valley also shall perish

Jeremiah48:10 Cursed is he who does the work of the Lord deceitfully
Jeremiah48:11 “Moab has been at ease from his youth; He has settled on his dregs
Jeremiah48:12 “Therefore behold
Jeremiah48:13 Moab shall be ashamed of Chemosh

Jeremiah48:19 O inhabitant of Aroer
Jeremiah48:20 Moab is shamed

Jeremiah48:23 On Kirjathaim and Beth Gamul and Beth Meon
Jeremiah48:24 On Kerioth and Bozrah

Jeremiah48:27 For was not Israel a derision to you? Was he found among thieves? For whenever you speak of him
Jeremiah48:28 You who dwell in Moab
Jeremiah48:29 “We have heard the pride of Moab (He is exceedingly proud)
Jeremiah48:30 “I know his wrath
Jeremiah48:31 Therefore I will wail for Moab
Jeremiah48:32 O vine of Sibmah! I will weep for you with the weeping of Jazer. Your plants have gone over the sea
Jeremiah48:33 Joy and gladness are taken From the plentiful field And from the land of Moab; I have caused wine to fail from the winepresses; No one will tread with joyous shouting— Not joyous shouting!
Jeremiah48:34 “From the cry of Heshbon to Elealeh and to Jahaz They have uttered their voice
Jeremiah48:35 “Moreover
Jeremiah48:36 Therefore My heart shall wail like flutes for Moab
Jeremiah48:37 “For every head shall be bald
Jeremiah48:38 A general lamentation On all the housetops of Moab
Jeremiah48:39 “They shall wail: ‘How she is broken down! How Moab has turned her back with shame!’ So Moab shall be a derision And a dismay to all those about her.”

Jeremiah50:5 They shall ask the way to Zion

Jeremiah50:7 All who found them have devoured them; And their adversaries said
Jeremiah50:8 “Move from the midst of Babylon
Jeremiah50:9 For behold
Jeremiah50:10 And Chaldea shall become plunder; All who plunder her shall be satisfied

Jeremiah50:13 Because of the wrath of the LordShe shall not be inhabited

Jeremiah50:18 Therefore thus says the Lord of hosts
Jeremiah50:19 But I will bring back Israel to his home
Jeremiah50:20 In those days and in that time

Jeremiah50:22 A sound of battle is in the land
Jeremiah50:23 How the hammer of the whole earth has been cut apart and broken! How Babylon has become a desolation among the nations!
Jeremiah50:24 I have laid a snare for you; You have indeed been trapped
Jeremiah50:25 The Lord has opened His armory
Jeremiah50:26 Come against her from the farthest border; Open her storehouses; Cast her up as heaps of ruins

Jeremiah50:31 “Behold

Jeremiah50:34 Their Redeemer is strong; The Lord of hosts is His name. He will thoroughly plead their case

Jeremiah50:36 A sword is against the soothsayers

Jeremiah51:1 Thus says the Lord: “Behold
Jeremiah51:2 And I will send winnowers to Babylon
Jeremiah51:3 Against her let the archer bend his bow
Jeremiah51:4 Thus the slain shall fall in the land of the Chaldeans
Jeremiah51:5 For Israel is not forsaken
Jeremiah51:6 Flee from the midst of Babylon
Jeremiah51:7 Babylon was a golden cup in the Lord’s hand
Jeremiah51:8 Babylon has suddenly fallen and been destroyed. Wail for her! Take balm for her pain; Perhaps she may be healed.
Jeremiah51:9 We would have healed Babylon
Jeremiah51:10 The Lord has revealed our righteousness. Come and let us declare in Zion the work of the Lord our God.
Jeremiah51:11 Make the arrows bright! Gather the shields! The Lord has raised up the spirit of the kings of the Medes. For His plan is against Babylon to destroy it
Jeremiah51:12 Set up the standard on the walls of Babylon; Make the guard strong
Jeremiah51:13 O you who dwell by many waters
Jeremiah51:14 The Lord of hosts has sworn by Himself: “Surely I will fill you with men
Jeremiah51:15 He has made the earth by His power; He has established the world by His wisdom
Jeremiah51:16 When He utters His voice— There is a multitude of waters in the heavens: “He causes the vapors to ascend from the ends of the earth; He makes lightnings for the rain; He brings the wind out of His treasuries.”
Jeremiah51:17 Everyone is dull-hearted
Jeremiah51:18 They are futile
Jeremiah51:19 The Portion of Jacob is not like them
Jeremiah51:20 “You are My battle-ax and weapons of war: For with you I will break the nation in pieces; With you I will destroy kingdoms;
Jeremiah51:21 With you I will break in pieces the horse and its rider; With you I will break in pieces the chariot and its rider;
Jeremiah51:22 With you also I will break in pieces man and woman; With you I will break in pieces old and young; With you I will break in pieces the young man and the maiden;
Jeremiah51:23 With you also I will break in pieces the shepherd and his flock; With you I will break in pieces the farmer and his yoke of oxen; And with you I will break in pieces governors and rulers.
Jeremiah51:24 “And I will repay Babylon And all the inhabitants of Chaldea For all the evil they have done In Zion in your sight
Jeremiah51:25 “Behold
Jeremiah51:26 They shall not take from you a stone for a corner Nor a stone for a foundation
Jeremiah51:27 Set up a banner in the land
Jeremiah51:28 Prepare against her the nations
Jeremiah51:29 And the land will tremble and sorrow; For every purpose of the Lord shall be performed against Babylon
Jeremiah51:30 The mighty men of Babylon have ceased fighting
Jeremiah51:31 One runner will run to meet another
Jeremiah51:32 The passages are blocked
Jeremiah51:33 For thus says the Lord of hosts
Jeremiah51:34 “Nebuchadnezzar the king of Babylon Has devoured me
Jeremiah51:35 Let the violence done to me and my flesh be upon Babylon
Jeremiah51:36 Therefore thus says the Lord: “Behold
Jeremiah51:37 Babylon shall become a heap
Jeremiah51:38 They shall roar together like lions
Jeremiah51:39 In their excitement I will prepare their feasts; I will make them drunk
Jeremiah51:40 “I will bring them down Like lambs to the slaughter
Jeremiah51:41 “Oh
Jeremiah51:42 The sea has come up over Babylon; She is covered with the multitude of its waves.
Jeremiah51:43 Her cities are a desolation
Jeremiah51:44 I will punish Bel in Babylon
Jeremiah51:45 “My people
Jeremiah51:46 And lest your heart faint
Jeremiah51:47 Therefore behold
Jeremiah51:48 Then the heavens and the earth and all that is in them Shall sing joyously over Babylon; For the plunderers shall come to her from the north
Jeremiah51:49 As Babylon has caused the slain of Israel to fall
Jeremiah51:50 You who have escaped the sword
Jeremiah51:51 We are ashamed because we have heard reproach. Shame has covered our faces
Jeremiah51:52 “Therefore behold
Jeremiah51:53 Though Babylon were to mount up to heaven
Jeremiah51:54 The sound of a cry comes from Babylon
Jeremiah51:55 Because the Lord is plundering Babylon And silencing her loud voice
Jeremiah51:56 Because the plunderer comes against her
Jeremiah51:57 “And I will make drunk Her princes and wise men

Jeremiah52:1 Zedekiah was twenty-one years old when he became king
Jeremiah52:2 He also did evil in the sight of the Lord
Jeremiah52:3 For because of the anger of the Lord this happened in Jerusalem and Judah
Jeremiah52:4 Now it came to pass in the ninth year of his reign
Jeremiah52:5 So the city was besieged until the eleventh year of King Zedekiah.
Jeremiah52:6 By the fourth month
Jeremiah52:7 Then the city wall was broken through
Jeremiah52:8 But the army of the Chaldeans pursued the king
Jeremiah52:9 So they took the king and brought him up to the king of Babylon at Riblah in the land of Hamath
Jeremiah52:10 Then the king of Babylon killed the sons of Zedekiah before his eyes. And he killed all the princes of Judah in Riblah.
Jeremiah52:11 He also put out the eyes of Zedekiah; and the king of Babylon bound him in bronze fetters
Jeremiah52:12 Now in the fifth month
Jeremiah52:13 He burned the house of the Lord and the king’s house; all the houses of Jerusalem
Jeremiah52:14 And all the army of the Chaldeans who were with the captain of the guard broke down all the walls of Jerusalem all around.
Jeremiah52:15 Then Nebuzaradan the captain of the guard carried away captive some of the poor people
Jeremiah52:16 But Nebuzaradan the captain of the guard left some of the poor of the land as vinedressers and farmers.
Jeremiah52:17 The bronze pillars that were in the house of the Lord
Jeremiah52:18 They also took away the pots
Jeremiah52:19 The basins
Jeremiah52:20 The two pillars
Jeremiah52:21 Now concerning the pillars: the height of one pillar was eighteen cubits
Jeremiah52:22 A capital of bronze was on it; and the height of one capital was five cubits
Jeremiah52:23 There were ninety-six pomegranates on the sides; all the pomegranates
Jeremiah52:24 The captain of the guard took Seraiah the chief priest
Jeremiah52:25 He also took out of the city an officer who had charge of the men of war
Jeremiah52:26 And Nebuzaradan the captain of the guard took these and brought them to the king of Babylon at Riblah.
Jeremiah52:27 Then the king of Babylon struck them and put them to death at Riblah in the land of Hamath. Thus Judah was carried away captive from its own land.
Jeremiah52:28 These are the people whom Nebuchadnezzar carried away captive: in the seventh year
Jeremiah52:29 in the eighteenth year of Nebuchadnezzar he carried away captive from Jerusalem eight hundred and thirty-two persons;
Jeremiah52:30 in the twenty-third year of Nebuchadnezzar
Jeremiah52:31 Now it came to pass in the thirty-seventh year of the captivity of Jehoiachin king of Judah
Jeremiah52:32 And he spoke kindly to him and gave him a more prominent seat than those of the kings who were with him in Babylon.
Jeremiah52:33 So Jehoiachin changed from his prison garments
Jeremiah52:34 And as for his provisions
Lamentations1:1 How lonely sits the city That was full of people! How like a widow is she
Lamentations1:2 She weeps bitterly in the night
Lamentations1:3 Judah has gone into captivity
Lamentations1:4 The roads to Zion mourn Because no one comes to the set feasts. All her gates are desolate; Her priests sigh
Lamentations1:5 Her adversaries have become the master
Lamentations1:6 And from the daughter of Zion All her splendor has departed. Her princes have become like deer That find no pasture
Lamentations1:7 In the days of her affliction and roaming
Lamentations1:8 Jerusalem has sinned gravely
Lamentations1:9 Her uncleanness is in her skirts; She did not consider her destiny; Therefore her collapse was awesome; She had no comforter. “O Lord
Lamentations1:10 The adversary has spread his hand Over all her pleasant things; For she has seen the nations enter her sanctuary
Lamentations1:11 All her people sigh
Lamentations1:12 “ Is it nothing to you
Lamentations1:13 “From above He has sent fire into my bones
Lamentations1:14 “The yoke of my transgressions was bound; They were woven together by His hands
Lamentations1:15 “The Lord has trampled underfoot all my mighty men in my midst; He has called an assembly against me To crush my young men; The Lord trampled as in a winepress The virgin daughter of Judah.
Lamentations1:16 “For these things I weep; My eye
Lamentations1:17 Zion spreads out her hands
Lamentations1:18 “The Lord is righteous
Lamentations1:19 “I called for my lovers
Lamentations1:20 “See
Lamentations1:21 “They have heard that I sigh
Lamentations1:22 “Let all their wickedness come before You
Lamentations2:1 How the Lord has covered the daughter of Zion With a cloud in His anger! He cast down from heaven to the earth The beauty of Israel
Lamentations2:2 The Lord has swallowed up and has not pitied All the dwelling places of Jacob. He has thrown down in His wrath The strongholds of the daughter of Judah; He has brought them down to the ground; He has profaned the kingdom and its princes.
Lamentations2:3 He has cut off in fierce anger Every horn of Israel; He has drawn back His right hand From before the enemy. He has blazed against Jacob like a flaming fire Devouring all around.
Lamentations2:4 Standing like an enemy
Lamentations2:5 The Lord was like an enemy. He has swallowed up Israel
Lamentations2:6 He has done violence to His tabernacle
Lamentations2:7 The Lord has spurned His altar
Lamentations2:8 The Lord has purposed to destroy The wall of the daughter of Zion. He has stretched out a line; He has not withdrawn His hand from destroying; Therefore He has caused the rampart and wall to lament; They languished together.
Lamentations2:9 Her gates have sunk into the ground; He has destroyed and broken her bars. Her king and her princes are among the nations; The Law is no more
Lamentations2:10 The elders of the daughter of Zion Sit on the ground and keep silence; They throw dust on their heads And gird themselves with sackcloth. The virgins of Jerusalem Bow their heads to the ground.
Lamentations2:11 My eyes fail with tears
Lamentations2:12 They say to their mothers
Lamentations2:13 How shall I console you? To what shall I liken you
Lamentations2:14 Your prophets have seen for you False and deceptive visions; They have not uncovered your iniquity
Lamentations2:15 All who pass by clap their hands at you; They hiss and shake their heads At the daughter of Jerusalem: “ Is this the city that is called ‘The perfection of beauty
Lamentations2:16 All your enemies have opened their mouth against you; They hiss and gnash their teeth. They say
Lamentations2:17 The Lord has done what He purposed; He has fulfilled His word Which He commanded in days of old. He has thrown down and has not pitied
Lamentations2:18 Their heart cried out to the Lord
Lamentations2:19 “Arise
Lamentations2:20 “See
Lamentations2:21 “Young and old lie On the ground in the streets; My virgins and my young men Have fallen by the sword; You have slain them in the day of Your anger
Lamentations2:22 “You have invited as to a feast day The terrors that surround me. In the day of the Lord’s anger There was no refugee or survivor. Those whom I have borne and brought up My enemies have destroyed.”
Lamentations3:1 I am the man who has seen affliction by the rod of His wrath.
Lamentations3:2 He has led me and made me walk In darkness and not in light.
Lamentations3:3 Surely He has turned His hand against me Time and time again throughout the day.
Lamentations3:4 He has aged my flesh and my skin
Lamentations3:5 He has besieged me And surrounded me with bitterness and woe.
Lamentations3:6 He has set me in dark places Like the dead of long ago.
Lamentations3:7 He has hedged me in so that I cannot get out; He has made my chain heavy.
Lamentations3:8 Even when I cry and shout
Lamentations3:9 He has blocked my ways with hewn stone; He has made my paths crooked.
Lamentations3:10 He has been to me a bear lying in wait
Lamentations3:11 He has turned aside my ways and torn me in pieces; He has made me desolate.
Lamentations3:12 He has bent His bow And set me up as a target for the arrow.
Lamentations3:13 He has caused the arrows of His quiver To pierce my loins.
Lamentations3:14 I have become the ridicule of all my people— Their taunting song all the day.
Lamentations3:15 He has filled me with bitterness
Lamentations3:16 He has also broken my teeth with gravel
Lamentations3:17 You have moved my soul far from peace; I have forgotten prosperity.
Lamentations3:18 And I said
Lamentations3:19 Remember my affliction and roaming
Lamentations3:20 My soul still remembers And sinks within me.
Lamentations3:21 This I recall to my mind
Lamentations3:22 Through the Lord’s mercies we are not consumed
Lamentations3:23 They are new every morning; Great is Your faithfulness.
Lamentations3:24 “The Lord is my portion
Lamentations3:25 The Lord is good to those who wait for Him
Lamentations3:26 It is good that one should hope and wait quietly For the salvation of the Lord.
Lamentations3:27 It is good for a man to bear The yoke in his youth.
Lamentations3:28 Let him sit alone and keep silent
Lamentations3:29 Let him put his mouth in the dust— There may yet be hope.
Lamentations3:30 Let him give his cheek to the one who strikes him
Lamentations3:31 For the Lord will not cast off forever.
Lamentations3:32 Though He causes grief
Lamentations3:33 For He does not afflict willingly
Lamentations3:34 To crush under one’s feet All the prisoners of the earth
Lamentations3:35 To turn aside the justice due a man Before the face of the Most High
Lamentations3:36 Or subvert a man in his cause— The Lord does not approve.
Lamentations3:37 Who is he who speaks and it comes to pass
Lamentations3:38 Is it not from the mouth of the Most High That woe and well-being proceed?
Lamentations3:39 Why should a living man complain
Lamentations3:40 Let us search out and examine our ways
Lamentations3:41 Let us lift our hearts and hands To God in heaven.
Lamentations3:42 We have transgressed and rebelled; You have not pardoned.
Lamentations3:43 You have covered Yourself with anger And pursued us; You have slain and not pitied.
Lamentations3:44 You have covered Yourself with a cloud
Lamentations3:45 You have made us an offscouring and refuse In the midst of the peoples.
Lamentations3:46 All our enemies Have opened their mouths against us.
Lamentations3:47 Fear and a snare have come upon us
Lamentations3:48 My eyes overflow with rivers of water For the destruction of the daughter of my people.
Lamentations3:49 My eyes flow and do not cease
Lamentations3:50 Till the Lord from heaven Looks down and sees.
Lamentations3:51 My eyes bring suffering to my soul Because of all the daughters of my city.
Lamentations3:52 My enemies without cause Hunted me down like a bird.
Lamentations3:53 They silenced my life in the pit And threw stones at me.
Lamentations3:54 The waters flowed over my head; I said
Lamentations3:55 I called on Your name
Lamentations3:56 You have heard my voice: “Do not hide Your ear From my sighing
Lamentations3:57 You drew near on the day I called on You
Lamentations3:58 O Lord
Lamentations3:59 O Lord
Lamentations3:60 You have seen all their vengeance
Lamentations3:61 You have heard their reproach
Lamentations3:62 The lips of my enemies And their whispering against me all the day.
Lamentations3:63 Look at their sitting down and their rising up; I am their taunting song.
Lamentations3:64 Repay them
Lamentations3:65 Give them a veiled heart; Your curse be upon them!
Lamentations3:66 In Your anger
Lamentations4:1 How the gold has become dim! How changed the fine gold! The stones of the sanctuary are scattered At the head of every street.
Lamentations4:2 The precious sons of Zion
Lamentations4:3 Even the jackals present their breasts To nurse their young; But the daughter of my people is cruel
Lamentations4:4 The tongue of the infant clings To the roof of its mouth for thirst; The young children ask for bread
Lamentations4:5 Those who ate delicacies Are desolate in the streets; Those who were brought up in scarlet Embrace ash heaps.
Lamentations4:6 The punishment of the iniquity of the daughter of my people Is greater than the punishment of the sin of Sodom
Lamentations4:7 Her Nazirites were brighter than snow And whiter than milk; They were more ruddy in body than rubies
Lamentations4:8 Now their appearance is blacker than soot; They go unrecognized in the streets; Their skin clings to their bones
Lamentations4:9 Those slain by the sword are better off Than those who die of hunger; For these pine away
Lamentations4:10 The hands of the compassionate women Have cooked their own children; They became food for them In the destruction of the daughter of my people.
Lamentations4:11 The Lord has fulfilled His fury
Lamentations4:12 The kings of the earth
Lamentations4:13 Because of the sins of her prophets And the iniquities of her priests
Lamentations4:14 They wandered blind in the streets; They have defiled themselves with blood
Lamentations4:15 They cried out to them
Lamentations4:16 The face of the Lord scattered them; He no longer regards them. The people do not respect the priests Nor show favor to the elders.
Lamentations4:17 Still our eyes failed us
Lamentations4:18 They tracked our steps So that we could not walk in our streets. Our end was near; Our days were over
Lamentations4:19 Our pursuers were swifter Than the eagles of the heavens. They pursued us on the mountains And lay in wait for us in the wilderness.
Lamentations4:20 The breath of our nostrils
Lamentations4:21 Rejoice and be glad
Lamentations4:22 The punishment of your iniquity is accomplished
Lamentations5:1 Remember
Lamentations5:2 Our inheritance has been turned over to aliens
Lamentations5:3 We have become orphans and waifs
Lamentations5:4 We pay for the water we drink
Lamentations5:5 They pursue at our heels; We labor and have no rest.
Lamentations5:6 We have given our hand to the Egyptians And the Assyrians
Lamentations5:7 Our fathers sinned and are no more
Lamentations5:8 Servants rule over us; There is none to deliver us from their hand.
Lamentations5:9 We get our bread at the risk of our lives
Lamentations5:10 Our skin is hot as an oven
Lamentations5:11 They ravished the women in Zion
Lamentations5:12 Princes were hung up by their hands
Lamentations5:13 Young men ground at the millstones; Boys staggered under loads of wood.
Lamentations5:14 The elders have ceased gathering at the gate
Lamentations5:15 The joy of our heart has ceased; Our dance has turned into mourning.
Lamentations5:16 The crown has fallen from our head. Woe to us
Lamentations5:17 Because of this our heart is faint; Because of these things our eyes grow dim;
Lamentations5:18 Because of Mount Zion which is desolate
Lamentations5:19 You
Lamentations5:20 Why do You forget us forever
Lamentations5:21 Turn us back to You
Lamentations5:22 Unless You have utterly rejected us
Ezekiel1:1 Now it came to pass in the thirtieth year
Ezekiel1:2 On the fifth day of the month
Ezekiel1:3 the word of the Lord came expressly to Ezekiel the priest
Ezekiel1:4 Then I looked
Ezekiel1:5 Also from within it came the likeness of four living creatures. And this was their appearance: they had the likeness of a man.
Ezekiel1:6 Each one had four faces
Ezekiel1:7 Their legs were straight
Ezekiel1:8 The hands of a man were under their wings on their four sides; and each of the four had faces and wings.
Ezekiel1:9 Their wings touched one another. The creatures did not turn when they went
Ezekiel1:10 As for the likeness of their faces
Ezekiel1:11 Thus were their faces. Their wings stretched upward; two wings of each one touched one another
Ezekiel1:12 And each one went straight forward; they went wherever the spirit wanted to go
Ezekiel1:13 As for the likeness of the living creatures
Ezekiel1:14 And the living creatures ran back and forth
Ezekiel1:15 Now as I looked at the living creatures
Ezekiel1:16 The appearance of the wheels and their workings was like the color of beryl
Ezekiel1:17 When they moved
Ezekiel1:18 As for their rims
Ezekiel1:19 When the living creatures went
Ezekiel1:20 Wherever the spirit wanted to go
Ezekiel1:21 When those went
Ezekiel1:22 The likeness of the firmament above the heads of the living creatures was like the color of an awesome crystal
Ezekiel1:23 And under the firmament their wings spread out straight
Ezekiel1:24 When they went
Ezekiel1:25 A voice came from above the firmament that was over their heads; whenever they stood
Ezekiel1:26 And above the firmament over their heads was the likeness of a throne
Ezekiel1:27 Also from the appearance of His waist and upward I saw
Ezekiel1:28 Like the appearance of a rainbow in a cloud on a rainy day

Ezekiel16:48 “ As I live

Ezekiel18:2 “What do you mean when you use this proverb concerning the land of Israel

Ezekiel18:4 “Behold
Ezekiel18:5 But if a man is just And does what is lawful and right;
Ezekiel18:6 If he has not eaten on the mountains
Ezekiel18:7 If he has not oppressed anyone
Ezekiel18:8 If he has not exacted usury Nor taken any increase
Ezekiel18:9 If he has walked in My statutes And kept My judgments faithfully— He is just; He shall surely live!” Says the Lord God.
Ezekiel18:10 “If he begets a son who is a robber Or a shedder of blood
Ezekiel18:11 And does none of those duties
Ezekiel18:12 If he has oppressed the poor and needy
Ezekiel18:13 If he has exacted usury Or taken increase— Shall he then live? He shall not live! If he has done any of these abominations
Ezekiel18:14 “ If
Ezekiel18:15 Who has not eaten on the mountains
Ezekiel18:16 Has not oppressed anyone
Ezekiel18:17 Who has withdrawn his hand from the poor And not received usury or increase
Ezekiel18:18 “ As for his father
Ezekiel18:19 “Yet you say
Ezekiel18:20 The soul who sins shall die. The son shall not bear the guilt of the father
Ezekiel18:21 “But if a wicked man turns from all his sins which he has committed
Ezekiel18:22 None of the transgressions which he has committed shall be remembered against him; because of the righteousness which he has done
Ezekiel18:23 Do I have any pleasure at all that the wicked should die?” says the Lord God
Ezekiel18:24 “But when a righteous man turns away from his righteousness and commits iniquity
Ezekiel18:25 “Yet you say
Ezekiel18:26 When a righteous man turns away from his righteousness
Ezekiel18:27 Again
Ezekiel18:28 Because he considers and turns away from all the transgressions which he committed
Ezekiel18:29 Yet the house of Israel says
Ezekiel18:30 “Therefore I will judge you
Ezekiel18:31 Cast away from you all the transgressions which you have committed
Ezekiel18:32 For I have no pleasure in the death of one who dies
Ezekiel19:1 “Moreover take up a lamentation for the princes of Israel
Ezekiel19:2 and say: ‘What is your mother? A lioness: She lay down among the lions; Among the young lions she nourished her cubs.
Ezekiel19:3 She brought up one of her cubs
Ezekiel19:4 The nations also heard of him; He was trapped in their pit
Ezekiel19:5 ‘When she saw that she waited
Ezekiel19:6 He roved among the lions
Ezekiel19:7 He knew their desolate places
Ezekiel19:8 Then the nations set against him from the provinces on every side
Ezekiel19:9 They put him in a cage with chains
Ezekiel19:10 ‘Your mother was like a vine in your bloodline
Ezekiel19:11 She had strong branches for scepters of rulers. She towered in stature above the thick branches
Ezekiel19:12 But she was plucked up in fury
Ezekiel19:13 And now she is planted in the wilderness
Ezekiel19:14 Fire has come out from a rod of her branches And devoured her fruit

Ezekiel20:9 But I acted for My name’s sake

Ezekiel20:11 And I gave them My statutes and showed them My judgments
Ezekiel20:12 Moreover I also gave them My Sabbaths
Ezekiel20:13 Yet the house of Israel rebelled against Me in the wilderness; they did not walk in My statutes; they despised My judgments

Ezekiel20:16 because they despised My judgments and did not walk in My statutes
Ezekiel20:17 Nevertheless My eye spared them from destruction. I did not make an end of them in the wilderness.

Ezekiel20:19 I am the Lord your God: Walk in My statutes
Ezekiel20:20 hallow My Sabbaths

Ezekiel22:8 You have despised My holy things and profaned My Sabbaths.
Ezekiel22:9 In you are men who slander to cause bloodshed; in you are those who eat on the mountains; in your midst they commit lewdness.
Ezekiel22:10 In you men uncover their fathers’ nakedness; in you they violate women who are set apart during their impurity.
Ezekiel22:11 One commits abomination with his neighbor’s wife; another lewdly defiles his daughter-in-law; and another in you violates his sister
Ezekiel22:12 In you they take bribes to shed blood; you take usury and increase; you have made profit from your neighbors by extortion
Ezekiel22:13 “Behold

Ezekiel23:10 They uncovered her nakedness
Ezekiel23:11 “Now although her sister Oholibah saw this

Ezekiel23:15 Girded with belts around their waists

Ezekiel23:23 The Babylonians
Ezekiel23:24 And they shall come against you With chariots

Ezekiel23:29 They will deal hatefully with you

Ezekiel23:37 For they have committed adultery
Ezekiel23:38 Moreover they have done this to Me: They have defiled My sanctuary on the same day and profaned My Sabbaths.
Ezekiel23:39 For after they had slain their children for their idols
Ezekiel23:40 “Furthermore you sent for men to come from afar
Ezekiel23:41 You sat on a stately couch
Ezekiel23:42 The sound of a carefree multitude was with her
Ezekiel23:43 Then I said concerning her who had grown old in adulteries
Ezekiel23:44 Yet they went in to her
Ezekiel23:45 But righteous men will judge them after the manner of adulteresses

Ezekiel23:49 They shall repay you for your lewdness

Ezekiel27:2 “Now
Ezekiel27:3 and say to Tyre
Ezekiel27:4 Your borders are in the midst of the seas. Your builders have perfected your beauty.
Ezekiel27:5 They made all your planks of fir trees from Senir; They took a cedar from Lebanon to make you a mast.
Ezekiel27:6 Of oaks from Bashan they made your oars; The company of Ashurites have inlaid your planks With ivory from the coasts of Cyprus.
Ezekiel27:7 Fine embroidered linen from Egypt was what you spread for your sail; Blue and purple from the coasts of Elishah was what covered you.
Ezekiel27:8 “Inhabitants of Sidon and Arvad were your oarsmen; Your wise men
Ezekiel27:9 Elders of Gebal and its wise men Were in you to caulk your seams; All the ships of the sea And their oarsmen were in you To market your merchandise.
Ezekiel27:10 “Those from Persia
Ezekiel27:11 Men of Arvad with your army were on your walls all around
Ezekiel27:12 “Tarshish was your merchant because of your many luxury goods. They gave you silver
Ezekiel27:13 Javan
Ezekiel27:14 Those from the house of Togarmah traded for your wares with horses
Ezekiel27:15 The men of Dedan were your traders; many isles were the market of your hand. They brought you ivory tusks and ebony as payment.
Ezekiel27:16 Syria was your merchant because of the abundance of goods you made. They gave you for your wares emeralds
Ezekiel27:17 Judah and the land of Israel were your traders. They traded for your merchandise wheat of Minnith
Ezekiel27:18 Damascus was your merchant because of the abundance of goods you made
Ezekiel27:19 Dan and Javan paid for your wares
Ezekiel27:20 Dedan was your merchant in saddlecloths for riding.
Ezekiel27:21 Arabia and all the princes of Kedar were your regular merchants. They traded with you in lambs
Ezekiel27:22 The merchants of Sheba and Raamah were your merchants. They traded for your wares the choicest spices
Ezekiel27:23 Haran
Ezekiel27:24 These were your merchants in choice items—in purple clothes
Ezekiel27:25 “The ships of Tarshish were carriers of your merchandise. You were filled and very glorious in the midst of the seas.
Ezekiel27:26 Your oarsmen brought you into many waters
Ezekiel27:27 “Your riches
Ezekiel27:28 The common-land will shake at the sound of the cry of your pilots.
Ezekiel27:29 “All who handle the oar
Ezekiel27:30 They will make their voice heard because of you; They will cry bitterly and cast dust on their heads; They will roll about in ashes;
Ezekiel27:31 They will shave themselves completely bald because of you
Ezekiel27:32 In their wailing for you They will take up a lamentation
Ezekiel27:33 ‘When your wares went out by sea
Ezekiel27:34 But you are broken by the seas in the depths of the waters; Your merchandise and the entire company will fall in your midst.
Ezekiel27:35 All the inhabitants of the isles will be astonished at you; Their kings will be greatly afraid
Ezekiel27:36 The merchants among the peoples will hiss at you; You will become a horror

Ezekiel31:1 Now it came to pass in the eleventh year
Ezekiel31:2 “Son of man
Ezekiel31:3 Indeed Assyria was a cedar in Lebanon
Ezekiel31:4 The waters made it grow; Underground waters gave it height
Ezekiel31:5 ‘Therefore its height was exalted above all the trees of the field; Its boughs were multiplied
Ezekiel31:6 All the birds of the heavens made their nests in its boughs; Under its branches all the beasts of the field brought forth their young; And in its shadow all great nations made their home.
Ezekiel31:7 ‘Thus it was beautiful in greatness and in the length of its branches
Ezekiel31:8 The cedars in the garden of God could not hide it; The fir trees were not like its boughs
Ezekiel31:9 I made it beautiful with a multitude of branches
Ezekiel31:10 “Therefore thus says the Lord God: ‘Because you have increased in height
Ezekiel31:11 therefore I will deliver it into the hand of the mighty one of the nations
Ezekiel31:12 And aliens
Ezekiel31:13 ‘On its ruin will remain all the birds of the heavens
Ezekiel31:14 ‘So that no trees by the waters may ever again exalt themselves for their height

Ezekiel40:1 In the twenty-fifth year of our captivity
Ezekiel40:2 In the visions of God He took me into the land of Israel and set me on a very high mountain; on it toward the south was something like the structure of a city.
Ezekiel40:3 He took me there
Ezekiel40:4 And the man said to me
Ezekiel40:5 Now there was a wall all around the outside of the temple. In the man’s hand was a measuring rod six cubits long
Ezekiel40:6 Then he went to the gateway which faced east; and he went up its stairs and measured the threshold of the gateway
Ezekiel40:7 Each gate chamber was one rod long and one rod wide; between the gate chambers was a space of five cubits; and the threshold of the gateway by the vestibule of the inside gate was one rod.
Ezekiel40:8 He also measured the vestibule of the inside gate
Ezekiel40:9 Then he measured the vestibule of the gateway
Ezekiel40:10 In the eastern gateway were three gate chambers on one side and three on the other; the three were all the same size; also the gateposts were of the same size on this side and that side.
Ezekiel40:11 He measured the width of the entrance to the gateway
Ezekiel40:12 There was a space in front of the gate chambers
Ezekiel40:13 Then he measured the gateway from the roof of one gate chamber to the roof of the other; the width was twenty-five cubits
Ezekiel40:14 He measured the gateposts
Ezekiel40:15 From the front of the entrance gate to the front of the vestibule of the inner gate was fifty cubits.
Ezekiel40:16 There were beveled window frames in the gate chambers and in their intervening archways on the inside of the gateway all around
Ezekiel40:17 Then he brought me into the outer court; and there were chambers and a pavement made all around the court; thirty chambers faced the pavement.
Ezekiel40:18 The pavement was by the side of the gateways
Ezekiel40:19 Then he measured the width from the front of the lower gateway to the front of the inner court exterior
Ezekiel40:20 On the outer court was also a gateway facing north
Ezekiel40:21 Its gate chambers
Ezekiel40:22 Its windows and those of its archways
Ezekiel40:23 A gate of the inner court was opposite the northern gateway
Ezekiel40:24 After that he brought me toward the south
Ezekiel40:25 There were windows in it and in its archways all around like those windows; its length was fifty cubits and its width twenty-five cubits.

Ezekiel40:27 There was also a gateway on the inner court
Ezekiel40:28 Then he brought me to the inner court through the southern gateway; he measured the southern gateway according to these same measurements.

Ezekiel40:30 There were archways all around
Ezekiel40:31 Its archways faced the outer court
Ezekiel40:32 And he brought me into the inner court facing east; he measured the gateway according to these same measurements.
Ezekiel40:33 Also its gate chambers
Ezekiel40:34 Its archways faced the outer court
Ezekiel40:35 Then he brought me to the north gateway and measured it according to these same measurements—
Ezekiel40:36 also its gate chambers
Ezekiel40:37 Its gateposts faced the outer court

Ezekiel46:1 ‘Thus says the Lord God: “The gateway of the inner court that faces toward the east shall be shut the six working days; but on the Sabbath it shall be opened
Ezekiel46:2 The prince shall enter by way of the vestibule of the gateway from the outside
Ezekiel46:3 Likewise the people of the land shall worship at the entrance to this gateway before the Lord on the Sabbaths and the New Moons.
Ezekiel46:4 The burnt offering that the prince offers to the Lord on the Sabbath day shall be six lambs without blemish
Ezekiel46:5 and the grain offering shall be one ephah for a ram

Ezekiel46:14 And you shall prepare a grain offering with it every morning
Ezekiel46:15 Thus they shall prepare the lamb
Ezekiel46:16 ‘Thus says the Lord God: “If the prince gives a gift of some of his inheritance to any of his sons

Ezekiel46:18 Moreover the prince shall not take any of the people’s inheritance by evicting them from their property; he shall provide an inheritance for his sons from his own property

Ezekiel47:5 Again he measured one thousand
Ezekiel47:6 He said to me
Ezekiel47:7 When I returned

Ezekiel47:10 It shall be that fishermen will stand by it from En Gedi to En Eglaim; they will be places for spreading their nets. Their fish will be of the same kinds as the fish of the Great Sea
Ezekiel47:11 But its swamps and marshes will not be healed; they will be given over to salt.
Ezekiel47:12 Along the bank of the river

Daniel1:1 In the third year of the reign of Jehoiakim king of Judah
Daniel1:2 And the Lord gave Jehoiakim king of Judah into his hand
Daniel1:3 Then the king instructed Ashpenaz
Daniel1:4 young men in whom there was no blemish
Daniel1:5 And the king appointed for them a daily provision of the king’s delicacies and of the wine which he drank
Daniel1:6 Now from among those of the sons of Judah were Daniel
Daniel1:7 To them the chief of the eunuchs gave names: he gave Daniel the name Belteshazzar; to Hananiah
Daniel1:8 But Daniel purposed in his heart that he would not defile himself with the portion of the king’s delicacies
Daniel1:9 Now God had brought Daniel into the favor and goodwill of the chief of the eunuchs.
Daniel1:10 And the chief of the eunuchs said to Daniel
Daniel1:11 So Daniel said to the steward whom the chief of the eunuchs had set over Daniel
Daniel1:12 “Please test your servants for ten days
Daniel1:13 Then let our appearance be examined before you
Daniel1:14 So he consented with them in this matter
Daniel1:15 And at the end of ten days their features appeared better and fatter in flesh than all the young men who ate the portion of the king’s delicacies.
Daniel1:16 Thus the steward took away their portion of delicacies and the wine that they were to drink
Daniel1:17 As for these four young men
Daniel1:18 Now at the end of the days
Daniel1:19 Then the king interviewed them
Daniel1:20 And in all matters of wisdom and understanding about which the king examined them
Daniel1:21 Thus Daniel continued until the first year of King Cyrus.
Daniel2:1 Now in the second year of Nebuchadnezzar’s reign

Daniel2:3 And the king said to them

Daniel2:5 The king answered and said to the Chaldeans
Daniel2:6 However
Daniel2:7 They answered again and said
Daniel2:8 The king answered and said
Daniel2:9 if you do not make known the dream to me
Daniel2:10 The Chaldeans answered the king
Daniel2:11 It is a difficult thing that the king requests
Daniel2:12 For this reason the king was angry and very furious
Daniel2:13 So the decree went out
Daniel2:14 Then with counsel and wisdom Daniel answered Arioch
Daniel2:15 he answered and said to Arioch the king’s captain
Daniel2:16 So Daniel went in and asked the king to give him time
Daniel2:17 Then Daniel went to his house
Daniel2:18 that they might seek mercies from the God of heaven concerning this secret
Daniel2:19 Then the secret was revealed to Daniel in a night vision. So Daniel blessed the God of heaven.
Daniel2:20 Daniel answered and said: “Blessed be the name of God forever and ever
Daniel2:21 And He changes the times and the seasons; He removes kings and raises up kings; He gives wisdom to the wise And knowledge to those who have understanding.
Daniel2:22 He reveals deep and secret things; He knows what is in the darkness

Daniel2:24 Therefore Daniel went to Arioch
Daniel2:25 Then Arioch quickly brought Daniel before the king

Daniel2:33 its legs of iron

Daniel2:35 Then the iron
Daniel2:36 “This is the dream. Now we will tell the interpretation of it before the king.
Daniel2:37 You
Daniel2:38 and wherever the children of men dwell
Daniel2:39 But after you shall arise another kingdom inferior to yours; then another

Daniel2:41 Whereas you saw the feet and toes

Daniel2:43 As you saw iron mixed with ceramic clay

Daniel11:11 “And the king of the South shall be moved with rage
Daniel11:12 When he has taken away the multitude
Daniel11:13 For the king of the North will return and muster a multitude greater than the former
Daniel11:14 “Now in those times many shall rise up against the king of the South. Also

Daniel11:16 But he who comes against him shall do according to his own will

Daniel11:19 Then he shall turn his face toward the fortress of his own land; but he shall stumble and fall

Daniel11:21 And in his place shall arise a vile person
Daniel11:22 With the force of a flood they shall be swept away from before him and be broken

Daniel11:24 He shall enter peaceably

Daniel11:28 While returning to his land with great riches
Daniel11:29 “At the appointed time he shall return and go toward the south; but it shall not be like the former or the latter.
Daniel11:30 For ships from Cyprus shall come against him; therefore he shall be grieved
Daniel11:31 And forces shall be mustered by him
Daniel11:32 Those who do wickedly against the covenant he shall corrupt with flattery; but the people who know their God shall be strong
Daniel11:33 And those of the people who understand shall instruct many; yet for many days they shall fall by sword and flame
Daniel11:34 Now when they fall
Daniel11:35 And some of those of understanding shall fall
Daniel11:36 “Then the king shall do according to his own will: he shall exalt and magnify himself above every god
Daniel11:37 He shall regard neither the God of his fathers nor the desire of women
Daniel11:38 But in their place he shall honor a god of fortresses; and a god which his fathers did not know he shall honor with gold and silver
Daniel11:39 Thus he shall act against the strongest fortresses with a foreign god
Daniel11:40 “At the time of the end the king of the South shall attack him; and the king of the North shall come against him like a whirlwind
Daniel11:41 He shall also enter the Glorious Land
Daniel11:42 He shall stretch out his hand against the countries
Daniel11:43 He shall have power over the treasures of gold and silver
Daniel11:44 But news from the east and the north shall trouble him; therefore he shall go out with great fury to destroy and annihilate many.
Daniel11:45 And he shall plant the tents of his palace between the seas and the glorious holy mountain; yet he shall come to his end

Hosea1:1 The word of the Lord that came to Hosea the son of Beeri
Hosea1:2 When the Lord began to speak by Hosea
Hosea1:3 So he went and took Gomer the daughter of Diblaim
Hosea1:4 Then the Lord said to him: “Call his name Jezreel
Hosea1:5 It shall come to pass in that day That I will break the bow of Israel in the Valley of Jezreel.”
Hosea1:6 And she conceived again and bore a daughter. Then God said to him: “Call her name Lo-Ruhamah
Hosea1:7 Yet I will have mercy on the house of Judah
Hosea1:8 Now when she had weaned Lo-Ruhamah
Hosea1:9 Then God said: “Call his name Lo-Ammi
Hosea1:10 “Yet the number of the children of Israel Shall be as the sand of the sea
Hosea1:11 Then the children of Judah and the children of Israel Shall be gathered together
Hosea2:1 Say to your brethren
Hosea2:2 “Bring charges against your mother
Hosea2:3 Lest I strip her naked And expose her
Hosea2:4 “I will not have mercy on her children
Hosea2:5 For their mother has played the harlot; She who conceived them has behaved shamefully. For she said
Hosea2:6 “Therefore
Hosea2:7 She will chase her lovers
Hosea2:8 For she did not know That I gave her grain
Hosea2:9 “Therefore I will return and take away My grain in its time And My new wine in its season
Hosea2:10 Now I will uncover her lewdness in the sight of her lovers
Hosea2:11 I will also cause all her mirth to cease
Hosea2:12 “And I will destroy her vines and her fig trees
Hosea2:13 I will punish her For the days of the Baals to which she burned incense. She decked herself with her earrings and jewelry
Hosea2:14 “Therefore
Hosea2:15 I will give her her vineyards from there
Hosea2:16 “And it shall be
Hosea2:17 For I will take from her mouth the names of the Baals
Hosea2:18 In that day I will make a covenant for them With the beasts of the field
Hosea2:19 “I will betroth you to Me forever; Yes
Hosea2:20 I will betroth you to Me in faithfulness
Hosea2:21 “It shall come to pass in that day That I will answer
Hosea2:22 The earth shall answer With grain
Hosea2:23 Then I will sow her for Myself in the earth
Hosea3:1 Then the Lord said to me
Hosea3:2 So I bought her for myself for fifteen shekels of silver
Hosea3:3 And I said to her
Hosea3:4 For the children of Israel shall abide many days without king or prince
Hosea3:5 Afterward the children of Israel shall return and seek the Lord their God and David their king. They shall fear the Lord and His goodness in the latter days.
Hosea4:1 Hear the word of the Lord
Hosea4:2 By swearing and lying
Hosea4:3 Therefore the land will mourn; And everyone who dwells there will waste away With the beasts of the field And the birds of the air; Even the fish of the sea will be taken away.
Hosea4:4 “Now let no man contend
Hosea4:5 Therefore you shall stumble in the day; The prophet also shall stumble with you in the night; And I will destroy your mother.
Hosea4:6 My people are destroyed for lack of knowledge. Because you have rejected knowledge
Hosea4:7 “The more they increased
Hosea4:8 They eat up the sin of My people; They set their heart on their iniquity.
Hosea4:9 And it shall be: like people
Hosea4:10 For they shall eat

Hosea4:12 My people ask counsel from their wooden idols
Hosea4:13 They offer sacrifices on the mountaintops
Hosea4:14 “I will not punish your daughters when they commit harlotry
Hosea4:15 “Though you

Hosea4:18 Their drink is rebellion

Hosea5:1 “Hear this
Hosea5:2 The revolters are deeply involved in slaughter

Hosea5:4 “They do not direct their deeds Toward turning to their God

Hosea5:7 They have dealt treacherously with the Lord
Hosea5:8 “Blow the ram’s horn in Gibeah
Hosea5:9 Ephraim shall be desolate in the day of rebuke; Among the tribes of Israel I make known what is sure.
Hosea5:10 “The princes of Judah are like those who remove a landmark; I will pour out My wrath on them like water.
Hosea5:11 Ephraim is oppressed and broken in judgment
Hosea5:12 Therefore I will be to Ephraim like a moth
Hosea5:13 “When Ephraim saw his sickness
Hosea5:14 For I will be like a lion to Ephraim
Hosea5:15 I will return again to My place Till they acknowledge their offense. Then they will seek My face; In their affliction they will earnestly seek Me.”

Hosea7:6 They prepare their heart like an oven
Hosea7:7 They are all hot

Hosea9:8 The watchman of Ephraim is with my God; But the prophet is a fowler’s snare in all his ways— Enmity in the house of his God.
Hosea9:9 They are deeply corrupted

Hosea10:4 They have spoken words

Joel1:2 Hear this
Joel1:3 Tell your children about it

Joel1:5 Awake

Joel1:7 He has laid waste My vine

Joel1:10 The field is wasted
Joel1:11 Be ashamed
Joel1:12 The vine has dried up
Joel1:13 Gird yourselves and lament
Joel1:14 Consecrate a fast
Joel1:15 Alas for the day! For the day of the Lord is at hand; It shall come as destruction from the Almighty.

Joel1:17 The seed shrivels under the clods
Joel1:18 How the animals groan! The herds of cattle are restless
Joel1:19 O Lord
Joel1:20 The beasts of the field also cry out to You
Joel2:1 Blow the trumpet in Zion
Joel2:2 A day of darkness and gloominess
Joel2:3 A fire devours before them
Joel2:4 Their appearance is like the appearance of horses; And like swift steeds
Joel2:5 With a noise like chariots Over mountaintops they leap
Joel2:6 Before them the people writhe in pain; All faces are drained of color.
Joel2:7 They run like mighty men
Joel2:8 They do not push one another; Every one marches in his own column. Though they lunge between the weapons
Joel2:9 They run to and fro in the city
Joel2:10 The earth quakes before them
Joel2:11 The Lord gives voice before His army
Joel2:12 “Now
Joel2:13 So rend your heart
Joel2:14 Who knows if He will turn and relent
Joel2:15 Blow the trumpet in Zion
Joel2:16 Gather the people
Joel2:17 Let the priests
Joel2:18 Then the Lord will be zealous for His land
Joel2:19 The Lord will answer and say to His people
Joel2:20 “But I will remove far from you the northern army
Joel2:21 Fear not
Joel2:22 Do not be afraid
Joel2:23 Be glad then
Joel2:24 The threshing floors shall be full of wheat
Joel2:25 “So I will restore to you the years that the swarming locust has eaten
Joel2:26 You shall eat in plenty and be satisfied
Joel2:27 Then you shall know that I am in the midst of Israel: I am the Lord your God And there is no other. My people shall never be put to shame.
Joel2:28 “And it shall come to pass afterward That I will pour out My Spirit on all flesh; Your sons and your daughters shall prophesy
Joel2:29 And also on My menservants and on My maidservants I will pour out My Spirit in those days.
Joel2:30 “And I will show wonders in the heavens and in the earth: Blood and fire and pillars of smoke.
Joel2:31 The sun shall be turned into darkness
Joel2:32 And it shall come to pass That whoever calls on the name of the LordShall be saved. For in Mount Zion and in Jerusalem there shall be deliverance

Amos2:5 But I will send a fire upon Judah

Amos3:5 Will a bird fall into a snare on the earth

Amos3:7 Surely the Lord God does nothing

Amos3:10 For they do not know to do right

Amos5:10 They hate the one who rebukes in the gate
Amos5:11 Therefore
Amos5:12 For I know your manifold transgressions And your mighty sins: Afflicting the just and taking bribes; Diverting the poor from justice at the gate.

Amos5:14 Seek good and not evil
Amos5:15 Hate evil
Amos5:16 Therefore the Lord God of hosts

Amos5:20 Is not the day of the Lord darkness
Amos5:21 “I hate

Amos5:24 But let justice run down like water

Amos5:26 You also carried Sikkuth your king And Chiun
Amos5:27 Therefore I will send you into captivity beyond Damascus

Obadiah1:1 The vision of Obadiah. Thus says the Lord God concerning Edom (We have heard a report from the Lord
Obadiah1:2 “Behold
Obadiah1:3 The pride of your heart has deceived you
Obadiah1:4 Though you ascend as high as the eagle
Obadiah1:5 “If thieves had come to you
Obadiah1:6 “Oh
Obadiah1:7 All the men in your confederacy Shall force you to the border; The men at peace with you Shall deceive you and prevail against you. Those who eat your bread shall lay a trap for you. No one is aware of it.
Obadiah1:8 “Will I not in that day
Obadiah1:9 Then your mighty men
Obadiah1:10 “For violence against your brother Jacob
Obadiah1:11 In the day that you stood on the other side— In the day that strangers carried captive his forces
Obadiah1:12 “But you should not have gazed on the day of your brother In the day of his captivity; Nor should you have rejoiced over the children of Judah In the day of their destruction; Nor should you have spoken proudly In the day of distress.
Obadiah1:13 You should not have entered the gate of My people In the day of their calamity. Indeed
Obadiah1:14 You should not have stood at the crossroads To cut off those among them who escaped; Nor should you have delivered up those among them who remained In the day of distress.
Obadiah1:15 “For the day of the Lord upon all the nations is near; As you have done
Obadiah1:16 For as you drank on My holy mountain
Obadiah1:17 “But on Mount Zion there shall be deliverance
Obadiah1:18 The house of Jacob shall be a fire
Obadiah1:19 The South shall possess the mountains of Esau
Obadiah1:20 And the captives of this host of the children of Israel Shall possess the land of the Canaanites As far as Zarephath. The captives of Jerusalem who are in Sepharad Shall possess the cities of the South.
Obadiah1:21 Then saviors shall come to Mount Zion To judge the mountains of Esau
Jonah1:1 Now the word of the Lord came to Jonah the son of Amittai
Jonah1:2 “Arise
Jonah1:3 But Jonah arose to flee to Tarshish from the presence of the Lord. He went down to Joppa
Jonah1:4 But the Lord sent out a great wind on the sea
Jonah1:5 Then the mariners were afraid; and every man cried out to his god
Jonah1:6 So the captain came to him
Jonah1:7 And they said to one another
Jonah1:8 Then they said to him
Jonah1:9 So he said to them
Jonah1:10 Then the men were exceedingly afraid
Jonah1:11 Then they said to him
Jonah1:12 And he said to them
Jonah1:13 Nevertheless the men rowed hard to return to land
Jonah1:14 Therefore they cried out to the Lord and said
Jonah1:15 So they picked up Jonah and threw him into the sea
Jonah1:16 Then the men feared the Lord exceedingly
Jonah1:17 Now the Lord had prepared a great fish to swallow Jonah. And Jonah was in the belly of the fish three days and three nights.
Jonah2:1 Then Jonah prayed to the Lord his God from the fish’s belly.
Jonah2:2 And he said: “I cried out to the Lord because of my affliction
Jonah2:3 For You cast me into the deep
Jonah2:4 Then I said
Jonah2:5 The waters surrounded me
Jonah2:6 I went down to the moorings of the mountains; The earth with its bars closed behind me forever; Yet You have brought up my life from the pit
Jonah2:7 “When my soul fainted within me
Jonah2:8 “Those who regard worthless idols Forsake their own Mercy.
Jonah2:9 But I will sacrifice to You With the voice of thanksgiving; I will pay what I have vowed. Salvation is of the Lord.”
Jonah2:10 So the Lord spoke to the fish
Jonah3:1 Now the word of the Lord came to Jonah the second time
Jonah3:2 “Arise
Jonah3:3 So Jonah arose and went to Nineveh
Jonah3:4 And Jonah began to enter the city on the first day’s walk. Then he cried out and said
Jonah3:5 So the people of Nineveh believed God
Jonah3:6 Then word came to the king of Nineveh; and he arose from his throne and laid aside his robe
Jonah3:7 And he caused it to be proclaimed and published throughout Nineveh by the decree of the king and his nobles
Jonah3:8 But let man and beast be covered with sackcloth
Jonah3:9 Who can tell if God will turn and relent
Jonah3:10 Then God saw their works
Jonah4:1 But it displeased Jonah exceedingly
Jonah4:2 So he prayed to the Lord
Jonah4:3 Therefore now
Jonah4:4 Then the Lord said
Jonah4:5 So Jonah went out of the city and sat on the east side of the city. There he made himself a shelter and sat under it in the shade
Jonah4:6 And the Lord God prepared a plant and made it come up over Jonah
Jonah4:7 But as morning dawned the next day God prepared a worm
Jonah4:8 And it happened
Jonah4:9 Then God said to Jonah
Jonah4:10 But the Lord said
Jonah4:11 And should I not pity Nineveh
Micah1:1 The word of the Lord that came to Micah of Moresheth in the days of Jotham
Micah1:2 Hear
Micah1:3 For behold
Micah1:4 The mountains will melt under Him
Micah1:5 All this is for the transgression of Jacob And for the sins of the house of Israel. What is the transgression of Jacob? Is it not Samaria? And what are the high places of Judah? Are they not Jerusalem?
Micah1:6 “Therefore I will make Samaria a heap of ruins in the field
Micah1:7 All her carved images shall be beaten to pieces
Micah1:8 Therefore I will wail and howl
Micah1:9 For her wounds are incurable. For it has come to Judah; It has come to the gate of My people— To Jerusalem.
Micah1:10 Tell it not in Gath
Micah1:11 Pass by in naked shame
Micah1:12 For the inhabitant of Maroth pined for good
Micah1:13 O inhabitant of Lachish
Micah1:14 Therefore you shall give presents to Moresheth Gath; The houses of Achzib shall be a lie to the kings of Israel.
Micah1:15 I will yet bring an heir to you
Micah1:16 Make yourself bald and cut off your hair
Micah2:1 Woe to those who devise iniquity
Micah2:2 They covet fields and take them by violence
Micah2:3 Therefore thus says the Lord: “Behold
Micah2:4 In that day one shall take up a proverb against you
Micah2:5 Therefore you will have no one to determine boundaries by lot In the assembly of the Lord.
Micah2:6 “Do not prattle
Micah2:7 You who are named the house of Jacob: “Is the Spirit of the Lord restricted? Are these His doings? Do not My words do good To him who walks uprightly?
Micah2:8 “Lately My people have risen up as an enemy— You pull off the robe with the garment From those who trust you
Micah2:9 The women of My people you cast out From their pleasant houses; From their children You have taken away My glory forever.
Micah2:10 “Arise and depart
Micah2:11 If a man should walk in a false spirit And speak a lie
Micah2:12 “I will surely assemble all of you
Micah2:13 The one who breaks open will come up before them; They will break out
Micah3:1 And I said: “Hear now
Micah3:2 You who hate good and love evil; Who strip the skin from My people
Micah3:3 Who also eat the flesh of My people
Micah3:4 Then they will cry to the Lord
Micah3:5 Thus says the Lord concerning the prophets Who make my people stray; Who chant “Peace” While they chew with their teeth
Micah3:6 “Therefore you shall have night without vision
Micah3:7 So the seers shall be ashamed
Micah3:8 But truly I am full of power by the Spirit of the Lord
Micah3:9 Now hear this
Micah3:10 Who build up Zion with bloodshed And Jerusalem with iniquity:
Micah3:11 Her heads judge for a bribe
Micah3:12 Therefore because of you Zion shall be plowed like a field
Micah4:1 Now it shall come to pass in the latter days That the mountain of the Lord’s house Shall be established on the top of the mountains
Micah4:2 Many nations shall come and say
Micah4:3 He shall judge between many peoples
Micah4:4 But everyone shall sit under his vine and under his fig tree
Micah4:5 For all people walk each in the name of his god
Micah4:6 “In that day
Micah4:7 I will make the lame a remnant
Micah4:8 And you
Micah4:9 Now why do you cry aloud? Is there no king in your midst? Has your counselor perished? For pangs have seized you like a woman in labor.
Micah4:10 Be in pain
Micah4:11 Now also many nations have gathered against you
Micah4:12 But they do not know the thoughts of the Lord
Micah4:13 “Arise and thresh
Micah5:1 Now gather yourself in troops
Micah5:2 “But you
Micah5:3 Therefore He shall give them up
Micah5:4 And He shall stand and feed His flock In the strength of the Lord
Micah5:5 And this One shall be peace. When the Assyrian comes into our land
Micah5:6 They shall waste with the sword the land of Assyria
Micah5:7 Then the remnant of Jacob Shall be in the midst of many peoples
Micah5:8 And the remnant of Jacob Shall be among the Gentiles
Micah5:9 Your hand shall be lifted against your adversaries
Micah5:10 “And it shall be in that day
Micah5:11 I will cut off the cities of your land And throw down all your strongholds.
Micah5:12 I will cut off sorceries from your hand
Micah5:13 Your carved images I will also cut off
Micah5:14 I will pluck your wooden images from your midst; Thus I will destroy your cities.
Micah5:15 And I will execute vengeance in anger and fury On the nations that have not heard.”
Micah6:1 Hear now what the Lord says: “Arise
Micah6:2 Hear
Micah6:3 “O My people
Micah6:4 For I brought you up from the land of Egypt
Micah6:5 O My people
Micah6:6 With what shall I come before the Lord
Micah6:7 Will the Lord be pleased with thousands of rams
Micah6:8 He has shown you
Micah6:9 The Lord’s voice cries to the city— Wisdom shall see Your name: “Hear the rod! Who has appointed it?
Micah6:10 Are there yet the treasures of wickedness In the house of the wicked
Micah6:11 Shall I count pure those with the wicked scales
Micah6:12 For her rich men are full of violence
Micah6:13 “Therefore I will also make you sick by striking you
Micah6:14 You shall eat
Micah6:15 “You shall sow
Micah6:16 For the statutes of Omri are kept; All the works of Ahab’s house are done; And you walk in their counsels
Micah7:1 Woe is me! For I am like those who gather summer fruits
Micah7:2 The faithful man has perished from the earth
Micah7:3 That they may successfully do evil with both hands— The prince asks for gifts
Micah7:4 The best of them is like a brier; The most upright is sharper than a thorn hedge; The day of your watchman and your punishment comes; Now shall be their perplexity.
Micah7:5 Do not trust in a friend; Do not put your confidence in a companion; Guard the doors of your mouth From her who lies in your bosom.
Micah7:6 For son dishonors father
Micah7:7 Therefore I will look to the Lord; I will wait for the God of my salvation; My God will hear me.
Micah7:8 Do not rejoice over me
Micah7:9 I will bear the indignation of the Lord
Micah7:10 Then she who is my enemy will see
Micah7:11 In the day when your walls are to be built
Micah7:12 In that day they shall come to you From Assyria and the fortified cities
Micah7:13 Yet the land shall be desolate Because of those who dwell in it
Micah7:14 Shepherd Your people with Your staff
Micah7:15 “As in the days when you came out of the land of Egypt
Micah7:16 The nations shall see and be ashamed of all their might; They shall put their hand over their mouth; Their ears shall be deaf.
Micah7:17 They shall lick the dust like a serpent; They shall crawl from their holes like snakes of the earth. They shall be afraid of the Lord our God
Micah7:18 Who is a God like You
Micah7:19 He will again have compassion on us
Micah7:20 You will give truth to Jacob And mercy to Abraham
Nahum1:1 The burden against Nineveh. The book of the vision of Nahum the Elkoshite.
Nahum1:2 God is jealous
Nahum1:3 The Lord is slow to anger and great in power
Nahum1:4 He rebukes the sea and makes it dry
Nahum1:5 The mountains quake before Him
Nahum1:6 Who can stand before His indignation? And who can endure the fierceness of His anger? His fury is poured out like fire
Nahum1:7 The Lord is good
Nahum1:8 But with an overflowing flood He will make an utter end of its place
Nahum1:9 What do you conspire against the Lord? He will make an utter end of it. Affliction will not rise up a second time.
Nahum1:10 For while tangled like thorns
Nahum1:11 From you comes forth one Who plots evil against the Lord
Nahum1:12 Thus says the Lord: “Though they are safe
Nahum1:13 For now I will break off his yoke from you
Nahum1:14 The Lord has given a command concerning you: “Your name shall be perpetuated no longer. Out of the house of your gods I will cut off the carved image and the molded image. I will dig your grave
Nahum1:15 Behold
Nahum2:1 He who scatters has come up before your face. Man the fort! Watch the road! Strengthen your flanks! Fortify your power mightily.
Nahum2:2 For the Lord will restore the excellence of Jacob Like the excellence of Israel
Nahum2:3 The shields of his mighty men are made red
Nahum2:4 The chariots rage in the streets
Nahum2:5 He remembers his nobles; They stumble in their walk; They make haste to her walls
Nahum2:6 The gates of the rivers are opened
Nahum2:7 It is decreed: She shall be led away captive
Nahum2:8 Though Nineveh of old was like a pool of water
Nahum2:9 Take spoil of silver! Take spoil of gold! There is no end of treasure
Nahum2:10 She is empty
Nahum2:11 Where is the dwelling of the lions
Nahum2:12 The lion tore in pieces enough for his cubs
Nahum2:13 “Behold
Nahum3:1 Woe to the bloody city! It is all full of lies and robbery. Its victim never departs.
Nahum3:2 The noise of a whip And the noise of rattling wheels
Nahum3:3 Horsemen charge with bright sword and glittering spear. There is a multitude of slain
Nahum3:4 Because of the multitude of harlotries of the seductive harlot
Nahum3:5 “Behold
Nahum3:6 I will cast abominable filth upon you
Nahum3:7 It shall come to pass that all who look upon you Will flee from you
Nahum3:8 Are you better than No Amon That was situated by the River
Nahum3:9 Ethiopia and Egypt were her strength
Nahum3:10 Yet she was carried away
Nahum3:11 You also will be drunk; You will be hidden; You also will seek refuge from the enemy.
Nahum3:12 All your strongholds are fig trees with ripened figs: If they are shaken
Nahum3:13 Surely
Nahum3:14 Draw your water for the siege! Fortify your strongholds! Go into the clay and tread the mortar! Make strong the brick kiln!
Nahum3:15 There the fire will devour you
Nahum3:16 You have multiplied your merchants more than the stars of heaven. The locust plunders and flies away.
Nahum3:17 Your commanders are like swarming locusts
Nahum3:18 Your shepherds slumber
Nahum3:19 Your injury has no healing
Habakkuk1:1 The burden which the prophet Habakkuk saw.
Habakkuk1:2 O Lord
Habakkuk1:3 Why do You show me iniquity
Habakkuk1:4 Therefore the law is powerless
Habakkuk1:5 “Look among the nations and watch— Be utterly astounded! For I will work a work in your days Which you would not believe
Habakkuk1:6 For indeed I am raising up the Chaldeans
Habakkuk1:7 They are terrible and dreadful; Their judgment and their dignity proceed from themselves.
Habakkuk1:8 Their horses also are swifter than leopards
Habakkuk1:9 “They all come for violence; Their faces are set like the east wind. They gather captives like sand.
Habakkuk1:10 They scoff at kings
Habakkuk1:11 Then his mind changes
Habakkuk1:12 Are You not from everlasting
Habakkuk1:13 You are of purer eyes than to behold evil
Habakkuk1:14 Why do You make men like fish of the sea
Habakkuk1:15 They take up all of them with a hook
Habakkuk1:16 Therefore they sacrifice to their net
Habakkuk1:17 Shall they therefore empty their net
Habakkuk2:1 I will stand my watch And set myself on the rampart
Habakkuk2:2 Then the Lord answered me and said: “Write the vision And make it plain on tablets
Habakkuk2:3 For the vision is yet for an appointed time; But at the end it will speak
Habakkuk2:4 “Behold the proud
Habakkuk2:5 “Indeed
Habakkuk2:6 “Will not all these take up a proverb against him
Habakkuk2:7 Will not your creditors rise up suddenly? Will they not awaken who oppress you? And you will become their booty.
Habakkuk2:8 Because you have plundered many nations
Habakkuk2:9 “Woe to him who covets evil gain for his house
Habakkuk2:10 You give shameful counsel to your house
Habakkuk2:11 For the stone will cry out from the wall
Habakkuk2:12 “Woe to him who builds a town with bloodshed
Habakkuk2:13 Behold
Habakkuk2:14 For the earth will be filled With the knowledge of the glory of the Lord
Habakkuk2:15 “Woe to him who gives drink to his neighbor
Habakkuk2:16 You are filled with shame instead of glory. You also—drink! And be exposed as uncircumcised! The cup of the Lord’s right hand will be turned against you
Habakkuk2:17 For the violence done to Lebanon will cover you
Habakkuk2:18 “What profit is the image
Habakkuk2:19 Woe to him who says to wood
Habakkuk2:20 “But the Lord is in His holy temple. Let all the earth keep silence before Him.”
Habakkuk3:1 A prayer of Habakkuk the prophet
Habakkuk3:2 O Lord
Habakkuk3:3 God came from Teman
Habakkuk3:4 His brightness was like the light; He had rays flashing from His hand
Habakkuk3:5 Before Him went pestilence
Habakkuk3:6 He stood and measured the earth; He looked and startled the nations. And the everlasting mountains were scattered
Habakkuk3:7 I saw the tents of Cushan in affliction; The curtains of the land of Midian trembled.
Habakkuk3:8 O Lord
Habakkuk3:9 Your bow was made quite ready; Oaths were sworn over Your arrows. You divided the earth with rivers.
Habakkuk3:10 The mountains saw You and trembled; The overflowing of the water passed by. The deep uttered its voice
Habakkuk3:11 The sun and moon stood still in their habitation; At the light of Your arrows they went
Habakkuk3:12 You marched through the land in indignation; You trampled the nations in anger.
Habakkuk3:13 You went forth for the salvation of Your people
Habakkuk3:14 You thrust through with his own arrows The head of his villages. They came out like a whirlwind to scatter me; Their rejoicing was like feasting on the poor in secret.
Habakkuk3:15 You walked through the sea with Your horses
Habakkuk3:16 When I heard
Habakkuk3:17 Though the fig tree may not blossom
Habakkuk3:18 Yet I will rejoice in the Lord
Habakkuk3:19 The Lord God is my strength; He will make my feet like deer’s feet

Matthew10:26 Therefore do not fear them. For there is nothing covered that will not be revealed
Matthew10:27 “Whatever I tell you in the dark
Matthew10:28 And do not fear those who kill the body but cannot kill the soul. But rather fear Him who is able to destroy both soul and body in hell.
Matthew10:29 Are not two sparrows sold for a copper coin? And not one of them falls to the ground apart from your Father’s will.
Matthew10:30 But the very hairs of your head are all numbered.
Matthew10:31 Do not fear therefore; you are of more value than many sparrows.
Matthew10:32 “Therefore whoever confesses Me before men
Matthew10:33 But whoever denies Me before men
