Matthew10:35 For I have come to ‘set a man against his father
Matthew10:36 and ‘a man’s enemies will be those of his own household.’
Matthew10:37 He who loves father or mother more than Me is not worthy of Me. And he who loves son or daughter more than Me is not worthy of Me.
Matthew10:38 And he who does not take his cross and follow after Me is not worthy of Me.
Matthew10:39 He who finds his life will lose it
Matthew10:40 “He who receives you receives Me
Matthew10:41 He who receives a prophet in the name of a prophet shall receive a prophet’s reward. And he who receives a righteous man in the name of a righteous man shall receive a righteous man’s reward.
Matthew10:42 And whoever gives one of these little ones only a cup of cold water in the name of a disciple

Matthew13:17 for assuredly

Matthew13:20 But he who received the seed on stony places
Matthew13:21 yet he has no root in himself
Matthew13:22 Now he who received seed among the thorns is he who hears the word
Matthew13:23 But he who received seed on the good ground is he who hears the word and understands it

Matthew13:34 All these things Jesus spoke to the multitude in parables; and without a parable He did not speak to them

Matthew23:4 For they bind heavy burdens
Matthew23:5 But all their works they do to be seen by men. They make their phylacteries broad and enlarge the borders of their garments.
Matthew23:6 They love the best places at feasts
Matthew23:7 greetings in the marketplaces
Matthew23:8 But you
Matthew23:9 Do not call anyone on earth your father; for One is your Father
Matthew23:10 And do not be called teachers; for One is your Teacher
Matthew23:11 But he who is greatest among you shall be your servant.
Matthew23:12 And whoever exalts himself will be humbled

Matthew23:15 “Woe to you
Matthew23:16 “Woe to you

Matthew23:18 And

Matthew23:24 Blind guides

Matthew23:39 for I say to you
Matthew24:1 Then Jesus went out and departed from the temple
Matthew24:2 And Jesus said to them
Matthew24:3 Now as He sat on the Mount of Olives
Matthew24:4 And Jesus answered and said to them: “Take heed that no one deceives you.
Matthew24:5 For many will come in My name
Matthew24:6 And you will hear of wars and rumors of wars. See that you are not troubled; for all these things must come to pass
Matthew24:7 For nation will rise against nation
Matthew24:8 All these are the beginning of sorrows.
Matthew24:9 “Then they will deliver you up to tribulation and kill you
Matthew24:10 And then many will be offended
Matthew24:11 Then many false prophets will rise up and deceive many.
Matthew24:12 And because lawlessness will abound
Matthew24:13 But he who endures to the end shall be saved.
Matthew24:14 And this gospel of the kingdom will be preached in all the world as a witness to all the nations
Matthew24:15 “Therefore when you see the ‘abomination of desolation
Matthew24:16 “then let those who are in Judea flee to the mountains.
Matthew24:17 Let him who is on the housetop not go down to take anything out of his house.
Matthew24:18 And let him who is in the field not go back to get his clothes.
Matthew24:19 But woe to those who are pregnant and to those who are nursing babies in those days!
Matthew24:20 And pray that your flight may not be in winter or on the Sabbath.
Matthew24:21 For then there will be great tribulation
Matthew24:22 And unless those days were shortened
Matthew24:23 “Then if anyone says to you
Matthew24:24 For false christs and false prophets will rise and show great signs and wonders to deceive
Matthew24:25 See
Matthew24:26 “Therefore if they say to you
Matthew24:27 For as the lightning comes from the east and flashes to the west
Matthew24:28 For wherever the carcass is
Matthew24:29 “Immediately after the tribulation of those days the sun will be darkened
Matthew24:30 Then the sign of the Son of Man will appear in heaven
Matthew24:31 And He will send His angels with a great sound of a trumpet
Matthew24:32 “Now learn this parable from the fig tree: When its branch has already become tender and puts forth leaves
Matthew24:33 So you also
Matthew24:34 Assuredly
Matthew24:35 Heaven and earth will pass away
Matthew24:36 “But of that day and hour no one knows
Matthew24:37 But as the days of Noah were
Matthew24:38 For as in the days before the flood
Matthew24:39 and did not know until the flood came and took them all away
Matthew24:40 Then two men will be in the field: one will be taken and the other left.
Matthew24:41 Two women will be grinding at the mill: one will be taken and the other left.
Matthew24:42 Watch therefore
Matthew24:43 But know this
Matthew24:44 Therefore you also be ready
Matthew24:45 “Who then is a faithful and wise servant
Matthew24:46 Blessed is that servant whom his master
Matthew24:47 Assuredly
Matthew24:48 But if that evil servant says in his heart
Matthew24:49 and begins to beat his fellow servants
Matthew24:50 the master of that servant will come on a day when he is not looking for him and at an hour that he is not aware of
Matthew24:51 and will cut him in two and appoint him his portion with the hypocrites. There shall be weeping and gnashing of teeth.
Matthew25:1 “Then the kingdom of heaven shall be likened to ten virgins who took their lamps and went out to meet the bridegroom.
Matthew25:2 Now five of them were wise
Matthew25:3 Those who were foolish took their lamps and took no oil with them
Matthew25:4 but the wise took oil in their vessels with their lamps.
Matthew25:5 But while the bridegroom was delayed
Matthew25:6 “And at midnight a cry was heard: ‘Behold
Matthew25:7 Then all those virgins arose and trimmed their lamps.
Matthew25:8 And the foolish said to the wise
Matthew25:9 But the wise answered
Matthew25:10 And while they went to buy
Matthew25:11 “Afterward the other virgins came also
Matthew25:12 But he answered and said
Matthew25:13 “Watch therefore
Matthew25:14 “For the kingdom of heaven is like a man traveling to a far country
Matthew25:15 And to one he gave five talents
Matthew25:16 Then he who had received the five talents went and traded with them
Matthew25:17 And likewise he who had received two gained two more also.
Matthew25:18 But he who had received one went and dug in the ground
Matthew25:19 After a long time the lord of those servants came and settled accounts with them.
Matthew25:20 “So he who had received five talents came and brought five other talents
Matthew25:21 His lord said to him
Matthew25:22 He also who had received two talents came and said
Matthew25:23 His lord said to him
Matthew25:24 “Then he who had received the one talent came and said
Matthew25:25 And I was afraid
Matthew25:26 “But his lord answered and said to him
Matthew25:27 So you ought to have deposited my money with the bankers
Matthew25:28 Therefore take the talent from him
Matthew25:29 ‘For to everyone who has
Matthew25:30 And cast the unprofitable servant into the outer darkness. There will be weeping and gnashing of teeth.’
Matthew25:31 “When the Son of Man comes in His glory
Matthew25:32 All the nations will be gathered before Him
Matthew25:33 And He will set the sheep on His right hand
Matthew25:34 Then the King will say to those on His right hand
Matthew25:35 for I was hungry and you gave Me food; I was thirsty and you gave Me drink; I was a stranger and you took Me in;
Matthew25:36 I was naked and you clothed Me; I was sick and you visited Me; I was in prison and you came to Me.’
Matthew25:37 “Then the righteous will answer Him
Matthew25:38 When did we see You a stranger and take You in
Matthew25:39 Or when did we see You sick
Matthew25:40 And the King will answer and say to them
Matthew25:41 “Then He will also say to those on the left hand
Matthew25:42 for I was hungry and you gave Me no food; I was thirsty and you gave Me no drink;
Matthew25:43 I was a stranger and you did not take Me in
Matthew25:44 “Then they also will answer Him
Matthew25:45 Then He will answer them
Matthew25:46 And these will go away into everlasting punishment

Matthew26:40 Then He came to the disciples and found them sleeping

Matthew26:42 Again

Matthew26:59 Now the chief priests
Matthew26:60 but found none. Even though many false witnesses came forward
Matthew26:61 and said

Matthew26:66 What do you think?” They answered and said

Matthew26:73 And a little later those who stood by came up and said to Peter
Matthew26:74 Then he began to curse and swear
Matthew26:75 And Peter remembered the word of Jesus who had said to him
Matthew27:1 When morning came
Matthew27:2 And when they had bound Him
Matthew27:3 Then Judas
Matthew27:4 saying
Matthew27:5 Then he threw down the pieces of silver in the temple and departed
Matthew27:6 But the chief priests took the silver pieces and said
Matthew27:7 And they consulted together and bought with them the potter’s field
Matthew27:8 Therefore that field has been called the Field of Blood to this day.
Matthew27:9 Then was fulfilled what was spoken by Jeremiah the prophet
Matthew27:10 and gave them for the potter’s field
Matthew27:11 Now Jesus stood before the governor. And the governor asked Him
Matthew27:12 And while He was being accused by the chief priests and elders
Matthew27:13 Then Pilate said to Him
Matthew27:14 But He answered him not one word
Matthew27:15 Now at the feast the governor was accustomed to releasing to the multitude one prisoner whom they wished.
Matthew27:16 And at that time they had a notorious prisoner called Barabbas.
Matthew27:17 Therefore
Matthew27:18 For he knew that they had handed Him over because of envy.
Matthew27:19 While he was sitting on the judgment seat
Matthew27:20 But the chief priests and elders persuaded the multitudes that they should ask for Barabbas and destroy Jesus.
Matthew27:21 The governor answered and said to them
Matthew27:22 Pilate said to them
Matthew27:23 Then the governor said
Matthew27:24 When Pilate saw that he could not prevail at all
Matthew27:25 And all the people answered and said
Matthew27:26 Then he released Barabbas to them; and when he had scourged Jesus
Matthew27:27 Then the soldiers of the governor took Jesus into the Praetorium and gathered the whole garrison around Him.
Matthew27:28 And they stripped Him and put a scarlet robe on Him.
Matthew27:29 When they had twisted a crown of thorns
Matthew27:30 Then they spat on Him
Matthew27:31 And when they had mocked Him
Matthew27:32 Now as they came out
Matthew27:33 And when they had come to a place called Golgotha
Matthew27:34 they gave Him sour wine mingled with gall to drink. But when He had tasted it
Matthew27:35 Then they crucified Him
Matthew27:36 Sitting down
Matthew27:37 And they put up over His head the accusation written against Him: THIS IS JESUS THE KING OF THE JEWS.
Matthew27:38 Then two robbers were crucified with Him
Matthew27:39 And those who passed by blasphemed Him
Matthew27:40 and saying
Matthew27:41 Likewise the chief priests also
Matthew27:42 “He saved others; Himself He cannot save. If He is the King of Israel
Matthew27:43 He trusted in God; let Him deliver Him now if He will have Him; for He said
Matthew27:44 Even the robbers who were crucified with Him reviled Him with the same thing.
Matthew27:45 Now from the sixth hour until the ninth hour there was darkness over all the land.
Matthew27:46 And about the ninth hour Jesus cried out with a loud voice
Matthew27:47 Some of those who stood there
Matthew27:48 Immediately one of them ran and took a sponge
Matthew27:49 The rest said
Matthew27:50 And Jesus cried out again with a loud voice
Matthew27:51 Then
Matthew27:52 and the graves were opened; and many bodies of the saints who had fallen asleep were raised;
Matthew27:53 and coming out of the graves after His resurrection
Matthew27:54 So when the centurion and those with him
Matthew27:55 And many women who followed Jesus from Galilee
Matthew27:56 among whom were Mary Magdalene
Matthew27:57 Now when evening had come
Matthew27:58 This man went to Pilate and asked for the body of Jesus. Then Pilate commanded the body to be given to him.
Matthew27:59 When Joseph had taken the body
Matthew27:60 and laid it in his new tomb which he had hewn out of the rock; and he rolled a large stone against the door of the tomb
Matthew27:61 And Mary Magdalene was there
Matthew27:62 On the next day
Matthew27:63 saying
Matthew27:64 Therefore command that the tomb be made secure until the third day
Matthew27:65 Pilate said to them
Matthew27:66 So they went and made the tomb secure

Mark1:1 The beginning of the gospel of Jesus Christ
Mark1:2 As it is written in the Prophets: “Behold
Mark1:3 “The voice of one crying in the wilderness: ‘Prepare the way of the Lord; Make His paths straight.’ ”
Mark1:4 John came baptizing in the wilderness and preaching a baptism of repentance for the remission of sins.
Mark1:5 Then all the land of Judea
Mark1:6 Now John was clothed with camel’s hair and with a leather belt around his waist
Mark1:7 And he preached
Mark1:8 I indeed baptized you with water
Mark1:9 It came to pass in those days that Jesus came from Nazareth of Galilee
Mark1:10 And immediately
Mark1:11 Then a voice came from heaven
Mark1:12 Immediately the Spirit drove Him into the wilderness.
Mark1:13 And He was there in the wilderness forty days
Mark1:14 Now after John was put in prison
Mark1:15 and saying
Mark1:16 And as He walked by the Sea of Galilee
Mark1:17 Then Jesus said to them
Mark1:18 They immediately left their nets and followed Him.
Mark1:19 When He had gone a little farther from there
Mark1:20 And immediately He called them
Mark1:21 Then they went into Capernaum
Mark1:22 And they were astonished at His teaching
Mark1:23 Now there was a man in their synagogue with an unclean spirit. And he cried out
Mark1:24 saying
Mark1:25 But Jesus rebuked him
Mark1:26 And when the unclean spirit had convulsed him and cried out with a loud voice
Mark1:27 Then they were all amazed
Mark1:28 And immediately His fame spread throughout all the region around Galilee.
Mark1:29 Now as soon as they had come out of the synagogue
Mark1:30 But Simon’s wife’s mother lay sick with a fever
Mark1:31 So He came and took her by the hand and lifted her up
Mark1:32 At evening
Mark1:33 And the whole city was gathered together at the door.
Mark1:34 Then He healed many who were sick with various diseases
Mark1:35 Now in the morning
Mark1:36 And Simon and those who were with Him searched for Him.
Mark1:37 When they found Him
Mark1:38 But He said to them
Mark1:39 And He was preaching in their synagogues throughout all Galilee
Mark1:40 Now a leper came to Him
Mark1:41 Then Jesus
Mark1:42 As soon as He had spoken
Mark1:43 And He strictly warned him and sent him away at once
Mark1:44 and said to him
Mark1:45 However
Mark2:1 And again He entered Capernaum after some days
Mark2:2 Immediately many gathered together
Mark2:3 Then they came to Him
Mark2:4 And when they could not come near Him because of the crowd
Mark2:5 When Jesus saw their faith
Mark2:6 And some of the scribes were sitting there and reasoning in their hearts
Mark2:7 “Why does this Man speak blasphemies like this? Who can forgive sins but God alone?”
Mark2:8 But immediately
Mark2:9 Which is easier
Mark2:10 But that you may know that the Son of Man has power on earth to forgive sins” —He said to the paralytic
Mark2:11 “I say to you
Mark2:12 Immediately he arose
Mark2:13 Then He went out again by the sea; and all the multitude came to Him
Mark2:14 As He passed by
Mark2:15 Now it happened

Mark2:17 When Jesus heard it
Mark2:18 The disciples of John and of the Pharisees were fasting. Then they came and said to Him
Mark2:19 And Jesus said to them
Mark2:20 But the days will come when the bridegroom will be taken away from them
Mark2:21 No one sews a piece of unshrunk cloth on an old garment; or else the new piece pulls away from the old
Mark2:22 And no one puts new wine into old wineskins; or else the new wine bursts the wineskins
Mark2:23 Now it happened that He went through the grainfields on the Sabbath; and as they went His disciples began to pluck the heads of grain.
Mark2:24 And the Pharisees said to Him
Mark2:25 But He said to them
Mark2:26 how he went into the house of God in the days of Abiathar the high priest
Mark2:27 And He said to them
Mark2:28 Therefore the Son of Man is also Lord of the Sabbath.”
Mark3:1 And He entered the synagogue again
Mark3:2 So they watched Him closely
Mark3:3 And He said to the man who had the withered hand
Mark3:4 Then He said to them
Mark3:5 And when He had looked around at them with anger
Mark3:6 Then the Pharisees went out and immediately plotted with the Herodians against Him
Mark3:7 But Jesus withdrew with His disciples to the sea. And a great multitude from Galilee followed Him
Mark3:8 and Jerusalem and Idumea and beyond the Jordan; and those from Tyre and Sidon
Mark3:9 So He told His disciples that a small boat should be kept ready for Him because of the multitude
Mark3:10 For He healed many
Mark3:11 And the unclean spirits
Mark3:12 But He sternly warned them that they should not make Him known.
Mark3:13 And He went up on the mountain and called to Him those He Himself wanted. And they came to Him.

Mark3:35 For whoever does the will of God is My brother and My sister and mother.”

Mark4:2 Then He taught them many things by parables
Mark4:3 “Listen! Behold
Mark4:4 And it happened
Mark4:5 Some fell on stony ground
Mark4:6 But when the sun was up it was scorched
Mark4:7 And some seed fell among thorns; and the thorns grew up and choked it
Mark4:8 But other seed fell on good ground and yielded a crop that sprang up
Mark4:9 And He said to them
Mark4:10 But when He was alone
Mark4:11 And He said to them

Mark4:16 These likewise are the ones sown on stony ground who
Mark4:17 and they have no root in themselves

Mark4:19 and the cares of this world
Mark4:20 But these are the ones sown on good ground

Mark6:2 And when the Sabbath had come

Mark6:4 But Jesus said to them
Mark6:5 Now He could do no mighty work there
Mark6:6 And He marveled because of their unbelief. Then He went about the villages in a circuit

Mark6:9 but to wear sandals

Mark6:15 Others said
Mark6:16 But when Herod heard

Mark6:26 And the king was exceedingly sorry; yet

Acts28:5 But he shook off the creature into the fire and suffered no harm.

Acts28:31 preaching the kingdom of God and teaching the things which concern the Lord Jesus Christ with all confidence
Romans1:1 Paul
Romans1:2 which He promised before through His prophets in the Holy Scriptures
Romans1:3 concerning His Son Jesus Christ our Lord
Romans1:4 and declared to be the Son of God with power according to the Spirit of holiness
Romans1:5 Through Him we have received grace and apostleship for obedience to the faith among all nations for His name
Romans1:6 among whom you also are the called of Jesus Christ;
Romans1:7 To all who are in Rome
Romans1:8 First
Romans1:9 For God is my witness
Romans1:10 making request if
Romans1:11 For I long to see you
Romans1:12 that is
Romans1:13 Now I do not want you to be unaware
Romans1:14 I am a debtor both to Greeks and to barbarians
Romans1:15 So
Romans1:16 For I am not ashamed of the gospel of Christ
Romans1:17 For in it the righteousness of God is revealed from faith to faith; as it is written
Romans1:18 For the wrath of God is revealed from heaven against all ungodliness and unrighteousness of men
Romans1:19 because what may be known of God is manifest in them
Romans1:20 For since the creation of the world His invisible attributes are clearly seen
Romans1:21 because
Romans1:22 Professing to be wise
Romans1:23 and changed the glory of the incorruptible God into an image made like corruptible man—and birds and four-footed animals and creeping things.
Romans1:24 Therefore God also gave them up to uncleanness
Romans1:25 who exchanged the truth of God for the lie
Romans1:26 For this reason God gave them up to vile passions. For even their women exchanged the natural use for what is against nature.
Romans1:27 Likewise also the men
Romans1:28 And even as they did not like to retain God in their knowledge
Romans1:29 being filled with all unrighteousness
Romans1:30 backbiters
Romans1:31 undiscerning
Romans1:32 who
Romans2:1 Therefore you are inexcusable
Romans2:2 But we know that the judgment of God is according to truth against those who practice such things.
Romans2:3 And do you think this
Romans2:4 Or do you despise the riches of His goodness
Romans2:5 But in accordance with your hardness and your impenitent heart you are treasuring up for yourself wrath in the day of wrath and revelation of the righteous judgment of God
Romans2:6 who “will render to each one according to his deeds”:
Romans2:7 eternal life to those who by patient continuance in doing good seek for glory
Romans2:8 but to those who are self-seeking and do not obey the truth
Romans2:9 tribulation and anguish
Romans2:10 but glory
Romans2:11 For there is no partiality with God.
Romans2:12 For as many as have sinned without law will also perish without law
Romans2:13 (for not the hearers of the law are just in the sight of God
Romans2:14 for when Gentiles
Romans2:15 who show the work of the law written in their hearts
Romans2:16 in the day when God will judge the secrets of men by Jesus Christ
Romans2:17 Indeed you are called a Jew
Romans2:18 and know His will
Romans2:19 and are confident that you yourself are a guide to the blind
Romans2:20 an instructor of the foolish
Romans2:21 You
Romans2:22 You who say
Romans2:23 You who make your boast in the law
Romans2:24 For “the name of God is blasphemed among the Gentiles because of you
Romans2:25 For circumcision is indeed profitable if you keep the law; but if you are a breaker of the law
Romans2:26 Therefore
Romans2:27 And will not the physically uncircumcised
Romans2:28 For he is not a Jew who is one outwardly
Romans2:29 but he is a Jew who is one inwardly; and circumcision is that of the heart
Romans3:1 What advantage then has the Jew
Romans3:2 Much in every way! Chiefly because to them were committed the oracles of God.
Romans3:3 For what if some did not believe? Will their unbelief make the faithfulness of God without effect?
Romans3:4 Certainly not! Indeed
Romans3:5 But if our unrighteousness demonstrates the righteousness of God
Romans3:6 Certainly not! For then how will God judge the world?
Romans3:7 For if the truth of God has increased through my lie to His glory
Romans3:8 And why not say
Romans3:9 What then? Are we better than they? Not at all. For we have previously charged both Jews and Greeks that they are all under sin.
Romans3:10 As it is written: “There is none righteous
Romans3:11 There is none who understands; There is none who seeks after God.
Romans3:12 They have all turned aside; They have together become unprofitable; There is none who does good
Romans3:13 “Their throat is an open tomb; With their tongues they have practiced deceit”; “The poison of asps is under their lips”;
Romans3:14 “Whose mouth is full of cursing and bitterness.”
Romans3:15 “Their feet are swift to shed blood;
Romans3:16 Destruction and misery are in their ways;
Romans3:17 And the way of peace they have not known.”
Romans3:18 “There is no fear of God before their eyes.”
Romans3:19 Now we know that whatever the law says
Romans3:20 Therefore by the deeds of the law no flesh will be justified in His sight
Romans3:21 But now the righteousness of God apart from the law is revealed
Romans3:22 even the righteousness of God
Romans3:23 for all have sinned and fall short of the glory of God
Romans3:24 being justified freely by His grace through the redemption that is in Christ Jesus
Romans3:25 whom God set forth as a propitiation by His blood
Romans3:26 to demonstrate at the present time His righteousness
Romans3:27 Where is boasting then? It is excluded. By what law? Of works? No
Romans3:28 Therefore we conclude that a man is justified by faith apart from the deeds of the law.
Romans3:29 Or is He the God of the Jews only? Is He not also the God of the Gentiles? Yes
Romans3:30 since there is one God who will justify the circumcised by faith and the uncircumcised through faith.
Romans3:31 Do we then make void the law through faith? Certainly not! On the contrary
Romans4:1 What then shall we say that Abraham our father has found according to the flesh?
Romans4:2 For if Abraham was justified by works
Romans4:3 For what does the Scripture say? “Abraham believed God
Romans4:4 Now to him who works
Romans4:5 But to him who does not work but believes on Him who justifies the ungodly
Romans4:6 just as David also describes the blessedness of the man to whom God imputes righteousness apart from works:
Romans4:7 “Blessed are those whose lawless deeds are forgiven
Romans4:8 Blessed is the man to whom the Lord shall not impute sin.”
Romans4:9 Does this blessedness then come upon the circumcised only
Romans4:10 How then was it accounted? While he was circumcised
Romans4:11 And he received the sign of circumcision
Romans4:12 and the father of circumcision to those who not only are of the circumcision
Romans4:13 For the promise that he would be the heir of the world was not to Abraham or to his seed through the law
Romans4:14 For if those who are of the law are heirs
Romans4:15 because the law brings about wrath; for where there is no law there is no transgression.
Romans4:16 Therefore it is of faith that it might be according to grace
Romans4:17 (as it is written
Romans4:18 who
Romans4:19 And not being weak in faith
Romans4:20 He did not waver at the promise of God through unbelief
Romans4:21 and being fully convinced that what He had promised He was also able to perform.
Romans4:22 And therefore “it was accounted to him for righteousness.”
Romans4:23 Now it was not written for his sake alone that it was imputed to him
Romans4:24 but also for us. It shall be imputed to us who believe in Him who raised up Jesus our Lord from the dead
Romans4:25 who was delivered up because of our offenses
Romans5:1 Therefore
Romans5:2 through whom also we have access by faith into this grace in which we stand
Romans5:3 And not only that
Romans5:4 and perseverance
Romans5:5 Now hope does not disappoint
Romans5:6 For when we were still without strength
Romans5:7 For scarcely for a righteous man will one die; yet perhaps for a good man someone would even dare to die.
Romans5:8 But God demonstrates His own love toward us

1Corinthians7:3 Let the husband render to his wife the affection due her
1Corinthians7:4 The wife does not have authority over her own body
1Corinthians7:5 Do not deprive one another except with consent for a time
1Corinthians7:6 But I say this as a concession
1Corinthians7:7 For I wish that all men were even as I myself. But each one has his own gift from God
1Corinthians7:8 But I say to the unmarried and to the widows: It is good for them if they remain even as I am;
1Corinthians7:9 but if they cannot exercise self-control
1Corinthians7:10 Now to the married I command
1Corinthians7:11 But even if she does depart
1Corinthians7:12 But to the rest I
1Corinthians7:13 And a woman who has a husband who does not believe
1Corinthians7:14 For the unbelieving husband is sanctified by the wife
1Corinthians7:15 But if the unbeliever departs
1Corinthians7:16 For how do you know
1Corinthians7:17 But as God has distributed to each one
1Corinthians7:18 Was anyone called while circumcised? Let him not become uncircumcised. Was anyone called while uncircumcised? Let him not be circumcised.
1Corinthians7:19 Circumcision is nothing and uncircumcision is nothing
1Corinthians7:20 Let each one remain in the same calling in which he was called.
1Corinthians7:21 Were you called while a slave? Do not be concerned about it; but if you can be made free
1Corinthians7:22 For he who is called in the Lord while a slave is the Lord’s freedman. Likewise he who is called while free is Christ’s slave.
1Corinthians7:23 You were bought at a price; do not become slaves of men.
1Corinthians7:24 Brethren
1Corinthians7:25 Now concerning virgins: I have no commandment from the Lord; yet I give judgment as one whom the Lord in His mercy has made trustworthy.
1Corinthians7:26 I suppose therefore that this is good because of the present distress— that it is good for a man to remain as he is:
1Corinthians7:27 Are you bound to a wife? Do not seek to be loosed. Are you loosed from a wife? Do not seek a wife.
1Corinthians7:28 But even if you do marry
1Corinthians7:29 But this I say
1Corinthians7:30 those who weep as though they did not weep
1Corinthians7:31 and those who use this world as not misusing it. For the form of this world is passing away.
1Corinthians7:32 But I want you to be without care. He who is unmarried cares for the things of the Lord—how he may please the Lord.
1Corinthians7:33 But he who is married cares about the things of the world—how he may please his wife.
1Corinthians7:34 There is a difference between a wife and a virgin. The unmarried woman cares about the things of the Lord
1Corinthians7:35 And this I say for your own profit
1Corinthians7:36 But if any man thinks he is behaving improperly toward his virgin
1Corinthians7:37 Nevertheless he who stands steadfast in his heart
1Corinthians7:38 So then he who gives her in marriage does well
1Corinthians7:39 A wife is bound by law as long as her husband lives; but if her husband dies
1Corinthians7:40 But she is happier if she remains as she is
1Corinthians8:1 Now concerning things offered to idols: We know that we all have knowledge. Knowledge puffs up
1Corinthians8:2 And if anyone thinks that he knows anything
1Corinthians8:3 But if anyone loves God
1Corinthians8:4 Therefore concerning the eating of things offered to idols
1Corinthians8:5 For even if there are so-called gods
1Corinthians8:6 yet for us there is one God
1Corinthians8:7 However
1Corinthians8:8 But food does not commend us to God; for neither if we eat are we the better
1Corinthians8:9 But beware lest somehow this liberty of yours become a stumbling block to those who are weak.
1Corinthians8:10 For if anyone sees you who have knowledge eating in an idol’s temple
1Corinthians8:11 And because of your knowledge shall the weak brother perish
1Corinthians8:12 But when you thus sin against the brethren
1Corinthians8:13 Therefore
1Corinthians9:1 Am I not an apostle? Am I not free? Have I not seen Jesus Christ our Lord? Are you not my work in the Lord?
1Corinthians9:2 If I am not an apostle to others
1Corinthians9:3 My defense to those who examine me is this:
1Corinthians9:4 Do we have no right to eat and drink?
1Corinthians9:5 Do we have no right to take along a believing wife
1Corinthians9:6 Or is it only Barnabas and I who have no right to refrain from working?
1Corinthians9:7 Who ever goes to war at his own expense? Who plants a vineyard and does not eat of its fruit? Or who tends a flock and does not drink of the milk of the flock?
1Corinthians9:8 Do I say these things as a mere man? Or does not the law say the same also?
1Corinthians9:9 For it is written in the law of Moses
1Corinthians9:10 Or does He say it altogether for our sakes? For our sakes
1Corinthians9:11 If we have sown spiritual things for you
1Corinthians9:12 If others are partakers of this right over you
1Corinthians9:13 Do you not know that those who minister the holy things eat of the things of the temple
1Corinthians9:14 Even so the Lord has commanded that those who preach the gospel should live from the gospel.
1Corinthians9:15 But I have used none of these things
1Corinthians9:16 For if I preach the gospel
1Corinthians9:17 For if I do this willingly
1Corinthians9:18 What is my reward then? That when I preach the gospel
1Corinthians9:19 For though I am free from all men
1Corinthians9:20 and to the Jews I became as a Jew
1Corinthians9:21 to those who are without law
1Corinthians9:22 to the weak I became as weak
1Corinthians9:23 Now this I do for the gospel’s sake
1Corinthians9:24 Do you not know that those who run in a race all run
1Corinthians9:25 And everyone who competes for the prize is temperate in all things. Now they do it to obtain a perishable crown
1Corinthians9:26 Therefore I run thus: not with uncertainty. Thus I fight: not as one who beats the air.
1Corinthians9:27 But I discipline my body and bring it into subjection
1Corinthians10:1 Moreover
1Corinthians10:2 all were baptized into Moses in the cloud and in the sea
1Corinthians10:3 all ate the same spiritual food
1Corinthians10:4 and all drank the same spiritual drink. For they drank of that spiritual Rock that followed them
1Corinthians10:5 But with most of them God was not well pleased
1Corinthians10:6 Now these things became our examples
1Corinthians10:7 And do not become idolaters as were some of them. As it is written
1Corinthians10:8 Nor let us commit sexual immorality
1Corinthians10:9 nor let us tempt Christ
1Corinthians10:10 nor complain
1Corinthians10:11 Now all these things happened to them as examples
1Corinthians10:12 Therefore let him who thinks he stands take heed lest he fall.
1Corinthians10:13 No temptation has overtaken you except such as is common to man; but God is faithful
1Corinthians10:14 Therefore
1Corinthians10:15 I speak as to wise men; judge for yourselves what I say.
1Corinthians10:16 The cup of blessing which we bless
1Corinthians10:17 For we
1Corinthians10:18 Observe Israel after the flesh: Are not those who eat of the sacrifices partakers of the altar?
1Corinthians10:19 What am I saying then? That an idol is anything
1Corinthians10:20 Rather
1Corinthians10:21 You cannot drink the cup of the Lord and the cup of demons; you cannot partake of the Lord’s table and of the table of demons.
1Corinthians10:22 Or do we provoke the Lord to jealousy? Are we stronger than He?
1Corinthians10:23 All things are lawful for me
1Corinthians10:24 Let no one seek his own
1Corinthians10:25 Eat whatever is sold in the meat market
1Corinthians10:26 for “the earth is the Lord’s
1Corinthians10:27 If any of those who do not believe invites you to dinner
1Corinthians10:28 But if anyone says to you
1Corinthians10:29 “Conscience
1Corinthians10:30 But if I partake with thanks
1Corinthians10:31 Therefore
1Corinthians10:32 Give no offense
1Corinthians10:33 just as I also please all men in all things
1Corinthians11:1 Imitate me
1Corinthians11:2 Now I praise you
1Corinthians11:3 But I want you to know that the head of every man is Christ
1Corinthians11:4 Every man praying or prophesying
1Corinthians11:5 But every woman who prays or prophesies with her head uncovered dishonors her head
1Corinthians11:6 For if a woman is not covered
1Corinthians11:7 For a man indeed ought not to cover his head
1Corinthians11:8 For man is not from woman
1Corinthians11:9 Nor was man created for the woman
1Corinthians11:10 For this reason the woman ought to have a symbol of authority on her head
1Corinthians11:11 Nevertheless
1Corinthians11:12 For as woman came from man
1Corinthians11:13 Judge among yourselves. Is it proper for a woman to pray to God with her head uncovered?
1Corinthians11:14 Does not even nature itself teach you that if a man has long hair
1Corinthians11:15 But if a woman has long hair
1Corinthians11:16 But if anyone seems to be contentious
1Corinthians11:17 Now in giving these instructions I do not praise you
1Corinthians11:18 For first of all
1Corinthians11:19 For there must also be factions among you
1Corinthians11:20 Therefore when you come together in one place
1Corinthians11:21 For in eating
1Corinthians11:22 What! Do you not have houses to eat and drink in? Or do you despise the church of God and shame those who have nothing? What shall I say to you? Shall I praise you in this? I do not praise you.
1Corinthians11:23 For I received from the Lord that which I also delivered to you: that the Lord Jesus on the same night in which He was betrayed took bread;
1Corinthians11:24 and when He had given thanks
1Corinthians11:25 In the same manner He also took the cup after supper
1Corinthians11:26 For as often as you eat this bread and drink this cup
1Corinthians11:27 Therefore whoever eats this bread or drinks this cup of the Lord in an unworthy manner will be guilty of the body and blood of the Lord.
1Corinthians11:28 But let a man examine himself
1Corinthians11:29 For he who eats and drinks in an unworthy manner eats and drinks judgment to himself
1Corinthians11:30 For this reason many are weak and sick among you
1Corinthians11:31 For if we would judge ourselves
1Corinthians11:32 But when we are judged
1Corinthians11:33 Therefore
1Corinthians11:34 But if anyone is hungry
1Corinthians12:1 Now concerning spiritual gifts
1Corinthians12:2 You know that you were Gentiles
1Corinthians12:3 Therefore I make known to you that no one speaking by the Spirit of God calls Jesus accursed
1Corinthians12:4 There are diversities of gifts
1Corinthians12:5 There are differences of ministries
1Corinthians12:6 And there are diversities of activities
1Corinthians12:7 But the manifestation of the Spirit is given to each one for the profit of all:
1Corinthians12:8 for to one is given the word of wisdom through the Spirit
1Corinthians12:9 to another faith by the same Spirit
1Corinthians12:10 to another the working of miracles
1Corinthians12:11 But one and the same Spirit works all these things
1Corinthians12:12 For as the body is one and has many members
1Corinthians12:13 For by one Spirit we were all baptized into one body— whether Jews or Greeks
1Corinthians12:14 For in fact the body is not one member but many.
1Corinthians12:15 If the foot should say
1Corinthians12:16 And if the ear should say
1Corinthians12:17 If the whole body were an eye
1Corinthians12:18 But now God has set the members
1Corinthians12:19 And if they were all one member
1Corinthians12:20 But now indeed there are many members
1Corinthians12:21 And the eye cannot say to the hand
1Corinthians12:22 No
1Corinthians12:23 And those members of the body which we think to be less honorable
1Corinthians12:24 but our presentable parts have no need. But God composed the body
1Corinthians12:25 that there should be no schism in the body
1Corinthians12:26 And if one member suffers
1Corinthians12:27 Now you are the body of Christ
1Corinthians12:28 And God has appointed these in the church: first apostles
1Corinthians12:29 Are all apostles? Are all prophets? Are all teachers? Are all workers of miracles?
1Corinthians12:30 Do all have gifts of healings? Do all speak with tongues? Do all interpret?
1Corinthians12:31 But earnestly desire the best gifts. And yet I show you a more excellent way.
1Corinthians13:1 Though I speak with the tongues of men and of angels
1Corinthians13:2 And though I have the gift of prophecy
1Corinthians13:3 And though I bestow all my goods to feed the poor
1Corinthians13:4 Love suffers long and is kind; love does not envy; love does not parade itself
1Corinthians13:5 does not behave rudely
1Corinthians13:6 does not rejoice in iniquity
1Corinthians13:7 bears all things
1Corinthians13:8 Love never fails. But whether there are prophecies
1Corinthians13:9 For we know in part and we prophesy in part.
1Corinthians13:10 But when that which is perfect has come
1Corinthians13:11 When I was a child
1Corinthians13:12 For now we see in a mirror
1Corinthians13:13 And now abide faith
1Corinthians14:1 Pursue love
1Corinthians14:2 For he who speaks in a tongue does not speak to men but to God
1Corinthians14:3 But he who prophesies speaks edification and exhortation and comfort to men.
1Corinthians14:4 He who speaks in a tongue edifies himself
1Corinthians14:5 I wish you all spoke with tongues
1Corinthians14:6 But now
1Corinthians14:7 Even things without life
1Corinthians14:8 For if the trumpet makes an uncertain sound
1Corinthians14:9 So likewise you
1Corinthians14:10 There are
1Corinthians14:11 Therefore
1Corinthians14:12 Even so you
1Corinthians14:13 Therefore let him who speaks in a tongue pray that he may interpret.
1Corinthians14:14 For if I pray in a tongue
1Corinthians14:15 What is the conclusion then? I will pray with the spirit
1Corinthians14:16 Otherwise
1Corinthians14:17 For you indeed give thanks well
1Corinthians14:18 I thank my God I speak with tongues more than you all;
1Corinthians14:19 yet in the church I would rather speak five words with my understanding
1Corinthians14:20 Brethren
1Corinthians14:21 In the law it is written: “With men of other tongues and other lips I will speak to this people; And yet
1Corinthians14:22 Therefore tongues are for a sign
1Corinthians14:23 Therefore if the whole church comes together in one place
1Corinthians14:24 But if all prophesy
1Corinthians14:25 And thus the secrets of his heart are revealed; and so
1Corinthians14:26 How is it then
1Corinthians14:27 If anyone speaks in a tongue
1Corinthians14:28 But if there is no interpreter
1Corinthians14:29 Let two or three prophets speak
1Corinthians14:30 But if anything is revealed to another who sits by
1Corinthians14:31 For you can all prophesy one by one
1Corinthians14:32 And the spirits of the prophets are subject to the prophets.
1Corinthians14:33 For God is not the author of confusion but of peace
1Corinthians14:34 Let your women keep silent in the churches
1Corinthians14:35 And if they want to learn something
1Corinthians14:36 Or did the word of God come originally from you? Or was it you only that it reached?
1Corinthians14:37 If anyone thinks himself to be a prophet or spiritual
1Corinthians14:38 But if anyone is ignorant
1Corinthians14:39 Therefore
1Corinthians14:40 Let all things be done decently and in order.
1Corinthians15:1 Moreover
1Corinthians15:2 by which also you are saved
1Corinthians15:3 For I delivered to you first of all that which I also received: that Christ died for our sins according to the Scriptures
1Corinthians15:4 and that He was buried
1Corinthians15:5 and that He was seen by Cephas
1Corinthians15:6 After that He was seen by over five hundred brethren at once
1Corinthians15:7 After that He was seen by James
1Corinthians15:8 Then last of all He was seen by me also
1Corinthians15:9 For I am the least of the apostles
1Corinthians15:10 But by the grace of God I am what I am
1Corinthians15:11 Therefore
1Corinthians15:12 Now if Christ is preached that He has been raised from the dead
1Corinthians15:13 But if there is no resurrection of the dead
1Corinthians15:14 And if Christ is not risen
1Corinthians15:15 Yes
1Corinthians15:16 For if the dead do not rise
1Corinthians15:17 And if Christ is not risen
1Corinthians15:18 Then also those who have fallen asleep in Christ have perished.
1Corinthians15:19 If in this life only we have hope in Christ
1Corinthians15:20 But now Christ is risen from the dead
1Corinthians15:21 For since by man came death
1Corinthians15:22 For as in Adam all die
1Corinthians15:23 But each one in his own order: Christ the firstfruits
1Corinthians15:24 Then comes the end
1Corinthians15:25 For He must reign till He has put all enemies under His feet.
1Corinthians15:26 The last enemy that will be destroyed is death.
1Corinthians15:27 For “He has put all things under His feet.” But when He says “all things are put under Him
1Corinthians15:28 Now when all things are made subject to Him
1Corinthians15:29 Otherwise
1Corinthians15:30 And why do we stand in jeopardy every hour?
1Corinthians15:31 I affirm
1Corinthians15:32 If
1Corinthians15:33 Do not be deceived: “Evil company corrupts good habits.”
1Corinthians15:34 Awake to righteousness
1Corinthians15:35 But someone will say
1Corinthians15:36 Foolish one
1Corinthians15:37 And what you sow
1Corinthians15:38 But God gives it a body as He pleases
1Corinthians15:39 All flesh is not the same flesh
1Corinthians15:40 There are also celestial bodies and terrestrial bodies; but the glory of the celestial is one
1Corinthians15:41 There is one glory of the sun
1Corinthians15:42 So also is the resurrection of the dead. The body is sown in corruption
1Corinthians15:43 It is sown in dishonor
1Corinthians15:44 It is sown a natural body
1Corinthians15:45 And so it is written
1Corinthians15:46 However
1Corinthians15:47 The first man was of the earth
1Corinthians15:48 As was the man of dust
1Corinthians15:49 And as we have borne the image of the man of dust
1Corinthians15:50 Now this I say
1Corinthians15:51 Behold
1Corinthians15:52 in a moment
1Corinthians15:53 For this corruptible must put on incorruption
1Corinthians15:54 So when this corruptible has put on incorruption
1Corinthians15:55 “O Death
1Corinthians15:56 The sting of death is sin
1Corinthians15:57 But thanks be to God
1Corinthians15:58 Therefore
1Corinthians16:1 Now concerning the collection for the saints
1Corinthians16:2 On the first day of the week let each one of you lay something aside
1Corinthians16:3 And when I come
1Corinthians16:4 But if it is fitting that I go also
1Corinthians16:5 Now I will come to you when I pass through Macedonia (for I am passing through Macedonia).
1Corinthians16:6 And it may be that I will remain
1Corinthians16:7 For I do not wish to see you now on the way; but I hope to stay a while with you
1Corinthians16:8 But I will tarry in Ephesus until Pentecost.
1Corinthians16:9 For a great and effective door has opened to me
1Corinthians16:10 And if Timothy comes
1Corinthians16:11 Therefore let no one despise him. But send him on his journey in peace
1Corinthians16:12 Now concerning our brother Apollos
1Corinthians16:13 Watch
1Corinthians16:14 Let all that you do be done with love.
1Corinthians16:15 I urge you
1Corinthians16:16 that you also submit to such
1Corinthians16:17 I am glad about the coming of Stephanas
1Corinthians16:18 For they refreshed my spirit and yours. Therefore acknowledge such men.
1Corinthians16:19 The churches of Asia greet you. Aquila and Priscilla greet you heartily in the Lord
1Corinthians16:20 All the brethren greet you. Greet one another with a holy kiss.
1Corinthians16:21 The salutation with my own hand—Paul’s.
1Corinthians16:22 If anyone does not love the Lord Jesus Christ
1Corinthians16:23 The grace of our Lord Jesus Christ be with you.
1Corinthians16:24 My love be with you all in Christ Jesus. Amen.

Ephesians6:20 for which I am an ambassador in chains; that in it I may speak boldly
Ephesians6:21 But that you also may know my affairs and how I am doing
Ephesians6:22 whom I have sent to you for this very purpose
Ephesians6:23 Peace to the brethren
Ephesians6:24 Grace be with all those who love our Lord Jesus Christ in sincerity. Amen.
Philippians1:1 Paul and Timothy
Philippians1:2 Grace to you and peace from God our Father and the Lord Jesus Christ.
Philippians1:3 I thank my God upon every remembrance of you
Philippians1:4 always in every prayer of mine making request for you all with joy
Philippians1:5 for your fellowship in the gospel from the first day until now
Philippians1:6 being confident of this very thing
Philippians1:7 just as it is right for me to think this of you all
Philippians1:8 For God is my witness
Philippians1:9 And this I pray
Philippians1:10 that you may approve the things that are excellent
Philippians1:11 being filled with the fruits of righteousness which are by Jesus Christ
Philippians1:12 But I want you to know
Philippians1:13 so that it has become evident to the whole palace guard
Philippians1:14 and most of the brethren in the Lord
Philippians1:15 Some indeed preach Christ even from envy and strife
Philippians1:16 The former preach Christ from selfish ambition
Philippians1:17 but the latter out of love
Philippians1:18 What then? Only that in every way
Philippians1:19 For I know that this will turn out for my deliverance through your prayer and the supply of the Spirit of Jesus Christ
Philippians1:20 according to my earnest expectation and hope that in nothing I shall be ashamed
Philippians1:21 For to me
Philippians1:22 But if I live on in the flesh
Philippians1:23 For I am hard-pressed between the two
Philippians1:24 Nevertheless to remain in the flesh is more needful for you.
Philippians1:25 And being confident of this
Philippians1:26 that your rejoicing for me may be more abundant in Jesus Christ by my coming to you again.
Philippians1:27 Only let your conduct be worthy of the gospel of Christ
Philippians1:28 and not in any way terrified by your adversaries
Philippians1:29 For to you it has been granted on behalf of Christ
Philippians1:30 having the same conflict which you saw in me and now hear is in me.
Philippians2:1 Therefore if there is any consolation in Christ
Philippians2:2 fulfill my joy by being like-minded
Philippians2:3 Let nothing be done through selfish ambition or conceit
Philippians2:4 Let each of you look out not only for his own interests
Philippians2:5 Let this mind be in you which was also in Christ Jesus
Philippians2:6 who
Philippians2:7 but made Himself of no reputation
Philippians2:8 And being found in appearance as a man
Philippians2:9 Therefore God also has highly exalted Him and given Him the name which is above every name
Philippians2:10 that at the name of Jesus every knee should bow
Philippians2:11 and that every tongue should confess that Jesus Christ is Lord
Philippians2:12 Therefore
Philippians2:13 for it is God who works in you both to will and to do for His good pleasure.
Philippians2:14 Do all things without complaining and disputing
Philippians2:15 that you may become blameless and harmless
Philippians2:16 holding fast the word of life
Philippians2:17 Yes
Philippians2:18 For the same reason you also be glad and rejoice with me.
Philippians2:19 But I trust in the Lord Jesus to send Timothy to you shortly
Philippians2:20 For I have no one like-minded
Philippians2:21 For all seek their own
Philippians2:22 But you know his proven character
Philippians2:23 Therefore I hope to send him at once
Philippians2:24 But I trust in the Lord that I myself shall also come shortly.
Philippians2:25 Yet I considered it necessary to send to you Epaphroditus
Philippians2:26 since he was longing for you all
Philippians2:27 For indeed he was sick almost unto death; but God had mercy on him
Philippians2:28 Therefore I sent him the more eagerly
Philippians2:29 Receive him therefore in the Lord with all gladness
Philippians2:30 because for the work of Christ he came close to death
Philippians3:1 Finally
Philippians3:2 Beware of dogs
Philippians3:3 For we are the circumcision
Philippians3:4 though I also might have confidence in the flesh. If anyone else thinks he may have confidence in the flesh
Philippians3:5 circumcised the eighth day
Philippians3:6 concerning zeal
Philippians3:7 But what things were gain to me
Philippians3:8 Yet indeed I also count all things loss for the excellence of the knowledge of Christ Jesus my Lord
Philippians3:9 and be found in Him
Philippians3:10 that I may know Him and the power of His resurrection
Philippians3:11 if
Philippians3:12 Not that I have already attained
Philippians3:13 Brethren
Philippians3:14 I press toward the goal for the prize of the upward call of God in Christ Jesus.
Philippians3:15 Therefore let us
Philippians3:16 Nevertheless
Philippians3:17 Brethren
Philippians3:18 For many walk
Philippians3:19 whose end is destruction
Philippians3:20 For our citizenship is in heaven
Philippians3:21 who will transform our lowly body that it may be conformed to His glorious body
Philippians4:1 Therefore
Philippians4:2 I implore Euodia and I implore Syntyche to be of the same mind in the Lord.
Philippians4:3 And I urge you also
Philippians4:4 Rejoice in the Lord always. Again I will say
Philippians4:5 Let your gentleness be known to all men. The Lord is at hand.
Philippians4:6 Be anxious for nothing
Philippians4:7 and the peace of God
Philippians4:8 Finally
Philippians4:9 The things which you learned and received and heard and saw in me
Philippians4:10 But I rejoiced in the Lord greatly that now at last your care for me has flourished again; though you surely did care
Philippians4:11 Not that I speak in regard to need
Philippians4:12 I know how to be abased
Philippians4:13 I can do all things through Christ who strengthens me.
Philippians4:14 Nevertheless you have done well that you shared in my distress.
Philippians4:15 Now you Philippians know also that in the beginning of the gospel
Philippians4:16 For even in Thessalonica you sent aid once and again for my necessities.
Philippians4:17 Not that I seek the gift
Philippians4:18 Indeed I have all and abound. I am full
Philippians4:19 And my God shall supply all your need according to His riches in glory by Christ Jesus.
Philippians4:20 Now to our God and Father be glory forever and ever. Amen.
Philippians4:21 Greet every saint in Christ Jesus. The brethren who are with me greet you.
Philippians4:22 All the saints greet you
Philippians4:23 The grace of our Lord Jesus Christ be with you all. Amen.
Colossians1:1 Paul
Colossians1:2 To the saints and faithful brethren in Christ who are in Colosse: Grace to you and peace from God our Father and the Lord Jesus Christ.
Colossians1:3 We give thanks to the God and Father of our Lord Jesus Christ
Colossians1:4 since we heard of your faith in Christ Jesus and of your love for all the saints;
Colossians1:5 because of the hope which is laid up for you in heaven
Colossians1:6 which has come to you
Colossians1:7 as you also learned from Epaphras
Colossians1:8 who also declared to us your love in the Spirit.
Colossians1:9 For this reason we also
Colossians1:10 that you may walk worthy of the Lord
Colossians1:11 strengthened with all might
Colossians1:12 giving thanks to the Father who has qualified us to be partakers of the inheritance of the saints in the light.
Colossians1:13 He has delivered us from the power of darkness and conveyed us into the kingdom of the Son of His love
Colossians1:14 in whom we have redemption through His blood
Colossians1:15 He is the image of the invisible God
Colossians1:16 For by Him all things were created that are in heaven and that are on earth
Colossians1:17 And He is before all things
Colossians1:18 And He is the head of the body
Colossians1:19 For it pleased the Father that in Him all the fullness should dwell
Colossians1:20 and by Him to reconcile all things to Himself
Colossians1:21 And you
Colossians1:22 in the body of His flesh through death
Colossians1:23 if indeed you continue in the faith
Colossians1:24 I now rejoice in my sufferings for you
Colossians1:25 of which I became a minister according to the stewardship from God which was given to me for you
Colossians1:26 the mystery which has been hidden from ages and from generations
Colossians1:27 To them God willed to make known what are the riches of the glory of this mystery among the Gentiles: which is Christ in you
Colossians1:28 Him we preach
Colossians1:29 To this end I also labor
Colossians2:1 For I want you to know what a great conflict I have for you and those in Laodicea
Colossians2:2 that their hearts may be encouraged
Colossians2:3 in whom are hidden all the treasures of wisdom and knowledge.
Colossians2:4 Now this I say lest anyone should deceive you with persuasive words.
Colossians2:5 For though I am absent in the flesh
Colossians2:6 As you therefore have received Christ Jesus the Lord
Colossians2:7 rooted and built up in Him and established in the faith
Colossians2:8 Beware lest anyone cheat you through philosophy and empty deceit
Colossians2:9 For in Him dwells all the fullness of the Godhead bodily;
Colossians2:10 and you are complete in Him
Colossians2:11 In Him you were also circumcised with the circumcision made without hands
Colossians2:12 buried with Him in baptism
Colossians2:13 And you
Colossians2:14 having wiped out the handwriting of requirements that was against us
Colossians2:15 Having disarmed principalities and powers
Colossians2:16 So let no one judge you in food or in drink
Colossians2:17 which are a shadow of things to come
Colossians2:18 Let no one cheat you of your reward
Colossians2:19 and not holding fast to the Head
Colossians2:20 Therefore
Colossians2:21 “Do not touch
Colossians2:22 which all concern things which perish with the using— according to the commandments and doctrines of men?
Colossians2:23 These things indeed have an appearance of wisdom in self-imposed religion
Colossians3:1 If then you were raised with Christ
Colossians3:2 Set your mind on things above
Colossians3:3 For you died
Colossians3:4 When Christ who is our life appears
Colossians3:5 Therefore put to death your members which are on the earth: fornication
Colossians3:6 Because of these things the wrath of God is coming upon the sons of disobedience
Colossians3:7 in which you yourselves once walked when you lived in them.
Colossians3:8 But now you yourselves are to put off all these: anger
Colossians3:9 Do not lie to one another
Colossians3:10 and have put on the new man who is renewed in knowledge according to the image of Him who created him
Colossians3:11 where there is neither Greek nor Jew
Colossians3:12 Therefore
Colossians3:13 bearing with one another
Colossians3:14 But above all these things put on love
Colossians3:15 And let the peace of God rule in your hearts
Colossians3:16 Let the word of Christ dwell in you richly in all wisdom
Colossians3:17 And whatever you do in word or deed
Colossians3:18 Wives
Colossians3:19 Husbands
Colossians3:20 Children
Colossians3:21 Fathers
Colossians3:22 Bondservants
Colossians3:23 And whatever you do
Colossians3:24 knowing that from the Lord you will receive the reward of the inheritance; for you serve the Lord Christ.
Colossians3:25 But he who does wrong will be repaid for what he has done
Colossians4:1 Masters
Colossians4:2 Continue earnestly in prayer
Colossians4:3 meanwhile praying also for us
Colossians4:4 that I may make it manifest
Colossians4:5 Walk in wisdom toward those who are outside
Colossians4:6 Let your speech always be with grace
Colossians4:7 Tychicus
Colossians4:8 I am sending him to you for this very purpose
Colossians4:9 with Onesimus
Colossians4:10 Aristarchus my fellow prisoner greets you
Colossians4:11 and Jesus who is called Justus. These are my only fellow workers for the kingdom of God who are of the circumcision; they have proved to be a comfort to me.
Colossians4:12 Epaphras
Colossians4:13 For I bear him witness that he has a great zeal for you
Colossians4:14 Luke the beloved physician and Demas greet you.
Colossians4:15 Greet the brethren who are in Laodicea
Colossians4:16 Now when this epistle is read among you
Colossians4:17 And say to Archippus
Colossians4:18 This salutation by my own hand—Paul. Remember my chains. Grace be with you. Amen.
1Thessalonians1:1 Paul
1Thessalonians1:2 We give thanks to God always for you all
1Thessalonians1:3 remembering without ceasing your work of faith
1Thessalonians1:4 knowing
1Thessalonians1:5 For our gospel did not come to you in word only
1Thessalonians1:6 And you became followers of us and of the Lord
1Thessalonians1:7 so that you became examples to all in Macedonia and Achaia who believe.
1Thessalonians1:8 For from you the word of the Lord has sounded forth
1Thessalonians1:9 For they themselves declare concerning us what manner of entry we had to you
1Thessalonians1:10 and to wait for His Son from heaven
1Thessalonians2:1 For you yourselves know
1Thessalonians2:2 But even after we had suffered before and were spitefully treated at Philippi
1Thessalonians2:3 For our exhortation did not come from error or uncleanness
1Thessalonians2:4 But as we have been approved by God to be entrusted with the gospel
1Thessalonians2:5 For neither at any time did we use flattering words
1Thessalonians2:6 Nor did we seek glory from men
1Thessalonians2:7 But we were gentle among you
1Thessalonians2:8 So
1Thessalonians2:9 For you remember
1Thessalonians2:10 You are witnesses
1Thessalonians2:11 as you know how we exhorted
1Thessalonians2:12 that you would walk worthy of God who calls you into His own kingdom and glory.
1Thessalonians2:13 For this reason we also thank God without ceasing
1Thessalonians2:14 For you
1Thessalonians2:15 who killed both the Lord Jesus and their own prophets
1Thessalonians2:16 forbidding us to speak to the Gentiles that they may be saved
1Thessalonians2:17 But we
1Thessalonians2:18 Therefore we wanted to come to you—even I
1Thessalonians2:19 For what is our hope
1Thessalonians2:20 For you are our glory and joy.
1Thessalonians3:1 Therefore
1Thessalonians3:2 and sent Timothy
1Thessalonians3:3 that no one should be shaken by these afflictions; for you yourselves know that we are appointed to this.
1Thessalonians3:4 For
1Thessalonians3:5 For this reason
1Thessalonians3:6 But now that Timothy has come to us from you
1Thessalonians3:7 therefore
1Thessalonians3:8 For now we live
1Thessalonians3:9 For what thanks can we render to God for you
1Thessalonians3:10 night and day praying exceedingly that we may see your face and perfect what is lacking in your faith?
1Thessalonians3:11 Now may our God and Father Himself
1Thessalonians3:12 And may the Lord make you increase and abound in love to one another and to all
1Thessalonians3:13 so that He may establish your hearts blameless in holiness before our God and Father at the coming of our Lord Jesus Christ with all His saints.
1Thessalonians4:1 Finally then
1Thessalonians4:2 for you know what commandments we gave you through the Lord Jesus.
1Thessalonians4:3 For this is the will of God
1Thessalonians4:4 that each of you should know how to possess his own vessel in sanctification and honor
1Thessalonians4:5 not in passion of lust
1Thessalonians4:6 that no one should take advantage of and defraud his brother in this matter
1Thessalonians4:7 For God did not call us to uncleanness
1Thessalonians4:8 Therefore he who rejects this does not reject man
1Thessalonians4:9 But concerning brotherly love you have no need that I should write to you
1Thessalonians4:10 and indeed you do so toward all the brethren who are in all Macedonia. But we urge you
1Thessalonians4:11 that you also aspire to lead a quiet life
1Thessalonians4:12 that you may walk properly toward those who are outside
1Thessalonians4:13 But I do not want you to be ignorant
1Thessalonians4:14 For if we believe that Jesus died and rose again
1Thessalonians4:15 For this we say to you by the word of the Lord
1Thessalonians4:16 For the Lord Himself will descend from heaven with a shout
1Thessalonians4:17 Then we who are alive and remain shall be caught up together with them in the clouds to meet the Lord in the air. And thus we shall always be with the Lord.
1Thessalonians4:18 Therefore comfort one another with these words.
1Thessalonians5:1 But concerning the times and the seasons
1Thessalonians5:2 For you yourselves know perfectly that the day of the Lord so comes as a thief in the night.
1Thessalonians5:3 For when they say
1Thessalonians5:4 But you
1Thessalonians5:5 You are all sons of light and sons of the day. We are not of the night nor of darkness.
1Thessalonians5:6 Therefore let us not sleep
1Thessalonians5:7 For those who sleep
1Thessalonians5:8 But let us who are of the day be sober
1Thessalonians5:9 For God did not appoint us to wrath
1Thessalonians5:10 who died for us
1Thessalonians5:11 Therefore comfort each other and edify one another
1Thessalonians5:12 And we urge you
1Thessalonians5:13 and to esteem them very highly in love for their work’s sake. Be at peace among yourselves.
1Thessalonians5:14 Now we exhort you
1Thessalonians5:15 See that no one renders evil for evil to anyone
1Thessalonians5:16 Rejoice always
1Thessalonians5:17 pray without ceasing
1Thessalonians5:18 in everything give thanks; for this is the will of God in Christ Jesus for you.
1Thessalonians5:19 Do not quench the Spirit.
1Thessalonians5:20 Do not despise prophecies.
1Thessalonians5:21 Test all things; hold fast what is good.
1Thessalonians5:22 Abstain from every form of evil.
1Thessalonians5:23 Now may the God of peace Himself sanctify you completely; and may your whole spirit
1Thessalonians5:24 He who calls you is faithful
1Thessalonians5:25 Brethren
1Thessalonians5:26 Greet all the brethren with a holy kiss.
1Thessalonians5:27 I charge you by the Lord that this epistle be read to all the holy brethren.
1Thessalonians5:28 The grace of our Lord Jesus Christ be with you. Amen.
2Thessalonians1:1 Paul
2Thessalonians1:2 Grace to you and peace from God our Father and the Lord Jesus Christ.
2Thessalonians1:3 We are bound to thank God always for you
2Thessalonians1:4 so that we ourselves boast of you among the churches of God for your patience and faith in all your persecutions and tribulations that you endure
2Thessalonians1:5 which is manifest evidence of the righteous judgment of God
2Thessalonians1:6 since it is a righteous thing with God to repay with tribulation those who trouble you
2Thessalonians1:7 and to give you who are troubled rest with us when the Lord Jesus is revealed from heaven with His mighty angels
2Thessalonians1:8 in flaming fire taking vengeance on those who do not know God
2Thessalonians1:9 These shall be punished with everlasting destruction from the presence of the Lord and from the glory of His power
2Thessalonians1:10 when He comes
2Thessalonians1:11 Therefore we also pray always for you that our God would count you worthy of this calling
2Thessalonians1:12 that the name of our Lord Jesus Christ may be glorified in you
2Thessalonians2:1 Now
2Thessalonians2:2 not to be soon shaken in mind or troubled
2Thessalonians2:3 Let no one deceive you by any means; for that Day will not come unless the falling away comes first
2Thessalonians2:4 who opposes and exalts himself above all that is called God or that is worshiped
2Thessalonians2:5 Do you not remember that when I was still with you I told you these things?
2Thessalonians2:6 And now you know what is restraining
2Thessalonians2:7 For the mystery of lawlessness is already at work; only He who now restrains will do so until He is taken out of the way.
2Thessalonians2:8 And then the lawless one will be revealed
2Thessalonians2:9 The coming of the lawless one is according to the working of Satan
2Thessalonians2:10 and with all unrighteous deception among those who perish
2Thessalonians2:11 And for this reason God will send them strong delusion
2Thessalonians2:12 that they all may be condemned who did not believe the truth but had pleasure in unrighteousness.
2Thessalonians2:13 But we are bound to give thanks to God always for you
2Thessalonians2:14 to which He called you by our gospel
2Thessalonians2:15 Therefore
2Thessalonians2:16 Now may our Lord Jesus Christ Himself
2Thessalonians2:17 comfort your hearts and establish you in every good word and work.
2Thessalonians3:1 Finally
2Thessalonians3:2 and that we may be delivered from unreasonable and wicked men; for not all have faith.
2Thessalonians3:3 But the Lord is faithful
2Thessalonians3:4 And we have confidence in the Lord concerning you
2Thessalonians3:5 Now may the Lord direct your hearts into the love of God and into the patience of Christ.
2Thessalonians3:6 But we command you
2Thessalonians3:7 For you yourselves know how you ought to follow us
2Thessalonians3:8 nor did we eat anyone’s bread free of charge
2Thessalonians3:9 not because we do not have authority
2Thessalonians3:10 For even when we were with you
2Thessalonians3:11 For we hear that there are some who walk among you in a disorderly manner
2Thessalonians3:12 Now those who are such we command and exhort through our Lord Jesus Christ that they work in quietness and eat their own bread.
2Thessalonians3:13 But as for you
2Thessalonians3:14 And if anyone does not obey our word in this epistle
2Thessalonians3:15 Yet do not count him as an enemy
2Thessalonians3:16 Now may the Lord of peace Himself give you peace always in every way. The Lord be with you all.
2Thessalonians3:17 The salutation of Paul with my own hand
2Thessalonians3:18 The grace of our Lord Jesus Christ be with you all. Amen.
1Timothy1:1 Paul
1Timothy1:2 To Timothy
1Timothy1:3 As I urged you when I went into Macedonia—remain in Ephesus that you may charge some that they teach no other doctrine
1Timothy1:4 nor give heed to fables and endless genealogies
1Timothy1:5 Now the purpose of the commandment is love from a pure heart
1Timothy1:6 from which some
1Timothy1:7 desiring to be teachers of the law
1Timothy1:8 But we know that the law is good if one uses it lawfully
1Timothy1:9 knowing this: that the law is not made for a righteous person
1Timothy1:10 for fornicators
1Timothy1:11 according to the glorious gospel of the blessed God which was committed to my trust.
1Timothy1:12 And I thank Christ Jesus our Lord who has enabled me
1Timothy1:13 although I was formerly a blasphemer
1Timothy1:14 And the grace of our Lord was exceedingly abundant
1Timothy1:15 This is a faithful saying and worthy of all acceptance
1Timothy1:16 However
1Timothy1:17 Now to the King eternal
1Timothy1:18 This charge I commit to you
1Timothy1:19 having faith and a good conscience
1Timothy1:20 of whom are Hymenaeus and Alexander
1Timothy2:1 Therefore I exhort first of all that supplications
1Timothy2:2 for kings and all who are in authority
1Timothy2:3 For this is good and acceptable in the sight of God our Savior
1Timothy2:4 who desires all men to be saved and to come to the knowledge of the truth.
1Timothy2:5 For there is one God and one Mediator between God and men
1Timothy2:6 who gave Himself a ransom for all
1Timothy2:7 for which I was appointed a preacher and an apostle—I am speaking the truth in Christ and not lying— a teacher of the Gentiles in faith and truth.
1Timothy2:8 I desire therefore that the men pray everywhere
1Timothy2:9 in like manner also
1Timothy2:10 but
1Timothy2:11 Let a woman learn in silence with all submission.
1Timothy2:12 And I do not permit a woman to teach or to have authority over a man
1Timothy2:13 For Adam was formed first
1Timothy2:14 And Adam was not deceived
1Timothy2:15 Nevertheless she will be saved in childbearing if they continue in faith
1Timothy3:1 This is a faithful saying: If a man desires the position of a bishop
1Timothy3:2 A bishop then must be blameless
1Timothy3:3 not given to wine
1Timothy3:4 one who rules his own house well
1Timothy3:5 (for if a man does not know how to rule his own house
1Timothy3:6 not a novice
1Timothy3:7 Moreover he must have a good testimony among those who are outside
1Timothy3:8 Likewise deacons must be reverent
1Timothy3:9 holding the mystery of the faith with a pure conscience.
1Timothy3:10 But let these also first be tested; then let them serve as deacons
1Timothy3:11 Likewise
1Timothy3:12 Let deacons be the husbands of one wife
1Timothy3:13 For those who have served well as deacons obtain for themselves a good standing and great boldness in the faith which is in Christ Jesus.
1Timothy3:14 These things I write to you
1Timothy3:15 but if I am delayed
1Timothy3:16 And without controversy great is the mystery of godliness: God was manifested in the flesh
1Timothy4:1 Now the Spirit expressly says that in latter times some will depart from the faith
1Timothy4:2 speaking lies in hypocrisy
1Timothy4:3 forbidding to marry
1Timothy4:4 For every creature of God is good
1Timothy4:5 for it is sanctified by the word of God and prayer.
1Timothy4:6 If you instruct the brethren in these things
1Timothy4:7 But reject profane and old wives’ fables
1Timothy4:8 For bodily exercise profits a little
1Timothy4:9 This is a faithful saying and worthy of all acceptance.
1Timothy4:10 For to this end we both labor and suffer reproach
1Timothy4:11 These things command and teach.
1Timothy4:12 Let no one despise your youth
1Timothy4:13 Till I come
1Timothy4:14 Do not neglect the gift that is in you
1Timothy4:15 Meditate on these things; give yourself entirely to them
1Timothy4:16 Take heed to yourself and to the doctrine. Continue in them
1Timothy5:1 Do not rebuke an older man
1Timothy5:2 older women as mothers
1Timothy5:3 Honor widows who are really widows.
1Timothy5:4 But if any widow has children or grandchildren
1Timothy5:5 Now she who is really a widow
1Timothy5:6 But she who lives in pleasure is dead while she lives.
1Timothy5:7 And these things command
1Timothy5:8 But if anyone does not provide for his own
1Timothy5:9 Do not let a widow under sixty years old be taken into the number
1Timothy5:10 well reported for good works: if she has brought up children
1Timothy5:11 But refuse the younger widows; for when they have begun to grow wanton against Christ
1Timothy5:12 having condemnation because they have cast off their first faith.
1Timothy5:13 And besides they learn to be idle
1Timothy5:14 Therefore I desire that the younger widows marry
1Timothy5:15 For some have already turned aside after Satan.
1Timothy5:16 If any believing man or woman has widows
1Timothy5:17 Let the elders who rule well be counted worthy of double honor
1Timothy5:18 For the Scripture says
1Timothy5:19 Do not receive an accusation against an elder except from two or three witnesses.
1Timothy5:20 Those who are sinning rebuke in the presence of all
1Timothy5:21 I charge you before God and the Lord Jesus Christ and the elect angels that you observe these things without prejudice
1Timothy5:22 Do not lay hands on anyone hastily
1Timothy5:23 No longer drink only water
1Timothy5:24 Some men’s sins are clearly evident
1Timothy5:25 Likewise
1Timothy6:1 Let as many bondservants as are under the yoke count their own masters worthy of all honor
1Timothy6:2 And those who have believing masters
1Timothy6:3 If anyone teaches otherwise and does not consent to wholesome words
1Timothy6:4 he is proud
1Timothy6:5 useless wranglings of men of corrupt minds and destitute of the truth
1Timothy6:6 Now godliness with contentment is great gain.
1Timothy6:7 For we brought nothing into this world
1Timothy6:8 And having food and clothing
1Timothy6:9 But those who desire to be rich fall into temptation and a snare
1Timothy6:10 For the love of money is a root of all kinds of evil
1Timothy6:11 But you
1Timothy6:12 Fight the good fight of faith
1Timothy6:13 I urge you in the sight of God who gives life to all things
1Timothy6:14 that you keep this commandment without spot
1Timothy6:15 which He will manifest in His own time
1Timothy6:16 who alone has immortality
1Timothy6:17 Command those who are rich in this present age not to be haughty
1Timothy6:18 Let them do good
1Timothy6:19 storing up for themselves a good foundation for the time to come
1Timothy6:20 O Timothy! Guard what was committed to your trust
1Timothy6:21 by professing it some have strayed concerning the faith. Grace be with you. Amen.
2Timothy1:1 Paul
2Timothy1:2 To Timothy
2Timothy1:3 I thank God
2Timothy1:4 greatly desiring to see you
2Timothy1:5 when I call to remembrance the genuine faith that is in you
2Timothy1:6 Therefore I remind you to stir up the gift of God which is in you through the laying on of my hands.
2Timothy1:7 For God has not given us a spirit of fear
2Timothy1:8 Therefore do not be ashamed of the testimony of our Lord
2Timothy1:9 who has saved us and called us with a holy calling
2Timothy1:10 but has now been revealed by the appearing of our Savior Jesus Christ
2Timothy1:11 to which I was appointed a preacher
2Timothy1:12 For this reason I also suffer these things; nevertheless I am not ashamed
2Timothy1:13 Hold fast the pattern of sound words which you have heard from me
2Timothy1:14 That good thing which was committed to you
2Timothy1:15 This you know
2Timothy1:16 The Lord grant mercy to the household of Onesiphorus
2Timothy1:17 but when he arrived in Rome
2Timothy1:18 The Lord grant to him that he may find mercy from the Lord in that Day—and you know very well how many ways he ministered to me at Ephesus.
2Timothy2:1 You therefore
2Timothy2:2 And the things that you have heard from me among many witnesses
2Timothy2:3 You therefore must endure hardship as a good soldier of Jesus Christ.
2Timothy2:4 No one engaged in warfare entangles himself with the affairs of this life
2Timothy2:5 And also if anyone competes in athletics
2Timothy2:6 The hardworking farmer must be first to partake of the crops.
2Timothy2:7 Consider what I say
2Timothy2:8 Remember that Jesus Christ
2Timothy2:9 for which I suffer trouble as an evildoer
2Timothy2:10 Therefore I endure all things for the sake of the elect
2Timothy2:11 This is a faithful saying: For if we died with Him
2Timothy2:12 If we endure
2Timothy2:13 If we are faithless
2Timothy2:14 Remind them of these things
2Timothy2:15 Be diligent to present yourself approved to God
2Timothy2:16 But shun profane and idle babblings
2Timothy2:17 And their message will spread like cancer. Hymenaeus and Philetus are of this sort
2Timothy2:18 who have strayed concerning the truth
2Timothy2:19 Nevertheless the solid foundation of God stands
2Timothy2:20 But in a great house there are not only vessels of gold and silver
2Timothy2:21 Therefore if anyone cleanses himself from the latter
2Timothy2:22 Flee also youthful lusts; but pursue righteousness
2Timothy2:23 But avoid foolish and ignorant disputes
2Timothy2:24 And a servant of the Lord must not quarrel but be gentle to all
2Timothy2:25 in humility correcting those who are in opposition
2Timothy2:26 and that they may come to their senses and escape the snare of the devil
2Timothy3:1 But know this
2Timothy3:2 For men will be lovers of themselves
2Timothy3:3 unloving
2Timothy3:4 traitors
2Timothy3:5 having a form of godliness but denying its power. And from such people turn away!
2Timothy3:6 For of this sort are those who creep into households and make captives of gullible women loaded down with sins
2Timothy3:7 always learning and never able to come to the knowledge of the truth.
2Timothy3:8 Now as Jannes and Jambres resisted Moses
2Timothy3:9 but they will progress no further
2Timothy3:10 But you have carefully followed my doctrine
2Timothy3:11 persecutions
2Timothy3:12 Yes
2Timothy3:13 But evil men and impostors will grow worse and worse
2Timothy3:14 But you must continue in the things which you have learned and been assured of
2Timothy3:15 and that from childhood you have known the Holy Scriptures
2Timothy3:16 All Scripture is given by inspiration of God
2Timothy3:17 that the man of God may be complete
2Timothy4:1 I charge you therefore before God and the Lord Jesus Christ
2Timothy4:2 Preach the word! Be ready in season and out of season. Convince
2Timothy4:3 For the time will come when they will not endure sound doctrine
2Timothy4:4 and they will turn their ears away from the truth
2Timothy4:5 But you be watchful in all things
2Timothy4:6 For I am already being poured out as a drink offering
2Timothy4:7 I have fought the good fight
2Timothy4:8 Finally
2Timothy4:9 Be diligent to come to me quickly;
2Timothy4:10 for Demas has forsaken me
2Timothy4:11 Only Luke is with me. Get Mark and bring him with you
2Timothy4:12 And Tychicus I have sent to Ephesus.
2Timothy4:13 Bring the cloak that I left with Carpus at Troas when you come—and the books
2Timothy4:14 Alexander the coppersmith did me much harm. May the Lord repay him according to his works.
2Timothy4:15 You also must beware of him
2Timothy4:16 At my first defense no one stood with me
2Timothy4:17 But the Lord stood with me and strengthened me
2Timothy4:18 And the Lord will deliver me from every evil work and preserve me for His heavenly kingdom. To Him be glory forever and ever. Amen!
2Timothy4:19 Greet Prisca and Aquila
2Timothy4:20 Erastus stayed in Corinth
2Timothy4:21 Do your utmost to come before winter. Eubulus greets you
2Timothy4:22 The Lord Jesus Christ be with your spirit. Grace be with you. Amen.

Titus1:2 in hope of eternal life which God
Titus1:3 but has in due time manifested His word through preaching
Titus1:4 To Titus
