Titus1:6 if a man is blameless
Titus1:7 For a bishop must be blameless
Titus1:8 but hospitable
Titus1:9 holding fast the faithful word as he has been taught
Titus1:10 For there are many insubordinate
Titus1:11 whose mouths must be stopped
Titus1:12 One of them
Titus1:13 This testimony is true. Therefore rebuke them sharply
Titus1:14 not giving heed to Jewish fables and commandments of men who turn from the truth.
Titus1:15 To the pure all things are pure
Titus1:16 They profess to know God
Titus2:1 But as for you
Titus2:2 that the older men be sober
Titus2:3 the older women likewise
Titus2:4 that they admonish the young women to love their husbands
Titus2:5 to be discreet
Titus2:6 Likewise
Titus2:7 in all things showing yourself to be a pattern of good works; in doctrine showing integrity
Titus2:8 sound speech that cannot be condemned
Titus2:9 Exhort bondservants to be obedient to their own masters
Titus2:10 not pilfering
Titus2:11 For the grace of God that brings salvation has appeared to all men
Titus2:12 teaching us that
Titus2:13 looking for the blessed hope and glorious appearing of our great God and Savior Jesus Christ
Titus2:14 who gave Himself for us
Titus2:15 Speak these things
Titus3:1 Remind them to be subject to rulers and authorities
Titus3:2 to speak evil of no one
Titus3:3 For we ourselves were also once foolish
Titus3:4 But when the kindness and the love of God our Savior toward man appeared
Titus3:5 not by works of righteousness which we have done
Titus3:6 whom He poured out on us abundantly through Jesus Christ our Savior
Titus3:7 that having been justified by His grace we should become heirs according to the hope of eternal life.
Titus3:8 This is a faithful saying
Titus3:9 But avoid foolish disputes
Titus3:10 Reject a divisive man after the first and second admonition
Titus3:11 knowing that such a person is warped and sinning
Titus3:12 When I send Artemas to you
Titus3:13 Send Zenas the lawyer and Apollos on their journey with haste
Titus3:14 And let our people also learn to maintain good works
Titus3:15 All who are with me greet you. Greet those who love us in the faith. Grace be with you all. Amen.
Philemon1:1 Paul
Philemon1:2 to the beloved Apphia
Philemon1:3 Grace to you and peace from God our Father and the Lord Jesus Christ.
Philemon1:4 I thank my God
Philemon1:5 hearing of your love and faith which you have toward the Lord Jesus and toward all the saints
Philemon1:6 that the sharing of your faith may become effective by the acknowledgment of every good thing which is in you in Christ Jesus.
Philemon1:7 For we have great joy and consolation in your love
Philemon1:8 Therefore
Philemon1:9 yet for love’s sake I rather appeal to you —being such a one as Paul
Philemon1:10 I appeal to you for my son Onesimus
Philemon1:11 who once was unprofitable to you
Philemon1:12 I am sending him back. You therefore receive him
Philemon1:13 whom I wished to keep with me
Philemon1:14 But without your consent I wanted to do nothing
Philemon1:15 For perhaps he departed for a while for this purpose
Philemon1:16 no longer as a slave but more than a slave—a beloved brother
Philemon1:17 If then you count me as a partner
Philemon1:18 But if he has wronged you or owes anything
Philemon1:19 I
Philemon1:20 Yes
Philemon1:21 Having confidence in your obedience
Philemon1:22 But
Philemon1:23 Epaphras
Philemon1:24 as do Mark
Philemon1:25 The grace of our Lord Jesus Christ be with your spirit. Amen.
Hebrews1:1 God
Hebrews1:2 has in these last days spoken to us by His Son
Hebrews1:3 who being the brightness of His glory and the express image of His person
Hebrews1:4 having become so much better than the angels
Hebrews1:5 For to which of the angels did He ever say: “You are My Son
Hebrews1:6 But when He again brings the firstborn into the world
Hebrews1:7 And of the angels He says: “Who makes His angels spirits And His ministers a flame of fire.”
Hebrews1:8 But to the Son He says: “Your throne
Hebrews1:9 You have loved righteousness and hated lawlessness; Therefore God
Hebrews1:10 And: “You
Hebrews1:11 They will perish
Hebrews1:12 Like a cloak You will fold them up
Hebrews1:13 But to which of the angels has He ever said: “Sit at My right hand
Hebrews1:14 Are they not all ministering spirits sent forth to minister for those who will inherit salvation?
Hebrews2:1 Therefore we must give the more earnest heed to the things we have heard
Hebrews2:2 For if the word spoken through angels proved steadfast
Hebrews2:3 how shall we escape if we neglect so great a salvation
Hebrews2:4 God also bearing witness both with signs and wonders
Hebrews2:5 For He has not put the world to come
Hebrews2:6 But one testified in a certain place
Hebrews2:7 You have made him a little lower than the angels; You have crowned him with glory and honor
Hebrews2:8 You have put all things in subjection under his feet.” For in that He put all in subjection under him
Hebrews2:9 But we see Jesus
Hebrews2:10 For it was fitting for Him
Hebrews2:11 For both He who sanctifies and those who are being sanctified are all of one
Hebrews2:12 saying: “I will declare Your name to My brethren; In the midst of the assembly I will sing praise to You.”
Hebrews2:13 And again: “I will put My trust in Him.” And again: “Here am I and the children whom God has given Me.”
Hebrews2:14 Inasmuch then as the children have partaken of flesh and blood
Hebrews2:15 and release those who through fear of death were all their lifetime subject to bondage.
Hebrews2:16 For indeed He does not give aid to angels
Hebrews2:17 Therefore
Hebrews2:18 For in that He Himself has suffered
Hebrews3:1 Therefore
Hebrews3:2 who was faithful to Him who appointed Him
Hebrews3:3 For this One has been counted worthy of more glory than Moses
Hebrews3:4 For every house is built by someone
Hebrews3:5 And Moses indeed was faithful in all His house as a servant
Hebrews3:6 but Christ as a Son over His own house
Hebrews3:7 Therefore
Hebrews3:8 Do not harden your hearts as in the rebellion
Hebrews3:9 Where your fathers tested Me
Hebrews3:10 Therefore I was angry with that generation
Hebrews3:11 So I swore in My wrath
Hebrews3:12 Beware
Hebrews3:13 but exhort one another daily
Hebrews3:14 For we have become partakers of Christ if we hold the beginning of our confidence steadfast to the end
Hebrews3:15 while it is said: “Today
Hebrews3:16 For who
Hebrews3:17 Now with whom was He angry forty years? Was it not with those who sinned
Hebrews3:18 And to whom did He swear that they would not enter His rest
Hebrews3:19 So we see that they could not enter in because of unbelief.
Hebrews4:1 Therefore
Hebrews4:2 For indeed the gospel was preached to us as well as to them; but the word which they heard did not profit them
Hebrews4:3 For we who have believed do enter that rest
Hebrews4:4 For He has spoken in a certain place of the seventh day in this way: “And God rested on the seventh day from all His works”;
Hebrews4:5 and again in this place: “They shall not enter My rest.”
Hebrews4:6 Since therefore it remains that some must enter it
Hebrews4:7 again He designates a certain day
Hebrews4:8 For if Joshua had given them rest
Hebrews4:9 There remains therefore a rest for the people of God.
Hebrews4:10 For he who has entered His rest has himself also ceased from his works as God did from His.
Hebrews4:11 Let us therefore be diligent to enter that rest
Hebrews4:12 For the word of God is living and powerful
Hebrews4:13 And there is no creature hidden from His sight
Hebrews4:14 Seeing then that we have a great High Priest who has passed through the heavens
Hebrews4:15 For we do not have a High Priest who cannot sympathize with our weaknesses
Hebrews4:16 Let us therefore come boldly to the throne of grace
Hebrews5:1 For every high priest taken from among men is appointed for men in things pertaining to God
Hebrews5:2 He can have compassion on those who are ignorant and going astray
Hebrews5:3 Because of this he is required as for the people
Hebrews5:4 And no man takes this honor to himself
Hebrews5:5 So also Christ did not glorify Himself to become High Priest
Hebrews5:6 As He also says in another place: “You are a priest forever According to the order of Melchizedek”;
Hebrews5:7 who
Hebrews5:8 though He was a Son
Hebrews5:9 And having been perfected
Hebrews5:10 called by God as High Priest “according to the order of Melchizedek
Hebrews5:11 of whom we have much to say
Hebrews5:12 For though by this time you ought to be teachers
Hebrews5:13 For everyone who partakes only of milk is unskilled in the word of righteousness
Hebrews5:14 But solid food belongs to those who are of full age
Hebrews6:1 Therefore
Hebrews6:2 of the doctrine of baptisms
Hebrews6:3 And this we will do if God permits.
Hebrews6:4 For it is impossible for those who were once enlightened
Hebrews6:5 and have tasted the good word of God and the powers of the age to come
Hebrews6:6 if they fall away
Hebrews6:7 For the earth which drinks in the rain that often comes upon it
Hebrews6:8 but if it bears thorns and briers
Hebrews6:9 But
Hebrews6:10 For God is not unjust to forget your work and labor of love which you have shown toward His name
Hebrews6:11 And we desire that each one of you show the same diligence to the full assurance of hope until the end
Hebrews6:12 that you do not become sluggish
Hebrews6:13 For when God made a promise to Abraham
Hebrews6:14 saying
Hebrews6:15 And so

Hebrews6:17 Thus God
Hebrews6:18 that by two immutable things
Hebrews6:19 This hope we have as an anchor of the soul

Hebrews7:1 For this Melchizedek
Hebrews7:2 to whom also Abraham gave a tenth part of all
Hebrews7:3 without father
Hebrews7:4 Now consider how great this man was
Hebrews7:5 And indeed those who are of the sons of Levi
Hebrews7:6 but he whose genealogy is not derived from them received tithes from Abraham and blessed him who had the promises.
Hebrews7:7 Now beyond all contradiction the lesser is blessed by the better.
Hebrews7:8 Here mortal men receive tithes
Hebrews7:9 Even Levi
Hebrews7:10 for he was still in the loins of his father when Melchizedek met him.
Hebrews7:11 Therefore
Hebrews7:12 For the priesthood being changed
Hebrews7:13 For He of whom these things are spoken belongs to another tribe
Hebrews7:14 For it is evident that our Lord arose from Judah
Hebrews7:15 And it is yet far more evident if
Hebrews7:16 who has come
Hebrews7:17 For He testifies: “You are a priest forever According to the order of Melchizedek.”
Hebrews7:18 For on the one hand there is an annulling of the former commandment because of its weakness and unprofitableness
Hebrews7:19 for the law made nothing perfect; on the other hand
Hebrews7:20 And inasmuch as He was not made priest without an oath
Hebrews7:21 (for they have become priests without an oath
Hebrews7:22 by so much more Jesus has become a surety of a better covenant.
Hebrews7:23 Also there were many priests
Hebrews7:24 But He
Hebrews7:25 Therefore He is also able to save to the uttermost those who come to God through Him
Hebrews7:26 For such a High Priest was fitting for us
Hebrews7:27 who does not need daily
Hebrews7:28 For the law appoints as high priests men who have weakness
Hebrews8:1 Now this is the main point of the things we are saying: We have such a High Priest
Hebrews8:2 a Minister of the sanctuary and of the true tabernacle which the Lord erected
Hebrews8:3 For every high priest is appointed to offer both gifts and sacrifices. Therefore it is necessary that this One also have something to offer.
Hebrews8:4 For if He were on earth
Hebrews8:5 who serve the copy and shadow of the heavenly things
Hebrews8:6 But now He has obtained a more excellent ministry
Hebrews8:7 For if that first covenant had been faultless
Hebrews8:8 Because finding fault with them
Hebrews8:9 not according to the covenant that I made with their fathers in the day when I took them by the hand to lead them out of the land of Egypt; because they did not continue in My covenant
Hebrews8:10 For this is the covenant that I will make with the house of Israel after those days
Hebrews8:11 None of them shall teach his neighbor
Hebrews8:12 For I will be merciful to their unrighteousness
Hebrews8:13 In that He says
Hebrews9:1 Then indeed
Hebrews9:2 For a tabernacle was prepared: the first part
Hebrews9:3 and behind the second veil
Hebrews9:4 which had the golden censer and the ark of the covenant overlaid on all sides with gold
Hebrews9:5 and above it were the cherubim of glory overshadowing the mercy seat. Of these things we cannot now speak in detail.
Hebrews9:6 Now when these things had been thus prepared
Hebrews9:7 But into the second part the high priest went alone once a year
Hebrews9:8 the Holy Spirit indicating this
Hebrews9:9 It was symbolic for the present time in which both gifts and sacrifices are offered which cannot make him who performed the service perfect in regard to the conscience—
Hebrews9:10 concerned only with foods and drinks
Hebrews9:11 But Christ came as High Priest of the good things to come
Hebrews9:12 Not with the blood of goats and calves
Hebrews9:13 For if the blood of bulls and goats and the ashes of a heifer
Hebrews9:14 how much more shall the blood of Christ
Hebrews9:15 And for this reason He is the Mediator of the new covenant
Hebrews9:16 For where there is a testament
Hebrews9:17 For a testament is in force after men are dead
Hebrews9:18 Therefore not even the first covenant was dedicated without blood.
Hebrews9:19 For when Moses had spoken every precept to all the people according to the law
Hebrews9:20 saying
Hebrews9:21 Then likewise he sprinkled with blood both the tabernacle and all the vessels of the ministry.
Hebrews9:22 And according to the law almost all things are purified with blood
Hebrews9:23 Therefore it was necessary that the copies of the things in the heavens should be purified with these
Hebrews9:24 For Christ has not entered the holy places made with hands
Hebrews9:25 not that He should offer Himself often
Hebrews9:26 He then would have had to suffer often since the foundation of the world; but now
Hebrews9:27 And as it is appointed for men to die once
Hebrews9:28 so Christ was offered once to bear the sins of many. To those who eagerly wait for Him He will appear a second time
Hebrews10:1 For the law
Hebrews10:2 For then would they not have ceased to be offered? For the worshipers
Hebrews10:3 But in those sacrifices there is a reminder of sins every year.
Hebrews10:4 For it is not possible that the blood of bulls and goats could take away sins.
Hebrews10:5 Therefore
Hebrews10:6 In burnt offerings and sacrifices for sin You had no pleasure.

Hebrews10:8 Previously saying
Hebrews10:9 then He said

Hebrews10:12 But this Man

Hebrews10:14 For by one offering He has perfected forever those who are being sanctified.

Hebrews10:17 then He adds

Hebrews10:19 Therefore

Hebrews10:23 Let us hold fast the confession of our hope without wavering
Hebrews10:24 And let us consider one another in order to stir up love and good works
Hebrews10:25 not forsaking the assembling of ourselves together
Hebrews10:26 For if we sin willfully after we have received the knowledge of the truth
Hebrews10:27 but a certain fearful expectation of judgment
Hebrews10:28 Anyone who has rejected Moses’ law dies without mercy on the testimony of two or three witnesses.
Hebrews10:29 Of how much worse punishment
Hebrews10:30 For we know Him who said
Hebrews10:31 It is a fearful thing to fall into the hands of the living God.
Hebrews10:32 But recall the former days in which
Hebrews10:33 partly while you were made a spectacle both by reproaches and tribulations
Hebrews10:34 for you had compassion on me in my chains
Hebrews10:35 Therefore do not cast away your confidence
Hebrews10:36 For you have need of endurance
Hebrews10:37 “For yet a little while
Hebrews10:38 Now the just shall live by faith; But if anyone draws back
Hebrews10:39 But we are not of those who draw back to perdition
Hebrews11:1 Now faith is the substance of things hoped for
Hebrews11:2 For by it the elders obtained a good testimony.
Hebrews11:3 By faith we understand that the worlds were framed by the word of God
Hebrews11:4 By faith Abel offered to God a more excellent sacrifice than Cain
Hebrews11:5 By faith Enoch was taken away so that he did not see death
Hebrews11:6 But without faith it is impossible to please Him
Hebrews11:7 By faith Noah
Hebrews11:8 By faith Abraham obeyed when he was called to go out to the place which he would receive as an inheritance. And he went out
Hebrews11:9 By faith he dwelt in the land of promise as in a foreign country
Hebrews11:10 for he waited for the city which has foundations
Hebrews11:11 By faith Sarah herself also received strength to conceive seed
Hebrews11:12 Therefore from one man
Hebrews11:13 These all died in faith
Hebrews11:14 For those who say such things declare plainly that they seek a homeland.
Hebrews11:15 And truly if they had called to mind that country from which they had come out
Hebrews11:16 But now they desire a better
Hebrews11:17 By faith Abraham
Hebrews11:18 of whom it was said
Hebrews11:19 concluding that God was able to raise him up
Hebrews11:20 By faith Isaac blessed Jacob and Esau concerning things to come.
Hebrews11:21 By faith Jacob
Hebrews11:22 By faith Joseph
Hebrews11:23 By faith Moses
Hebrews11:24 By faith Moses
Hebrews11:25 choosing rather to suffer affliction with the people of God than to enjoy the passing pleasures of sin
Hebrews11:26 esteeming the reproach of Christ greater riches than the treasures in Egypt; for he looked to the reward.
Hebrews11:27 By faith he forsook Egypt
Hebrews11:28 By faith he kept the Passover and the sprinkling of blood
Hebrews11:29 By faith they passed through the Red Sea as by dry land
Hebrews11:30 By faith the walls of Jericho fell down after they were encircled for seven days.
Hebrews11:31 By faith the harlot Rahab did not perish with those who did not believe
Hebrews11:32 And what more shall I say? For the time would fail me to tell of Gideon and Barak and Samson and Jephthah
Hebrews11:33 who through faith subdued kingdoms
Hebrews11:34 quenched the violence of fire
Hebrews11:35 Women received their dead raised to life again. Others were tortured
Hebrews11:36 Still others had trial of mockings and scourgings
Hebrews11:37 They were stoned
Hebrews11:38 of whom the world was not worthy. They wandered in deserts and mountains
Hebrews11:39 And all these
Hebrews11:40 God having provided something better for us
Hebrews12:1 Therefore we also
Hebrews12:2 looking unto Jesus
Hebrews12:3 For consider Him who endured such hostility from sinners against Himself
Hebrews12:4 You have not yet resisted to bloodshed
Hebrews12:5 And you have forgotten the exhortation which speaks to you as to sons: “My son
Hebrews12:6 For whom the Lord loves He chastens
Hebrews12:7 If you endure chastening
Hebrews12:8 But if you are without chastening
Hebrews12:9 Furthermore
Hebrews12:10 For they indeed for a few days chastened us as seemed best to them
Hebrews12:11 Now no chastening seems to be joyful for the present
Hebrews12:12 Therefore strengthen the hands which hang down
Hebrews12:13 and make straight paths for your feet
Hebrews12:14 Pursue peace with all people
Hebrews12:15 looking carefully lest anyone fall short of the grace of God; lest any root of bitterness springing up cause trouble
Hebrews12:16 lest there be any fornicator or profane person like Esau
Hebrews12:17 For you know that afterward
Hebrews12:18 For you have not come to the mountain that may be touched and that burned with fire
Hebrews12:19 and the sound of a trumpet and the voice of words
Hebrews12:20 (For they could not endure what was commanded: “And if so much as a beast touches the mountain
Hebrews12:21 And so terrifying was the sight that Moses said
Hebrews12:22 But you have come to Mount Zion and to the city of the living God
Hebrews12:23 to the general assembly and church of the firstborn who are registered in heaven

Hebrews12:28 Therefore
Hebrews12:29 For our God is a consuming fire.
Hebrews13:1 Let brotherly love continue.
Hebrews13:2 Do not forget to entertain strangers
Hebrews13:3 Remember the prisoners as if chained with them—those who are mistreated—since you yourselves are in the body also.
Hebrews13:4 Marriage is honorable among all
Hebrews13:5 Let your conduct be without covetousness; be content with such things as you have. For He Himself has said
Hebrews13:6 So we may boldly say: “The Lord is my helper; I will not fear. What can man do to me?”
Hebrews13:7 Remember those who rule over you
Hebrews13:8 Jesus Christ is the same yesterday
Hebrews13:9 Do not be carried about with various and strange doctrines. For it is good that the heart be established by grace
Hebrews13:10 We have an altar from which those who serve the tabernacle have no right to eat.
Hebrews13:11 For the bodies of those animals
Hebrews13:12 Therefore Jesus also
Hebrews13:13 Therefore let us go forth to Him
Hebrews13:14 For here we have no continuing city
Hebrews13:15 Therefore by Him let us continually offer the sacrifice of praise to God
Hebrews13:16 But do not forget to do good and to share
Hebrews13:17 Obey those who rule over you
Hebrews13:18 Pray for us; for we are confident that we have a good conscience
Hebrews13:19 But I especially urge you to do this
Hebrews13:20 Now may the God of peace who brought up our Lord Jesus from the dead
Hebrews13:21 make you complete in every good work to do His will
Hebrews13:22 And I appeal to you
Hebrews13:23 Know that our brother Timothy has been set free
Hebrews13:24 Greet all those who rule over you
Hebrews13:25 Grace be with you all. Amen.
James1:1 James
James1:2 My brethren
James1:3 knowing that the testing of your faith produces patience.
James1:4 But let patience have its perfect work
James1:5 If any of you lacks wisdom
James1:6 But let him ask in faith
James1:7 For let not that man suppose that he will receive anything from the Lord;
James1:8 he is a double-minded man
James1:9 Let the lowly brother glory in his exaltation
James1:10 but the rich in his humiliation
James1:11 For no sooner has the sun risen with a burning heat than it withers the grass; its flower falls
James1:12 Blessed is the man who endures temptation; for when he has been approved
James1:13 Let no one say when he is tempted
James1:14 But each one is tempted when he is drawn away by his own desires and enticed.
James1:15 Then
James1:16 Do not be deceived
James1:17 Every good gift and every perfect gift is from above
James1:18 Of His own will He brought us forth by the word of truth
James1:19 So then
James1:20 for the wrath of man does not produce the righteousness of God.
James1:21 Therefore lay aside all filthiness and overflow of wickedness
James1:22 But be doers of the word
James1:23 For if anyone is a hearer of the word and not a doer
James1:24 for he observes himself
James1:25 But he who looks into the perfect law of liberty and continues in it
James1:26 If anyone among you thinks he is religious
James1:27 Pure and undefiled religion before God and the Father is this: to visit orphans and widows in their trouble
James2:1 My brethren
James2:2 For if there should come into your assembly a man with gold rings
James2:3 and you pay attention to the one wearing the fine clothes and say to him
James2:4 have you not shown partiality among yourselves
James2:5 Listen
James2:6 But you have dishonored the poor man. Do not the rich oppress you and drag you into the courts?
James2:7 Do they not blaspheme that noble name by which you are called?
James2:8 If you really fulfill the royal law according to the Scripture
James2:9 but if you show partiality
James2:10 For whoever shall keep the whole law
James2:11 For He who said
James2:12 So speak and so do as those who will be judged by the law of liberty.
James2:13 For judgment is without mercy to the one who has shown no mercy. Mercy triumphs over judgment.
James2:14 What does it profit
James2:15 If a brother or sister is naked and destitute of daily food
James2:16 and one of you says to them
James2:17 Thus also faith by itself
James2:18 But someone will say
James2:19 You believe that there is one God. You do well. Even the demons believe—and tremble!
James2:20 But do you want to know
James2:21 Was not Abraham our father justified by works when he offered Isaac his son on the altar?
James2:22 Do you see that faith was working together with his works
James2:23 And the Scripture was fulfilled which says
James2:24 You see then that a man is justified by works
James2:25 Likewise
James2:26 For as the body without the spirit is dead
James3:1 My brethren
James3:2 For we all stumble in many things. If anyone does not stumble in word
James3:3 Indeed
James3:4 Look also at ships: although they are so large and are driven by fierce winds
James3:5 Even so the tongue is a little member and boasts great things. See how great a forest a little fire kindles!
James3:6 And the tongue is a fire
James3:7 For every kind of beast and bird
James3:8 But no man can tame the tongue. It is an unruly evil
James3:9 With it we bless our God and Father
James3:10 Out of the same mouth proceed blessing and cursing. My brethren
James3:11 Does a spring send forth fresh water and bitter from the same opening?
James3:12 Can a fig tree
James3:13 Who is wise and understanding among you? Let him show by good conduct that his works are done in the meekness of wisdom.
James3:14 But if you have bitter envy and self-seeking in your hearts
James3:15 This wisdom does not descend from above
James3:16 For where envy and self-seeking exist
James3:17 But the wisdom that is from above is first pure
James3:18 Now the fruit of righteousness is sown in peace by those who make peace.
James4:1 Where do wars and fights come from among you? Do they not come from your desires for pleasure that war in your members?
James4:2 You lust and do not have. You murder and covet and cannot obtain. You fight and war. Yet you do not have because you do not ask.
James4:3 You ask and do not receive
James4:4 Adulterers and adulteresses! Do you not know that friendship with the world is enmity with God? Whoever therefore wants to be a friend of the world makes himself an enemy of God.
James4:5 Or do you think that the Scripture says in vain
James4:6 But He gives more grace. Therefore He says: “God resists the proud
James4:7 Therefore submit to God. Resist the devil and he will flee from you.
James4:8 Draw near to God and He will draw near to you. Cleanse your hands
James4:9 Lament and mourn and weep! Let your laughter be turned to mourning and your joy to gloom.
James4:10 Humble yourselves in the sight of the Lord
James4:11 Do not speak evil of one another
James4:12 There is one Lawgiver
James4:13 Come now
James4:14 whereas you do not know what will happen tomorrow. For what is your life? It is even a vapor that appears for a little time and then vanishes away.
James4:15 Instead you ought to say
James4:16 But now you boast in your arrogance. All such boasting is evil.
James4:17 Therefore

James5:2 Your riches are corrupted
James5:3 Your gold and silver are corroded

James5:8 You also be patient. Establish your hearts

James5:10 My brethren
James5:11 Indeed we count them blessed who endure. You have heard of the perseverance of Job and seen the end intended by the Lord—that the Lord is very compassionate and merciful.
James5:12 But above all
James5:13 Is anyone among you suffering? Let him pray. Is anyone cheerful? Let him sing psalms.
James5:14 Is anyone among you sick? Let him call for the elders of the church
James5:15 And the prayer of faith will save the sick

James5:17 Elijah was a man with a nature like ours
James5:18 And he prayed again
James5:19 Brethren
James5:20 let him know that he who turns a sinner from the error of his way will save a soul from death and cover a multitude of sins.

Revelation1:12 Then I turned to see the voice that spoke with me. And having turned I saw seven golden lampstands
Revelation1:13 and in the midst of the seven lampstands One like the Son of Man
Revelation1:14 His head and hair were white like wool
Revelation1:15 His feet were like fine brass

Revelation1:17 And when I saw Him
Revelation1:18 I am He who lives

Revelation1:20 The mystery of the seven stars which you saw in My right hand

Revelation7:2 Then I saw another angel ascending from the east
Revelation7:3 saying
Revelation7:4 And I heard the number of those who were sealed. One hundred and forty-four thousand of all the tribes of the children of Israel were sealed:
Revelation7:5 of the tribe of Judah twelve thousand were sealed; of the tribe of Reuben twelve thousand were sealed; of the tribe of Gad twelve thousand were sealed;
Revelation7:6 of the tribe of Asher twelve thousand were sealed; of the tribe of Naphtali twelve thousand were sealed; of the tribe of Manasseh twelve thousand were sealed;
Revelation7:7 of the tribe of Simeon twelve thousand were sealed; of the tribe of Levi twelve thousand were sealed; of the tribe of Issachar twelve thousand were sealed;
Revelation7:8 of the tribe of Zebulun twelve thousand were sealed; of the tribe of Joseph twelve thousand were sealed; of the tribe of Benjamin twelve thousand were sealed.

Revelation7:10 and crying out with a loud voice

Revelation7:17 for the Lamb who is in the midst of the throne will shepherd them and lead them to living fountains of waters. And God will wipe away every tear from their eyes.”

Revelation8:9 And a third of the living creatures in the sea died

Revelation8:13 And I looked
Revelation9:1 Then the fifth angel sounded: And I saw a star fallen from heaven to the earth. To him was given the key to the bottomless pit.
Revelation9:2 And he opened the bottomless pit
Revelation9:3 Then out of the smoke locusts came upon the earth. And to them was given power
Revelation9:4 They were commanded not to harm the grass of the earth
Revelation9:5 And they were not given authority to kill them
Revelation9:6 In those days men will seek death and will not find it; they will desire to die
Revelation9:7 The shape of the locusts was like horses prepared for battle. On their heads were crowns of something like gold
Revelation9:8 They had hair like women’s hair
Revelation9:9 And they had breastplates like breastplates of iron
Revelation9:10 They had tails like scorpions
Revelation9:11 And they had as king over them the angel of the bottomless pit
Revelation9:12 One woe is past. Behold
Revelation9:13 Then the sixth angel sounded: And I heard a voice from the four horns of the golden altar which is before God
Revelation9:14 saying to the sixth angel who had the trumpet
Revelation9:15 So the four angels
Revelation9:16 Now the number of the army of the horsemen was two hundred million; I heard the number of them.
Revelation9:17 And thus I saw the horses in the vision: those who sat on them had breastplates of fiery red
Revelation9:18 By these three plagues a third of mankind was killed—by the fire and the smoke and the brimstone which came out of their mouths.
Revelation9:19 For their power is in their mouth and in their tails; for their tails are like serpents
Revelation9:20 But the rest of mankind
Revelation9:21 And they did not repent of their murders or their sorceries or their sexual immorality or their thefts.

Revelation12:2 Then being with child

Revelation12:5 She bore a male Child who was to rule all nations with a rod of iron. And her Child was caught up to God and His throne.
